[SKIP] test_syntax_validity_after_deterministic[gsh_11_scoring_engine.sas-/******************************************************************************\n * PROGRAM:     gsh_11_scoring_engine.sas\n * PURPOSE:     Multi-model scoring engine for insurance underwriting\n * ENGINE VER:  4.2.1\n * EFFECTIVE:   2026-01-01\n * ACTUARY:     J. Richardson, FCAS, MAAA \u2014 Signed off 2025-12-15\n * DESCRIPTION: Implements a comprehensive scoring pipeline that combines\n *              territory risk, claims history, credit scoring, and model\n *              monitoring into a unified premium calculation engine.\n *              Supports AUTO, HOME, and COMMERCIAL lines of business.\n * REVISION:    4.2.1 \u2014 Added Gini drift detection and competitive positioning\n * COPYRIGHT:   (c) 2026 Actuarial Analytics Division\n ******************************************************************************/\n\n/* ============================================================================\n   SECTION 1: ENVIRONMENT SETUP \u2014 Libraries and global parameters\n   ============================================================================ */\n\n/* Connect to underwriting and actuarial data stores */\nLIBNAME policy  '/data/underwriting/policy'  ACCESS=READONLY;\nLIBNAME claims  '/data/underwriting/claims'  ACCESS=READONLY;\nLIBNAME actuary '/data/actuarial/models'     ACCESS=READONLY;\nLIBNAME scoredb '/data/scoring/output';\n\n/* Global processing options for scoring run */\nOPTIONS MPRINT MLOGIC SYMBOLGEN COMPRESS=YES\n        FULLSTIMER NOFMTERR MINOPERATOR\n        ERRORABEND;\n\n/* --- Model version and effective date parameters --- */\n%LET model_version    = 4.2.1;\n%LET effective_date   = 01JAN2026;\n%LET rundate          = %SYSFUNC(TODAY(), DATE9.);\n%LET min_premium      = 250;\n%LET max_premium      = 50000;\n%LET score_floor      = 300;\n%LET score_ceiling    = 850;\n%LET gini_threshold   = 0.05;\n%LET monitoring_window = 12;\n%LET n_sub_territories = 5;\n\n/* --- Load external scoring function library --- */\n%INCLUDE '/config/models/score_functions.sas';\n\n/* ============================================================================\n   SECTION 2: MACRO \u2014 load_model_params\n   Purpose: Load model coefficients from the actuarial parameter table\n            and create macro variables for downstream scoring macros.\n   ============================================================================ */\n\n%MACRO load_model_params(model_name=, version=);\n\n    %PUT NOTE: Loading parameters for model=&model_name version=&version;\n\n    /* Validate version format before proceeding */\n    %IF %LENGTH(&version) = 0 %THEN %DO;\n        %PUT ERROR: Version parameter is required for load_model_params;\n        %ABORT CANCEL;\n    %END;\n\n    /* Extract coefficients for the specified model and version */\n    PROC SQL NOPRINT;\n        SELECT coefficient_name,\n               coefficient_value,\n               coefficient_type,\n               effective_from,\n               effective_to\n        INTO :coeff_names  SEPARATED BY '|',\n             :coeff_values SEPARATED BY '|',\n             :coeff_types  SEPARATED BY '|',\n             :eff_from     SEPARATED BY '|',\n             :eff_to       SEPARATED BY '|'\n        FROM actuary.model_parameters\n        WHERE model_id    = "&model_name"\n          AND version_id  = "&version"\n          AND status       = 'ACTIVE'\n        ORDER BY coefficient_order;\n\n        /* Store count of loaded coefficients */\n        %LET n_coefficients = &SQLOBS;\n    QUIT;\n\n    %PUT NOTE: Found &n_coefficients coefficients for &model_name v&version;\n\n    /* Create individual macro variables from each coefficient row */\n    DATA _NULL_;\n        LENGTH cname $32 cvalue 8 ctype $10;\n        %DO i = 1 %TO &n_coefficients;\n            cname  = SCAN("&coeff_names",  &i, '|');\n            cvalue = INPUT(SCAN("&coeff_values", &i, '|'), BEST12.);\n            ctype  = SCAN("&coeff_types",  &i, '|');\n\n            /* Store coefficient value and type as macro variables */\n            CALL SYMPUT(STRIP(cname), STRIP(PUT(cvalue, BEST12.)));\n            CALL SYMPUT(STRIP(cname) || '_type', STRIP(ctype));\n        %END;\n    RUN;\n\n    /* Version validation: check that coefficients are within effective range */\n    %IF "&effective_date"d < "&eff_from"d %THEN %DO;\n        %PUT WARNING: Model &model_name v&version is not yet effective;\n    %END;\n\n%MEND load_model_params;\n\n/* ============================================================================\n   SECTION 3: MACRO \u2014 territory_score\n   Purpose: Calculate geographic risk scores based on state and\n            sub-territory definitions from the actuarial tables.\n   ============================================================================ */\n\n%MACRO territory_score(state=);\n\n    %PUT NOTE: Computing territory score for state=&state;\n\n    /* -----------------------------------------------------------------\n       Assign base state-level risk factor using SELECT/WHEN construct.\n       Each state maps to a risk tier and numeric factor.\n       ----------------------------------------------------------------- */\n    DATA work.state_factor;\n        SET policy.policy_master(WHERE=(policy_state = "&state"));\n\n        LENGTH state_risk_factor 8 state_tier $10;\n\n        SELECT (policy_state);\n            WHEN ('FL') DO; state_risk_factor = 1.85; state_tier = 'HIGH';     END;\n            WHEN ('CA') DO; state_risk_factor = 1.62; state_tier = 'HIGH';     END;\n            WHEN ('TX') DO; state_risk_factor = 1.45; state_tier = 'MEDIUM';   END;\n            WHEN ('NY') DO; state_risk_factor = 1.55; state_tier = 'HIGH';     END;\n            WHEN ('IL') DO; state_risk_factor = 1.20; state_tier = 'MEDIUM';   END;\n            WHEN ('OH') DO; state_risk_factor = 1.05; state_tier = 'LOW';      END;\n            WHEN ('PA') DO; state_risk_factor = 1.10; state_tier = 'LOW';      END;\n            WHEN ('GA') DO; state_risk_factor = 1.30; state_tier = 'MEDIUM';   END;\n            WHEN ('NC') DO; state_risk_factor = 1.25; state_tier = 'MEDIUM';   END;\n            OTHERWISE   DO; state_risk_factor = 1.00; state_tier = 'BASELINE'; END;\n        END;\n    RUN;\n\n    /* Join state factors with territory definitions for granular geo risk */\n    PROC SQL;\n        CREATE TABLE work.territory_detail AS\n        SELECT a.policy_id,\n               a.policy_state,\n               a.zip_code,\n               a.state_risk_factor,\n               a.state_tier,\n               b.territory_code,\n               b.territory_factor,\n               b.catastrophe_zone,\n               b.urban_rural_ind,\n               a.state_risk_factor * b.territory_factor AS combined_geo_factor\n        FROM work.state_factor AS a\n        INNER JOIN actuary.territory_defs AS b\n            ON a.policy_state = b.state_code\n           AND a.zip_code BETWEEN b.zip_start AND b.zip_end\n        ORDER BY a.policy_id;\n    QUIT;\n\n    /* -----------------------------------------------------------------\n       Loop over sub-territories to apply micro-level adjustments.\n       Each sub-territory receives a progressive loading factor.\n       ----------------------------------------------------------------- */\n    %DO sub = 1 %TO &n_sub_territories;\n\n        DATA work.sub_territory_&sub;\n            SET work.territory_detail(WHERE=(territory_code = "&sub"));\n\n            /* Apply sub-territory specific loading factor */\n            sub_terr_loading = 1.0 + (0.02 * &sub);\n            adjusted_geo_factor = combined_geo_factor * sub_terr_loading;\n\n            /* Regulatory cap: geographic factor must stay within bounds */\n            IF adjusted_geo_factor > 3.50 THEN adjusted_geo_factor = 3.50;\n            IF adjusted_geo_factor < 0.50 THEN adjusted_geo_factor = 0.50;\n\n            /* Flag records that hit the regulatory cap */\n            IF adjusted_geo_factor IN (3.50, 0.50) THEN\n                geo_cap_flag = 'Y';\n            ELSE\n                geo_cap_flag = 'N';\n        RUN;\n\n    %END;\n\n    /* Consolidate all sub-territory results into a single dataset */\n    DATA work.territory_scores;\n        SET work.sub_territory_1\n            work.sub_territory_2\n            work.sub_territory_3\n            work.sub_territory_4\n            work.sub_territory_5;\n    RUN;\n\n    %PUT NOTE: Territory scoring complete for state=&state;\n\n%MEND territory_score;\n\n/* ============================================================================\n   SECTION 4: MACRO \u2014 claim_score\n   Purpose: Calculate claims-based risk scores by line of business (LOB).\n            Each LOB uses a distinct actuarial model:\n              AUTO       \u2014 Frequency/Severity model\n              HOME       \u2014 Catastrophe exposure model\n              COMMERCIAL \u2014 Multi-peril array-based model\n   ============================================================================ */\n\n%MACRO claim_score(lob=);\n\n    %PUT NOTE: Computing claims score for LOB=&lob;\n\n    /* =====================================================================\n       AUTO LINE: Frequency/Severity model\n       Uses retained accumulators to compute per-policy claim metrics.\n       ===================================================================== */\n    %IF &lob = AUTO %THEN %DO;\n\n        DATA work.auto_claims_score;\n            SET claims.auto_claims;\n            BY policy_id claim_date;\n\n            /* Frequency component: count of claims in experience period */\n            RETAIN claim_count 0 total_incurred 0;\n            IF FIRST.policy_id THEN DO;\n                claim_count    = 0;\n                total_incurred = 0;\n            END;\n\n            claim_count    + 1;\n            total_incurred + incurred_amount;\n\n            /* Severity component: average claim size at policy level */\n            IF LAST.policy_id THEN DO;\n                avg_severity = total_incurred / claim_count;\n\n                /* Frequency score: exponential decay penalizes many claims */\n                freq_score = EXP(-0.5 * claim_count) * 100;\n\n                /* Severity score: log-transformed, penalizes large losses */\n                IF avg_severity > 0 THEN\n                    sev_score = MAX(0, 100 - 15 * LOG(avg_severity / 1000));\n                ELSE\n                    sev_score = 100;\n\n                /* Combined auto score: weighted 60/40 freq vs severity */\n                auto_claim_score = 0.6 * freq_score + 0.4 * sev_score;\n                OUTPUT;\n            END;\n        RUN;\n\n    %END;\n\n    /* =====================================================================\n       HOME LINE: Catastrophe exposure model\n       Separates catastrophe losses from attritional losses for scoring.\n       ===================================================================== */\n    %IF &lob = HOME %THEN %DO;\n\n        DATA work.home_claims_score;\n            SET claims.home_claims;\n            BY policy_id;\n\n            /* Retained accumulators for loss separation */\n            RETAIN cat_exposure 0 non_cat_loss 0;\n\n            IF FIRST.policy_id THEN DO;\n                cat_exposure  = 0;\n                non_cat_loss  = 0;\n            END;\n\n            /* Separate catastrophe vs attritional losses */\n            IF catastrophe_ind = 'Y' THEN\n                cat_exposure + incurred_amount;\n            ELSE\n                non_cat_loss + incurred_amount;\n\n            IF LAST.policy_id THEN DO;\n                /* Catastrophe loading: log-based score with $10K threshold */\n                cat_score = MAX(0, 100 - 20 * LOG(1 + cat_exposure / 10000));\n\n                /* Attritional loss score: linear decay at $5K per point */\n                attrit_score = MAX(0, 100 - 10 * (non_cat_loss / 5000));\n\n                /* Blended homeowners score: 55% cat / 45% attritional */\n                home_claim_score = 0.55 * cat_score + 0.45 * attrit_score;\n                OUTPUT;\n            END;\n        RUN;\n\n    %END;\n\n    /* =====================================================================\n       COMMERCIAL LINE: Multi-peril scoring with ARRAY processing\n       Scores six peril types independently and blends with weights.\n       ===================================================================== */\n    %IF &lob = COMMERCIAL %THEN %DO;\n\n        DATA work.commercial_claims_score;\n            SET claims.commercial_claims;\n            BY policy_id;\n\n            /* Define peril arrays for multi-peril scoring */\n            ARRAY peril_losses{6} fire_loss theft_loss liability_loss\n                                  flood_loss wind_loss other_loss;\n            ARRAY peril_weights{6} _TEMPORARY_ (0.25 0.15 0.30 0.10 0.10 0.10);\n            ARRAY peril_scores{6}  fire_scr theft_scr liab_scr\n                                   flood_scr wind_scr other_scr;\n\n            /* Retain peril accumulators across observations */\n            RETAIN fire_loss theft_loss liability_loss\n                   flood_loss wind_loss other_loss 0;\n\n            IF FIRST.policy_id THEN DO;\n                DO k = 1 TO 6;\n                    peril_losses{k} = 0;\n                END;\n            END;\n\n            /* Accumulate losses into the appropriate peril bucket */\n            SELECT (peril_code);\n                WHEN ('FIRE')      fire_loss      + incurred_amount;\n                WHEN ('THEFT')     theft_loss     + incurred_amount;\n                WHEN ('LIABILITY') liability_loss + incurred_amount;\n                WHEN ('FLOOD')     flood_loss     + incurred_amount;\n                WHEN ('WIND')      wind_loss      + incurred_amount;\n                OTHERWISE          other_loss     + incurred_amount;\n            END;\n\n            IF LAST.policy_id THEN DO;\n                /* Score each peril independently and compute weighted composite */\n                commercial_claim_score = 0;\n                DO k = 1 TO 6;\n                    IF peril_losses{k} > 0 THEN\n                        peril_scores{k} = MAX(0, 100 - 12 * LOG(peril_losses{k} / 2000));\n                    ELSE\n                        peril_scores{k} = 100;\n\n                    commercial_claim_score + peril_weights{k} * peril_scores{k};\n                END;\n                OUTPUT;\n            END;\n        RUN;\n\n    %END;\n\n    /* -----------------------------------------------------------------\n       Loss ratio benchmarking: compute actual loss ratios by year\n       for the given LOB to establish performance baselines.\n       ----------------------------------------------------------------- */\n    PROC MEANS DATA=claims.all_claims(WHERE=(lob_code = "&lob"))\n               NOPRINT NWAY;\n        CLASS accident_year;\n        VAR incurred_amount earned_premium;\n        OUTPUT OUT=work.loss_ratio_bench(DROP=_TYPE_ _FREQ_)\n               SUM(incurred_amount)  = total_incurred\n               SUM(earned_premium)   = total_premium\n               MEAN(incurred_amount) = avg_claim;\n    RUN;\n\n    /* -----------------------------------------------------------------\n       Claims trend analysis: use LAG functions to detect deterioration\n       or improvement in the loss ratio over a multi-year horizon.\n       ----------------------------------------------------------------- */\n    DATA work.claims_trend;\n        SET work.loss_ratio_bench;\n\n        /* Compute current-year loss ratio */\n        loss_ratio = total_incurred / total_premium;\n\n        /* Lag values for 3-year trend detection */\n        prev_lr_1 = LAG1(loss_ratio);\n        prev_lr_2 = LAG2(loss_ratio);\n        prev_lr_3 = LAG3(loss_ratio);\n\n        /* Simple linear trend slope over 3 periods */\n        IF _N_ >= 4 THEN DO;\n            trend_slope = (loss_ratio - prev_lr_3) / 3;\n\n            /* Classify trend direction */\n            IF trend_slope > 0.05 THEN\n                trend_flag = 'DETERIORATING';\n            ELSE IF trend_slope < -0.05 THEN\n                trend_flag = 'IMPROVING';\n            ELSE\n                trend_flag = 'STABLE';\n        END;\n    RUN;\n\n    %PUT NOTE: Claims scoring complete for LOB=&lob;\n\n%MEND claim_score;\n\n/* ============================================================================\n   SECTION 5: MACRO \u2014 credit_score_blend\n   Purpose: Blend external bureau credit scores with internal behavioral\n            scores using configurable weights, with normalization and\n            floor/ceiling enforcement.\n   ============================================================================ */\n\n%MACRO credit_score_blend(weight_bureau=, weight_internal=);\n\n    %PUT NOTE: Blending credit scores (bureau=&weight_bureau, internal=&weight_internal);\n\n    /* Validate weights sum to 1.0 */\n    %LET total_weight = %SYSEVALF(&weight_bureau + &weight_internal);\n    %IF &total_weight NE 1.0 %THEN %DO;\n        %PUT WARNING: Credit blend weights sum to &total_weight \u2014 normalizing;\n    %END;\n\n    /* -----------------------------------------------------------------\n       Merge bureau and internal credit data by policy, then compute\n       a weighted blend with adjustments for payment history and tenure.\n       ----------------------------------------------------------------- */\n    DATA work.credit_blended;\n        MERGE policy.bureau_credit  (IN=a KEEP=policy_id bureau_score bureau_date)\n              policy.internal_score (IN=b KEEP=policy_id internal_score payment_score\n                                                         tenure_months);\n        BY policy_id;\n\n        /* Retain for tracking score band transitions */\n        RETAIN prev_blended_score . score_band_count 0;\n\n        /* Keep only records present in both sources */\n        IF a AND b;\n\n        /* Normalize bureau score to 0\u2013100 scale using floor/ceiling */\n        IF bureau_score >= &score_floor AND bureau_score <= &score_ceiling THEN\n            norm_bureau = (bureau_score - &score_floor) /\n                          (&score_ceiling - &score_floor) * 100;\n        ELSE IF bureau_score < &score_floor THEN\n            norm_bureau = 0;\n        ELSE\n            norm_bureau = 100;\n\n        /* Normalize internal score (already 0\u2013100 scale, enforce bounds) */\n        norm_internal = MAX(0, MIN(100, internal_score));\n\n        /* Compute weighted blend of normalized scores */\n        blended_score = &weight_bureau * norm_bureau +\n                        &weight_internal * norm_internal;\n\n        /* Payment history bonus: reward excellent payment track record */\n        IF payment_score >= 95 THEN\n            blended_score = MIN(100, blended_score + 5);\n        ELSE IF payment_score < 70 THEN\n            blended_score = MAX(0, blended_score - 10);\n\n        /* Tenure adjustment: reward long-standing policyholders */\n        IF tenure_months > 60 THEN\n            blended_score = MIN(100, blended_score + 3);\n        ELSE IF tenure_months > 36 THEN\n            blended_score = MIN(100, blended_score + 1);\n\n        /* Final floor and ceiling enforcement */\n        IF blended_score < 0   THEN blended_score = 0;\n        IF blended_score > 100 THEN blended_score = 100;\n\n        /* Assign score band based on blended score thresholds */\n        LENGTH score_band $14;\n        IF blended_score >= 80      THEN score_band = 'PREFERRED';\n        ELSE IF blended_score >= 60 THEN score_band = 'STANDARD';\n        ELSE IF blended_score >= 40 THEN score_band = 'NON-STANDARD';\n        ELSE                             score_band = 'HIGH-RISK';\n\n        /* Track band count and retain previous score for transitions */\n        score_band_count + 1;\n        prev_blended_score = blended_score;\n    RUN;\n\n    %PUT NOTE: Credit score blending complete;\n\n%MEND credit_score_blend;\n\n/* ============================================================================\n   SECTION 6: MACRO \u2014 final_premium_calc\n   Purpose: Compute the final premium by applying all rating factors\n            multiplicatively, enforcing min/max bounds, applying\n            discount/surcharge rules, and checking competitive position.\n   ============================================================================ */\n\n%MACRO final_premium_calc(base_rate=);\n\n    %PUT NOTE: Computing final premium with base_rate=&base_rate;\n\n    /* -----------------------------------------------------------------\n       Merge all scoring components into a unified rating table.\n       Territory scores are required; claims and credit are optional.\n       ----------------------------------------------------------------- */\n    DATA work.premium_calc;\n        MERGE work.territory_scores   (IN=a KEEP=policy_id adjusted_geo_factor\n                                                            policy_state)\n              work.auto_claims_score  (IN=b KEEP=policy_id auto_claim_score)\n              work.home_claims_score  (IN=c KEEP=policy_id home_claim_score)\n              work.credit_blended     (IN=d KEEP=policy_id blended_score score_band);\n        BY policy_id;\n\n        /* Must have territory score at minimum */\n        IF a;\n\n        /* Convert claim scores to multiplicative factors */\n        IF auto_claim_score NE . THEN\n            claim_factor = 2.0 - (auto_claim_score / 100);\n        ELSE IF home_claim_score NE . THEN\n            claim_factor = 2.0 - (home_claim_score / 100);\n        ELSE\n            claim_factor = 1.0;\n\n        /* Convert credit score to multiplicative factor */\n        credit_factor = 2.0 - (blended_score / 100);\n        IF credit_factor = . THEN credit_factor = 1.0;\n\n        /* Multiplicative premium calculation: base * geo * claims * credit */\n        raw_premium = &base_rate\n                    * adjusted_geo_factor\n                    * claim_factor\n                    * credit_factor;\n\n        /* Apply discount/surcharge based on score band */\n        %IF "&score_band" = "PREFERRED" %THEN %DO;\n            discount_pct = 0.15;\n        %END;\n        %ELSE %IF "&score_band" = "HIGH-RISK" %THEN %DO;\n            discount_pct = -0.20;  /* Surcharge for high-risk */\n        %END;\n        %ELSE %DO;\n            discount_pct = 0.00;\n        %END;\n\n        adjusted_premium = raw_premium * (1.0 - discount_pct);\n\n        /* Enforce minimum and maximum premium bounds */\n        IF adjusted_premium < &min_premium THEN\n            final_premium = &min_premium;\n        ELSE IF adjusted_premium > &max_premium THEN\n            final_premium = &max_premium;\n        ELSE\n            final_premium = ROUND(adjusted_premium, 1.00);\n\n        /* Flag policies hitting min/max premium boundaries */\n        LENGTH boundary_flag $3;\n        IF final_premium = &min_premium THEN boundary_flag = 'MIN';\n        ELSE IF final_premium = &max_premium THEN boundary_flag = 'MAX';\n        ELSE boundary_flag = ' ';\n    RUN;\n\n    /* -----------------------------------------------------------------\n       Competitive positioning: compare our premiums to market benchmarks\n       to ensure we remain within competitive corridors.\n       ----------------------------------------------------------------- */\n    PROC SQL;\n        CREATE TABLE work.competitive_check AS\n        SELECT a.policy_id,\n               a.final_premium,\n               a.raw_premium,\n               a.score_band,\n               a.policy_state,\n               b.market_avg_premium,\n               b.market_25th_pctl,\n               b.market_75th_pctl,\n               CASE\n                   WHEN a.final_premium < b.market_25th_pctl THEN 'BELOW MARKET'\n                   WHEN a.final_premium > b.market_75th_pctl THEN 'ABOVE MARKET'\n                   ELSE 'COMPETITIVE'\n               END AS market_position\n        FROM work.premium_calc AS a\n        LEFT JOIN actuary.market_benchmarks AS b\n            ON a.policy_state = b.state_code\n        ORDER BY a.policy_id;\n    QUIT;\n\n    %PUT NOTE: Final premium calculation complete;\n\n%MEND final_premium_calc;\n\n/* ============================================================================\n   SECTION 7: MACRO \u2014 model_monitoring\n   Purpose: Track ongoing model performance including:\n            - Actual vs expected loss ratios by accident year\n            - Gini coefficient drift detection\n            - Score stability metrics across rating periods\n   ============================================================================ */\n\n%MACRO model_monitoring;\n\n    %PUT NOTE: Running model monitoring suite;\n\n    /* -----------------------------------------------------------------\n       Part A: Actual vs Expected loss ratios by accident year and LOB.\n       Flags deviations that exceed tolerance thresholds.\n       ----------------------------------------------------------------- */\n    PROC SQL;\n        CREATE TABLE work.actual_vs_expected AS\n        SELECT a.accident_year,\n               a.lob_code,\n               SUM(a.incurred_amount) / SUM(a.earned_premium) AS actual_lr,\n               b.expected_lr,\n               CALCULATED actual_lr - b.expected_lr AS lr_deviation,\n               CASE\n                   WHEN ABS(CALCULATED lr_deviation) > 0.10 THEN 'ALERT'\n                   WHEN ABS(CALCULATED lr_deviation) > 0.05 THEN 'WATCH'\n                   ELSE 'OK'\n               END AS monitoring_status\n        FROM claims.all_claims AS a\n        INNER JOIN actuary.expected_results AS b\n            ON a.accident_year = b.accident_year\n           AND a.lob_code      = b.lob_code\n        WHERE a.accident_year >= YEAR(TODAY()) - &monitoring_window\n        GROUP BY a.accident_year, a.lob_code\n        ORDER BY a.lob_code, a.accident_year;\n    QUIT;\n\n    /* -----------------------------------------------------------------\n       Part B: Gini coefficient drift detection\n       Computes a Lorenz curve to measure model discriminatory power.\n       ----------------------------------------------------------------- */\n    DATA work.gini_drift;\n        SET work.competitive_check END=last;\n        BY policy_id;\n\n        /* Cumulative distribution trackers for Lorenz curve */\n        RETAIN cum_policies 0 cum_losses 0\n               total_policies 0 total_losses 0\n               prev_pct_pol 0 gini_area 0;\n\n        /* Initialize totals on first observation */\n        IF _N_ = 1 THEN DO;\n            total_policies = n_policies;\n            total_losses   = n_losses;\n        END;\n\n        cum_policies + 1;\n        cum_losses   + final_premium;\n\n        /* Compute cumulative percentages */\n        pct_policies = cum_policies / total_policies;\n        pct_losses   = cum_losses / total_losses;\n\n        /* Incremental Lorenz curve area using trapezoidal rule */\n        prev_pct_losses = LAG(pct_losses);\n        IF _N_ > 1 THEN DO;\n            lorenz_area = (pct_policies - prev_pct_pol) *\n                          (pct_losses + prev_pct_losses) / 2;\n            gini_area + lorenz_area;\n        END;\n\n        prev_pct_pol = pct_policies;\n    RUN;\n\n    /* -----------------------------------------------------------------\n       Part C: Score stability metrics across score bands.\n       Summarizes premium distribution and factor averages.\n       ----------------------------------------------------------------- */\n    PROC MEANS DATA=work.premium_calc\n               N MEAN STD MIN MAX P25 P50 P75\n               NOPRINT NWAY;\n        CLASS score_band;\n        VAR final_premium adjusted_geo_factor claim_factor credit_factor;\n        OUTPUT OUT=work.score_stability(DROP=_TYPE_ _FREQ_)\n               MEAN(final_premium)        = avg_premium\n               STD(final_premium)         = std_premium\n               MEAN(adjusted_geo_factor)  = avg_geo\n               MEAN(claim_factor)         = avg_claim\n               MEAN(credit_factor)        = avg_credit\n               N(final_premium)           = n_policies;\n    RUN;\n\n    /* Final Gini computation and drift check */\n    DATA work.gini_summary;\n        SET work.gini_drift END=last;\n        RETAIN gini_coeff 0;\n\n        gini_coeff + lorenz_area;\n\n        IF last THEN DO;\n            /* Gini = 1 - 2 * area under Lorenz curve */\n            gini_coeff = 1 - 2 * gini_coeff;\n\n            /* Check drift against baseline Gini of 0.40 */\n            IF ABS(gini_coeff - 0.40) > &gini_threshold THEN\n                drift_flag = 'DRIFT DETECTED';\n            ELSE\n                drift_flag = 'STABLE';\n\n            OUTPUT;\n        END;\n    RUN;\n\n    %PUT NOTE: Model monitoring complete;\n\n%MEND model_monitoring;\n\n/* ============================================================================\n   SECTION 8: MAIN PROGRAM \u2014 Execute the scoring pipeline\n   Purpose: Orchestrate the full scoring engine by invoking each macro\n            in sequence: load parameters, score territory/claims/credit,\n            calculate premiums, run monitoring, and produce diagnostics.\n   ============================================================================ */\n\n/* --- Step 1: Load model parameters for the current version --- */\n%load_model_params(model_name=UW_SCORE, version=&model_version);\n\n/* --- Step 2: Compute territory scores for key underwriting states --- */\n%territory_score(state=FL);\n%territory_score(state=CA);\n%territory_score(state=TX);\n\n/* --- Step 3: Compute claims scores for each line of business --- */\n%claim_score(lob=AUTO);\n%claim_score(lob=HOME);\n%claim_score(lob=COMMERCIAL);\n\n/* --- Step 4: Blend credit scores with configured weights --- */\n%credit_score_blend(weight_bureau=0.65, weight_internal=0.35);\n\n/* --- Step 5: Calculate final premiums with base rate --- */\n%final_premium_calc(base_rate=1200);\n\n/* --- Step 6: Run model monitoring diagnostics --- */\n%model_monitoring;\n\n/* --- Score distribution analysis: frequency tables --- */\nPROC FREQ DATA=work.premium_calc;\n    TABLES score_band / NOCUM;\n    TABLES boundary_flag / NOCUM;\n    TITLE "Score Band Distribution \u2014 Model v&model_version";\nRUN;\n\n/* --- Summary statistics for final premiums by score band --- */\nPROC MEANS DATA=work.premium_calc\n           N MEAN STD MIN P25 P50 P75 MAX;\n    CLASS score_band;\n    VAR final_premium raw_premium;\n    TITLE "Premium Summary by Score Band";\nRUN;\n\n/* --- Write final scored output to permanent scoring library --- */\nDATA scoredb.scored_policies;\n    SET work.premium_calc;\n\n    /* Attach metadata to each scored record */\n    LENGTH model_version_lbl $10 engine_version $10;\n    model_version_lbl = "&model_version";\n    scoring_date      = TODAY();\n    engine_version    = "4.2.1";\n\n    FORMAT scoring_date DATE9.;\nRUN;\n\n/* --- Error handling: validate output record counts --- */\nPROC SQL NOPRINT;\n    SELECT COUNT(*) INTO :n_scored TRIMMED\n    FROM scoredb.scored_policies;\nQUIT;\n\n%IF &n_scored = 0 %THEN %DO;\n    %PUT ERROR: Scoring engine produced zero records \u2014 aborting pipeline;\n    %ABORT CANCEL;\n%END;\n%ELSE %DO;\n    %PUT NOTE: ========================================================;\n    %PUT NOTE: Scoring engine complete;\n    %PUT NOTE: Policies scored: &n_scored;\n    %PUT NOTE: Model version:   &model_version;\n    %PUT NOTE: Run date:        &rundate;\n    %PUT NOTE: ========================================================;\n%END;\n\n/* End of scoring engine */\n-gold_meta25]

Chen, CDMP \u2014 Chief Data Steward\n * DOMAIN:      Enterprise Data Quality Management\n * DESCRIPTION: Implements a comprehensive data governance framework that\n *              performs automated data profiling, completeness validation,\n *              business rule enforcement, cross-source consistency checks,\n *              and timeliness monitoring. Produces a weighted composite\n *              quality scorecard with ODS Excel reporting.\n * REVISION:    3.1.0 \u2014 Added cross-source consistency and timeliness checks\n * COPYRIGHT:   (c) 2026 Data Governance Office\n ******************************************************************************/\n\n/* ============================================================================\n   SECTION 1: ENVIRONMENT SETUP \u2014 Libraries and global parameters\n   ============================================================================ */\n\n/* Connect to source systems and quality output stores */\nLIBNAME source '/data/enterprise/source'     ACCESS=READONLY;\nLIBNAME dq     '/data/governance/quality';\nLIBNAME meta   '/data/governance/metadata';\nLIBNAME report '/data/governance/reports';\n\n/* Global processing options */\nOPTIONS MPRINT MLOGIC SYMBOLGEN COMPRESS=YES\n        FULLSTIMER NOFMTERR MINOPERATOR;\n\n/* --- Quality threshold parameters --- */\n%LET completeness_threshold = 95;\n%LET validity_threshold     = 98;\n%LET consistency_threshold  = 99;\n%LET freshness_max_days     = 3;\n%LET run_date               = %SYSFUNC(TODAY(), DATE9.);\n%LET run_datetime           = %SYSFUNC(DATETIME(), DATETIME20.);\n%LET dq_version             = 3.1.0;\n%LET steward_name           = M. Chen;\n%LET domain_name            = Enterprise Data Quality;\n\n/* --- Scorecard dimension weights (must sum to 1.0) --- */\n%LET weight_completeness    = 0.30;\n%LET weight_validity        = 0.30;\n%LET weight_consistency     = 0.25;\n%LET weight_timeliness      = 0.15;\n\n/* ============================================================================\n   SECTION 2: MACRO \u2014 profile_table\n   Purpose: Automated data profiling for any table. Captures metadata\n            (variable names, types, lengths), row counts, distinct counts\n            per column, numeric distributions via PROC UNIVARIATE, and\n            categorical frequency analysis via PROC FREQ.\n            Results compiled into a standardized profile dataset.\n   ============================================================================ */\n\n%MACRO profile_table(lib=, table=);\n\n    %PUT NOTE: ========================================;\n    %PUT NOTE: Profiling &lib..&table \u2014 started %SYSFUNC(TIME(), TIME8.);\n    %PUT NOTE: ========================================;\n\n    /* --- Step 2a: Capture variable metadata via PROC CONTENTS --- */\n    PROC CONTENTS DATA=&lib..&table\n                  OUT=work.contents_&table (KEEP=NAME TYPE LENGTH FORMAT LABEL NOBS)\n                  NOPRINT;\n    RUN;\n\n    /* --- Step 2b: Row count and distinct count per column --- */\n    PROC SQL NOPRINT;\n        /* Total row count for the table */\n        SELECT COUNT(*) INTO :total_rows TRIMMED\n        FROM &lib..&table;\n\n        /* Get list of all column names into macro variable */\n        SELECT NAME INTO :col_list SEPARATED BY ' '\n        FROM work.contents_&table;\n\n        %LET n_cols = &SQLOBS;\n    QUIT;\n\n    /* Loop over each column to compute distinct value counts */\n    %DO i = 1 %TO &n_cols;\n        %LET col_name = %SCAN(&col_list, &i, %STR( ));\n\n        PROC SQL NOPRINT;\n            SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT &col_name) INTO :distinct_&col_name TRIMMED\n            FROM &lib..&table;\n        QUIT;\n    %END;\n\n    /* --- Step 2c: Numeric distribution statistics via PROC UNIVARIATE --- */\n    PROC UNIVARIATE DATA=&lib..&table NOPRINT;\n        VAR _NUMERIC_;\n        OUTPUT OUT=work.univar_&table\n            N=n NMISS=nmiss MEAN=mean STD=std\n            MIN=min MAX=max MEDIAN=median\n            Q1=q1 Q3=q3 P1=p1 P99=p99;\n    RUN;\n\n    /* --- Step 2d: Categorical frequency analysis via PROC FREQ --- */\n    PROC FREQ DATA=&lib..&table NOPRINT;\n        TABLES _CHARACTER_ / OUT=work.freq_&table\n                             MISSING NOCUM;\n    RUN;\n\n    /* --- Step 2e: Compile profile into standardized format --- */\n    DATA meta.profile_&table;\n        LENGTH table_name $32 column_name $32 column_type $4\n               total_rows 8 distinct_count 8 null_count 8\n               profile_date 8 profiled_by $20;\n        FORMAT profile_date DATE9.;\n\n        SET work.contents_&table;\n\n        table_name   = "&table";\n        profile_date = TODAY();\n        profiled_by  = "&steward_name";\n\n        /* Map SAS type codes to labels */\n        IF TYPE = 1 THEN column_type = 'NUM';\n        ELSE column_type = 'CHAR';\n\n        total_rows = &total_rows;\n\n        /* Assign distinct count from macro variables */\n        column_name    = NAME;\n        distinct_count = INPUT(SYMGET('distinct_' || STRIP(NAME)), BEST.);\n\n        /* Compute null count as total minus non-null distinct */\n        null_count = total_rows - distinct_count;\n\n        OUTPUT;\n    RUN;\n\n    %PUT NOTE: Profiling &lib..&table \u2014 completed. &n_cols columns, &total_rows rows.;\n\n%MEND profile_table;\n\n/* ============================================================================\n   SECTION 3: MACRO \u2014 check_completeness\n   Purpose: Validates required columns for null/blank values. Computes\n            completeness percentage per column and flags failures against\n            the configurable threshold. Uses %DO loop over required columns\n            and PROC SQL to count nulls/blanks per column.\n   ============================================================================ */\n\n%MACRO check_completeness(lib=, table=, required_cols=);\n\n    %PUT NOTE: ========================================;\n    %PUT NOTE: Completeness check for &lib..&table;\n    %PUT NOTE: Required columns: &required_cols;\n    %PUT NOTE: ========================================;\n\n    /* Count the number of required columns to iterate over */\n    %LET n_req = %SYSFUNC(COUNTW(&required_cols, %STR( )));\n\n    /* Initialize empty results table with correct structure */\n    DATA work.completeness_&table;\n        LENGTH table_name $32 column_name $32\n               total_rows 8 non_null_count 8\n               completeness_pct 8 threshold 8\n               check_status $4 check_date 8;\n        FORMAT completeness_pct 6.2 check_date DATE9.;\n        STOP;\n    RUN;\n\n    /* --- Loop over each required column to check completeness --- */\n    %DO j = 1 %TO &n_req;\n        %LET req_col = %SCAN(&required_cols, &j, %STR( ));\n\n        /* Count nulls and blanks for this column via PROC SQL */\n        PROC SQL NOPRINT;\n            SELECT COUNT(*) INTO :total_count TRIMMED\n            FROM &lib..&table;\n\n            SELECT COUNT(*) INTO :non_null_ct TRIMMED\n            FROM &lib..&table\n            WHERE &req_col IS NOT NULL\n              AND &req_col NE '';\n        QUIT;\n\n        /* Compute completeness percentage and flag pass/fail */\n        DATA work._comp_row;\n            LENGTH table_name $32 column_name $32\n                   total_rows 8 non_null_count 8\n                   completeness_pct 8 threshold 8\n                   check_status $4 check_date 8;\n            FORMAT completeness_pct 6.2 check_date DATE9.;\n\n            table_name      = "&table";\n            column_name     = "&req_col";\n            total_rows      = &total_count;\n            non_null_count  = &non_null_ct;\n\n            /* Calculate completeness as a percentage */\n            IF total_rows > 0 THEN\n                completeness_pct = (non_null_count / total_rows) * 100;\n            ELSE\n                completeness_pct = 0;\n\n            threshold  = &completeness_threshold;\n            check_date = TODAY();\n\n            /* Flag pass or fail against configurable threshold */\n            %IF %SYSEVALF(&non_null_ct / &total_count * 100) < &completeness_threshold %THEN %DO;\n                check_status = 'FAIL';\n                %PUT WARNING: Column &req_col in &table \u2014 completeness below threshold;\n            %END;\n            %ELSE %DO;\n                check_status = 'PASS';\n            %END;\n\n            OUTPUT;\n        RUN;\n\n        /* Append this column result to the completeness results table */\n        PROC APPEND BASE=work.completeness_&table\n                    DATA=work._comp_row FORCE;\n        RUN;\n\n    %END;\n\n    /* Store final completeness results in DQ library */\n    DATA dq.completeness_&table;\n        SET work.completeness_&table;\n    RUN;\n\n    %PUT NOTE: Completeness check for &table completed \u2014 &n_req columns checked.;\n\n%MEND check_completeness;\n\n/* ============================================================================\n   SECTION 4: MACRO \u2014 check_validity\n   Purpose: Validates data against business rules defined in a rules dataset.\n            Reads each rule via SET, uses CALL EXECUTE to generate dynamic\n            validation SQL, then loops over rules to count violations.\n            Results aggregated into a quality scorecard.\n   ============================================================================ */\n\n%MACRO check_validity(lib=, table=, rules_ds=);\n\n    %PUT NOTE: ========================================;\n    %PUT NOTE: Validity check for &lib..&table;\n    %PUT NOTE: Rules dataset: &rules_ds;\n    %PUT NOTE: ========================================;\n\n    /* --- Read the rules table and generate dynamic validation SQL --- */\n    DATA _NULL_;\n        SET &rules_ds END=last;\n\n        /* Each observation contains a business rule definition */\n        LENGTH rule_sql $500 rule_id $10 rule_desc $200\n               target_column $32 rule_type $20;\n\n        /* Generate CALL EXECUTE for each rule validation query */\n        CALL EXECUTE('PROC SQL NOPRINT;');\n        CALL EXECUTE('SELECT COUNT(*) INTO :violations_' || STRIP(rule_id));\n        CALL EXECUTE(' TRIMMED FROM ' || "&lib..&table");\n        CALL EXECUTE(' WHERE ' || STRIP(rule_condition) || ';');\n        CALL EXECUTE('QUIT;');\n\n        /* Track total number of rules for downstream loop */\n        IF last THEN CALL SYMPUT('n_rules', STRIP(PUT(_N_, BEST.)));\n    RUN;\n\n    /* Initialize validity results table */\n    DATA work.validity_&table;\n        LENGTH table_name $32 rule_id $10 rule_desc $200\n               target_column $32 rule_type $20\n               total_rows 8 violation_count 8\n               validity_pct 8 threshold 8\n               check_status $4 check_date 8;\n        FORMAT validity_pct 6.2 check_date DATE9.;\n        STOP;\n    RUN;\n\n    /* --- Loop over rules to collect violation counts and score --- */\n    %DO r = 1 %TO &n_rules;\n\n        /* Read the r-th rule from the rules dataset */\n        DATA work._rule_result;\n            SET &rules_ds (FIRSTOBS=&r OBS=&r);\n\n            LENGTH table_name $32 violation_count 8\n                   validity_pct 8 threshold 8\n                   check_status $4 check_date 8;\n            FORMAT validity_pct 6.2 check_date DATE9.;\n\n            table_name = "&table";\n            check_date = TODAY();\n            threshold  = &validity_threshold;\n\n            /* Retrieve violation count from generated macro variable */\n            violation_count = INPUT(SYMGET('violations_' || STRIP(rule_id)), BEST.);\n        RUN;\n\n        /* Get total row count for percentage calculation */\n        PROC SQL NOPRINT;\n            SELECT COUNT(*) INTO :rule_total TRIMMED\n            FROM &lib..&table;\n        QUIT;\n\n        /* Compute validity percentage and determine pass/fail */\n        DATA work._rule_result;\n            SET work._rule_result;\n            total_rows = &rule_total;\n\n            /* Calculate validity percentage */\n            IF total_rows > 0 THEN\n                validity_pct = ((total_rows - violation_count) / total_rows) * 100;\n            ELSE\n                validity_pct = 0;\n\n            /* Determine pass/fail status against threshold */\n            IF validity_pct >= &validity_threshold THEN\n                check_status = 'PASS';\n            ELSE\n                check_status = 'FAIL';\n        RUN;\n\n        /* Append to validity results */\n        PROC APPEND BASE=work.validity_&table\n                    DATA=work._rule_result FORCE;\n        RUN;\n\n    %END;\n\n    /* Store validity results in DQ library */\n    DATA dq.validity_&table;\n        SET work.validity_&table;\n    RUN;\n\n    %PUT NOTE: Validity check for &table completed \u2014 &n_rules rules evaluated.;\n\n%MEND check_validity;\n\n/* ============================================================================\n   SECTION 5: MACRO \u2014 check_consistency\n   Purpose: Compares two data sources on a shared key to find discrepancies.\n            Uses PROC SQL FULL JOIN and a DATA step to classify records\n            as match, mismatch, missing_left, or missing_right.\n            PROC MEANS computes the overall match rate.\n   ============================================================================ */\n\n%MACRO check_consistency(src1=, src2=, key=, compare_cols=);\n\n    %PUT NOTE: ========================================;\n    %PUT NOTE: Consistency check: &src1 vs &src2;\n    %PUT NOTE: Key: &key | Compare columns: &compare_cols;\n    %PUT NOTE: ========================================;\n\n    /* Count comparison columns for loop processing */\n    %LET n_compare = %SYSFUNC(COUNTW(&compare_cols, %STR( )));\n\n    /* --- Full outer join to detect all discrepancies --- */\n    PROC SQL;\n        CREATE TABLE work.consistency_raw AS\n        SELECT COALESCE(a.&key, b.&key) AS &key,\n               /* Include comparison columns from both left and right sources */\n               %DO c = 1 %TO &n_compare;\n                   %LET comp_col = %SCAN(&compare_cols, &c, %STR( ));\n                   a.&comp_col AS left_&comp_col,\n                   b.&comp_col AS right_&comp_col,\n               %END;\n               /* Classification logic for match status */\n               CASE\n                   WHEN a.&key IS NULL THEN 'MISSING_LEFT'\n                   WHEN b.&key IS NULL THEN 'MISSING_RIGHT'\n                   WHEN 1=1\n                       %DO c = 1 %TO &n_compare;\n                           %LET comp_col = %SCAN(&compare_cols, &c, %STR( ));\n                           AND a.&comp_col = b.&comp_col\n                       %END;\n                   THEN 'MATCH'\n                   ELSE 'MISMATCH'\n               END AS match_status LENGTH=15\n        FROM &src1 AS a\n        FULL JOIN &src2 AS b\n            ON a.&key = b.&key;\n    QUIT;\n\n    /* --- Classify and enrich discrepancy records via DATA step --- */\n    DATA work.consistency_detail (KEEP=&key match_status\n                                       mismatch_columns check_date)\n         work.consistency_mismatches (WHERE=(match_status='MISMATCH'));\n\n        LENGTH match_status $15 mismatch_columns $500 check_date 8;\n        FORMAT check_date DATE9.;\n\n        SET work.consistency_raw;\n        check_date = TODAY();\n\n        /* Build list of mismatched columns for diagnosis */\n        mismatch_columns = '';\n        %DO c = 1 %TO &n_compare;\n            %LET comp_col = %SCAN(&compare_cols, &c, %STR( ));\n            IF left_&comp_col NE right_&comp_col THEN\n                mismatch_columns = CATX(', ', mismatch_columns, "&comp_col");\n        %END;\n\n        OUTPUT work.consistency_detail;\n        IF match_status = 'MISMATCH' THEN OUTPUT work.consistency_mismatches;\n    RUN;\n\n    /* --- Compute match rate statistics via PROC MEANS --- */\n    PROC MEANS DATA=work.consistency_detail NOPRINT NWAY;\n        CLASS match_status;\n        OUTPUT OUT=work.consistency_stats (DROP=_TYPE_ _FREQ_)\n               N=record_count;\n    RUN;\n\n    /* --- Calculate overall match rate via PROC SQL --- */\n    PROC SQL NOPRINT;\n        SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN match_status='MATCH' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) /\n               COUNT(*) * 100 FORMAT=6.2\n        INTO :match_rate TRIMMED\n        FROM work.consistency_detail;\n    QUIT;\n\n    /* Store consistency results in DQ library */\n    DATA dq.consistency_%SCAN(&src1, 2, .)_%SCAN(&src2, 2, .);\n        SET work.consistency_detail;\n        match_rate = &match_rate;\n    RUN;\n\n    %PUT NOTE: Consistency check complete. Match rate: &match_rate.%;\n\n%MEND check_consistency;\n\n/* ============================================================================\n   SECTION 6: MACRO \u2014 check_timeliness\n   Purpose: Checks data freshness by computing the lag between the most\n            recent record date and the current date. Uses PROC SQL to get\n            max date, DATA step with %SYSFUNC to compute lag, and flags\n            stale data when lag exceeds max_lag_days.\n   ============================================================================ */\n\n%MACRO check_timeliness(lib=, table=, date_col=, max_lag_days=);\n\n    %PUT NOTE: ========================================;\n    %PUT NOTE: Timeliness check for &lib..&table;\n    %PUT NOTE: Date column: &date_col | Max lag: &max_lag_days days;\n    %PUT NOTE: ========================================;\n\n    /* --- Get the most recent date value via PROC SQL --- */\n    PROC SQL NOPRINT;\n        SELECT MAX(&date_col) FORMAT=DATE9. INTO :max_date TRIMMED\n        FROM &lib..&table;\n\n        SELECT COUNT(*) INTO :table_rows TRIMMED\n        FROM &lib..&table;\n    QUIT;\n\n    /* --- Compute lag and determine freshness status via DATA step --- */\n    DATA dq.timeliness_&table;\n        LENGTH table_name $32 date_column $32\n               max_date 8 current_date 8 lag_days 8\n               max_allowed_lag 8 freshness_status $10\n               check_date 8 total_rows 8;\n        FORMAT max_date DATE9. current_date DATE9. check_date DATE9.;\n\n        table_name      = "&table";\n        date_column     = "&date_col";\n        max_date        = INPUT("&max_date", DATE9.);\n        current_date    = TODAY();\n        check_date      = TODAY();\n        max_allowed_lag = &max_lag_days;\n        total_rows      = &table_rows;\n\n        /* Calculate the lag in days using INTCK */\n        lag_days = INTCK('DAY', max_date, current_date);\n\n        /* Flag as STALE if lag exceeds maximum allowed days */\n        %IF %SYSEVALF(%SYSFUNC(TODAY()) - %SYSFUNC(INPUTN(&max_date, DATE9.)) > &max_lag_days) %THEN %DO;\n            freshness_status = 'STALE';\n            %PUT WARNING: &table is STALE \u2014 &date_col lag exceeds &max_lag_days days;\n        %END;\n        %ELSE %DO;\n            freshness_status = 'CURRENT';\n        %END;\n\n        OUTPUT;\n    RUN;\n\n    %PUT NOTE: Timeliness check for &table completed. Max date: &max_date;\n\n%MEND check_timeliness;\n\n/* ============================================================================\n   SECTION 7: MACRO \u2014 dq_scorecard\n   Purpose: Compiles all quality check results into a weighted composite\n            score. Uses PROC SQL to aggregate dimension scores, DATA step\n            to compute the weighted composite, PROC REPORT for formatted\n            scorecard, and ODS Excel for output distribution.\n   ============================================================================ */\n\n%MACRO dq_scorecard;\n\n    %PUT NOTE: ========================================;\n    %PUT NOTE: Building DQ Scorecard \u2014 &run_date;\n    %PUT NOTE: ========================================;\n\n    /* --- Aggregate completeness dimension scores via PROC SQL --- */\n    PROC SQL;\n        CREATE TABLE work.score_completeness AS\n        SELECT 'COMPLETENESS' AS dimension LENGTH=20,\n               AVG(completeness_pct) AS dimension_score FORMAT=6.2,\n               SUM(CASE WHEN check_status='FAIL' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fail_count,\n               COUNT(*) AS total_checks,\n               &weight_completeness AS weight\n        FROM dq.completeness_customers\n        OUTER UNION CORR\n        SELECT 'COMPLETENESS', AVG(completeness_pct),\n               SUM(CASE WHEN check_status='FAIL' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END),\n               COUNT(*), &weight_completeness\n        FROM dq.completeness_transactions\n        OUTER UNION CORR\n        SELECT 'COMPLETENESS', AVG(completeness_pct),\n               SUM(CASE WHEN check_status='FAIL' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END),\n               COUNT(*), &weight_completeness\n        FROM dq.completeness_products\n        OUTER UNION CORR\n        SELECT 'COMPLETENESS', AVG(completeness_pct),\n               SUM(CASE WHEN check_status='FAIL' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END),\n               COUNT(*), &weight_completeness\n        FROM dq.completeness_accounts\n        OUTER UNION CORR\n        SELECT 'COMPLETENESS', AVG(completeness_pct),\n               SUM(CASE WHEN check_status='FAIL' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END),\n               COUNT(*), &weight_completeness\n        FROM dq.completeness_orders;\n    QUIT;\n\n    /* --- Aggregate validity dimension scores via PROC SQL --- */\n    PROC SQL;\n        CREATE TABLE work.score_validity AS\n        SELECT 'VALIDITY' AS dimension LENGTH=20,\n               AVG(validity_pct) AS dimension_score FORMAT=6.2,\n               SUM(CASE WHEN check_status='FAIL' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fail_count,\n               COUNT(*) AS total_checks,\n               &weight_validity AS weight\n        FROM dq.validity_customers\n        OUTER UNION CORR\n        SELECT 'VALIDITY', AVG(validity_pct),\n               SUM(CASE WHEN check_status='FAIL' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END),\n               COUNT(*), &weight_validity\n        FROM dq.validity_transactions\n        OUTER UNION CORR\n        SELECT 'VALIDITY', AVG(validity_pct),\n               SUM(CASE WHEN check_status='FAIL' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END),\n               COUNT(*), &weight_validity\n        FROM dq.validity_products;\n    QUIT;\n\n    /* --- Combine all dimension scores into a single table --- */\n    DATA work.dq_dimensions;\n        SET work.score_completeness\n            work.score_validity;\n    RUN;\n\n    /* --- Add consistency scores via PROC SQL INSERT --- */\n    PROC SQL;\n        INSERT INTO work.dq_dimensions\n        SELECT 'CONSISTENCY' AS dimension,\n               match_rate AS dimension_score,\n               CASE WHEN match_rate < &consistency_threshold THEN 1 ELSE 0 END\n                   AS fail_count,\n               1 AS total_checks,\n               &weight_consistency AS weight\n        FROM dq.consistency_customers_ext_customers;\n    QUIT;\n\n    /* --- Add timeliness scores via DATA step --- */\n    DATA work.timeliness_scores;\n        SET dq.timeliness_customers\n            dq.timeliness_transactions\n            dq.timeliness_orders\n            dq.timeliness_accounts;\n\n        LENGTH dimension $20;\n        dimension = 'TIMELINESS';\n\n        /* Score based on freshness status \u2014 degrade if stale */\n        IF freshness_status = 'CURRENT' THEN dimension_score = 100;\n        ELSE dimension_score = MAX(0, 100 - (lag_days - max_allowed_lag) * 10);\n\n        weight       = &weight_timeliness;\n        fail_count   = (freshness_status = 'STALE');\n        total_checks = 1;\n    RUN;\n\n    /* Append timeliness to combined dimensions */\n    PROC APPEND BASE=work.dq_dimensions\n                DATA=work.timeliness_scores (KEEP=dimension dimension_score\n                     fail_count total_checks weight) FORCE;\n    RUN;\n\n    /* --- Compute weighted composite score via DATA step --- */\n    DATA work.composite_score;\n        SET work.dq_dimensions END=last;\n\n        RETAIN weighted_sum 0 weight_sum 0\n               total_fails 0 total_checks_all 0;\n\n        weighted_sum     + (dimension_score * weight);\n        weight_sum       + weight;\n        total_fails      + fail_count;\n        total_checks_all + total_checks;\n\n        IF last THEN DO;\n            composite_score = weighted_sum / weight_sum;\n            overall_status  = IFC(composite_score >= 90, 'EXCELLENT',\n                              IFC(composite_score >= 75, 'GOOD',\n                              IFC(composite_score >= 60, 'FAIR', 'POOR')));\n            scorecard_date  = TODAY();\n            FORMAT scorecard_date DATE9. composite_score 6.2;\n            OUTPUT;\n        END;\n    RUN;\n\n    /* --- Generate formatted scorecard report via PROC REPORT --- */\n    PROC REPORT DATA=work.dq_dimensions NOWD\n                STYLE(HEADER)=[BACKGROUNDCOLOR=steelblue COLOR=white];\n        COLUMNS dimension dimension_score fail_count total_checks weight;\n        DEFINE dimension       / GROUP 'Quality Dimension';\n        DEFINE dimension_score / ANALYSIS MEAN 'Score (%)' FORMAT=6.2;\n        DEFINE fail_count      / ANALYSIS SUM 'Failures';\n        DEFINE total_checks    / ANALYSIS SUM 'Total Checks';\n        DEFINE weight          / DISPLAY 'Weight' FORMAT=4.2;\n\n        COMPUTE AFTER;\n            LINE ' ';\n            LINE 'Data Governance Scorecard \u2014 Generated &run_date';\n            LINE 'Framework Version: &dq_version';\n        ENDCOMP;\n    RUN;\n\n    /* --- ODS Excel output for distribution to stakeholders --- */\n    ODS EXCEL FILE="/data/governance/reports/dq_scorecard_&run_date..xlsx"\n             STYLE=SEASIDE\n             OPTIONS(SHEET_NAME='DQ Scorecard'\n                     EMBEDDED_TITLES='YES');\n\n        /* Print composite score summary */\n        PROC PRINT DATA=work.composite_score NOOBS LABEL;\n            TITLE 'Enterprise Data Quality Composite Score';\n            TITLE2 "Run Date: &run_date | Framework: &dq_version";\n        RUN;\n\n        /* Print dimension-level detail */\n        PROC PRINT DATA=work.dq_dimensions NOOBS LABEL;\n            TITLE 'Quality Dimension Detail';\n        RUN;\n\n    ODS EXCEL CLOSE;\n\n    /* Store scorecard history in report library */\n    DATA report.dq_scorecard_history;\n        SET report.dq_scorecard_history\n            work.composite_score;\n    RUN;\n\n    %PUT NOTE: DQ Scorecard generated successfully.;\n    %PUT NOTE: Report saved to /data/governance/reports/;\n\n%MEND dq_scorecard;\n\n/* ============================================================================\n   SECTION 8: MAIN PROGRAM \u2014 Execute all quality checks\n   Runs all governance checks in sequence: profiling, completeness,\n   validity, consistency, timeliness, and scorecard generation.\n   ============================================================================ */\n\n/* --- 8a: Profile 5 source tables --- */\n%PUT NOTE: ============================================================;\n%PUT NOTE: PHASE 1 \u2014 DATA PROFILING;\n%PUT NOTE: ============================================================;\n\n%profile_table(lib=source, table=customers);\n%profile_table(lib=source, table=transactions);\n%profile_table(lib=source, table=products);\n%profile_table(lib=source, table=accounts);\n%profile_table(lib=source, table=orders);\n\n/* --- 8b: Completeness checks for 5 tables --- */\n%PUT NOTE: ============================================================;\n%PUT NOTE: PHASE 2 \u2014 COMPLETENESS CHECKS;\n%PUT NOTE: ============================================================;\n\n%check_completeness(lib=source, table=customers,\n    required_cols=customer_id name email phone address);\n%check_completeness(lib=source, table=transactions,\n    required_cols=txn_id customer_id amount txn_date status);\n%check_completeness(lib=source, table=products,\n    required_cols=product_id product_name category price);\n%check_completeness(lib=source, table=accounts,\n    required_cols=account_id customer_id account_type open_date balance);\n%check_completeness(lib=source, table=orders,\n    required_cols=order_id customer_id order_date total_amount ship_date);\n\n/* --- 8c: Validity checks for 3 tables with business rule sets --- */\n%PUT NOTE: ============================================================;\n%PUT NOTE: PHASE 3 \u2014 BUSINESS RULE VALIDATION;\n%PUT NOTE: ============================================================;\n\n%check_validity(lib=source, table=customers,\n    rules_ds=meta.rules_customers);\n%check_validity(lib=source, table=transactions,\n    rules_ds=meta.rules_transactions);\n%check_validity(lib=source, table=products,\n    rules_ds=meta.rules_products);\n\n/* --- 8d: Consistency checks for 2 cross-source comparisons --- */\n%PUT NOTE: ============================================================;\n%PUT NOTE: PHASE 4 \u2014 CROSS-SOURCE CONSISTENCY;\n%PUT NOTE: ============================================================;\n\n%check_consistency(src1=source.customers, src2=meta.ext_customers,\n    key=customer_id, compare_cols=name email phone);\n%check_consistency(src1=source.accounts, src2=meta.ext_accounts,\n    key=account_id, compare_cols=account_type balance status);\n\n/* --- 8e: Timeliness checks for 4 tables --- */\n%PUT NOTE: ============================================================;\n%PUT NOTE: PHASE 5 \u2014 DATA FRESHNESS CHECKS;\n%PUT NOTE: ============================================================;\n\n%check_timeliness(lib=source, table=customers,\n    date_col=last_update_dt, max_lag_days=7);\n%check_timeliness(lib=source, table=transactions,\n    date_col=txn_date, max_lag_days=1);\n%check_timeliness(lib=source, table=orders,\n    date_col=order_date, max_lag_days=2);\n%check_timeliness(lib=source, table=accounts,\n    date_col=last_activity_dt, max_lag_days=3);\n\n/* --- 8f: Generate DQ Scorecard --- */\n%PUT NOTE: ============================================================;\n%PUT NOTE: PHASE 6 \u2014 DQ SCORECARD GENERATION;\n%PUT NOTE: ============================================================;\n\n%dq_scorecard;\n\n/* --- 8g: Error handling \u2014 Compile and print all failures --- */\n%PUT NOTE: ============================================================;\n%PUT NOTE: PHASE 7 \u2014 FAILURE SUMMARY;\n%PUT NOTE: ============================================================;\n\n/* Combine all completeness failure records for review */\nDATA work.all_failures;\n    LENGTH source_check $30 table_name $32 detail $200\n           check_status $4 check_date 8;\n    FORMAT check_date DATE9.;\n\n    /* Gather completeness failures from all 5 tables */\n    SET dq.completeness_customers (IN=a WHERE=(check_status='FAIL'))\n        dq.completeness_transactions (IN=b WHERE=(check_status='FAIL'))\n        dq.completeness_products (IN=c WHERE=(check_status='FAIL'))\n        dq.completeness_accounts (IN=d WHERE=(check_status='FAIL'))\n        dq.completeness_orders (IN=e WHERE=(check_status='FAIL'));\n\n    IF a OR b OR c OR d OR e THEN source_check = 'COMPLETENESS';\n    detail = CATX(' | ', 'Column:', column_name,\n                  'Completeness:', PUT(completeness_pct, 6.2));\nRUN;\n\n/* Gather validity failures from all 3 tables */\nDATA work.validity_failures;\n    LENGTH source_check $30 table_name $32 detail $200\n           check_status $4 check_date 8;\n    FORMAT check_date DATE9.;\n\n    SET dq.validity_customers (IN=a WHERE=(check_status='FAIL'))\n        dq.validity_transactions (IN=b WHERE=(check_status='FAIL'))\n        dq.validity_products (IN=c WHERE=(check_status='FAIL'));\n\n    source_check = 'VALIDITY';\n    detail = CATX(' | ', 'Rule:', rule_id,\n                  'Violations:', PUT(violation_count, BEST.));\nRUN;\n\n/* Append validity failures to combined failure table */\nPROC APPEND BASE=work.all_failures\n            DATA=work.validity_failures FORCE;\nRUN;\n\n/* Print failure summary report to log and output */\nTITLE 'DATA GOVERNANCE FRAMEWORK \u2014 FAILURE SUMMARY';\nTITLE2 "Run Date: &run_date | Framework Version: &dq_version";\nTITLE3 "Data Steward: &steward_name | Domain: &domain_name";\n\nPROC PRINT DATA=work.all_failures NOOBS LABEL;\n    VAR source_check table_name detail check_status check_date;\n    LABEL source_check = 'Check Type'\n          table_name   = 'Table'\n          detail       = 'Details'\n          check_status = 'Status'\n          check_date   = 'Date';\nRUN;\n\nTITLE;\n\n/* --- Final program status log --- */\n%PUT NOTE: ============================================================;\n%PUT NOTE: Data Governance Framework execution complete.;\n%PUT NOTE: Run date: &run_date;\n%PUT NOTE: Framework version: &dq_version;\n%PUT NOTE: Steward: &steward_name;\n%PUT NOTE: Domain: &domain_name;\n%PUT NOTE: ============================================================;\n\n/* End of gsh_12_data_governance.sas */\n-gold_meta26]

Produces consolidated PDF reports for portfolio\n *                managers and the risk oversight committee.\n * Dependencies : None (self-contained analytics suite)\n * Frequency    : Daily / Monthly / Ad-hoc\n * Portfolio ID : Configurable via macro parameters\n * Analyst      : K. Nakamura\n * Change Log   :\n *   2026-01-10  v1.0  Initial return calculation framework    (K. Nakamura)\n *   2026-01-25  v1.5  Added VaR and Monte Carlo simulation    (R. Gupta)\n *   2026-02-05  v2.0  Brinson-Fachler attribution engine      (K. Nakamura)\n *   2026-02-14  v2.2  Style analysis with factor regression   (S.
