staging.risk_scored;\n    set staging.enriched_applications;\n    length risk_bucket $12;\n\n    /* LTV score component */\n    select;\n        when (ltv_ratio <= 0.60) ltv_points = 40;\n        when (ltv_ratio <= 0.75) ltv_points = 30;\n        when (ltv_ratio <= 0.85) ltv_points = 20;\n        when (ltv_ratio <= 0.95) ltv_points = 10;\n        otherwise                ltv_points = 0;\n    end;\n\n    /* DTI score component */\n    select;\n        when (dti_ratio <= 0.28) dti_points = 30;\n        when (dti_ratio <= 0.36) dti_points = 20;\n        when (dti_ratio <= 0.43) dti_points = 10;\n        otherwise                dti_points = 0;\n    end;\n\n    /* FICO score component */\n    select;\n        when (fico_adj >= 760) fico_points = 50;\n        when (fico_adj >= 700) fico_points = 35;\n        when (fico_adj >= 660) fico_points = 20;\n        when (fico_adj >= 620) fico_points = 10;\n        otherwise              fico_points = 0;\n    end;\n\n    /* Total raw score */\n    raw_score = ltv_points + dti_points + fico_points;\n\n    /* Assign risk bucket */\n    select;\n        when (raw_score >= 100) risk_bucket = 'PRIME';\n        when (raw_score >= 70)  risk_bucket = 'NEAR_PRIME';\n        when (raw_score >= 40)  risk_bucket = 'SUBPRIME';\n        otherwise               risk_bucket = 'DEEP_SUB';\n    end;\nrun;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 5: Apply logistic regression coefficients for PD estimation\n   Coefficients pre-estimated from historical default model\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ndata staging.pd_estimates;\n    set staging.risk_scored;\n\n    /* Model intercept and coefficients */\n    _intercept = -3.258;\n    _beta_ltv  =  2.145;\n    _beta_dti  =  1.873;\n    _beta_fico = -0.0042;\n    _beta_util =  0.952;\n    _beta_inq  =  0.187;\n\n    /* Linear predictor (log-odds) */\n    logit = _intercept\n          + _beta_ltv  * ltv_ratio\n          + _beta_dti  * dti_ratio\n          + _beta_fico * fico_adj\n          + _beta_util * credit_util\n          + _beta_inq  * inquiries_6m;\n\n    /* Probability of default */\n    prob_default = 1 / (1 + exp(-logit));\n\n    /* Expected loss given default assumption */\n    lgd = 0.40;\n    expected_loss = prob_default * lgd * loan_amount;\n\n    drop _intercept _beta_: logit;\n    format prob_default percent8.4 expected_loss dollar12.2;\nrun;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 6: Apply policy overrides and final credit decision\n decision based on PD threshold */\n    if prob_default <= 0.03 then decision = 'APPROVE';\n    else if prob_default <= 0.08 then decision = 'REVIEW';\n    else decision = 'DECLINE';\n\n    /* Policy overrides */\n    override_reason = '';\n    if bankruptcy_flag = 'Y' then do;\n        decision = 'DECLINE';\n        override_reason = 'Active bankruptcy on file';\n    end;\n    else if fico_adj < end;\n\n    decision_date = today();\n    format decision_date date9.;\nrun;\n-gold_meta40]

Purpose: Supply chain analytics - lead time analysis, supplier performance,\n *          backorder tracking, safety stock computation and ABC classification\n * Author:  Supply Chain Analytics Team\n * Date:    2026-02-19\n ******************************************************************************/\n\n/* --- Library references for supply chain data --- */\nlibname raw     '/data/supplychain/raw';\nlibname staging '/data/supplychain/staging';\nlibname tgt     '/data/supplychain/target';\n\noptions mprint nocenter;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 1: Compute lead times and delivery reliability per supplier\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ndata staging.delivery_metrics;\n    set raw.purchase_orders;\n    where order_status = 'DELIVERED';\n\n    /* Actual lead time in calendar days */\n    lead_time_days = delivery_date - order_date;\n\n    /* Promised vs actual delivery variance */\n    delivery_variance = delivery_date - promised_date;\n\n    /* On-time flag: delivered on or before promised date */\n    if delivery_variance <= 0 then on_time = 1;\n    else on_time = 0;\n\n    /* Quality acceptance flag */\n    if qty_accepted >= qty_ordered * 0.95 then quality_pass = 1;\n    else quality_pass = 0;\n\n    format delivery_date order_date promised_date date9.;\nrun;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 2: Aggregate supplier performance metrics\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nproc sql;\n    create table staging.supplier_scorecard as\n    select s.supplier_id,\n           s.supplier_name,\n           s.supplier_region,\n           count(*) as total_orders,\n           avg(d.lead_time_days) as avg_lead_time,\n           std(d.lead_time_days) as std_lead_time,\n           sum(d.on_time) / count(*) as otd_rate format=percent8.1,\n           sum(d.quality_pass) / count(*) as quality_rate format=percent8.1,\n           sum(d.qty_accepted * d.unit_cost) as total_spend format=dollar12.2\n    from staging.delivery_metrics as d\n    inner join raw.suppliers as s\n        on d.supplier_id = s.supplier_id\n    group by s.supplier_id, s.supplier_name, s.supplier_region\n    having count(*) >= 5;\nquit;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 3: Track backorder accumulation using RETAIN\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nproc sort data=raw.inventory_transactions out=work.inv_sorted;\n    by product_id transaction_date;\nrun;\n\ndata staging.backorder_tracking;\n    set work.inv_sorted;\n    by product_id;\n    retain running_backorder 0;\n\n    /* Reset accumulator at start of each product */\n    if first.product_id then running_backorder = 0;\n\n    /* Accumulate backorders: positive = demand, negative = receipt */\n    if transaction_type = 'DEMAND' then\n        running_backorder = running_backorder + quantity;\n    else if transaction_type = 'RECEIPT' then\n        running_backorder = max(0, running_backorder - quantity);\n\n    /* Flag critical backorder levels */\n    if running_backorder > reorder_point then\n        backorder_alert = 'CRITICAL';\n    else if running_backorder > 0 then\n        backorder_alert = 'WARNING';\n    else\n        backorder_alert = 'OK';\nrun;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 4: Safety stock computation using demand variability\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nproc means data=raw.daily_demand\n           mean std noprint;\n    by product_id;\n    var daily_qty;\n    output out=staging.demand_stats\n        mean=avg_daily_demand\n        std=std_daily_demand;\nrun;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 5: ABC staging.demand_stats;\n\n    /* Service level z-score (95% = 1.645) */\n    z_score = 1.645;\n    avg_lead_time = 14;\n    std_lead_time = 3;\n\n    /* Safety stock formula */\n    safety_stock = z_score * sqrt(\n        (avg_lead_time * std_daily_demand**2) +\n        (avg_daily_demand**2 * std_lead_time**2)\n    );\n\n    reorder_point = (avg_daily_demand * avg_lead_time) + safety_stock;\n\n    /* ABC classification thresholds by category */\n    array rev_pct{3} _temporary_ (0.80 0.95 1.00);\n    array abc_label{3} $1 _temporary_ ('A' 'B' 'C');\n\n    /* Assign ABC class based on cumulative revenue percentage */\n    length abc_class $1;\n    if cumulative_rev_pct <= rev_pct{1} then abc_class = abc_label{1};\n    else if cumulative_rev_pct <= rev_pct{2} then abc_class = abc_label{2};\n    else abc_class = abc_label{3};\n\n    /* Set review frequency based on ABC class */\n    array review_days{3} _temporary_ (7 14 30);\n    if abc_class = 'A' then review_cycle = review_days{1};\n    else if abc_class = 'B' then review_cycle = review_days{2};\n    else review_cycle = review_days{3};\n\n    format safety_stock reorder_point comma10.;\n    drop z_score avg_lead_time std_lead_time;\nrun;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 6: Generate exception report for critical items\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nproc sort data=tgt.product_classification\n          out=work.exceptions;\n    by abc_class descending safety_stock;\n    where abc_class = 'A' and safety_stock > 1000;\nrun;\n\ntitle 'Supply Chain Exception Report - High Safety Stock A-Items';\nproc print data=work.exceptions noobs label;\n    var product_id abc_class avg_daily_demand safety_stock reorder_point review_cycle;\n    label product_id       = 'Product'\n          abc_class        = 'ABC Class'\n          avg_daily_demand = 'Avg Daily Demand'\n e.sale_date,\n           e.rep_id,\n           r.rep_name,\n           r.hire_date,\n           e.territory,\n           e.region,\n           e.fiscal_year,\n           e.fiscal_qtr,\n           p.product_name,\n           p.product_category,\n           p.product_line,\n           c.customer_name,\n           c.customer_segment,\n           c.industry,\n           e.net_revenue,\n           e.margin_pct,\n           q.quarterly_quota\n    from staging.enriched_sales as e\n    inner join raw.sales_reps as r\n        on e.rep_id = r.rep_id\n    inner join raw.products as p\n        on ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nproc means data=staging.sales_fact\n           sum mean n noprint;\n    class region territory fiscal_qtr;\n    var -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 4: Compute attainment percentages and performance bands\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nproc sort data=staging.sales_fact;\n    by rep_id fiscal_year fiscal_qtr;\nrun;\n\ndata staging.rep_attainment;\n    set staging.sales_fact;\n    by rep_id fiscal_year fiscal_qtr;\n    retain qtd_revenue;\n\n    /* Accumulate revenue within rep-quarter */\n    if first.fiscal_qtr then qtd_revenue = 0;\n    qtd_revenue + net_revenue;\n\n    /* Output one record per rep per quarter */\n    if last.fiscal_qtr then do;\n        if quarterly_quota > 0 then\n            attainment_pct = qtd_revenue / quarterly_quota;\n attainment_pct >= 1.00 then perf_band = 'ON_TARGET';\n        else if attainment_pct >= 0.75 then perf_band = 'DEVELOPING';\n        else perf_band = 'AT_RISK';\n\n        format attainment_pct percent8.1 qtd_revenue dollar12.2;\n        output;\n    end;\nrun;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 5: Rep performance quartiles using PROC RANK\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nproc rank data=staging.rep_attainment\n          out=staging.rep_ranked\n          groups=4;\n    var [SKIP] test_syntax_validity_after_deterministic[gsm_15_ab_testing.sas-/******************************************************************************\n * Program: gsm_15_ab_testing.sas\n * Purpose: A/B testing analysis pipeline for digital experiments\n *          Defines test groups, computes summary statistics, tests for\n *          significance, and generates experiment conclusion reports\n * Author:  Product Analytics Team\n * Date:    2026-02-19\n ******************************************************************************/\n\n/* --- Library references for experiment data --- */\nlibname raw     '/data/experiments/raw';\nlibname staging '/data/experiments/staging';\nlibname tgt     '/data/experiments/target';\n\noptions mprint nocenter;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 1: Define test/control groups from assignment log\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ndata staging.experiment_groups;\n    set raw.assignment_log;\n    where experiment_id = 'EXP-2026-042';\n    length group_label $10;\n\n    /* Validate group assignment */\n    if group_code = 'T' then group_label = 'TEST';\n    else if group_code = 'C' then group_label = 'CONTROL';\n    else delete;\n\n    /* Compute days since exposure */\n    exposure_days = today() - assignment_date;\n\n    /* Only include users with sufficient observation window */\n    if exposure_days >= 14;\n\n    /* Flag users with valid conversion events */\n    if conversion_date ne . then do;\n        converted = 1;\n        days_to_convert = conversion_date - assignment_date;\n    end;\n    else do;\n        converted = 0;\n        days_to_convert = .;\n    end;\n\n    /* Revenue attribution */\n    if revenue = . then revenue = 0;\n\n    format assignment_date conversion_date date9.;\nrun;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 2: Compute group summary statistics\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nproc means data=staging.experiment_groups\n           n mean std stderr min max noprint;\n    class group_label;\n    var converted revenue days_to_convert;\n    output out=staging.group_stats\n        n=n_users\n        mean(converted)=conversion_rate\n        mean(revenue)=avg_revenue\n        std(converted)=std_conversion\n        std(revenue)=std_revenue\n        stderr(converted)=se_conversion\n        stderr(revenue)=se_revenue;\nrun;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 3: Statistical significance testing\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nproc ttest data=staging.experiment_groups\n           alpha=0.05;\n    class group_label;\n    var converted revenue;\n    ods output ttests=staging.ttest_results\n              statistics=staging.ttest_stats;\nrun;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 4: Compute lift and confidence intervals via SQL\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nproc sql;\n    create table staging.lift_analysis as\n    select t.group_label,\n           t.conversion_rate,\n           t.avg_revenue,\n           t.n_users,\n           t.se_conversion,\n           t.se_revenue,\n           /* Conversion lift vs control */\n           case when t.group_label = 'TEST' then\n               (t.conversion_rate - c.conversion_rate) / c.conversion_rate\n           else 0 end as conversion_lift format=percent8.2,\n           /* Revenue lift vs control */\n           case when t.group_label = 'TEST' then\n               (t.avg_revenue - c.avg_revenue) / c.avg_revenue\n           else 0 end as revenue_lift format=percent8.2,\n           /* 95% CI lower bound for conversion difference */\n           case when t.group_label = 'TEST' then\n               (t.conversion_rate - c.conversion_rate)\n               - 1.96 * sqrt(t.se_conversion**2 + c.se_conversion**2)\n           else 0 end as conv_ci_lower,\n           /* 95% CI upper bound for conversion difference */\n           case when t.group_label = 'TEST' then\n               (t.conversion_rate - c.conversion_rate)\n               + 1.96 * sqrt(t.se_conversion**2 + c.se_conversion**2)\n           else 0 end as conv_ci_upper\n    from staging.group_stats(where=(_type_=1)) as t,\n         staging.group_stats(where=(_type_=1 and group_label='CONTROL')) as c;\nquit;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 5: Flag winners/losers and compute minimum detectable effect\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ndata staging.experiment_decision;\n    set staging.lift_analysis;\n    where group_label = 'TEST';\n    length winner_flag $12 decision $20;\n\n    /* Merge with t-test p-value */\n    set staging.ttest_results(where=(variable='converted') keep=variable probt);\n\n    /* Statistical significance check */\n    if probt < 0.05 then do;\n        stat_significant = 1;\n        if conversion_lift > 0 then do;\n            winner_flag = 'TEST_WINS';\n            decision = 'LAUNCH';\n        end;\n        else do;\n            winner_flag = 'CONTROL_WINS';\n            decision = 'REVERT';\n        end;\n    end;\n    else do;\n        stat_significant = 0;\n        winner_flag = 'INCONCLUSIVE';\n        decision = 'EXTEND_OR_STOP';\n    end;\n\n    /* Minimum Detectable Effect (MDE) for the given sample size */\n    /* MDE = 2.8 * sqrt(p*(1-p) / n) for 80% power, alpha=0.05 */\n    baseline_rate = conversion_rate\n                  - (conversion_lift * conversion_rate / (1 + conversion_lift));\n    mde = 2.8 * sqrt(baseline_rate * (1 - baseline_rate) / n_users);\n\n    format mde percent8.4 probt pvalue6.4;\nrun;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 6: Experiment conclusion report dataset\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ndata tgt.experiment_report;\n    length experiment_id $20 metric_name $30 metric_value 8\n           conclusion $100;\n\n    set staging.experiment_decision;\n\n    experiment_id = 'EXP-2026-042';\n    report_date = today();\n\n    /* Output key metrics as name-value pairs */\n    metric_name = 'CONVERSION_LIFT';\n    metric_value = conversion_lift;\n    conclusion = catx(' ', 'Conversion lift:', put(conversion_lift, percent8.2),\n                       '| p-value:', put(probt, pvalue6.4));\n    output;\n\n    metric_name = 'REVENUE_LIFT';\n    metric_value = revenue_lift;\n    conclusion = catx(' ', 'Revenue lift:', put(revenue_lift, percent8.2));\n    output;\n\n    metric_name = 'SAMPLE_SIZE';\n    metric_value = n_users;\n    conclusion = catx(' ', 'Test group size:', put(n_users, comma10.));\n    output;\n\n    metric_name = 'MDE';\n    metric_value = mde;\n    conclusion = catx(' ', 'Minimum Detectable Effect:', put(mde, percent8.4));\n    output;\n\n    metric_name = 'DECISION';\n    metric_value = (decision = 'LAUNCH');\n    conclusion = catx(' ', 'Final decision:', decision, '| Winner:', winner_flag);\n    output;\n\n    keep experiment_id report_date metric_name metric_value conclusion;\n    format report_date date9.;\nrun;\n-gold_meta44]

Supports both initial and incremental loads\n *              for multiple source tables via parameterized macro.\n *****************************************************************************/\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 1: Environment Setup                                        */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Source operational database */\nlibname raw '/data/source/operational' access=readonly;\n\n/* ETL staging area */\nlibname staging '/data/etl/staging';\n\n/* Target data warehouse */\nlibname tgt '/data/warehouse/target';\n\n/* ETL control table library */\nlibname ctl '/data/etl/control';\n\n/* Control parameters */\n%let etl_run_id = %sysfunc(datetime(), datetime20.);\n%let batch_size = 50000;\n%let default_watermark = 01JAN2020;\n\noptions mprint mlogic symbolgen compress=yes;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 2: Incremental Load Macro Definition                        */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Macro for delta-load with watermark-based change detection */\n%macro incremental_load(src_lib=, src_table=, tgt_lib=, tgt_table=,\n                        key_col=, ts_col=modified_dt);\n\n    %put NOTE: ================================================;\n    %put NOTE: Processing &src_lib..&src_table -> &tgt_lib..&tgt_table;\n    %put NOTE: Key: &key_col | Timestamp column: &ts_col;\n    %put NOTE: ================================================;\n\n    /* Retrieve last successful watermark from control table */\n    %local watermark delta_count;\n    %let watermark = ;\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select put(max(watermark_ts), datetime20.)\n            into :watermark trimmed\n        from ctl.etl_watermarks\n        where table_name = "&tgt_table";\n    quit;\n\n    /* Determine load type based on watermark existence */\n    %if &watermark = %then %do;\n        /* ------- INITIAL LOAD: no prior watermark found ------- */\n        %put NOTE: No watermark found - performing INITIAL LOAD for &tgt_table;\n\n        data staging.delta_&tgt_table;\n            set &src_lib..&src_table;\n            /* Add ETL tracking metadata */\n            etl_load_dt = datetime();\n            etl_run_id = "&etl_run_id";\n            etl_load_type = "INITIAL";\n            format etl_load_dt datetime20.;\n        run;\n    %end;\n    %else %do;\n        /* ------- INCREMENTAL LOAD: extract only changed records ------- */\n        %put NOTE: Watermark &watermark found - INCREMENTAL LOAD for &tgt_table;\n\n        data staging.delta_&tgt_table;\n            set &src_lib..&src_table;\n            where &ts_col > input("&watermark", datetime20.);\n            /* Add ETL tracking metadata */\n            etl_load_dt = datetime();\n            etl_run_id = "&etl_run_id";\n            etl_load_type = "INCREMENTAL";\n            format etl_load_dt datetime20.;\n        run;\n    %end;\n\n    /* Count extracted delta records */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select count(*) into :delta_count trimmed\n        from staging.delta_&tgt_table;\n    quit;\n\n    %put NOTE: Extracted &delta_count records for &tgt_table;\n\n    /* Upsert: remove stale rows then insert fresh delta */\n    proc sql;\n        delete from &tgt_lib..&tgt_table\n        where &key_col in (\n            select &key_col from staging.delta_&tgt_table\n        );\n\n        insert into &tgt_lib..&tgt_table\n        select * from staging.delta_&tgt_table;\n    quit;\n\n    /* Update watermark control table */\n    proc sql;\n        insert into ctl.etl_watermarks\n            (table_name, watermark_ts, records_loaded, load_type, load_dt)\n        select "&tgt_table",\n               max(&ts_col),\n               &delta_count,\n               case when "&watermark" = ""\n                    then "INITIAL" else "INCREMENTAL" end,\n               datetime()\n        from staging.delta_&tgt_table;\n    quit;\n\n    %put NOTE: Watermark updated for &tgt_table;\n\n%mend incremental_load;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 3: Batch Processing via Loop                                */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Table configuration for batch ETL */\n%let table_count = 4;\n%let tbl1_src = customers;  %let tbl1_tgt = dim_customer;  %let tbl1_key = customer_id;\n%let tbl2_src = products;   %let tbl2_tgt = dim_product;   %let tbl2_key = product_id;\n%let tbl3_src = orders;     %let tbl3_tgt = fact_orders;   %let tbl3_key = order_id;\n%let tbl4_src = shipments;  %let tbl4_tgt = fact_shipping; %let tbl4_key = shipment_id;\n\n/* Driver macro: loop over configured tables and invoke ETL */\n%macro run_etl_batch;\n    %do i = 1 %to &table_count;\n        %put NOTE: --- Processing table &i of &table_count ---;\n\n        %incremental_load(\n            src_lib   = raw,\n            src_table = &&tbl&i._src,\n            tgt_lib   = tgt,\n            tgt_table = &&tbl&i._tgt,\n            key_col   = &&tbl&i._key,\n            ts_col    = modified_dt\n        );\n    %end;\n%mend run_etl_batch;\n\n/* Execute batch ETL */\n%run_etl_batch;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 4: Post-Load Error Check                                    */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Open-code conditional: verify batch completed without errors */\n%if &syserr > 0 %then %do;\n    %put ERROR: ETL batch failed (SYSERR=&syserr). Halting validation.;\n    data staging.etl_error_log;\n        length process $50 error_msg $200;\n        process = "INCREMENTAL_ETL";\n        error_msg = "Batch failed with SYSERR=&syserr";\n        error_dt = datetime();\n        format error_dt datetime20.;\n    run;\n%end;\n%else %do;\n    %put NOTE: ETL batch completed without errors;\n%end;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 5: Load Summary Record                                      */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Create audit record for this ETL run */\ndata tgt.etl_load_summary;\n    length batch_id $30 load_status $20;\n    batch_id = "&etl_run_id";\n    load_dt = datetime();\n    tables_processed = &table_count;\n    load_status = "COMPLETE";\n    format load_dt datetime20.;\nrun;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 6: Post-Load Validation                                     */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Validate target table row counts after load */\nproc sql;\n    title "Post-Load Target Table Counts";\n    select "dim_customer" as table_name, count(*) as row_count\n        from tgt.dim_customer\n    union all\n    select "dim_product", count(*)\n        from tgt.dim_product\n    union all\n    select "fact_orders", count(*)\n        from tgt.fact_orders\n    union all\n    select "fact_shipping", count(*)\n        from tgt.fact_shipping;\nquit;\n\ntitle;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 7: Cleanup                                                  */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Remove staging delta tables */\nproc datasets lib=staging nolist;\n    delete delta_dim_customer\n */\n/* SECTION 1: Environment Setup                                        */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Data sources and output locations &output_format;\n    %put NOTE: ================================================;\n\n    /* Conditional ODS destination based on output_format parameter */\n    %if %upcase(&output_format) = PDF %then listing;\n        %put NOTE: Using default listing output;\n    %end;\n\n    /* Filter source data for specified region and period */\n    data staging.region_&region;\n        set $15;\n        if pct_achieved >= 100 then perf_flag = "EXCEEDS";\n        else if pct_achieved >= 80 then perf_flag = "ON_TRACK";\n        else perf_flag = "AT_RISK";\n    run;\n\n    /* Loop over Purpose:     Longitudinal panel analysis across multiple survey waves\n * Author:      Survey Analytics Team\n * Created:     2026-02-19\n * Environment: SAS 9.4M5+\n * Description: Processes multiple survey waves using parameterized macros.\n *              Computes wave-level statistics, builds cross-wave panel,\n *              and calculates longitudinal change metrics using LAG.\n *****************************************************************************/\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 1: Environment Setup                                        */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Panel survey data library */\nlibname raw '/data/survey/raw' access=readonly;\n\n/* Staging and analysis output */\nlibname staging '/data/survey/staging';\nlibname tgt '/data/survey/analysis';\n\n/* Wave configuration */\n%let total_waves = 4;\n%let wave1_date = 01JAN2024;\n%let wave2_date = 01APR2024;\n%let wave3_date = 01JUL2024;\n%let wave4_date = 01OCT2024;\n\noptions mprint mlogic symbolgen nocenter;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 2: Wave Processing Macro                                    */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Macro to extract and summarize a single survey wave */\n%macro process_wave(wave_num=, wave_date=);\n\n    %put NOTE: =============================================;\n    %put NOTE: Processing Wave &wave_num (date: &wave_date);\n    %put NOTE: =============================================;\n\n    /* Check if wave data exists before processing */\n    %let dsid = %sysfunc(open(raw.survey_responses));\n    %if &dsid = 0 %then %do;\n        %put WARNING: Cannot open raw.survey_responses for wave &wave_num;\n        %put WARNING: Skipping wave &wave_num processing;\n        %goto wave_exit;\n    %end;\n    %let rc = %sysfunc(close(&dsid));\n\n    /* Extract respondents for this specific wave */\n    data staging.wave_&wave_num;\n        set raw.survey_responses;\n        where wave_id = &wave_num\n          and response_date >= "&wave_date"d;\n\n        /* Derive composite satisfaction score */\n        satisfaction_score = mean(of q1_rating q2_rating q3_rating\n                                    q4_rating q5_rating);\n\n        /* Classification of engagement level */\n        length engagement $15;\n        if satisfaction_score >= 4.0 then engagement = "HIGH";\n        else if satisfaction_score >= 2.5 then engagement = "MODERATE";\n        else engagement = "LOW";\n\n        /* Track completion rate */\n        array questions{5} q1_rating q2_rating q3_rating\n                           q4_rating q5_rating;\n        items_answered = 0;\n        do j = 1 to 5;\n            if questions{j} > 0 then items_answered + 1;\n        end;\n        completion_pct = (items_answered / 5) * 100;\n\n        wave_label = "Wave &wave_num";\n        format response_date date9.;\n        drop j;\n    run;\n\n    /* Wave-level descriptive statistics */\n    proc means data=staging.wave_&wave_num\n               n mean std min max median maxdec=2;\n        title "Wave &wave_num Summary Statistics";\n        var satisfaction_score completion_pct\n            q1_rating q2_rating q3_rating q4_rating q5_rating;\n        output out=staging.stats_wave_&wave_num\n            n=n_respondents\n            mean=avg_satisfaction\n            std=sd_satisfaction;\n    run;\n\n    %wave_exit:\n    %put NOTE: Wave &wave_num processing complete;\n\n%mend process_wave;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 3: Process All Waves via Loop                               */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Driver macro to iterate over all configured waves */\n%macro run_all_waves;\n    %do w = 1 %to &total_waves;\n        %process_wave(\n            wave_num = &w,\n            wave_date = &&wave&w._date\n        );\n    %end;\n%mend run_all_waves;\n\n/* Execute wave processing */\n%run_all_waves;\n\n/* Open-code conditional: verify all waves processed */\n%if &syserr > 0 %then %do;\n    %put ERROR: Wave processing encountered errors (SYSERR=&syserr);\n    %put ERROR: Longitudinal panel may be incomplete;\n%end;\n%else %do;\n    %put NOTE: All &total_waves waves processed successfully;\n%end;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 4: Build Longitudinal Panel                                 */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Join all waves into a single panel dataset keyed by respondent */\nproc sql;\n    create table staging.longitudinal_panel as\n    select coalesce(w1.respondent_id, w2.respondent_id,\n                    w3.respondent_id, w4.respondent_id)\n               as respondent_id,\n           w1.satisfaction_score as score_w1,\n           w2.satisfaction_score as score_w2,\n           w3.satisfaction_score as score_w3,\n           w4.satisfaction_score as score_w4,\n           w1.engagement as engage_w1,\n           w4.engagement as engage_w4,\n           /* Participation tracking */\n           (w1.respondent_id is not null) as in_wave1,\n           (w2.respondent_id is not null) as in_wave2,\n           (w3.respondent_id is not null) as in_wave3,\n           (w4.respondent_id is not null) as in_wave4\n    from staging.wave_1 as w1\n    full join staging.wave_2 as w2\n        on w1.respondent_id = w2.respondent_id\n    full join staging.wave_3 as w3\n        on coalesce(w1.respondent_id, w2.respondent_id)\n           = w3.respondent_id\n    full join staging.wave_4 as w4\n        on coalesce(w1.respondent_id, w2.respondent_id,\n                    w3.respondent_id) = w4.respondent_id\n    order by calculated respondent_id;\nquit;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 5: Longitudinal Change Analysis                             */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Compute wave-over-wave changes using LAG function */\ndata tgt.wave_changes;\n    set staging.longitudinal_panel;\n\n    /* Count number of waves each respondent participated in */\n    waves_participated = sum(in_wave1, in_wave2, in_wave3, in_wave4);\n\n    /* Calculate overall trajectory */\n    if score_w1 > 0 and score_w4 > 0 then\n        overall_change = score_w4 - score_w1;\n    else\n        overall_change = .;\n\n    /* Classify trajectory */\n    length trajectory $15;\n    if overall_change > 0.5 then trajectory = "IMPROVING";\n    else if overall_change < -0.5 then trajectory = "DECLINING";\n    else if overall_change ne . then trajectory = "STABLE";\n    else trajectory = "INSUFFICIENT";\n\n    /* Flag attrition risk: participated in wave 1 but not wave 4 */\n    if in_wave1 = 1 and in_wave4 = 0 then attrition_flag = 1;\n    else attrition_flag = 0;\n\n    format overall_change 8.2;\nrun;\n\n/* Trajectory distribution report */\nproc freq data=tgt.wave_changes;\n    title "Respondent Trajectory Distribution";\n    tables trajectory / nocum;\n    tables trajectory * waves_participated / nocum nopercent;\nrun;\n\ntitle;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 6: Cleanup                                                  */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\nlibname raw clear;\nlibname staging clear;\n\n%put NOTE: Longitudinal analysis completed at %sysfunc(datetime(), datetime20.);\n-gold_meta48]

[SKIP] test_syntax_validity_after_deterministic[gsm_20_audit_trail.sas-/*****************************************************************************\n * Program:     gsm_20_audit_trail.sas\n * Purpose:     Automated change detection and audit trail generation\n * Author:      Data Governance Team\n * Created:     2026-02-19\n * Environment: SAS 9.4M5+\n * Description: Compares current vs. previous snapshots of monitored tables\n *              to detect INSERTs, UPDATEs, and DELETEs. Uses parameterized\n *              macro with loop-driven batch processing for multiple tables.\n *****************************************************************************/\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 1: Environment Setup                                        */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Current production snapshot */\nlibname curr '/data/production/current' access=readonly;\n\n/* Previous day snapshot for comparison */\nlibname prev '/data/production/previous' access=readonly;\n\n/* Audit trail output library */\nlibname audit '/data/governance/audit_trail';\n\n/* Staging library for intermediate results */\nlibname staging '/data/governance/staging';\n\n/* Audit control parameters */\n%let audit_date = %sysfunc(today(), date9.);\n%let audit_user = %sysget(USERNAME);\n%let log_level = DETAIL;\n\noptions mprint mlogic symbolgen nocenter;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 2: Audit Detection Macro                                    */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Macro to detect changes between current and previous snapshots */\n%macro audit_table(table_name=, pk_col=);\n\n    %put NOTE: =============================================;\n    %put NOTE: Auditing table: &table_name;\n    %put NOTE: Primary key: &pk_col;\n    %put NOTE: Audit date: &audit_date;\n    %put NOTE: =============================================;\n\n    /* Step 1: Identify new records (INSERTs) - in current but not previous */\n    proc sql;\n        create table staging.ins_&table_name as\n        select c.*,\n               "INSERT" as change_type length=10,\n               "&audit_date"d as audit_dt format=date9.\n        from curr.&table_name as c\n        left join prev.&table_name as p\n            on c.&pk_col = p.&pk_col\n        where p.&pk_col is null;\n    quit;\n\n    /* Step 2: Identify removed records (DELETEs) - in previous but not current */\n    proc sql;\n        create table staging.del_&table_name as\n        select p.*,\n               "DELETE" as change_type length=10,\n               "&audit_date"d as audit_dt format=date9.\n        from prev.&table_name as p\n        left join curr.&table_name as c\n            on p.&pk_col = c.&pk_col\n        where c.&pk_col is null;\n    quit;\n\n    /* Step 3: Identify modified records (UPDATEs) via checksum comparison */\n    proc sql;\n        create table staging.upd_&table_name as\n        select c.*,\n               "UPDATE" as change_type length=10,\n               "&audit_date"d as audit_dt format=date9.\n        from curr.&table_name as c\n        inner join prev.&table_name as p\n            on c.&pk_col = p.&pk_col\n        where md5(cats(of c.:)) ne md5(cats(of p.:));\n    quit;\n\n    /* Step 4: Combine all detected changes into unified audit log */\n    data staging.changes_&table_name;\n        set staging.ins_&table_name\n            staging.del_&table_name\n            staging.upd_&table_name;\n\n        /* Add audit metadata */\n        length table_source $50 audit_user $30;\n        table_source = "&table_name";\n        audit_user = "&audit_user";\n        audit_seq = _n_;\n\n        format audit_dt date9.;\n    run;\n\n    /* Conditional logging based on log level */\n    %if &log_level = DETAIL %then %do;\n        /* Detailed per-record logging */\n        proc sql noprint;\n            select count(*) into :n_ins trimmed\n            from staging.ins_&table_name;\n            select count(*) into :n_del trimmed\n            from staging.del_&table_name;\n            select count(*) into :n_upd trimmed\n            from staging.upd_&table_name;\n        quit;\n\n        %put NOTE: &table_name audit results:;\n        %put NOTE:   Inserts: &n_ins;\n        %put NOTE:   Deletes: &n_del;\n        %put NOTE:   Updates: &n_upd;\n    %end;\n    %else %do;\n        /* Summary-only logging */\n        proc sql noprint;\n            select count(*) into :n_total trimmed\n            from staging.changes_&table_name;\n        quit;\n        %put NOTE: &table_name - Total changes: &n_total;\n    %end;\n\n%mend audit_table;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 3: Batch Audit via Loop                                     */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Configure tables to monitor */\n%let n_tables = 5;\n%let tab1 = customer_master;  %let pk1 = customer_id;\n%let tab2 = product_catalog;  %let pk2 = product_id;\n%let tab3 = order_header;     %let pk3 = order_id;\n%let tab4 = supplier_info;    %let pk4 = supplier_id;\n%let tab5 = employee_roster;  %let pk5 = employee_id;\n\n/* Driver macro: iterate over all monitored tables */\n%macro run_audit_batch;\n    %do t = 1 %to &n_tables;\n        %put NOTE: --- Audit batch item &t of &n_tables ---;\n        %audit_table(\n            table_name = &&tab&t,\n            pk_col     = &&pk&t\n        );\n    %end;\n%mend run_audit_batch;\n\n/* Execute the batch audit */\n%run_audit_batch;\n\n/* Open-code conditional: verify audit batch completed */\n%if &syserr > 0 %then %do;\n    %put ERROR: Audit batch encountered errors (SYSERR=&syserr);\n    %put ERROR: Some tables may not have been audited;\n%end;\n%else %do;\n    %put NOTE: Audit batch completed for all &n_tables tables;\n%end;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 4: Consolidated Audit Report                                */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Merge all table-level change logs into master audit trail */\ndata audit.master_audit_trail;\n    set staging.changes_customer_master\n        staging.changes_product_catalog\n        staging.changes_order_header\n        staging.changes_supplier_info\n        staging.changes_employee_roster;\n\n    /* Global sequence number */\n    master_seq = _n_;\nrun;\n\n/* Change type distribution by table */\nproc freq data=audit.master_audit_trail;\n    title "Audit Trail Summary - &audit_date";\n    tables table_source * change_type / nocum nopercent;\n    tables change_type / nocum;\nrun;\n\n/* Top tables by change volume */\nproc sql;\n    title "Change Volume by Table";\n    select table_source,\n           change_type,\n           count(*) as change_count\n    from audit.master_audit_trail\n    group by table_source, change_type\n    order by calculated change_count desc;\nquit;\n\ntitle;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 5: Cleanup                                                  */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\nlibname curr clear;\nlibname prev clear;\nlibname staging clear;\n\n%put NOTE: Audit trail generation completed at [SKIP] test_deterministic_fires_for_known_patterns[gsh_01_enterprise_etl.sas-/******************************************************************************\n * Program Name : gsh_01_enterprise_etl.sas\n * Author       : Data Engineering Team \u2014 Financial Systems Division\n * Created      : 2026-01-15\n * Modified     : 2026-02-19\n * Purpose      : Enterprise ETL pipeline for the Financial Data Warehouse.\n *                Extracts data from raw source libraries, applies cleansing\n *                and business transformations, loads dimension and fact tables\n *                into the data mart layer using SCD Type 2 methodology.\n * Dependencies : common_macros.sas\n * Frequency    : Daily (scheduled via Control-M, Job ID FIN-DWH-001)\n * Change Log   :\n *   2026-01-15  v1.0  Initial development                    (J. Martin)\n *   2026-01-28  v1.1  Added SCD Type 2 dimension loading     (S. Chen)\n *   2026-02-10  v1.2  Enhanced error handling and audit trail (A. Patel)\n *   2026-02-19  v1.3  Added archival step and date dimension  (J. Martin)\n ******************************************************************************/\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 1: Environment Setup \u2014 Libraries, Options, and Includes           */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\noptions mprint mlogic symbolgen nocenter ls=200 ps=65\n        validvarname=v7 nofmterr compress=binary;\n\n/* --- Library references for each data layer --- */\nlibname raw      '/data/finance/raw'       access=readonly;\nlibname staging  '/data/finance/staging';\nlibname cleansed '/data/finance/cleansed';\nlibname mart     '/data/finance/mart';\nlibname archive  '/data/finance/archive';\nlibname tgt      '/data/finance/target';\n\n/* Global macro variables for this run */\n%let etl_run_id    = %sysfunc(putn(%sysfunc(datetime()), datetime20.));\n%let process_date  = %sysfunc(today(), yymmdd10.);\n%let valid_sources = 1;  /* Flag: set to 0 if any source validation fails */\n%let etl_rc        = 0;  /* Return code accumulator */\n%let n_sources     = 6;  /* Number of source tables to validate */\n\n/* Include shared macro library */\n%include '/etl/common/common_macros.sas';\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 2: Utility Macro Definitions                                      */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: check_source_data                                               */\n/*   Validates that a source table exists and contains a minimum number   */\n/*   of observations. Sets &valid_sources to 0 if validation fails.       */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro check_source_data(lib=, table=, min_rows=100);\n\n    %local dsid nobs rc table_exists;\n\n    /* Check if the table exists in the library */\n    %let table_exists = %sysfunc(exist(&lib..&table));\n\n    %if &table_exists = 0 %then %do;\n        %put ERROR: [check_source_data] Table &lib..&table does not exist.;\n        %let valid_sources = 0;\n        %return;\n    %end;\n\n    /* Open the dataset and retrieve observation count */\n    %let dsid = %sysfunc(open(&lib..&table));\n    %if &dsid > 0 %then %do;\n        %let nobs = %sysfunc(attrn(&dsid, NOBS));\n        %let rc   = %sysfunc(close(&dsid));\n\n        %if &nobs < &min_rows %then %do;\n            %put WARNING: [check_source_data] &lib..&table has only &nobs rows (minimum: &min_rows).;\n            %let valid_sources = 0;\n        %end;\n        %else %do;\n            %put NOTE: [check_source_data] &lib..&table validated OK \u2014 &nobs observations found.;\n        %end;\n    %end;\n    %else %do;\n        %put ERROR: [check_source_data] Unable to open &lib..&table \u2014 check permissions.;\n        %let valid_sources = 0;\n    %end;\n\n%mend check_source_data;\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: log_step                                                        */\n/*   Inserts an audit record into mart.etl_audit_log for traceability.    */\n/*   Captures run ID, step name, status, row counts, and timestamp.       */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro log_step(step_name=, status=OK, detail=, row_count=0);\n\n    %local log_ts;\n    %let log_ts = %sysfunc(datetime(), datetime20.);\n\n    proc sql noprint;\n        insert into mart.etl_audit_log\n            (run_id, step_name, status, detail, row_count, log_timestamp)\n        values\n            ("&etl_run_id", "&step_name", "&status", "&detail",\n             &row_count, "&log_ts");\n    quit;\n\n    %if &status = ERROR %then %do;\n        %let etl_rc = 1;\n        %put ERROR: [log_step] Step &step_name failed \u2014 &detail;\n    %end;\n\n%mend log_step;\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: load_dimension                                                  */\n/*   Implements SCD Type 2 dimension loading with effective dating.        */\n/*   Compares staging rows against existing dimension, expires changed     */\n/*   records and inserts new versions with current effective dates.         */\n/*   Parameters:                                                           */\n/*     dim_table   - Target dimension table (e.g., mart.customer_dim)      */\n/*     src_table   - Staging source table                                  */\n/*     natural_key - Business key column(s) for matching                   */\n/*     scd_type    - SCD type (1 or 2); default 2                          */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro load_dimension(dim_table=, src_table=, natural_key=, scd_type=2);\n\n    %local src_count new_count upd_count;\n\n    %put NOTE: [load_dimension] Loading &dim_table from &src_table (SCD Type &scd_type).;\n\n    /* Step 1: Identify new and changed records via left join */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        create table work._dim_changes as\n        select s.*,\n               d.dim_surrogate_key,\n               d.effective_from as existing_eff_from,\n               case\n                   when d.dim_surrogate_key is null then 'NEW'\n                   else 'UPDATE'\n               end as _change_type\n        from &src_table as s\n        left join &dim_table (where=(current_flag = 'Y')) as d\n            on s.&natural_key = d.&natural_key;\n\n        select count(*) into :src_count trimmed from &src_table;\n        select count(*) into :new_count trimmed\n            from work._dim_changes where _change_type = 'NEW';\n        select count(*) into :upd_count trimmed\n            from work._dim_changes where _change_type = 'UPDATE';\n    quit;\n\n    %put NOTE: [load_dimension] Source=&src_count New=&new_count Updated=&upd_count;\n\n    %if &scd_type = 2 %then %do;\n\n        /* Step 2a: Expire changed records in the dimension */\n        proc sql;\n            update &dim_table\n            set current_flag   = 'N',\n                effective_to   = "&process_date"d\n            where current_flag = 'Y'\n              and &natural_key in (\n                  select &natural_key from work._dim_changes\n                  where _change_type = 'UPDATE'\n              );\n        quit;\n\n        /* Step 2b: Insert new versions of changed and genuinely new records */\n        proc sql;\n            insert into &dim_table\n            select monotonic()   as dim_surrogate_key,\n                   s.*,\n                   "&process_date"d  as effective_from,\n                   '31DEC9999'd      as effective_to,\n                   'Y'               as current_flag\n            from work._dim_changes as s;\n        quit;\n\n    %end;\n    %else %do;\n\n        /* SCD Type 1: Simple overwrite of existing rows */\n        proc sql;\n            delete from &dim_table\n            where &natural_key in (\n                select &natural_key from work._dim_changes\n            );\n\n            insert into &dim_table\n            select * from work._dim_changes;\n        quit;\n\n    %end;\n\n    %log_step(step_name=load_dim_&dim_table, status=OK,\n              detail=New=&new_count Updated=&upd_count, row_count=&src_count);\n\n    /* Clean up temp table */\n    proc datasets lib=work nolist; delete _dim_changes; quit;\n\n%mend load_dimension;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 3: Source Validation Loop                                         */\n/*   Iterates over all expected source tables and validates each one.        */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n%macro validate_all_sources;\n    %local i;\n\n    /* Define source tables as indexed macro variable arrays */\n    %let src_lib1 = raw;   %let src_tbl1 = customers;\n    %let src_lib2 = raw;   %let src_tbl2 = products;\n    %let src_lib3 = raw;   %let src_tbl3 = transactions;\n    %let src_lib4 = raw;   %let src_tbl4 = accounts;\n    %let src_lib5 = raw;   %let src_tbl5 = fx_rates;\n    %let src_lib6 = raw;   %let src_tbl6 = calendar;\n\n    %do i = 1 %to &n_sources;\n        %put NOTE: Validating source &i of &n_sources: &&src_lib&i...&&src_tbl&i;\n        %check_source_data(lib=&&src_lib&i, table=&&src_tbl&i, min_rows=10);\n    %end;\n\n    %put NOTE: [validate_all_sources] Validation complete. valid_sources=&valid_sources;\n%mend validate_all_sources;\n\n/* Execute the validation loop */\n%validate_all_sources;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 4: Early Exit Check                                               */\n/*   Abort the pipeline if any source validation failed.                     */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n%if &valid_sources = 0 %then %do;\n    %log_step(step_name=VALIDATION, status=ERROR,\n              detail=One or more source tables failed validation);\n    %put ERROR: ETL pipeline aborted due to source validation failure.;\n    endsas;\n%end;\n\n%log_step(step_name=VALIDATION, status=OK, detail=All 6 sources validated);\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 5: Customer Dimension \u2014 Extract and Transform                     */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\ndata staging.customer_stg (drop=_:);\n    set raw.customers;\n\n    /* Standardize name fields */\n    first_name = propcase(strip(first_name));\n    last_name  = propcase(strip(last_name));\n    full_name  = catx(' ', first_name, last_name);\n\n    /* Derive customer age band from date of birth */\n    if not missing(date_of_birth) then do;\n        _age = intck('YEAR', date_of_birth, "&process_date"d);\n        select;\n            when (_age < 25)           age_band = '18-24';\n            when (25 <= _age < 35)     age_band = '25-34';\n            when (35 <= _age < 45)     age_band = '35-44';\n            when (45 <= _age < 55)     age_band = '45-54';\n            when (55 <= _age < 65)     age_band = '55-64';\n            otherwise                  age_band = '65+';\n        end;\n    end;\n    else do;\n        age_band = 'Unknown';\n    end;\n\n    /* Clean and validate email address */\n    email = lowcase(strip(email));\n    if index(email, '@') = 0 then email = '';\n\n    /* Map state to sales region */\n    length region $20;\n    select (upcase(state));\n        when ('NY','NJ','CT','MA','PA') region = 'Northeast';\n        when ('IL','OH','MI','WI','MN') region = 'Midwest';\n        when ('TX','FL','GA','NC','VA') region = 'South';\n        when ('CA','WA','OR','AZ','CO') region = 'West';\n        otherwise                       region = 'Other';\n    end;\n\n    /* Record metadata */\n    load_date     = "&process_date"d;\n    source_system = 'CORE_BANKING';\n    format load_date yymmdd10.;\nrun;\n\n%log_step(step_name=CUSTOMER_EXTRACT, status=OK,\n          detail=Customer staging table created);\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 6: Customer Deduplication via PROC SQL                            */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\nproc sql;\n    create table staging.customer_dedup as\n    select a.*\n    from staging.customer_stg as a\n    inner join (\n        select customer_id,\n               min(monotonic()) as first_row\n        from staging.customer_stg\n        group by customer_id\n    ) as b\n        on a.customer_id = b.customer_id\n    order by a.customer_id;\n\n    /* Capture dedup metrics */\n    select count(distinct customer_id) into :dedup_count trimmed\n        from staging.customer_dedup;\nquit;\n\n%put NOTE: [Dedup] Unique customers retained: &dedup_count;\n%log_step(step_name=CUSTOMER_DEDUP, status=OK,\n          detail=&dedup_count unique customers, row_count=&dedup_count);\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 7: Dimension Loading \u2014 Invoke SCD Type 2 Macros                   */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n/* Load customer dimension with SCD Type 2 history tracking */\n%load_dimension(dim_table=mart.customer_dim,\n                src_table=staging.customer_dedup,\n                natural_key=customer_id, scd_type=2);\n\n/* Load product dimension with SCD Type 2 */\n%load_dimension(dim_table=mart.product_dim,\n                src_table=staging.product_stg,\n                natural_key=product_id, scd_type=2);\n\n/* Load date/calendar dimension \u2014 SCD Type 1 (overwrite) since dates are static */\n%load_dimension(dim_table=mart.date_dim,\n                src_table=staging.calendar_stg,\n                natural_key=calendar_date, scd_type=1);\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 8: Fact Table Extraction \u2014 Transactions with Multi-Source Merge    */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\ndata staging.fact_transactions\n     staging.fact_txn_rejects;\n\n    merge raw.transactions  (in=a)\n          raw.accounts      (in=b keep=account_id account_type currency_code)\n          raw.fx_rates      (in=c keep=currency_code fx_rate_to_usd);\n    by account_id;\n\n    if a;  /* Keep only rows with a transaction record */\n\n    /* Convert to base currency (USD) */\n    if currency_code ne 'USD' and not missing(fx_rate_to_usd) then\n        amount_usd = amount * fx_rate_to_usd;\n    else\n        amount_usd = amount;\n\n    /* Classify transaction type into broad category */\n    length txn_category $15;\n    select (upcase(transaction_type));\n        when ('DEP','XFER_IN','INT_CREDIT')  txn_category = 'INFLOW';\n        when ('WDR','XFER_OUT','FEE','TAX')  txn_category = 'OUTFLOW';\n        otherwise                             txn_category = 'OTHER';\n    end;\n\n    /* Derive fiscal period attributes */\n    fiscal_year    = year(transaction_date);\n    fiscal_quarter = ceil(month(transaction_date) / 3);\n    fiscal_month   = month(transaction_date);\n\n    /* Data quality check \u2014 reject incomplete records */\n    _valid = 1;\n    if missing(amount) then _valid = 0;\n    if missing(transaction_date) then _valid = 0;\n    if missing(account_id) then _valid = 0;\n\n    if _valid = 0 then output staging.fact_txn_rejects;\n    else output staging.fact_transactions;\n\n    drop _valid;\nrun;\n\n%log_step(step_name=FACT_EXTRACT, status=OK,\n          detail=Transaction fact staging and rejects created);\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 9: Fact Aggregation \u2014 Daily Account Summaries via PROC SQL        */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\nproc sql;\n    create table staging.daily_account_summary as\n    select t.account_id,\n           t.transaction_date,\n           t.account_type,\n           sum(case when t.txn_category = 'INFLOW'\n                    then t.amount_usd else 0 end)   as total_inflow,\n           sum(case when t.txn_category = 'OUTFLOW'\n                    then t.amount_usd else 0 end)   as total_outflow,\n           sum(t.amount_usd)                        as net_amount,\n           count(*)                                 as txn_count,\n           min(t.amount_usd)                        as min_txn_amount,\n           max(t.amount_usd)                        as max_txn_amount\n    from staging.fact_transactions as t\n    group by t.account_id, t.transaction_date, t.account_type\n    order by t.account_id, t.transaction_date;\nquit;\n\n%log_step(step_name=DAILY_SUMMARY, status=OK,\n          detail=Daily account summaries aggregated);\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 10: Running Balances with RETAIN                                  */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\ndata mart.account_balances;\n    set staging.daily_account_summary;\n    by account_id transaction_date;\n\n    retain running_balance   0\n           cumulative_inflow  0\n           cumulative_outflow 0\n           days_active        0;\n\n    /* Reset accumulators for each new account */\n    if first.account_id then do;\n        running_balance    = 0;\n        cumulative_inflow  = 0;\n        cumulative_outflow = 0;\n        days_active        = 0;\n    end;\n\n    /* Accumulate running totals */\n    running_balance    = running_balance + net_amount;\n    cumulative_inflow  = cumulative_inflow + total_inflow;\n    cumulative_outflow = cumulative_outflow + abs(total_outflow);\n    days_active + 1;\n\n    /* Derived metrics */\n    if cumulative_inflow > 0 then\n        outflow_ratio = cumulative_outflow / cumulative_inflow;\n    else\n        outflow_ratio = .;\n\n    avg_daily_net = running_balance / days_active;\n\n    /* Flag accounts with concerning patterns */\n    length balance_flag $10;\n    if running_balance < 0 then balance_flag = 'NEGATIVE';\n    else if running_balance < 1000 then balance_flag = 'LOW';\n    else balance_flag = 'NORMAL';\n\n    format running_balance cumulative_inflow cumulative_outflow\n           avg_daily_net dollar15.2;\nrun;\n\n%log_step(step_name=RUNNING_BALANCE, status=OK,\n          detail=Account running balances computed);\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 11: Executive Summary via PROC SQL                                */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\nproc sql;\n    create table mart.executive_summary as\n    select b.account_type,\n           b.balance_flag,\n           count(distinct b.account_id)  as account_count,\n           sum(b.running_balance)        as total_balance,\n           avg(b.running_balance)        as avg_balance,\n           sum(b.cumulative_inflow)      as total_inflows,\n           sum(b.cumulative_outflow)     as total_outflows,\n           avg(b.outflow_ratio)          as avg_outflow_ratio\n    from mart.account_balances as b\n    where b.transaction_date = (\n        select max(transaction_date) from mart.account_balances\n    )\n    group by b.account_type, b.balance_flag\n    order by b.account_type, b.balance_flag;\nquit;\n\n%log_step(step_name=EXEC_SUMMARY, status=OK,\n          detail=Executive summary table generated);\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 12: Error Detection and Conditional Handling                      */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n%if &syserr ne 0 or &etl_rc ne 0 %then %do;\n    %put ERROR: ETL pipeline encountered errors. syserr=&syserr etl_rc=&etl_rc;\n\n    %log_step(step_name=ERROR_HANDLER, status=ERROR,\n              detail=Pipeline errors detected \u2014 review log);\n\n    /* Write error flag file for downstream job monitoring */\n    data _null_;\n        file '/data/finance/flags/etl_error.flag';\n        put "ETL_RUN_ID=&etl_run_id";\n        put "ERROR_TIME=%sysfunc(datetime(), datetime20.)";\n        put "SYSERR=&syserr";\n        put "ETL_RC=&etl_rc";\n    run;\n\n    /* Send error notification via email */\n    filename mymail email\n        to=('dw-ops@company.com')\n        subject="ETL FAILURE: gsh_01_enterprise_etl \u2014 &process_date"\n        type='text/plain';\n    data _null_;\n        file mymail;\n        put "The Financial DWH ETL pipeline failed on &process_date.";\n        put "Run ID : &etl_run_id";\n        put "SYSERR : &syserr";\n        put "ETL_RC : &etl_rc";\n        put "Please review the SAS log for details.";\n    run;\n    filename mymail clear;\n%end;\n%else %do;\n    %put NOTE: ETL pipeline completed successfully. No errors detected.;\n%end;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 13: Archival \u2014 Move Aged Data to Archive Library                  */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\ndata archive.transactions_%sysfunc(compress(&process_date, -)) (compress=yes);\n    set raw.transactions;\n    where transaction_date <= "&process_date"d - 365;\n\n    /* Add archive metadata */\n    archive_date   = "&process_date"d;\n    archive_run_id = "&etl_run_id";\n    format archive_date yymmdd10.;\nrun;\n\n/* Remove archived records from the active raw layer */\nproc sql;\n    delete from raw.transactions\n    where transaction_date <= "&process_date"d - 365;\nquit;\n\n%log_step(step_name=ARCHIVAL, status=OK,\n          detail=Historical transactions archived and purged from raw);\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 14: Final Cleanup and Audit Trail                                 */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n/* Clear intermediate staging datasets */\nproc datasets lib=staging nolist;\n    delete customer_stg\n           customer_dedup\n           fact_transactions\n           fact_txn_rejects\n           daily_account_summary;\nquit;\n\n/* Record final pipeline completion in audit log */\n%log_step(step_name=PIPELINE_COMPLETE, status=OK,\n          detail=All ETL steps completed successfully);\n\n/* Write completion flag for downstream batch scheduler */\ndata _null_;\n    file '/data/finance/flags/etl_complete.flag';\n    put "ETL_RUN_ID=&etl_run_id";\n    put "COMPLETION_TIME=%sysfunc(datetime(), datetime20.)";\n    put "PROCESS_DATE=&process_date";\n    put "STATUS=SUCCESS";\nrun;\n\n/* Reset options to defaults */\noptions nomprint nomlogic nosymbolgen;\n\n%put NOTE: ===== gsh_01_enterprise_etl.sas completed at %sysfunc(datetime(), datetime20.) =====;\n-gold_meta15]
