Nakamura;\n%let report_title    = Executive Management Report;\n%let run_timestamp   = %sysfunc(datetime());\n%let run_date        = %sysfunc(today(), yymmdd10.);\n\n/* --- Distribution and delivery parameters --- */\n%let dist_list       = ceo@corp.com cfo@corp.com coo@corp.com vp_sales@corp.com vp_hr@corp.com;\n%let report_style    = journal;\n%let output_path     = /reports/executive/output;\n%let report_rc       = 0;\n%let sections_done   = 0;\n\n/* --- Load shared report formatting templates --- */\n%include '/reports/shared/report_templates.sas';\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 2: Macro Definitions                                              */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* %setup_ods \u2014 Initialize ODS output destination based on format    */\n/*   Parameters: format= (PDF|HTML|EXCEL), title=, style=            */\n/*   Opens the appropriate ODS destination with formatting options    */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n%macro setup_ods(format=PDF, title=Executive Report, style=journal);\n    %local output_file timestamp_str;\n    %let timestamp_str = %sysfunc(putn(%sysfunc(datetime()), datetime15.));\n    %let output_file   = &output_path./exec_report_&report_period._&timestamp_str;\n    %put NOTE: ========================================================;\n    %put NOTE: Setting up ODS output \u2014 Format: &format, Style: &style;\n    %put NOTE: Output file: &output_file;\n    %put NOTE: ========================================================;\n\n    /* --- Select ODS destination based on format parameter --- */\n    %if &format = PDF %then %do;\n        ods pdf file="&output_file..pdf"\n            style=&style\n            bookmarkgen=yes\n            pdftoc=3\n            compress=9\n            author="&report_author"\n            title="&title \u2014 &report_period";\n        ods pdf select all;\n    %end;\n\n    %if &format = HTML %then %do;\n        ods html path="&output_path"\n            file="exec_report_&report_period..html"\n            style=&style\n            contents="exec_report_&report_period._toc.html"\n            frame="exec_report_&report_period._frame.html"\n            headtext='<link rel="stylesheet" href="report_style.css">';\n        ods html select all;\n    %end;\n\n    %if &format = EXCEL %then %do;\n        ods excel file="&output_file..xlsx"\n            style=&style\n            options(sheet_name="Cover"\n                    embedded_titles="yes"\n                    embedded_footnotes="yes"\n                    frozen_headers="yes");\n        ods excel select all;\n    %end;\n\n    /* --- Set global titles and footnotes --- */\n    title1 j=c bold height=14pt "&title";\n    title2 j=c height=11pt "Period: &report_period | Fiscal Year: &fiscal_year";\n    title3 j=c height=10pt "Prepared by: &report_author | Date: &run_date";\n    footnote1 j=l "Confidential \u2014 For Executive Distribution Only";\n    footnote2 j=r "Generated: &run_date at %sysfunc(putn(%sysfunc(time()), time8.))";\n%mend setup_ods;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* %exec_summary \u2014 Executive summary with key financial KPIs         */\n/*   Parameters: period= (report period identifier)                   */\n/*   Outputs: work.exec_kpis, work.exec_period_change                */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n%macro exec_summary(period=);\n    %local n_metrics;\n    %put NOTE: ========================================================;\n    %put NOTE: Generating Executive Summary \u2014 Period: &period;\n    %put NOTE: ========================================================;\n\n    /* --- Step 1: Extract key financial metrics from finance tables --- */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        create table work.exec_kpis as\n        select  'Revenue'          as metric_name length=30,\n                sum(revenue_amount)           as current_value format=comma20.2,\n                sum(prior_revenue)            as prior_value   format=comma20.2,\n                sum(budget_amount)            as budget_value  format=comma20.2\n        from finance.income_statement\n        where period = "&period"\n        union all\n        select  'EBITDA'           as metric_name,\n                sum(ebitda_amount)            as current_value,\n                sum(prior_ebitda)             as prior_value,\n                sum(ebitda_budget)            as budget_value\n        from finance.income_statement\n        where period = "&period"\n        union all\n        select  'Net Income'       as metric_name,\n                sum(net_income)               as current_value,\n                sum(prior_net_income)         as prior_value,\n                sum(ni_budget)                as budget_value\n        from finance.income_statement\n        where period = "&period"\n        union all\n        select  'Gross Margin %'   as metric_name,\n                sum(gross_profit)/sum(revenue_amount) * 100 as current_value,\n                sum(prior_gp)/sum(prior_revenue) * 100      as prior_value,\n                sum(gp_budget)/sum(budget_amount) * 100     as budget_value\n        from finance.income_statement\n        where period = "&period";\n\n        select count(*) into :n_metrics trimmed\n        from work.exec_kpis;\n    quit;\n\n    %put NOTE: Extracted &n_metrics key performance indicators.;\n\n    /* --- Step 2: Compute period-over-period changes --- */\n    data work.exec_period_change;\n        set work.exec_kpis;\n        length trend $10 status $20;\n\n        /* Calculate percentage change from prior period */\n        if prior_value ne 0 then\n            pct_change = ((current_value - prior_value) / abs(prior_value)) * 100;\n        else\n            pct_change = .;\n\n        /* Calculate budget variance */\n        if budget_value ne 0 then\n            budget_var = ((current_value - budget_value) / abs(budget_value)) * 100;\n        else\n            budget_var = .;\n\n        /* Assign trend indicator for direction arrow */\n        if pct_change > 5 then trend = 'UP';\n        else if pct_change < -5 then trend = 'DOWN';\n        else trend = 'FLAT';\n\n        /* Traffic light status based on budget performance */\n        if budget_var >= 0 then status = 'GREEN';\n        else if budget_var >= -5 then status = 'AMBER';\n        else status = 'RED';\n\n        format pct_change budget_var 8.1;\n    run;\n\n    /* --- Step 3: Executive KPI report with traffic light formatting --- */\n    proc report data=work.exec_period_change nowd\n        style(header)=[backgroundcolor=cx003366 foreground=white\n                        font_weight=bold font_size=10pt];\n\n        columns metric_name current_value prior_value pct_change\n                budget_value budget_var status;\n\n        define metric_name   / group   'Key Metric'         style=[font_weight=bold];\n        define current_value / sum     'Current Period'     format=comma20.2;\n        define prior_value   / sum     'Prior Period'       format=comma20.2;\n        define pct_change    / display 'Change %'           format=8.1;\n        define budget_value  / sum     'Budget'             format=comma20.2;\n        define budget_var    / display 'Variance %'         format=8.1;\n        define status        / display 'Status'             style=[font_weight=bold];\n\n        /* --- Conditional formatting: traffic light cell styling --- */\n        compute status;\n            if status = 'GREEN' then\n                call define(_col_, 'style',\n                    'style=[backgroundcolor=cx90EE90 foreground=cx006400]');\n            else if status = 'AMBER' then\n                call define(_col_, 'style',\n                    'style=[backgroundcolor=cxFFD700 foreground=cx8B4513]');\n            else if status = 'RED' then\n                call define(_col_, 'style',\n                    'style=[backgroundcolor=cxFF6347 foreground=white]');\n        endcomp;\n\n        /* --- Summary line at bottom of report --- */\n        compute after;\n            line ' ';\n            line "Report Period: &period | Generated: &run_date";\n        endcomp;\n    run;\n\n    %let sections_done = %eval(&sections_done + 1);\n    %put NOTE: Executive Summary complete. Sections done: &sections_done;\n%mend exec_summary;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* %sales_section \u2014 Regional sales analysis with pipeline metrics    */\n/*   Parameters: region= (specific region name or ALL for iteration)  */\n/*   Outputs: work.sales_pipeline, work.sales_quota, work.sales_chart */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n%macro sales_section(region=ALL);\n    %local i n_regions reg;\n    %put NOTE: ========================================================;\n    %put NOTE: Generating Sales Section \u2014 Region: &region;\n    %put NOTE: ========================================================;\n\n    /* --- Step 1: Extract pipeline and bookings data via PROC SQL --- */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        create table work.sales_pipeline as\n        select  s.region,\n                s.product_line,\n                s.sales_rep,\n                sum(s.pipeline_value)     as pipeline       format=comma18.2,\n                sum(s.bookings)           as bookings       format=comma18.2,\n                sum(s.quota_amount)       as quota          format=comma18.2,\n                count(distinct s.deal_id) as deal_count,\n                sum(s.bookings) / sum(s.quota_amount) * 100\n                                          as attainment_pct format=8.1\n        from sales.opportunities s\n        where s.close_date between &period_start_dt and &period_end_dt\n            %if &region ne ALL %then\n                and s.region = "&region";\n        group by s.region, s.product_line, s.sales_rep\n        order by s.region, attainment_pct desc;\n    quit;\n\n    /* --- Step 2: Product x Region cross-tabulation --- */\n    proc tabulate data=work.sales_pipeline\n        style=[font_size=9pt cellpadding=4];\n        class region product_line;\n        var bookings pipeline;\n        table product_line='Product Line' all='Total',\n              region='Region' * (bookings='Bookings'*sum*f=comma16.2\n                                 pipeline='Pipeline'*sum*f=comma16.2)\n              all='Grand Total' * (bookings='Bookings'*sum*f=comma16.2\n                                   pipeline='Pipeline'*sum*f=comma16.2)\n              / box='Sales Analysis';\n    run;\n\n    /* --- Step 3: Quota attainment calculation per rep --- */\n    data work.sales_quota;\n        set work.sales_pipeline;\n        length performance_tier $15;\n\n        /* Classify performance tier based on quota attainment */\n        if attainment_pct >= 120 then performance_tier = 'Exceptional';\n        else if attainment_pct >= 100 then performance_tier = 'On Target';\n        else if attainment_pct >= 80 then performance_tier = 'Below Target';\n        else performance_tier = 'At Risk';\n\n        /* Flag sales reps needing management attention */\n        needs_review = (attainment_pct < 70);\n        format needs_review yesno.;\n    run;\n\n    /* --- Step 4: Regional detail iteration if ALL specified --- */\n    %if &region = ALL %then %do;\n        %let n_regions = 4;\n        %do i = 1 %to &n_regions;\n            %let reg = %scan(NORTH SOUTH EAST WEST, &i, %str( ));\n            %put NOTE: Processing regional detail for &reg region;\n\n            /* Regional summary statistics per product line */\n            proc sql noprint;\n                create table work.region_&reg as\n                select  product_line,\n                        sum(bookings)        as total_bookings  format=comma18.2,\n                        sum(pipeline)        as total_pipeline  format=comma18.2,\n                        sum(quota)           as total_quota     format=comma18.2,\n                        mean(attainment_pct) as avg_attainment  format=8.1,\n                        count(distinct sales_rep) as rep_count\n                from work.sales_pipeline\n                where region = "&reg"\n                group by product_line;\n            quit;\n        %end;\n    %end;\n\n    /* --- Step 5: Prepare chart-ready dataset for PROC SGPLOT --- */\n    data work.sales_chart;\n        set work.sales_pipeline;\n        /* Aggregate to region level for bar/line chart visualization */\n        keep region bookings pipeline quota attainment_pct;\n    run;\n\n    /* Sort for chart readiness */\n    proc sort data=work.sales_chart nodupkey;\n        by region;\n    run;\n\n    %let sections_done = %eval(&sections_done + 1);\n    %put NOTE: Sales Section complete. Sections done: &sections_done;\n%mend sales_section;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* %hr_section \u2014 Workforce analytics: headcount, turnover, diversity */\n/*   Outputs: work.hr_headcount, work.hr_diversity, work.hr_comp,    */\n/*            work.hr_new_hires, work.hr_departures                   */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n%macro hr_section;\n    %local total_hc turnover_rate n_hires n_departures;\n    %put NOTE: ========================================================;\n    %put NOTE: Generating HR / Workforce Analytics Section;\n    %put NOTE: ========================================================;\n\n    /* --- Step 1: Compute headcount and turnover rates --- */\n    data work.hr_headcount;\n        set hr.employee_roster end=eof;\n        where employment_status = 'ACTIVE'\n           or (termination_date between &period_start_dt and &period_end_dt);\n\n        /* Calculate employee tenure in months and years */\n        tenure_months = intck('month', hire_date, &period_end_dt);\n        tenure_years  = tenure_months / 12;\n\n        /* Flag turnover within the reporting period */\n        if employment_status = 'TERMINATED' and\n           termination_date between &period_start_dt and &period_end_dt\n        then is_turnover = 1;\n        else is_turnover = 0;\n\n        /* Retain running totals for headcount and turnover counts */\n        retain active_count 0 turnover_count 0;\n        if employment_status = 'ACTIVE' then active_count + 1;\n        if is_turnover then turnover_count + 1;\n\n        /* On last record, compute turnover rate and store in macro vars */\n        if eof then do;\n            turnover_rate = (turnover_count / active_count) * 100;\n            call symputx('total_hc', active_count);\n            call symputx('turnover_rate', put(turnover_rate, 8.2));\n        end;\n\n        format tenure_years 5.1 turnover_rate 8.2;\n    run;\n\n    %put NOTE: Total headcount: &total_hc | Turnover rate: &turnover_rate pct;\n\n    /* --- Step 2: Diversity metrics via PROC FREQ --- */\n    proc freq data=work.hr_headcount noprint;\n        where employment_status = 'ACTIVE';\n        tables gender              / out=work.hr_div_gender;\n        tables ethnicity           / out=work.hr_div_ethnicity;\n        tables department * gender / out=work.hr_div_dept_gender;\n    run;\n\n    /* Combine diversity dimension tables into single reporting dataset */\n    data work.hr_diversity;\n        length category $30 value $50;\n        set work.hr_div_gender    (in=a rename=(gender=value))\n            work.hr_div_ethnicity (in=b rename=(ethnicity=value));\n        if a then category = 'Gender';\n        if b then category = 'Ethnicity';\n        pct = percent;\n        format pct 8.1;\n        keep category value count pct;\n    run;\n\n    /* --- Step 3: Compensation analysis via PROC MEANS --- */\n    proc means data=work.hr_headcount noprint\n        where=(employment_status = 'ACTIVE');\n        class department job_level;\n        var base_salary total_compensation;\n        output out=work.hr_comp\n            mean(base_salary)=avg_salary\n            median(base_salary)=med_salary\n            min(base_salary)=min_salary\n            max(base_salary)=max_salary\n            mean(total_compensation)=avg_total_comp\n            n(base_salary)=emp_count;\n    run;\n\n    /* --- Step 4: New hires and departures via PROC SQL --- */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        /* New hires within the reporting period */\n        create table work.hr_new_hires as\n        select employee_id, employee_name, department, job_title,\n               hire_date, base_salary\n        from hr.employee_roster\n        where hire_date between &period_start_dt and &period_end_dt\n        order by department, hire_date;\n\n        select count(*) into :n_hires trimmed\n        from work.hr_new_hires;\n\n        /* Departures within the reporting period */\n        create table work.hr_departures as\n        select employee_id, employee_name, department, job_title,\n               termination_date, termination_reason, tenure_months\n        from work.hr_headcount\n        where is_turnover = 1\n        order by department, termination_date;\n\n        select count(*) into :n_departures trimmed\n        from work.hr_departures;\n    quit;\n\n    %put NOTE: New hires: &n_hires | Departures: &n_departures;\n    %let sections_done = %eval(&sections_done + 1);\n    %put NOTE: HR Section complete. Sections done: &sections_done;\n%mend hr_section;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* %finance_section \u2014 Financial statements: income, balance sheet,   */\n/*   budget variance with formatted P&L and YTD accumulation          */\n/*   Outputs: work.income_stmt, work.balance_sheet, work.ytd_accum,  */\n/*            work.budget_variance                                     */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n%macro finance_section;\n    %local n_line_items total_revenue total_expenses;\n    %put NOTE: ========================================================;\n    %put NOTE: Generating Finance Section \u2014 Fiscal Year: &fiscal_year;\n    %put NOTE: ========================================================;\n\n    /* --- Step 1: Income statement line items via PROC SQL --- */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        create table work.income_stmt as\n        select  line_item_id,\n                line_item_name,\n                line_item_category,\n                display_order,\n                indent_level,\n                sum(case when period = "&report_period"\n                    then amount else 0 end)     as current_amount\n                    format=comma20.2,\n                sum(case when period = "&report_period"\n                    then budget else 0 end)     as budget_amount\n                    format=comma20.2,\n                sum(amount)                     as ytd_amount\n                    format=comma20.2,\n                sum(budget)                     as ytd_budget\n                    format=comma20.2\n        from finance.gl_detail\n        where fiscal_year = &fiscal_year\n          and line_item_category in ('REVENUE','COGS','OPEX','OTHER_INCOME',\n                                     'INTEREST','TAX')\n        group by line_item_id, line_item_name, line_item_category,\n                 display_order, indent_level\n        order by display_order;\n\n        select count(*) into :n_line_items trimmed\n        from work.income_stmt;\n\n        select sum(current_amount) into :total_revenue trimmed\n        from work.income_stmt\n        where line_item_category = 'REVENUE';\n\n        select sum(current_amount) into :total_expenses trimmed\n        from work.income_stmt\n        where line_item_category in ('COGS','OPEX');\n    quit;\n\n    %put NOTE: Income statement: &n_line_items line items processed.;\n\n    /* --- Step 2: Balance sheet computations (assets, liabilities, equity) --- */\n    data work.balance_sheet;\n        set finance.trial_balance end=eof;\n        where fiscal_year = &fiscal_year\n          and period = "&report_period";\n\n        length section $20;\n\n        /* Classify accounts into balance sheet sections */\n        if account_type in ('CASH','AR','INVENTORY','PREPAID','OTHER_CA')\n            then section = 'Current Assets';\n        else if account_type in ('PPE','INTANGIBLE','INVESTMENT','OTHER_NCA')\n            then section = 'Non-Current Assets';\n        else if account_type in ('AP','ACCRUED','ST_DEBT','OTHER_CL')\n            then section = 'Current Liabilities';\n        else if account_type in ('LT_DEBT','PENSION','DEFERRED_TAX','OTHER_NCL')\n            then section = 'Non-Current Liabilities';\n        else if account_type in ('COMMON','RETAINED','AOCI','TREASURY')\n            then section = 'Equity';\n\n        /* Compute net amounts based on normal balances */\n        if section in ('Current Assets','Non-Current Assets') then\n            net_amount = debit_balance - credit_balance;\n        else\n            net_amount = credit_balance - debit_balance;\n\n        /* Running totals using RETAIN for balance sheet equation check */\n        retain total_assets 0 total_liabilities 0 total_equity 0;\n        if section in ('Current Assets','Non-Current Assets') then\n            total_assets + net_amount;\n        else if section in ('Current Liabilities','Non-Current Liabilities') then\n            total_liabilities + net_amount;\n        else if section = 'Equity' then\n            total_equity + net_amount;\n\n        /* On last record, validate Assets = Liabilities + Equity */\n        if eof then do;\n            bs_difference = total_assets - total_liabilities - total_equity;\n            if abs(bs_difference) > 0.01 then\n                put 'WARNING: Balance sheet out of balance by '\n                    bs_difference comma20.2;\n        end;\n\n        format net_amount total_assets total_liabilities total_equity\n               bs_difference comma20.2;\n    run;\n\n    /* --- Step 3: Formatted P&L report with indentation levels --- */\n    proc report data=work.income_stmt nowd\n        style(header)=[backgroundcolor=cx003366 foreground=white font_weight=bold];\n\n        columns display_order indent_level line_item_name\n                current_amount budget_amount ytd_amount ytd_budget;\n\n        define display_order   / order noprint;\n        define indent_level    / display noprint;\n        define line_item_name  / display 'Line Item'\n            style=[font_size=9pt];\n        define current_amount  / sum 'Current Period' format=comma20.2;\n        define budget_amount   / sum 'Budget'         format=comma20.2;\n        define ytd_amount      / sum 'YTD Actual'     format=comma20.2;\n        define ytd_budget      / sum 'YTD Budget'     format=comma20.2;\n\n        /* Apply indentation styling based on hierarchy level */\n        compute line_item_name;\n            if indent_level = 0 then\n                call define(_col_, 'style',\n                    'style=[font_weight=bold backgroundcolor=cxE8E8E8]');\n            else if indent_level = 1 then\n                call define(_col_, 'style',\n                    'style=[indent=15pt]');\n            else if indent_level = 2 then\n                call define(_col_, 'style',\n                    'style=[indent=30pt font_style=italic]');\n        endcomp;\n\n        /* Highlight negative variances in red */\n        compute current_amount;\n            if current_amount.sum < 0 then\n                call define(_col_, 'style',\n                    'style=[foreground=red]');\n        endcomp;\n\n        rbreak after / summarize\n            style=[font_weight=bold backgroundcolor=cx003366 foreground=white];\n    run;\n\n    /* --- Step 4: YTD accumulation with RETAIN for running totals --- */\n    data work.ytd_accum;\n        set finance.monthly_actuals;\n        where fiscal_year = &fiscal_year;\n        by line_item_id;\n\n        retain ytd_running 0;\n\n        /* Reset YTD accumulator at start of each line item group */\n        if first.line_item_id then ytd_running = 0;\n        ytd_running + monthly_amount;\n\n        /* Calculate YTD variance against prorated annual budget */\n        months_elapsed  = month(period_date);\n        prorated_budget = (annual_budget / 12) * months_elapsed;\n        ytd_variance    = ytd_running - prorated_budget;\n        ytd_var_pct     = (ytd_variance / prorated_budget) * 100;\n\n        format ytd_running prorated_budget ytd_variance comma20.2\n               ytd_var_pct 8.1;\n    run;\n\n    /* --- Step 5: Budget vs actual variance analysis --- */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        create table work.budget_variance as\n        select  a.line_item_category,\n                a.line_item_name,\n                a.current_amount,\n                a.budget_amount,\n                a.current_amount - a.budget_amount as variance\n                    format=comma20.2,\n                case when a.budget_amount ne 0 then\n                    (a.current_amount - a.budget_amount) /\n                    abs(a.budget_amount) * 100\n                else . end                         as var_pct\n                    format=8.1,\n                case when calculated var_pct > 5 then 'FAVORABLE'\n                     when calculated var_pct < -5 then 'UNFAVORABLE'\n                     else 'ON_TRACK' end           as var_status length=15\n        from work.income_stmt a\n        order by abs(calculated variance) desc;\n    quit;\n\n    %let sections_done = %eval(&sections_done + 1);\n    %put NOTE: Finance Section complete. Sections done: &sections_done;\n%mend finance_section;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* %appendix \u2014 Supporting tables: top customers, AR aging, notes     */\n/*   Outputs: work.top_customers, rptout (AR aging print),           */\n/*            work.methodology                                        */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n%macro appendix;\n    %put NOTE: ========================================================;\n    %put NOTE: Generating Appendix \u2014 Supporting Tables and Notes;\n    %put NOTE: ========================================================;\n\n    /* --- Appendix A: Top 20 customers by revenue --- */\n    proc sql outobs=20;\n        create table work.top_customers as\n        select  c.customer_id,\n                c.customer_name,\n                c.industry_segment,\n                sum(s.revenue_amount)    as total_revenue   format=comma18.2,\n                count(distinct s.order_id) as order_count,\n                max(s.order_date)        as last_order_date format=yymmdd10.,\n                sum(s.revenue_amount) /\n                    (select sum(revenue_amount)\n                     from sales.order_detail\n                     where order_date between &period_start_dt\n                                         and &period_end_dt) * 100\n                                         as revenue_share   format=8.1\n        from sales.order_detail s\n        inner join sales.customer_master c\n            on s.customer_id = c.customer_id\n        where s.order_date between &period_start_dt and &period_end_dt\n        group by c.customer_id, c.customer_name, c.industry_segment\n        order by total_revenue desc;\n    quit;\n\n    /* --- Appendix B: Open AR aging report --- */\n    proc print data=finance.ar_open_items noobs label\n        style(header)=[backgroundcolor=cx003366 foreground=white];\n        where open_balance > 0;\n        var customer_name invoice_id invoice_date due_date\n            open_balance aging_bucket days_past_due;\n        label customer_name  = 'Customer'\n              invoice_id     = 'Invoice #'\n              invoice_date   = 'Invoice Date'\n              due_date       = 'Due Date'\n              open_balance   = 'Open Balance'\n              aging_bucket   = 'Aging Bucket'\n              days_past_due  = 'Days Past Due';\n        sum open_balance;\n    run;\n\n    /* --- Appendix C: Methodology notes and assumptions --- */\n    data work.methodology;\n        length section $50 description $500;\n        section = 'Revenue Recognition';\n        description = 'Revenue is recognized per ASC 606 at point of delivery for product sales and ratably over the service period for subscriptions.';\n        output;\n        section = 'EBITDA Calculation';\n        description = 'EBITDA excludes stock-based compensation, restructuring charges, and one-time items per the company Non-GAAP policy.';\n        output;\n        section = 'Headcount Methodology';\n        description = 'Headcount reflects active employees as of period end. Contractors and temporary staff are excluded from the count.';\n        output;\n        section = 'Regional Allocation';\n        description = 'Shared services costs are allocated to regions based on headcount-weighted revenue contribution ratios.';\n        output;\n        section = 'Budget Variance';\n        description = 'Favorable variances (actuals exceeding budget for revenue, below budget for expenses) are shown in green.';\n        output;\n    run;\n\n    %let sections_done = %eval(&sections_done + 1);\n    %put NOTE: Appendix complete. Sections done: &sections_done;\n%mend appendix;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* %close_ods \u2014 Finalize ODS output and log report metadata          */\n/*   Closes active ODS destinations and records generation metadata   */\n/*   to the report output library for audit trail purposes            */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n%macro close_ods;\n    %put NOTE: ========================================================;\n    %put NOTE: Closing ODS Output and Logging Report Metadata;\n    %put NOTE: ========================================================;\n\n    /* --- Clear titles and footnotes before closing --- */\n    title;\n    footnote;\n\n    /* --- Close the active ODS destination --- */\n    %if &report_format = PDF %then %do;\n        ods pdf close;\n    %end;\n    %if &report_format = HTML %then %do;\n        ods html close;\n    %end;\n    %if &report_format = EXCEL %then %do;\n        ods excel close;\n    %end;\n\n    /* --- Log report generation metadata for audit trail --- */\n    data rptout.report_log;\n        length report_id $20 report_name $60 format $10 author $30\n               status $15 distribution $200;\n        report_id     = "RPT-EXEC-&report_period";\n        report_name   = "&report_title";\n        format        = "&report_format";\n        author        = "&report_author";\n        generated_dt  = datetime();\n        sections_cnt  = &sections_done;\n        status        = ifc(&report_rc = 0, 'SUCCESS', 'ERROR');\n        distribution  = "&dist_list";\n        run_duration  = datetime() - &run_timestamp;\n        format generated_dt datetime20. run_duration time8.;\n    run;\n\n    %put NOTE: Report metadata logged to rptout.report_log;\n%mend close_ods;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 3: Main Program Execution                                         */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n%put NOTE: ============================================================;\n%put NOTE: EXECUTIVE MANAGEMENT REPORT SUITE v3.0;\n%put NOTE: Period: &report_period | Format: &report_format;\n%put NOTE: Author: &report_author | Date: &run_date;\n%put NOTE: ============================================================;\n\n/* --- Step 1: Initialize ODS output destination --- */\n%setup_ods(format=&report_format, title=&report_title, style=&report_style);\n\n/* --- Step 2: Generate report sections in sequence --- */\n%exec_summary(period=&report_period);\n\n%sales_section(region=ALL);\n\n%hr_section;\n\n%finance_section;\n\n%appendix;\n\n/* --- Step 3: Close ODS and log report metadata --- */\n%close_ods;\n\n/* --- Step 4: Retrieve email distribution for delivery --- */\nproc sql noprint;\n    select trim(distribution) into :email_list separated by ' '\n    from rptout.report_log\n    where report_id = "RPT-EXEC-&report_period";\nquit;\n\n/* --- Step 5: Error handling and final status reporting --- */\n%if &report_rc ne 0 %then %do;\n    %put ERROR: Report generation failed with return code &report_rc;\n    %put ERROR: Review log for details. Sections completed: &sections_done;\n\n    /* Log error details to persistent error table */\n    data rptout.report_errors;\n        length report_id $20 error_msg $200 error_dt 8;\n        report_id = "RPT-EXEC-&report_period";\n        error_msg = "Generation failed at section &sections_done";\n        error_dt  = datetime();\n        format error_dt datetime20.;\n    run;\n%end;\n%else %do;\n    %put NOTE: Report generation completed successfully.;\n    %put NOTE: All &sections_done sections generated without errors.;\n%end;\n\n/* --- Cleanup temporary work datasets --- */\nproc datasets lib=work nolist nowarn;\n    delete exec_kpis exec_period_change\n           sales_pipeline sales_quota sales_chart\n           region_NORTH region_SOUTH region_EAST region_WEST\n           hr_headcount hr_diversity hr_div_gender hr_div_ethnicity\n           hr_div_dept_gender hr_comp hr_new_hires hr_departures\n           income_stmt balance_sheet ytd_accum budget_variance\n           top_customers methodology;\nquit;\n\n/* --- Reset options to defaults --- */\noptions nomprint nomlogic nosymbolgen;\n\n%put NOTE: ============================================================;\n%put NOTE: EXECUTIVE REPORT SUITE COMPLETE;\n%put NOTE: Period: &report_period;\n%put NOTE: Status: %sysfunc(ifc(&report_rc=0, SUCCESS, FAILED));\n%put NOTE: Report delivered to distribution list;\n%put NOTE: ============================================================;\n-gold_meta29]

'/data/finance/reporting';\n\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/* Step 1: Clean and validate raw financial transactions              */\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\ndata staging.txn_clean;\n    set raw.transactions\n        (where=(txn_date between '01JAN2025'd and '31DEC2025'd));\n\n    /* Standardize transaction amounts */\n    if txn_amount = . then txn_amount = 0;\n\n    if txn_amount < 0 then do;\n        txn_type = 'DEBIT';\n        abs_amount = abs(txn_amount);\n    end;\n    else do;\n        txn_type = 'CREDIT';\n        abs_amount = txn_amount;\n    end;\n\n    /* Derive quarter from transaction date */\n    txn_quarter = qtr(txn_date);\n    txn_year = year(txn_date);\n    quarter_label = cats('Q', txn_quarter, '_', txn_year);\n\n    /* Remove invalid account numbers */\n    if account_id = '' then delete;\n    if length(account_id) < 8 then delete;\n\n    /* Flag high-value transactions for compliance review */\n    if abs_amount > 100000 then high_value_flag = 'Y';\n    else high_value_flag = 'N';\n\n    /* Compute account age in days */\n    days_since_open = txn_date - account_open_date;\n\n    format txn_date account_open_date date9.\n           txn_amount abs_amount dollar15.2;\nrun;\n\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/* Step 2: Sort transactions by account and quarter                   */\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nproc sort data=staging.txn_clean\n          out=staging.txn_sorted;\n    by account_id txn_quarter;\nrun;\n\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/* Step 3: Quarterly aggregate statistics per account                 */\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nproc means data=staging.txn_sorted noprint nway;\n    class account_id txn_quarter quarter_label;\n    var abs_amount days_since_open;\n    output out=staging.quarterly_stats(drop=_type_ _freq_)\n        sum(abs_amount)   = total_amount\n        mean(abs_amount)  = avg_amount\n        max(abs_amount)   = max_amount\n        min(abs_amount)   = min_amount\n        n(abs_amount)     = txn_count\n        mean(days_since_open) = avg_account_age;\nrun;\n\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/* Step 4: Join with account master and compute utilization ratios    */\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nproc sql;\n    create table staging.account_summary as\n    select\n        q.account_id,\n        q.txn_quarter,\n        q.quarter_label,\n        q.total_amount,\n        q.avg_amount,\n        q.txn_count,\n        a.credit_limit,\n        a.account_type,\n        a.branch_code,\n        a.relationship_manager,\n        /* Utilization ratio: total spend vs credit limit */\n        case\n            when a.credit_limit > 0\n            then q.total_amount / a.credit_limit\n            else .\n        end as utilization_ratio,\n        /* Average transaction size */\n        case\n            when q.txn_count > 0\n            then q.total_amount / q.txn_count\n            else 0\n        end as avg_txn_size\n    from staging.quarterly_stats q\n    left join raw.account_master a\n        on q.account_id = a.account_id\n    order by q.account_id, q.txn_quarter;\nquit;\n\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/* Step 5: Categorize accounts as performing or non-performing        */\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\ndata tgt.account_classification;\n    set staging.account_summary;\n\n    length performance_category $20 risk_tier $10;\n\n    /* Classify based on utilization and transaction frequency */\n    if utilization_ratio = . then performance_category = 'UNSCORED';\n    else if utilization_ratio > 0.9 then performance_category = 'HIGH_RISK';\n    else if utilization_ratio > 0.7 then performance_category = 'WATCH_LIST';\n    else if utilization_ratio > 0.3 then performance_category = 'PERFORMING';\n    else performance_category = 'LOW_USAGE';\n\n    /* Assign risk tier for regulatory reporting */\n    select (performance_category);\n        when ('HIGH_RISK')   risk_tier = 'TIER_3';\n        when ('WATCH_LIST')  risk_tier = 'TIER_2';\n        when ('PERFORMING')  risk_tier = 'TIER_1';\n        when ('LOW_USAGE')   risk_tier = 'TIER_1';\n        otherwise            risk_tier = 'TIER_UNK';\n    end;\n\n    /* Calculate composite risk score */\n    risk_score = (utilization_ratio * 0.4) +\n                 (min(txn_count / 100, 1) * 0.3) +\n                 (min(total_amount / 500000, 1) * 0.3);\n\n    format utilization_ratio risk_score percent8.2\n           total_amount avg_amount dollar15.2;\nrun;\n\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/* Step 6: Generate formatted executive report                        */\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\ntitle1 'Quarterly Financial Summary Report';\ntitle2 'Account Performance Classification';\n\nproc report data=tgt.account_classification nowd;\n    column branch_code performance_category account_id\n           total_amount utilization_ratio risk_score;\n\n    define branch_code / group 'Branch';\n    define performance_category / group 'Category';\n    define account_id / group 'Account ID';\n    define total_amount / analysis sum format=dollar15.2 'Total Amount';\n    define utilization_ratio / analysis mean format=percent8.2 'Avg Utilization';\n    define risk_score / analysis mean format=8.4 'Risk Score';\n\n    break after branch_code / summarize suppress;\n    rbreak after / summarize;\nrun;\n\nfootnote1 'Source: Finance Data Warehouse | Confidential';\ntitle;\nfootnote;\n-gold_meta30]

gsm_02_customer_segmentation.sas                               */\n/* Purpose:    Customer RFM segmentation for marketing analytics              */\n/*             Computes Recency, Frequency, */\n/*============================================================================*/\n\n/* Global options */\noptions nocenter nodate mprint;\n\n/* Library references */\nlibname raw '/data/crm/raw' access=readonly;\nlibname staging '/data/crm/staging';\nlibname tgt '/data/crm/analytics';\n\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/* Step 1: Compute RFM base metrics from transaction history          */\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\ndata staging.rfm_base;\n    set raw.customer_transactions\n        (where=(order_date >= '01JAN2024'd));\n\n    /* Keep only completed orders */\n    if order_status = 'COMPLETE';\n\n    /* Calculate days since last purchase (recency) */\n    recency_days = today() - order_date;\n\n    /* Revenue after returns and discounts */\n    net_revenue = (quantity * unit_price) - discount_amount;\n    if net_revenue < 0 then net_revenue = 0;\n\n    format order_date date9. */\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nproc sql;\n    create table staging.rfm_metrics as\n    select\n        customer_id,\n        min(recency_days) as format=date9.,\n        count(distinct product_category) as category_breadth\n    from staging.rfm_base\n    group by customer_id\n    having frequency >= 1;\nquit;\n\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/* Step 3: Assign RFM quintile ranks using PROC RANK                  */\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nproc rank data=staging.rfm_metrics\n          out=staging.rfm_ranked groups=5;\n    var recency frequency monetary;\n    ranks r_rank f_rank m_rank;\nrun;\n\n/* Invert recency rank: lower recency = better (more recent) */\ndata staging.rfm_scored;\n    set staging.rfm_ranked;\n\n    /* Recency: 0=most recent quintile, 4=oldest - invert to 1-5 */\n    r_score = 5 - r_rank;\n\n    /* Frequency and Monetary: higher is better, shift to 1-5 */\n    f_score = f_rank + 1;\n    m_score = m_rank + 1;\n\n    /* Composite RFM score (weighted) */\n    rfm_composite = (r_score * 0.35) + (f_score * 0.35) + (m_score * 0.30);\n\n    /* Create RFM cell code (e.g., "5-4-3") */\n    length rfm_cell $10;\n    rfm_cell = catx('-', r_score, f_score, m_score);\nrun;\n\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/* Step 4: Assign customer segment labels based on RFM scores         */\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\ndata tgt.customer_segments;\n    set staging.rfm_scored;\n\n    length segment_name $30 segment_code $5;\n\n    /* Classify customers into business segments */\n    select;\n        when (r_score >= 4 and f_score >= 4 and m_score >= 4) do;\n            segment_name = 'Champions';\n            segment_code = 'CHAMP';\n        end;\n        when (r_score >= 4 and f_score >= 3) do;\n            segment_name = 'Loyal Customers';\n            segment_code = 'LOYAL';\n        end;\n        when (r_score >= 4 and f_score <= 2) do;\n            segment_name = 'New Customers';\n            segment_code = 'NEW';\n        end;\n        when (r_score >= 3 and m_score >= 3) do;\n            segment_name = 'Potential Loyalists';\n            segment_code = 'POTLYL';\n        end;\n        when (r_score <= 2 and f_score >= 3) do;\n            segment_name = 'At Risk';\n            segment_code = 'ATRSK';\n        end;\n        when (r_score <= 2 and f_score <= 2 and m_score <= 2) do;\n            segment_name = 'Hibernating';\n            segment_code = 'HIBER';\n        end;\n        otherwise do;\n            segment_name = 'Need Attention';\n            segment_code = 'NTATN';\n        end;\n    end;\n\n    format monetary avg_order_value dollar12.2\n           rfm_composite 5.2;\nrun;\n\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/* Step 5: Summarize segment distribution                             */\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\ntitle 'Customer Segment Distribution';\n\nproc freq data=tgt.customer_segments;\n    tables segment_name / nocum out=staging.segment_dist;\nrun;\n\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/* Step 6: Profile each segment with descriptive statistics           */\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nproc means data=tgt.customer_segments n mean median std min max;\n    class segment_name;\n    var recency frequency monetary avg_order_value\n        category_breadth rfm_composite;\n    output out=tgt.segment_profiles(drop=_type_ _freq_)\n        mean(recency) = avg_recency\n        mean(frequency) = avg_frequency\n        mean(monetary) = avg_monetary\n        mean(rfm_composite) = avg_rfm_score;\nrun;\n\ntitle;\n-gold_meta31]

*/\n/*============================================================================*/\n\n/* Global options */\noptions nocenter nodate compress=yes;\n\n/* Library references for warehouse data */\nlibname wh '/data/warehouse/inventory' access=readonly;\nlibname staging '/data/warehouse/staging';\nlibname tgt '/data/warehouse/reports';\n\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/* Step 1: Compute days-of-supply and reorder points                  */\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\ndata staging.inventory_metrics;\n    set wh.current_stock;\n\n    /* Calculate average daily demand over trailing 90 days */\n    if trailing_90d_demand > 0 then\n        avg_daily_demand = trailing_90d_demand / 90;\n    else\n        avg_daily_demand = 0;\n\n    /* Days of supply: how many days current stock can cover */\n    if avg_daily_demand > 0 then\n        days_of_supply = qty_on_hand / avg_daily_demand;\n    else\n        days_of_supply = 999;\n\n    /* Lead time in days from supplier */\n    lead_time_days Flag negative balance as data quality issue */\n    if running_qty < 0 then negative_balance_flag = 'Y';\n    else negative_balance_flag = 'N';\n\n    format txn_date '5-9 years'\n        10  -< 20  = '10-19 years'\n        20  - high = '20+ years';\n\n    /* Age bracket format */\n    value age_bracket\n        low -< 25  = 'Under 25'\n        25  -< 35  = '25-34'\n        35  -< 45  = '35-44'\n        45  -< 55  = '45-54'\n        55  - high = '55+';\nrun;\n\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/* Step 3: Join employees with department and manager details         */\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nproc sql;\n    create table tgt.employee_detail as\n    select\n e.job_grade,\n        d.department_name,\n        d.division_name,\n        d.cost_center,\n        m.first_name as mgr_first_name,\n        m.last_name as mgr_last_name\n    from staging.employee_derived e\n    left join hr.department_master d\n        on e.department_id = d.department_id\n    left join hr.employee_master m\n        on e.manager_id = m.employee_id\n    order by d.department_name, e.last_name;\nquit;\n\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/* Step 4: Cross-tabulation by department and job grade               */\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nproc tabulate data=tgt.employee_detail format=comma10.;\n    class department_name job_grade;\n    var annual_salary tenure_years;\n\n    table department_name='Department' all='Total',\n          job_grade='Grade' * (annual_salary='Salary' * (n mean)\n                               tenure_years='Tenure' * mean)\n          all='All Grades' * (annual_salary='Salary' * (n mean)\n                              tenure_years='Tenure' * mean);\nrun;\n\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/* Step 5: Compensation analysis by department                        */\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nproc means data=tgt.employee_detail n mean median std min max;\n    class department_name;\n    var annual_salary compa_ratio tenure_years years_to_retire;\n    output out=tgt.comp_summary(drop=_type_ _freq_)\n        mean(annual_salary) = avg_salary\n        median(annual_salary) = med_salary\n        mean(compa_ratio) = avg_compa_ratio\n        mean(tenure_years) = avg_tenure\n        n(employee_id) = headcount;\nrun;\n\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/* Step 6: Generate HTML report output                                */\n/*--------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nods html file='/reports/hr/workforce_analysis.html'\n         style=seaside;\n\nproc print data=tgt.comp_summary noobs label;\n    var department_name headcount avg_salary med_salary\n        avg_compa_ratio avg_tenure;\n    label department_name = avg_tenure      = 'Avg Tenure (Yrs)';\n    format avg_salary med_salary dollar12.2\n           avg_compa_ratio 6.3\n           avg_tenure 5.1;\nrun;\n\nods html close;\n\ntitle;\n-gold_meta34]
[SKIP] test_deterministic_fires_for_known_patterns[gsm_06_survey_analysis.sas-/*****************************************************************************\n * Program: gsm_06_survey_analysis.sas\n * Purpose: Analyze employee engagement survey data\n * Author:  Analytics Team\n * Date:    2026-02-19\n * Description:\n *   Processes raw survey responses, recodes Likert scales, computes\n *   composite engagement scores, and produces statistical analyses\n *   by demographic groups including chi-square tests and correlations.\n *****************************************************************************/\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Global settings and library references                             */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\noptions mprint mlogic symbolgen nocenter ls=120 ps=60;\n\nlibname raw     'C:/data/survey/raw' access=readonly;\nlibname staging 'C:/data/survey/staging';\nlibname tgt     'C:/data/survey/output';\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 1: Recode Likert scales and compute composite scores          */\n/*   - Reverse-code negatively worded items (Q3, Q7, Q12)            */\n/*   - Compute engagement, satisfaction, and leadership composites    */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\ndata staging.survey_scored;\n    set raw.survey_responses;\n\n    /* Reverse code negatively worded items (1->5, 2->4, etc.) */\n    q3_r  = 6 - q3;\n    q7_r  = 6 - q7;\n    q12_r = 6 - q12;\n\n    /* Engagement composite: average of Q1, Q2, Q3(reversed), Q4 */\n    engagement_score = mean(q1, q2, q3_r, q4);\n\n    /* Satisfaction composite: average of Q5, Q6, Q7(reversed), Q8 */\n    satisfaction_score = mean(q5, q6, q7_r, q8);\n\n    /* Leadership composite: average of Q9, Q10, Q11, Q12(reversed) */\n    leadership_score = mean(q9, q10, q11, q12_r);\n\n    /* Overall composite score */\n    overall_score = mean(engagement_score, satisfaction_score, leadership_score);\n\n    /* Categorize overall score into engagement tiers */\n    length engagement_tier $15;\n    if overall_score >= 4.0 then engagement_tier = 'Highly Engaged';\n    else if overall_score >= 3.0 then engagement_tier = 'Engaged';\n    else if overall_score >= 2.0 then engagement_tier = 'Neutral';\n    else engagement_tier = 'Disengaged';\n\n    /* Calculate response completeness */\n    array all_q{12} q1-q12;\n    n_answered = 0;\n    do i = 1 to 12;\n        if not missing(all_q{i}) then n_answered + 1;\n    end;\n    pct_complete = (n_answered / 12) * 100;\n\n    format engagement_score satisfaction_score leadership_score\n           overall_score 5.2 pct_complete 5.1;\n    drop i;\nrun;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 2: Response distributions with chi-square tests               */\n/*   - Cross-tabulate engagement tier by department and tenure group  */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\nproc freq data=staging.survey_scored;\n    tables engagement_tier * department / chisq nocum nopercent expected;\n    tables engagement_tier * tenure_group / chisq nocum;\n    title 'Engagement Survey: Response Distributions';\nrun;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 3: Group comparison - mean scores by department               */\n/*   - Produce department-level aggregates with confidence limits     */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\nproc means data=staging.survey_scored n mean std min max clm;\n    class department;\n    var engagement_score satisfaction_score leadership_score overall_score;\n    output out=staging.dept_means\n        mean(engagement_score)   = mean_engagement\n        mean(satisfaction_score) = mean_satisfaction\n        mean(leadership_score)   = mean_leadership\n        mean(overall_score)      = mean_overall\n        std(overall_score)       = std_overall\n        n(overall_score)         = n_respondents;\n    title 'Mean Scores by Department';\nrun;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 4: Scale reliability - inter-item correlations                */\n/*   - Examine correlations within each composite scale               */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\nproc corr data=staging.survey_scored nosimple;\n    var q1 q2 q3_r q4;\n    title 'Engagement Scale: Inter-Item Correlations';\nrun;\n\nproc corr data=staging.survey_scored nosimple;\n    var q5 q6 q7_r q8;\n    title 'Satisfaction Scale: Inter-Item Correlations';\nrun;\n\nproc corr data=staging.survey_scored nosimple;\n    var q9 q10 q11 q12_r;\n    title 'Leadership Scale: Inter-Item Correlations';\nrun;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 5: Demographic group summary via SQL                          */\n/*   - Summarize composite scores and response rates by demographics  */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\nproc sql;\n    create table staging.demographic_summary as\n    select\n        department,\n        tenure_group,\n        gender,\n        count(*) as n_respondents,\n        mean(engagement_score) as avg_engagement format=5.2,\n        mean(satisfaction_score) as avg_satisfaction format=5.2,\n        mean(leadership_score) as avg_leadership format=5.2,\n        mean(overall_score) as avg_overall format=5.2,\n        mean(pct_complete) as avg_completion format=5.1,\n        sum(case when engagement_tier = 'Highly Engaged' then 1 else 0 end)\n            as n_highly_engaged,\n        calculated n_highly_engaged / calculated n_respondents * 100\n            as pct_highly_engaged format=5.1\n    from staging.survey_scored\n    group by department, tenure_group, gender\n    having n_respondents >= 5\n    order by department, tenure_group, gender;\nquit;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 6: Flag outlier respondents                                   */\n/*   - Straight-line responding, low completion, extreme scores       */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\ndata tgt.survey_flagged;\n    set staging.survey_scored;\n\n    /* Flag low completion rate */\n    flag_incomplete = (pct_complete < 50);\n\n    /* Detect straight-line responding (zero variance in answers) */\n    array items{12} q1-q12;\n    _min = min(of items{*});\n    _max = max(of items{*});\n    flag_straightline = (_min = _max and not missing(_min));\n\n    /* Flag extreme outliers on overall score */\n    flag_outlier_low  = (overall_score < 1.5 and not missing(overall_score));\n    flag_outlier_high = (overall_score > 4.8 and not missing(overall_score));\n\n    /* Combined quality flag */\n    flag_quality_concern = (flag_incomplete or flag_straightline\n                           or flag_outlier_low or flag_outlier_high);\n\n    /* Build quality notes for flagged respondents */\n    length quality_notes $200;\n    quality_notes = '';\n    if flag_incomplete then\n        quality_notes = catx('; ', quality_notes, 'Low completion');\n    if flag_straightline then\n        quality_notes = catx('; ', quality_notes, 'Straight-line responding');\n    if flag_outlier_low then\n        quality_notes = catx('; ', quality_notes, 'Extremely low scores');\n    if flag_outlier_high then\n        quality_notes = catx('; ', quality_notes, 'Extremely high scores');\n\n    drop _min _max;\nrun;\n\n/* End of survey analysis program */\n-gold_meta35]

[SKIP] test_deterministic_fires_for_known_patterns[gsm_07_time_series.sas-/*****************************************************************************\n * Program: gsm_07_time_series.sas\n * Purpose: Time series analysis for monthly retail sales data\n * Author:  Analytics Team\n * Date:    2026-02-19\n * Description:\n *   Analyzes monthly sales trends using lag functions for moving averages,\n *   performs year-over-year comparisons, date arithmetic with INTCK/INTNX,\n *   seasonal aggregation, and interpolation of missing periods.\n *****************************************************************************/\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Global settings and library references                             */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\noptions mprint mlogic nocenter ls=132 ps=60;\n\nlibname raw     'C:/data/timeseries/raw' access=readonly;\nlibname staging 'C:/data/timeseries/staging';\nlibname tgt     'C:/data/timeseries/output';\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 1: Moving averages using LAG functions                        */\n/*   - Compute 3-month and 6-month moving averages for trend analysis */\n/*   - Carry forward cumulative revenue with RETAIN                   */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\ndata staging.sales_moving_avg;\n    set raw.monthly_sales;\n    by region_id;\n\n    retain cumulative_revenue 0;\n\n    /* Reset cumulative counter at start of each region */\n    if first.region_id then cumulative_revenue = 0;\n    cumulative_revenue + net_revenue;\n\n    /* Lag values for moving average calculation */\n    lag1_rev = lag1(net_revenue);\n    lag2_rev = lag2(net_revenue);\n    lag3_rev = lag3(net_revenue);\n    lag4_rev = lag4(net_revenue);\n    lag5_rev = lag5(net_revenue);\n\n    /* 3-month moving average (requires at least 3 data points) */\n    if _n_ >= 3 then\n        ma_3m = mean(net_revenue, lag1_rev, lag2_rev);\n\n    /* 6-month moving average (requires at least 6 data points) */\n    if _n_ >= 6 then\n        ma_6m = mean(net_revenue, lag1_rev, lag2_rev,\n                     lag3_rev, lag4_rev, lag5_rev);\n\n    /* Month-over-month percent change */\n    if not missing(lag1_rev) and lag1_rev > 0 then\n        mom_change_pct = ((net_revenue - lag1_rev) / lag1_rev) * 100;\n\n    /* Trend direction indicator */\n    length trend_direction $10;\n    if mom_change_pct > 5 then trend_direction = 'UP';\n    else if mom_change_pct < -5 then trend_direction = 'DOWN';\n    else if not missing(mom_change_pct) then trend_direction = 'STABLE';\n    else trend_direction = 'N/A';\n\n    format cumulative_revenue comma15.2\n           ma_3m ma_6m comma12.2\n           mom_change_pct 6.1;\nrun;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 2: Interpolate missing months                                 */\n/*   - Fill gaps in time series with linear interpolation             */\n/*   - Detect date continuity breaks                                  */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\ndata staging.sales_interpolated;\n    set staging.sales_moving_avg;\n    by region_id sale_month;\n\n    /* Track previous known value for interpolation */\n    prev_rev = lag1(net_revenue);\n\n    /* Simple carry-forward interpolation for missing values */\n    if missing(net_revenue) and not missing(prev_rev) then do;\n        net_revenue = prev_rev;\n        interpolated_flag = 1;\n    end;\n    else interpolated_flag = 0;\n\n    /* Check for month continuity gaps */\n    expected_month = intnx('month', lag1(sale_month), 1, 'beginning');\n    if not missing(expected_month) and sale_month ne expected_month then\n        gap_detected = 1;\n    else\n        gap_detected = 0;\n\n    format expected_month monyy7.;\nrun;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 3: Year-over-year comparison via SQL                          */\n/*   - Join current period to same period prior year                  */\n/*   - Compute YoY change percentages for revenue and moving averages */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\nproc sql;\n    create table staging.yoy_comparison as\n    select\n        a.region_id,\n        a.sale_month,\n        year(a.sale_month) as current_year,\n        a.net_revenue as current_revenue,\n        b.net_revenue as prior_year_revenue,\n        case\n            when b.net_revenue > 0 then\n                ((a.net_revenue - b.net_revenue) / b.net_revenue) * 100\n            else .\n        end as yoy_change_pct format=6.1,\n        a.ma_3m as current_ma3,\n        b.ma_3m as prior_year_ma3,\n        a.cumulative_revenue\n    from staging.sales_moving_avg a\n    left join staging.sales_moving_avg b\n        on a.region_id = b.region_id\n        and a.sale_month = intnx('year', b.sale_month, 1, 'same')\n    order by a.region_id, a.sale_month;\nquit;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 4: Date arithmetic - compute fiscal periods and intervals     */\n/*   - Fiscal year begins in July                                     */\n/*   - Calculate fiscal quarter, days in month, daily average revenue */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\ndata staging.sales_fiscal;\n    set staging.yoy_comparison;\n\n    /* Fiscal year starts in July */\n    fiscal_year_start = intnx('year.7', sale_month, 0, 'beginning');\n    fiscal_year = year(fiscal_year_start);\n\n    /* Fiscal quarter within fiscal year */\n    months_into_fy = intck('month', fiscal_year_start, sale_month);\n    fiscal_quarter = floor(months_into_fy / 3) + 1;\n\n    /* Calendar days in the sales month */\n    month_start = intnx('month', sale_month, 0, 'beginning');\n    month_end   = intnx('month', sale_month, 0, 'end');\n    days_in_month = intck('day', month_start, month_end) + 1;\n\n    /* Daily average revenue */\n    if days_in_month > 0 then\n        daily_avg_revenue = current_revenue / days_in_month;\n\n    /* Months elapsed since baseline date */\n    months_since_start = intck('month', '01JAN2020'd, sale_month);\n\n    /* Revenue per day of month for normalization */\n    if days_in_month > 0 then\n        normalized_revenue = (current_revenue / days_in_month) * 30;\n\n    format fiscal_year_start month_start month_end date9.\n           daily_avg_revenue normalized_revenue comma10.2;\nrun;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 5: Seasonal aggregation with CLASS statement                  */\n/*   - Summarize revenue metrics by fiscal year and quarter           */\n/*   - Produce seasonal performance summary for reporting             */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\nproc means data=staging.sales_fiscal n mean sum std median;\n    class fiscal_year fiscal_quarter;\n    var current_revenue daily_avg_revenue yoy_change_pct;\n    output out=tgt.seasonal_summary\n        mean(current_revenue)  = avg_revenue\n        sum(current_revenue)   = total_revenue\n        mean(yoy_change_pct)   = avg_yoy_change\n        std(current_revenue)   = std_revenue\n        n(current_revenue)     = n_months;\n    title 'Seasonal Revenue Summary by Fiscal Year and Quarter';\nrun;\n\n/* End of time series analysis program */\n-gold_meta36]

[SKIP] test_deterministic_fires_for_known_patterns[gsm_09_cohort_analysis.sas-/*****************************************************************************\n * Program: gsm_09_cohort_analysis.sas\n * Purpose: Clinical cohort analysis for treatment outcome study\n * Author:  Biostatistics Team\n * Date:    2026-02-19\n * Description:\n *   Defines patient cohorts based on inclusion/exclusion criteria,\n *   constructs age-sex matched pairs, computes follow-up duration,\n *   and analyzes treatment outcomes with baseline characteristics.\n *****************************************************************************/\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Global settings and library references                             */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\noptions mprint mlogic nocenter ls=120 ps=60;\n\nlibname raw     'C:/data/clinical/raw' access=readonly;\nlibname staging 'C:/data/clinical/staging';\nlibname tgt     'C:/data/clinical/output';\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 1: Define cohort inclusion/exclusion criteria                  */\n/*   - Include adults 18+ with diagnosis code in target range         */\n/*   - Exclude patients with prior treatment or missing consent       */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\ndata staging.eligible_patients;\n    set raw.patient_registry;\n\n    /* Inclusion criteria */\n    age_at_index = intck('year', date_of_birth, index_date);\n    include_age = (age_at_index >= 18 and age_at_index <= 85);\n    include_diagnosis = (primary_dx_code in ('E11.0','E11.1','E11.2',\n                                             'E11.3','E11.4','E11.5'));\n    include_enrollment = (enrollment_start <= index_date\n                         and enrollment_end >= index_date + 365);\n\n    /* Exclusion criteria */\n    exclude_prior_tx = (prior_treatment_flag = 1);\n    exclude_no_consent = (informed_consent ne 'Y');\n    exclude_comorbidity = (charlson_index > 6);\n\n    /* Apply all criteria */\n    if include_age and include_diagnosis and include_enrollment\n       and not exclude_prior_tx and not exclude_no_consent\n       and not exclude_comorbidity;\n\n    /* Assign treatment group based on treatment_arm variable */\n    length cohort_group $10;\n    if treatment_arm = 1 then cohort_group = 'TREATMENT';\n    else if treatment_arm = 0 then cohort_group = 'CONTROL';\n    else cohort_group = 'UNKNOWN';\n\n    /* Age group for matching */\n    length age_group $10;\n    if age_at_index < 30 then age_group = '18-29';\n    else if age_at_index < 45 then age_group = '30-44';\n    else if age_at_index < 60 then age_group = '45-59';\n    else if age_at_index < 75 then age_group = '60-74';\n    else age_group = '75-85';\n\n    format index_date enrollment_start enrollment_end date9.;\nrun;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 2: Build matched cohort pairs using age-sex matching          */\n/*   - Match each treatment patient with a control of same age/sex   */\n/*   - Use 1:1 greedy matching via SQL                               */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\nproc sql;\n    /* Create matched pairs: treatment to control on age_group + gender */\n    create table staging.matched_cohort as\n    select\n        t.patient_id as treatment_id,\n        c.patient_id as control_id,\n        t.age_group,\n        t.gender,\n        t.age_at_index as treatment_age,\n        c.age_at_index as control_age,\n        abs(t.age_at_index - c.age_at_index) as age_diff\n    from staging.eligible_patients t\n    inner join staging.eligible_patients c\n        on t.age_group = c.age_group\n        and t.gender = c.gender\n        and t.cohort_group = 'TREATMENT'\n        and c.cohort_group = 'CONTROL'\n    group by t.patient_id\n    having age_diff = min(age_diff)\n    order by t.patient_id;\n\n    /* Count matched vs unmatched */\n    title 'Cohort Matching Summary';\n    select cohort_group,\n           count(*) as n_patients\n    from staging.eligible_patients\n    group by cohort_group;\nquit;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 3: Compute follow-up duration with RETAIN                     */\n/*   - Track cumulative days at risk per patient                      */\n/*   - Flag censoring events                                          */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\ndata staging.followup;\n    set raw.patient_encounters;\n    by patient_id encounter_date;\n\n    retain days_at_risk 0 first_encounter_date;\n\n    /* Reset at start of each patient */\n    if first.patient_id then do;\n        days_at_risk = 0;\n        first_encounter_date = encounter_date;\n    end;\n\n    /* Accumulate days since previous encounter */\n    if not first.patient_id then\n        days_at_risk + intck('day', lag1(encounter_date), encounter_date);\n\n    /* Total follow-up from first to current encounter */\n    total_followup_days = intck('day', first_encounter_date, encounter_date);\n\n    /* Censor at 365 days or study end date */\n    study_end = '31DEC2025'd;\n    if encounter_date > study_end then do;\n        censored = 1;\n        total_followup_days = intck('day', first_encounter_date, study_end);\n    end;\n    else censored = 0;\n\n    /* Flag outcome event (primary endpoint) */\n    outcome_event = (outcome_code in ('HOSP','ER','DEATH'));\n\n    format first_encounter_date encounter_date study_end date9.;\nrun;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 4: Outcome analysis using PROC FREQ                           */\n/*   - Chi-square test for outcome events by treatment group          */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\nproc freq data=staging.followup;\n    where last.patient_id;\n    tables cohort_group * outcome_event / chisq relrisk nocum;\n    title 'Outcome Event Rates by Treatment Group';\nrun;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 5: Baseline characteristics table                             */\n/*   - Summarize demographics and clinical measures by cohort group   */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\nproc means data=staging.eligible_patients n mean std median min max;\n    class cohort_group;\n    var age_at_index charlson_index baseline_hba1c baseline_bmi\n        systolic_bp diastolic_bp;\n    output out=tgt.baseline_characteristics\n        mean(age_at_index)    = mean_age\n        mean(charlson_index)  = mean_charlson\n        mean(baseline_hba1c)  = mean_hba1c\n        mean(baseline_bmi)    = mean_bmi\n        std(age_at_index)     = sd_age\n        std(baseline_hba1c)   = sd_hba1c\n        n(age_at_index)       = n_patients;\n    title 'Baseline Characteristics by Cohort';\nrun;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 6: Compute hazard indicators                                  */\n/*   - Create time-to-event dataset with hazard rate estimates        */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\ndata tgt.hazard_indicators;\n    set staging.followup;\n    by patient_id;\n\n    /* Keep only the last record per patient */\n    if last.patient_id;\n\n    /* Compute hazard rate estimate (events / person-time) */\n    person_years = total_followup_days / 365.25;\n    if person_years > 0 then\n        hazard_rate = outcome_event / person_years;\n    else\n        hazard_rate = .;\n\n    /* Risk category based on follow-up and outcome */\n    length risk_category $12;\n    if outcome_event = 1 and total_followup_days <= 90 then\n        risk_category = 'HIGH';\n    else if outcome_event = 1 then\n        risk_category = 'MODERATE';\n    else if censored = 1 then\n        risk_category = 'CENSORED';\n    else\n        risk_category = 'LOW';\n\n    format hazard_rate 8.4 person_years 6.2;\nrun;\n\n/* End of cohort analysis program */\n-gold_meta38]
