[SKIP] test_no_lineage_violations_in_deterministic[gsh_01_enterprise_etl.sas-/******************************************************************************\n * Program Name : gsh_01_enterprise_etl.sas\n * Author       : Data Engineering Team \u2014 Financial Systems Division\n * Created      : 2026-01-15\n * Modified     : 2026-02-19\n * Purpose      : Enterprise ETL pipeline for the Financial Data Warehouse.\n *                Extracts data from raw source libraries, applies cleansing\n *                and business transformations, loads dimension and fact tables\n *                into the data mart layer using SCD Type 2 methodology.\n * Dependencies : common_macros.sas\n * Frequency    : Daily (scheduled via Control-M, Job ID FIN-DWH-001)\n * Change Log   :\n *   2026-01-15  v1.0  Initial development                    (J. Martin)\n *   2026-01-28  v1.1  Added SCD Type 2 dimension loading     (S. Chen)\n *   2026-02-10  v1.2  Enhanced error handling and audit trail (A. Patel)\n *   2026-02-19  v1.3  Added archival step and date dimension  (J. Martin)\n ******************************************************************************/\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 1: Environment Setup \u2014 Libraries, Options, and Includes           */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\noptions mprint mlogic symbolgen nocenter ls=200 ps=65\n        validvarname=v7 nofmterr compress=binary;\n\n/* --- Library references for each data layer --- */\nlibname raw      '/data/finance/raw'       access=readonly;\nlibname staging  '/data/finance/staging';\nlibname cleansed '/data/finance/cleansed';\nlibname mart     '/data/finance/mart';\nlibname archive  '/data/finance/archive';\nlibname tgt      '/data/finance/target';\n\n/* Global macro variables for this run */\n%let etl_run_id    = %sysfunc(putn(%sysfunc(datetime()), datetime20.));\n%let process_date  = %sysfunc(today(), yymmdd10.);\n%let valid_sources = 1;  /* Flag: set to 0 if any source validation fails */\n%let etl_rc        = 0;  /* Return code accumulator */\n%let n_sources     = 6;  /* Number of source tables to validate */\n\n/* Include shared macro library */\n%include '/etl/common/common_macros.sas';\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 2: Utility Macro Definitions                                      */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: check_source_data                                               */\n/*   Validates that a source table exists and contains a minimum number   */\n/*   of observations. Sets &valid_sources to 0 if validation fails.       */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro check_source_data(lib=, table=, min_rows=100);\n\n    %local dsid nobs rc table_exists;\n\n    /* Check if the table exists in the library */\n    %let table_exists = %sysfunc(exist(&lib..&table));\n\n    %if &table_exists = 0 %then %do;\n        %put ERROR: [check_source_data] Table &lib..&table does not exist.;\n        %let valid_sources = 0;\n        %return;\n    %end;\n\n    /* Open the dataset and retrieve observation count */\n    %let dsid = %sysfunc(open(&lib..&table));\n    %if &dsid > 0 %then %do;\n        %let nobs = %sysfunc(attrn(&dsid, NOBS));\n        %let rc   = %sysfunc(close(&dsid));\n\n        %if &nobs < &min_rows %then %do;\n            %put WARNING: [check_source_data] &lib..&table has only &nobs rows (minimum: &min_rows).;\n            %let valid_sources = 0;\n        %end;\n        %else %do;\n            %put NOTE: [check_source_data] &lib..&table validated OK \u2014 &nobs observations found.;\n        %end;\n    %end;\n    %else %do;\n        %put ERROR: [check_source_data] Unable to open &lib..&table \u2014 check permissions.;\n        %let valid_sources = 0;\n    %end;\n\n%mend check_source_data;\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: log_step                                                        */\n/*   Inserts an audit record into mart.etl_audit_log for traceability.    */\n/*   Captures run ID, step name, status, row counts, and timestamp.       */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro log_step(step_name=, status=OK, detail=, row_count=0);\n\n    %local log_ts;\n    %let log_ts = %sysfunc(datetime(), datetime20.);\n\n    proc sql noprint;\n        insert into mart.etl_audit_log\n            (run_id, step_name, status, detail, row_count, log_timestamp)\n        values\n            ("&etl_run_id", "&step_name", "&status", "&detail",\n             &row_count, "&log_ts");\n    quit;\n\n    %if &status = ERROR %then %do;\n        %let etl_rc = 1;\n        %put ERROR: [log_step] Step &step_name failed \u2014 &detail;\n    %end;\n\n%mend log_step;\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: load_dimension                                                  */\n/*   Implements SCD Type 2 dimension loading with effective dating.        */\n/*   Compares staging rows against existing dimension, expires changed     */\n/*   records and inserts new versions with current effective dates.         */\n/*   Parameters:                                                           */\n/*     dim_table   - Target dimension table (e.g., mart.customer_dim)      */\n/*     src_table   - Staging source table                                  */\n/*     natural_key - Business key column(s) for matching                   */\n/*     scd_type    - SCD type (1 or 2); default 2                          */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro load_dimension(dim_table=, src_table=, natural_key=, scd_type=2);\n\n    %local src_count new_count upd_count;\n\n    %put NOTE: [load_dimension] Loading &dim_table from &src_table (SCD Type &scd_type).;\n\n    /* Step 1: Identify new and changed records via left join */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        create table work._dim_changes as\n        select s.*,\n               d.dim_surrogate_key,\n               d.effective_from as existing_eff_from,\n               case\n                   when d.dim_surrogate_key is null then 'NEW'\n                   else 'UPDATE'\n               end as _change_type\n        from &src_table as s\n        left join &dim_table (where=(current_flag = 'Y')) as d\n            on s.&natural_key = d.&natural_key;\n\n        select count(*) into :src_count trimmed from &src_table;\n        select count(*) into :new_count trimmed\n            from work._dim_changes where _change_type = 'NEW';\n        select count(*) into :upd_count trimmed\n            from work._dim_changes where _change_type = 'UPDATE';\n    quit;\n\n    %put NOTE: [load_dimension] Source=&src_count New=&new_count Updated=&upd_count;\n\n    %if &scd_type = 2 %then %do;\n\n        /* Step 2a: Expire changed records in the dimension */\n        proc sql;\n            update &dim_table\n            set current_flag   = 'N',\n                effective_to   = "&process_date"d\n            where current_flag = 'Y'\n              and &natural_key in (\n                  select &natural_key from work._dim_changes\n                  where _change_type = 'UPDATE'\n              );\n        quit;\n\n        /* Step 2b: Insert new versions of changed and genuinely new records */\n        proc sql;\n            insert into &dim_table\n            select monotonic()   as dim_surrogate_key,\n                   s.*,\n                   "&process_date"d  as effective_from,\n                   '31DEC9999'd      as effective_to,\n                   'Y'               as current_flag\n            from work._dim_changes as s;\n        quit;\n\n    %end;\n    %else %do;\n\n        /* SCD Type 1: Simple overwrite of existing rows */\n        proc sql;\n            delete from &dim_table\n            where &natural_key in (\n                select &natural_key from work._dim_changes\n            );\n\n            insert into &dim_table\n            select * from work._dim_changes;\n        quit;\n\n    %end;\n\n    %log_step(step_name=load_dim_&dim_table, status=OK,\n              detail=New=&new_count Updated=&upd_count, row_count=&src_count);\n\n    /* Clean up temp table */\n    proc datasets lib=work nolist; delete _dim_changes; quit;\n\n%mend load_dimension;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 3: Source Validation Loop                                         */\n/*   Iterates over all expected source tables and validates each one.        */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n%macro validate_all_sources;\n    %local i;\n\n    /* Define source tables as indexed macro variable arrays */\n    %let src_lib1 = raw;   %let src_tbl1 = customers;\n    %let src_lib2 = raw;   %let src_tbl2 = products;\n    %let src_lib3 = raw;   %let src_tbl3 = transactions;\n    %let src_lib4 = raw;   %let src_tbl4 = accounts;\n    %let src_lib5 = raw;   %let src_tbl5 = fx_rates;\n    %let src_lib6 = raw;   %let src_tbl6 = calendar;\n\n    %do i = 1 %to &n_sources;\n        %put NOTE: Validating source &i of &n_sources: &&src_lib&i...&&src_tbl&i;\n        %check_source_data(lib=&&src_lib&i, table=&&src_tbl&i, min_rows=10);\n    %end;\n\n    %put NOTE: [validate_all_sources] Validation complete. valid_sources=&valid_sources;\n%mend validate_all_sources;\n\n/* Execute the validation loop */\n%validate_all_sources;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 4: Early Exit Check                                               */\n/*   Abort the pipeline if any source validation failed.                     */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n%if &valid_sources = 0 %then %do;\n    %log_step(step_name=VALIDATION, status=ERROR,\n              detail=One or more source tables failed validation);\n    %put ERROR: ETL pipeline aborted due to source validation failure.;\n    endsas;\n%end;\n\n%log_step(step_name=VALIDATION, status=OK, detail=All 6 sources validated);\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 5: Customer Dimension \u2014 Extract and Transform                     */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\ndata staging.customer_stg (drop=_:);\n    set raw.customers;\n\n    /* Standardize name fields */\n    first_name = propcase(strip(first_name));\n    last_name  = propcase(strip(last_name));\n    full_name  = catx(' ', first_name, last_name);\n\n    /* Derive customer age band from date of birth */\n    if not missing(date_of_birth) then do;\n        _age = intck('YEAR', date_of_birth, "&process_date"d);\n        select;\n            when (_age < 25)           age_band = '18-24';\n            when (25 <= _age < 35)     age_band = '25-34';\n            when (35 <= _age < 45)     age_band = '35-44';\n            when (45 <= _age < 55)     age_band = '45-54';\n            when (55 <= _age < 65)     age_band = '55-64';\n            otherwise                  age_band = '65+';\n        end;\n    end;\n    else do;\n        age_band = 'Unknown';\n    end;\n\n    /* Clean and validate email address */\n    email = lowcase(strip(email));\n    if index(email, '@') = 0 then email = '';\n\n    /* Map state to sales region */\n    length region $20;\n    select (upcase(state));\n        when ('NY','NJ','CT','MA','PA') region = 'Northeast';\n        when ('IL','OH','MI','WI','MN') region = 'Midwest';\n        when ('TX','FL','GA','NC','VA') region = 'South';\n        when ('CA','WA','OR','AZ','CO') region = 'West';\n        otherwise                       region = 'Other';\n    end;\n\n    /* Record metadata */\n    load_date     = "&process_date"d;\n    source_system = 'CORE_BANKING';\n    format load_date yymmdd10.;\nrun;\n\n%log_step(step_name=CUSTOMER_EXTRACT, status=OK,\n          detail=Customer staging table created);\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 6: Customer Deduplication via PROC SQL                            */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\nproc sql;\n    create table staging.customer_dedup as\n    select a.*\n    from staging.customer_stg as a\n    inner join (\n        select customer_id,\n               min(monotonic()) as first_row\n        from staging.customer_stg\n        group by customer_id\n    ) as b\n        on a.customer_id = b.customer_id\n    order by a.customer_id;\n\n    /* Capture dedup metrics */\n    select count(distinct customer_id) into :dedup_count trimmed\n        from staging.customer_dedup;\nquit;\n\n%put NOTE: [Dedup] Unique customers retained: &dedup_count;\n%log_step(step_name=CUSTOMER_DEDUP, status=OK,\n          detail=&dedup_count unique customers, row_count=&dedup_count);\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 7: Dimension Loading \u2014 Invoke SCD Type 2 Macros                   */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n/* Load customer dimension with SCD Type 2 history tracking */\n%load_dimension(dim_table=mart.customer_dim,\n                src_table=staging.customer_dedup,\n                natural_key=customer_id, scd_type=2);\n\n/* Load product dimension with SCD Type 2 */\n%load_dimension(dim_table=mart.product_dim,\n                src_table=staging.product_stg,\n                natural_key=product_id, scd_type=2);\n\n/* Load date/calendar dimension \u2014 SCD Type 1 (overwrite) since dates are static */\n%load_dimension(dim_table=mart.date_dim,\n                src_table=staging.calendar_stg,\n                natural_key=calendar_date, scd_type=1);\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 8: Fact Table Extraction \u2014 Transactions with Multi-Source Merge    */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\ndata staging.fact_transactions\n     staging.fact_txn_rejects;\n\n    merge raw.transactions  (in=a)\n          raw.accounts      (in=b keep=account_id account_type currency_code)\n          raw.fx_rates      (in=c keep=currency_code fx_rate_to_usd);\n    by account_id;\n\n    if a;  /* Keep only rows with a transaction record */\n\n    /* Convert to base currency (USD) */\n    if currency_code ne 'USD' and not missing(fx_rate_to_usd) then\n        amount_usd = amount * fx_rate_to_usd;\n    else\n        amount_usd = amount;\n\n    /* Classify transaction type into broad category */\n    length txn_category $15;\n    select (upcase(transaction_type));\n        when ('DEP','XFER_IN','INT_CREDIT')  txn_category = 'INFLOW';\n        when ('WDR','XFER_OUT','FEE','TAX')  txn_category = 'OUTFLOW';\n        otherwise                             txn_category = 'OTHER';\n    end;\n\n    /* Derive fiscal period attributes */\n    fiscal_year    = year(transaction_date);\n    fiscal_quarter = ceil(month(transaction_date) / 3);\n    fiscal_month   = month(transaction_date);\n\n    /* Data quality check \u2014 reject incomplete records */\n    _valid = 1;\n    if missing(amount) then _valid = 0;\n    if missing(transaction_date) then _valid = 0;\n    if missing(account_id) then _valid = 0;\n\n    if _valid = 0 then output staging.fact_txn_rejects;\n    else output staging.fact_transactions;\n\n    drop _valid;\nrun;\n\n%log_step(step_name=FACT_EXTRACT, status=OK,\n          detail=Transaction fact staging and rejects created);\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 9: Fact Aggregation \u2014 Daily Account Summaries via PROC SQL        */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\nproc sql;\n    create table staging.daily_account_summary as\n    select t.account_id,\n           t.transaction_date,\n           t.account_type,\n           sum(case when t.txn_category = 'INFLOW'\n                    then t.amount_usd else 0 end)   as total_inflow,\n           sum(case when t.txn_category = 'OUTFLOW'\n                    then t.amount_usd else 0 end)   as total_outflow,\n           sum(t.amount_usd)                        as net_amount,\n           count(*)                                 as txn_count,\n           min(t.amount_usd)                        as min_txn_amount,\n           max(t.amount_usd)                        as max_txn_amount\n    from staging.fact_transactions as t\n    group by t.account_id, t.transaction_date, t.account_type\n    order by t.account_id, t.transaction_date;\nquit;\n\n%log_step(step_name=DAILY_SUMMARY, status=OK,\n          detail=Daily account summaries aggregated);\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 10: Running Balances with RETAIN                                  */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\ndata mart.account_balances;\n    set staging.daily_account_summary;\n    by account_id transaction_date;\n\n    retain running_balance   0\n           cumulative_inflow  0\n           cumulative_outflow 0\n           days_active        0;\n\n    /* Reset accumulators for each new account */\n    if first.account_id then do;\n        running_balance    = 0;\n        cumulative_inflow  = 0;\n        cumulative_outflow = 0;\n        days_active        = 0;\n    end;\n\n    /* Accumulate running totals */\n    running_balance    = running_balance + net_amount;\n    cumulative_inflow  = cumulative_inflow + total_inflow;\n    cumulative_outflow = cumulative_outflow + abs(total_outflow);\n    days_active + 1;\n\n    /* Derived metrics */\n    if cumulative_inflow > 0 then\n        outflow_ratio = cumulative_outflow / cumulative_inflow;\n    else\n        outflow_ratio = .;\n\n    avg_daily_net = running_balance / days_active;\n\n    /* Flag accounts with concerning patterns */\n    length balance_flag $10;\n    if running_balance < 0 then balance_flag = 'NEGATIVE';\n    else if running_balance < 1000 then balance_flag = 'LOW';\n    else balance_flag = 'NORMAL';\n\n    format running_balance cumulative_inflow cumulative_outflow\n           avg_daily_net dollar15.2;\nrun;\n\n%log_step(step_name=RUNNING_BALANCE, status=OK,\n          detail=Account running balances computed);\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 11: Executive Summary via PROC SQL                                */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\nproc sql;\n    create table mart.executive_summary as\n    select b.account_type,\n           b.balance_flag,\n           count(distinct b.account_id)  as account_count,\n           sum(b.running_balance)        as total_balance,\n           avg(b.running_balance)        as avg_balance,\n           sum(b.cumulative_inflow)      as total_inflows,\n           sum(b.cumulative_outflow)     as total_outflows,\n           avg(b.outflow_ratio)          as avg_outflow_ratio\n    from mart.account_balances as b\n    where b.transaction_date = (\n        select max(transaction_date) from mart.account_balances\n    )\n    group by b.account_type, b.balance_flag\n    order by b.account_type, b.balance_flag;\nquit;\n\n%log_step(step_name=EXEC_SUMMARY, status=OK,\n          detail=Executive summary table generated);\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 12: Error Detection and Conditional Handling                      */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n%if &syserr ne 0 or &etl_rc ne 0 %then %do;\n    %put ERROR: ETL pipeline encountered errors. syserr=&syserr etl_rc=&etl_rc;\n\n    %log_step(step_name=ERROR_HANDLER, status=ERROR,\n              detail=Pipeline errors detected \u2014 review log);\n\n    /* Write error flag file for downstream job monitoring */\n    data _null_;\n        file '/data/finance/flags/etl_error.flag';\n        put "ETL_RUN_ID=&etl_run_id";\n        put "ERROR_TIME=%sysfunc(datetime(), datetime20.)";\n        put "SYSERR=&syserr";\n        put "ETL_RC=&etl_rc";\n    run;\n\n    /* Send error notification via email */\n    filename mymail email\n        to=('dw-ops@company.com')\n        subject="ETL FAILURE: gsh_01_enterprise_etl \u2014 &process_date"\n        type='text/plain';\n    data _null_;\n        file mymail;\n        put "The Financial DWH ETL pipeline failed on &process_date.";\n        put "Run ID : &etl_run_id";\n        put "SYSERR : &syserr";\n        put "ETL_RC : &etl_rc";\n        put "Please review the SAS log for details.";\n    run;\n    filename mymail clear;\n%end;\n%else %do;\n    %put NOTE: ETL pipeline completed successfully. No errors detected.;\n%end;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 13: Archival \u2014 Move Aged Data to Archive Library                  */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\ndata archive.transactions_%sysfunc(compress(&process_date, -)) (compress=yes);\n    set raw.transactions;\n    where transaction_date <= "&process_date"d - 365;\n\n    /* Add archive metadata */\n    archive_date   = "&process_date"d;\n    archive_run_id = "&etl_run_id";\n    format archive_date yymmdd10.;\nrun;\n\n/* Remove archived records from the active raw layer */\nproc sql;\n    delete from raw.transactions\n    where transaction_date <= "&process_date"d - 365;\nquit;\n\n%log_step(step_name=ARCHIVAL, status=OK,\n          detail=Historical transactions archived and purged from raw);\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 14: Final Cleanup and Audit Trail                                 */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n/* Clear intermediate staging datasets */\nproc datasets lib=staging nolist;\n    delete customer_stg\n           customer_dedup\n           fact_transactions\n           fact_txn_rejects\n           daily_account_summary;\nquit;\n\n/* Record final pipeline completion in audit log */\n%log_step(step_name=PIPELINE_COMPLETE, status=OK,\n          detail=All ETL steps completed successfully);\n\n/* Write completion flag for downstream batch scheduler */\ndata _null_;\n    file '/data/finance/flags/etl_complete.flag';\n    put "ETL_RUN_ID=&etl_run_id";\n    put "COMPLETION_TIME=%sysfunc(datetime(), datetime20.)";\n    put "PROCESS_DATE=&process_date";\n    put "STATUS=SUCCESS";\nrun;\n\n/* Reset options to defaults */\noptions nomprint nomlogic nosymbolgen;\n\n%put NOTE: ===== gsh_01_enterprise_etl.sas completed at %sysfunc(datetime(), datetime20.) =====;\n-gold_meta15]
    ('C:\\Users\\labou\\Desktop\\Stage\\backend\\tests\\test_regression.py', 133, 'Skipped: gsh_01_enterprise_etl.sas: no deterministic rule matched')

[SKIP] test_no_lineage_violations_in_deterministic[gsh_02_macro_framework.sas-/******************************************************************************\n * Program Name : gsh_02_macro_framework.sas\n * Author       : Analytics Platform Team\n * Created      : 2025-11-20\n * Modified     : 2026-02-19\n * Purpose      : Reusable macro framework for standardized enterprise\n *                reporting. Provides parameterized macros for date range\n *                calculation, dynamic format creation, and multi-format\n *                report generation. Supports batch execution across\n *                multiple regions and periods.\n * Usage        : Invoked by quarterly, monthly, and ad-hoc report jobs.\n * Dependencies : None (self-contained macro library)\n * Change Log   :\n *   2025-11-20  v1.0  Initial framework                     (L. Torres)\n *   2025-12-15  v1.1  Added batch_reports macro              (L. Torres)\n *   2026-01-10  v1.2  Enhanced ODS output format support     (R. Kim)\n *   2026-02-19  v1.3  Parameter validation and error guards  (L. Torres)\n ******************************************************************************/\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 1: Environment Setup                                              */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\noptions mprint mlogic symbolgen nocenter ls=200 ps=65\n        validvarname=v7 nofmterr;\n\n/* --- Library references --- */\nlibname raw      '/data/analytics/raw'      access=readonly;\nlibname staging  '/data/analytics/staging';\nlibname mart     '/data/analytics/mart';\nlibname rptout   '/data/analytics/reports';\nlibname archive  '/data/analytics/archive';\n\n/* Global configuration for this reporting run */\n%let report_base_path = /data/analytics/output;\n%let default_period   = QTR;\n%let default_as_of    = %sysfunc(today(), yymmdd10.);\n%let framework_rc     = 0;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 2: Macro Definitions                                              */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: validate_params                                                 */\n/*   Validates that all required parameters were supplied to a calling     */\n/*   macro. Emits ERROR messages and sets &framework_rc for any missing    */\n/*   required values. Uses COUNTW and SCAN for list iteration.             */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro validate_params(macro_name=, required_params=);\n\n    %local i n param_name param_val;\n\n    %let n = %sysfunc(countw(&required_params, %str( )));\n\n    %do i = 1 %to &n;\n        %let param_name = %scan(&required_params, &i, %str( ));\n        %let param_val  = &&&param_name;\n\n        %if %length(&param_val) = 0 %then %do;\n            %put ERROR: [&macro_name] Required parameter &param_name is missing or blank.;\n            %let framework_rc = 1;\n        %end;\n    %end;\n\n    %if &framework_rc ne 0 %then %do;\n        %put ERROR: [&macro_name] Aborting due to missing required parameters.;\n    %end;\n\n%mend validate_params;\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: get_date_range                                                  */\n/*   Calculates a date range (start and end) based on a period type       */\n/*   relative to an as-of date. Returns values in global macro vars       */\n/*   &range_start and &range_end. Uses %SYSFUNC(INTNX) and PUTN.         */\n/*   Supported periods: MTH, QTR, YTD, YR, CUSTOM                        */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro get_date_range(period=QTR, as_of=&default_as_of);\n\n    %global range_start range_end range_label;\n    %local as_of_sas;\n\n    /* Convert as_of to SAS date numeric */\n    %let as_of_sas = %sysfunc(inputn(&as_of, yymmdd10.));\n\n    %if &period = MTH %then %do;\n        %let range_start = %sysfunc(intnx(MONTH, &as_of_sas, 0, B), yymmdd10.);\n        %let range_end   = %sysfunc(intnx(MONTH, &as_of_sas, 0, E), yymmdd10.);\n        %let range_label = %sysfunc(putn(&as_of_sas, monyy7.));\n    %end;\n    %else %if &period = QTR %then %do;\n        %let range_start = %sysfunc(intnx(QTR, &as_of_sas, 0, B), yymmdd10.);\n        %let range_end   = %sysfunc(intnx(QTR, &as_of_sas, 0, E), yymmdd10.);\n        %let range_label = Q%sysfunc(ceil(%sysfunc(month(&as_of_sas))/3))_%sysfunc(year(&as_of_sas));\n    %end;\n    %else %if &period = YTD %then %do;\n        %let range_start = %sysfunc(intnx(YEAR, &as_of_sas, 0, B), yymmdd10.);\n        %let range_end   = %sysfunc(putn(&as_of_sas, yymmdd10.));\n        %let range_label = YTD_%sysfunc(year(&as_of_sas));\n    %end;\n    %else %if &period = YR %then %do;\n        %let range_start = %sysfunc(intnx(YEAR, &as_of_sas, 0, B), yymmdd10.);\n        %let range_end   = %sysfunc(intnx(YEAR, &as_of_sas, 0, E), yymmdd10.);\n        %let range_label = FY_%sysfunc(year(&as_of_sas));\n    %end;\n    %else %do;\n        %put ERROR: [get_date_range] Unsupported period type: &period;\n        %let framework_rc = 1;\n        %return;\n    %end;\n\n    %put NOTE: [get_date_range] Period=&period Start=&range_start End=&range_end Label=&range_label;\n\n%mend get_date_range;\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: create_format                                                   */\n/*   Dynamically creates a SAS format from a lookup dataset using         */\n/*   PROC FORMAT CNTLIN= option. Builds a CNTLIN-compatible dataset       */\n/*   from the specified data, start column, and label column.             */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro create_format(fmtname=, data=, start_col=, label_col=, type=C);\n\n    %validate_params(macro_name=create_format,\n                     required_params=fmtname data start_col label_col);\n    %if &framework_rc ne 0 %then %return;\n\n    /* Build CNTLIN dataset */\n    data work._fmt_cntlin;\n        set &data (keep=&start_col &label_col) end=_last;\n\n        retain fmtname "&fmtname" type "&type";\n\n        rename &start_col = start\n               &label_col = label;\n\n        /* Track record count for logging */\n        _n + 1;\n        if _last then\n            call symputx('_fmt_count', _n, 'L');\n    run;\n\n    /* Generate the format */\n    proc format cntlin=work._fmt_cntlin;\n    run;\n\n    %put NOTE: [create_format] Format $&fmtname created with &_fmt_count entries.;\n\n    proc datasets lib=work nolist; delete _fmt_cntlin; quit;\n\n%mend create_format;\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: standard_report                                                 */\n/*   Generates a standardized report for a given input dataset.           */\n/*   Supports HTML, PDF, and RTF output. Iterates over analysis vars,     */\n/*   runs PROC MEANS for summary statistics, PROC SQL for top-N,          */\n/*   and CALL SYMPUT to capture summary values for titles.                 */\n/*   Parameters:                                                           */\n/*     report_name   - Name identifier for the output files               */\n/*     input_ds      - Input dataset (libname.dataset)                    */\n/*     class_vars    - Classification variable(s) for grouping            */\n/*     analysis_vars - Space-separated list of numeric analysis vars      */\n/*     output_fmt    - Output format: HTML, PDF, or RTF                   */\n/*     where_clause  - Optional filter condition                          */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro standard_report(report_name=, input_ds=, class_vars=, analysis_vars=,\n                       output_fmt=HTML, where_clause=1=1);\n\n    %local i n_vars current_var total_obs grand_mean;\n\n    %validate_params(macro_name=standard_report,\n                     required_params=report_name input_ds class_vars analysis_vars);\n    %if &framework_rc ne 0 %then %return;\n\n    %let n_vars = %sysfunc(countw(&analysis_vars, %str( )));\n\n    %put NOTE: [standard_report] Generating &report_name with &n_vars analysis variables.;\n    %put NOTE: [standard_report] Output format: &output_fmt;\n\n    /* Get total observation count and first variable grand mean via DATA step */\n    data _null_;\n        set &input_ds end=_last;\n        where &where_clause;\n        retain _count 0 _sum 0;\n        _count + 1;\n        _sum + %scan(&analysis_vars, 1, %str( ));\n        if _last then do;\n            call symputx('total_obs', _count, 'G');\n            if _count > 0 then\n                call symputx('grand_mean', put(_sum / _count, best12.), 'G');\n            else\n                call symputx('grand_mean', '0', 'G');\n        end;\n    run;\n\n    %put NOTE: [standard_report] Total observations: &total_obs  Grand mean: &grand_mean;\n\n    /* --- Select output destination based on format parameter --- */\n    %if %upcase(&output_fmt) = HTML %then %do;\n        ods html file="&report_base_path/&report_name..html"\n                 style=HTMLBlue;\n    %end;\n    %else %if %upcase(&output_fmt) = PDF %then %do;\n        ods pdf file="&report_base_path/&report_name..pdf"\n                style=Printer;\n    %end;\n    %else %if %upcase(&output_fmt) = RTF %then %do;\n        ods rtf file="&report_base_path/&report_name..rtf"\n                style=RTF bodytitle;\n    %end;\n    %else %do;\n        %put ERROR: [standard_report] Unsupported output format: &output_fmt;\n        %let framework_rc = 1;\n        %return;\n    %end;\n\n    title1 "Enterprise Report: &report_name";\n    title2 "Period: &range_label (&range_start to &range_end)";\n    title3 "Generated: &default_as_of \u2014 Total Records: &total_obs";\n    footnote "Confidential \u2014 Internal Use Only";\n\n    /* --- Iterate over each analysis variable for individual summaries --- */\n    %do i = 1 %to &n_vars;\n        %let current_var = %scan(&analysis_vars, &i, %str( ));\n\n        title4 "Analysis Variable: &current_var (&i of &n_vars)";\n\n        /* PROC MEANS for descriptive statistics */\n        proc means data=&input_ds n mean std min q1 median q3 max maxdec=2;\n            where &where_clause;\n            class &class_vars;\n            var &current_var;\n            output out=work._summary_&current_var\n                   n=n mean=avg std=std_dev min=min_val max=max_val;\n        run;\n\n        /* PROC SQL for top-10 records by this variable */\n        proc sql outobs=10;\n            title5 "Top 10 Records by &current_var";\n            select &class_vars,\n                   &current_var,\n                   &current_var / &grand_mean as index_to_mean format=percent8.1\n            from &input_ds\n            where &where_clause\n            order by &current_var descending;\n        quit;\n\n    %end;\n\n    /* --- Cross-tabulation summary across all analysis variables --- */\n    proc sql;\n        create table rptout.&report_name._summary as\n        select &class_vars,\n               count(*) as record_count,\n               %do i = 1 %to &n_vars;\n                   %let current_var = %scan(&analysis_vars, &i, %str( ));\n                   mean(&current_var) as avg_&current_var,\n                   sum(&current_var)  as total_&current_var\n                   %if &i < &n_vars %then ,;\n               %end;\n        from &input_ds\n        where &where_clause\n        group by &class_vars\n        order by record_count descending;\n    quit;\n\n    /* Close ODS destination */\n    %if %upcase(&output_fmt) = HTML %then %do; ods html close; %end;\n    %else %if %upcase(&output_fmt) = PDF %then %do; ods pdf close; %end;\n    %else %if %upcase(&output_fmt) = RTF %then %do; ods rtf close; %end;\n\n    title; footnote;\n\n    %put NOTE: [standard_report] Report &report_name generated successfully (&output_fmt).;\n\n    /* Clean up per-variable summary tables */\n    proc datasets lib=work nolist;\n        %do i = 1 %to &n_vars;\n            %let current_var = %scan(&analysis_vars, &i, %str( ));\n            delete _summary_&current_var;\n        %end;\n    quit;\n\n%mend standard_report;\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: batch_reports                                                   */\n/*   Executes standard_report for each region in a list. Iterates         */\n/*   using %DO with COUNTW/SCAN. Uses region-specific data filtering      */\n/*   and calls get_date_range for period setup. Supports conditional       */\n/*   inclusion of a year-over-year comparison section.                     */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro batch_reports(region_list=, period=QTR, as_of=&default_as_of,\n                     output_fmt=HTML, include_yoy=N);\n\n    %local i n_regions current_region;\n\n    %validate_params(macro_name=batch_reports,\n                     required_params=region_list);\n    %if &framework_rc ne 0 %then %return;\n\n    /* Calculate date range for this batch */\n    %get_date_range(period=&period, as_of=&as_of);\n\n    %let n_regions = %sysfunc(countw(&region_list, %str( )));\n    %put NOTE: [batch_reports] Processing &n_regions regions for period &range_label.;\n\n    /* --- Main region iteration loop --- */\n    %do i = 1 %to &n_regions;\n        %let current_region = %scan(&region_list, &i, %str( ));\n        %put NOTE: [batch_reports] Region &i of &n_regions: &current_region;\n\n        /* Extract region-specific data for the date range */\n        data staging.region_&current_region;\n            set raw.sales_data;\n            where region = "&current_region"\n              and transaction_date between "&range_start"d and "&range_end"d;\n        run;\n\n        /* Generate the standard report for this region */\n        %standard_report(\n            report_name   = &current_region._&range_label,\n            input_ds      = staging.region_&current_region,\n            class_vars    = product_category department,\n            analysis_vars = revenue units_sold margin_pct,\n            output_fmt    = &output_fmt,\n            where_clause  = 1=1\n        );\n\n        /* --- Optional year-over-year comparison section --- */\n        %if %upcase(&include_yoy) = Y %then %do;\n            %put NOTE: [batch_reports] Including YoY comparison for &current_region;\n\n            /* Get prior year range */\n            %local py_start py_end;\n            %let py_start = %sysfunc(intnx(YEAR, %sysfunc(inputn(&range_start, yymmdd10.)), -1, S), yymmdd10.);\n            %let py_end   = %sysfunc(intnx(YEAR, %sysfunc(inputn(&range_end, yymmdd10.)), -1, S), yymmdd10.);\n\n            /* Build YoY comparison dataset */\n            proc sql;\n                create table staging.yoy_&current_region as\n                select cy.product_category,\n                       cy.revenue           as cy_revenue,\n                       py.revenue           as py_revenue,\n                       cy.revenue - py.revenue as revenue_change,\n                       case when py.revenue > 0\n                            then (cy.revenue - py.revenue) / py.revenue\n                            else . end       as revenue_pct_change format=percent8.1\n                from (\n                    select product_category,\n                           sum(revenue) as revenue\n                    from raw.sales_data\n                    where region = "&current_region"\n                      and transaction_date between "&range_start"d and "&range_end"d\n                    group by product_category\n                ) as cy\n                left join (\n                    select product_category,\n                           sum(revenue) as revenue\n                    from raw.sales_data\n                    where region = "&current_region"\n                      and transaction_date between "&py_start"d and "&py_end"d\n                    group by product_category\n                ) as py\n                    on cy.product_category = py.product_category\n                order by revenue_change descending;\n            quit;\n        %end;\n\n        /* Clean up region staging data */\n        proc datasets lib=staging nolist;\n            delete region_&current_region;\n            %if %upcase(&include_yoy) = Y %then %do;\n                delete yoy_&current_region;\n            %end;\n        quit;\n\n    %end;  /* End region loop */\n\n    %put NOTE: [batch_reports] Batch complete. &n_regions regions processed.;\n\n%mend batch_reports;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 3: Data Preparation \u2014 Lookup Formats and Base Data                */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n/* Create dynamic format for product category labels */\ndata staging.product_lookup;\n    set raw.product_master (keep=product_code product_description);\nrun;\n\n%create_format(fmtname=PRODLBL,\n               data=staging.product_lookup,\n               start_col=product_code,\n               label_col=product_description,\n               type=C);\n\n/* Create dynamic format for department labels */\ndata staging.dept_lookup;\n    set raw.department_master (keep=dept_code dept_name);\nrun;\n\n%create_format(fmtname=DEPTLBL,\n               data=staging.dept_lookup,\n               start_col=dept_code,\n               label_col=dept_name,\n               type=C);\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 4: Main Reporting Execution \u2014 Quarterly Regional Reports          */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n/* Set up date range for this quarter */\n%get_date_range(period=QTR, as_of=&default_as_of);\n\n/* Execute batch reports for all five major sales regions */\n%batch_reports(\n    region_list = Northeast Midwest South West International,\n    period      = QTR,\n    as_of       = &default_as_of,\n    output_fmt  = HTML,\n    include_yoy = Y\n);\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 5: Consolidated Summary \u2014 Cross-Region Aggregation                */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n/* Build a summary-of-summaries across all regions */\nproc sql;\n    create table mart.quarterly_summary as\n    select 'ALL_REGIONS'           as region,\n           a.product_category,\n           sum(a.record_count)     as total_records,\n           sum(a.total_revenue)    as total_revenue format=dollar15.2,\n           avg(a.avg_revenue)      as weighted_avg_revenue format=dollar12.2,\n           sum(a.total_units_sold) as total_units,\n           avg(a.avg_margin_pct)   as avg_margin format=percent8.1\n    from (\n        select * from rptout.Northeast_&range_label._summary\n        union all\n        select * from rptout.Midwest_&range_label._summary\n        union all\n        select * from rptout.South_&range_label._summary\n        union all\n        select * from rptout.West_&range_label._summary\n        union all\n        select * from rptout.International_&range_label._summary\n    ) as a\n    group by a.product_category\n    order by total_revenue descending;\nquit;\n\n/* Generate a consolidated executive report */\n%standard_report(\n    report_name   = Executive_Consolidated_&range_label,\n    input_ds      = mart.quarterly_summary,\n    class_vars    = product_category,\n    analysis_vars = total_revenue total_units avg_margin,\n    output_fmt    = PDF,\n    where_clause  = 1=1\n);\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 6: Trend Analysis \u2014 Rolling Quarterly Metrics                     */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\ndata mart.rolling_trends;\n    set mart.quarterly_summary;\n    by product_category;\n\n    retain q1_revenue q2_revenue q3_revenue q4_revenue 0\n           quarter_idx 0;\n\n    /* Reset for each product category */\n    if first.product_category then do;\n        q1_revenue  = 0;\n        q2_revenue  = 0;\n        q3_revenue  = 0;\n        q4_revenue  = 0;\n        quarter_idx = 0;\n    end;\n\n    quarter_idx + 1;\n\n    /* Store quarter-specific revenue */\n    select (quarter_idx);\n        when (1) q1_revenue = total_revenue;\n        when (2) q2_revenue = total_revenue;\n        when (3) q3_revenue = total_revenue;\n        when (4) q4_revenue = total_revenue;\n        otherwise;\n    end;\n\n    /* Compute rolling average on last record per category */\n    if last.product_category then do;\n        rolling_avg_revenue = mean(q1_revenue, q2_revenue, q3_revenue, q4_revenue);\n        revenue_volatility  = std(q1_revenue, q2_revenue, q3_revenue, q4_revenue);\n\n        /* Flag high-volatility categories */\n        length volatility_flag $10;\n        if revenue_volatility > rolling_avg_revenue * 0.3 then\n            volatility_flag = 'HIGH';\n        else if revenue_volatility > rolling_avg_revenue * 0.15 then\n            volatility_flag = 'MEDIUM';\n        else\n            volatility_flag = 'LOW';\n\n        output;\n    end;\n\n    format q1_revenue q2_revenue q3_revenue q4_revenue\n           rolling_avg_revenue revenue_volatility dollar15.2;\nrun;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 7: Reporting Metadata and Cleanup                                 */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n/* Store report execution metadata */\nproc sql;\n    insert into mart.report_execution_log\n        (report_date, period, range_start, range_end,\n         regions_processed, output_format, status, run_timestamp)\n    values\n        ("&default_as_of"d, "&default_period", "&range_start"d, "&range_end"d,\n         5, "HTML+PDF", "COMPLETE", %sysfunc(datetime()));\nquit;\n\n/* Clean up staging datasets */\nproc datasets lib=staging nolist;\n    delete product_lookup dept_lookup;\nquit;\n\n/* Final status messages */\n%if &framework_rc ne 0 %then %do;\n    %put WARNING: Macro framework completed with errors. Review log for details.;\n    %put WARNING: framework_rc = &framework_rc;\n%end;\n%else %do;\n    %put NOTE: Macro framework completed successfully. All reports generated.;\n%end;\n\noptions nomprint nomlogic nosymbolgen;\n\n%put NOTE: ===== gsh_02_macro_framework.sas completed at %sysfunc(datetime(), datetime20.) =====;\n-gold_meta16]

'/dwh/archive';\nlibname ctrl    '/dwh/control';\n\n/* Global job control variables */\n%let job_name      = DWH_NIGHTLY_LOAD;\n%let job_run_id    = %sysfunc(putn(%sysfunc(datetime()), datetime20.));\n%let job_status    = RUNNING;\n%let job_rc        = 0;\n%let process_dt    = %sysfunc(today(), yymmdd10.);\n%let n_src_tables  = 5;\n\n/* Load shared utility macros */\n%include '/dwh/common/etl_utilities.sas';\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 2: Utility Macro Definitions                                      */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: extract_table                                                   */\n/*   Extracts data from a source library into a staging dataset.          */\n/*   Supports optional WHERE filter for incremental loads.                 */\n/*   Uses %SYSFUNC(EXIST) to verify source, CALL SYMPUTX for counts.      */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro extract_table(src_lib=, src_table=, tgt_ds=, filter=);\n\n    %local src_exist row_count;\n\n    %put NOTE: [extract_table] Extracting &src_lib..&src_table -> &tgt_ds;\n\n    /* Validate source table exists */\n    %let src_exist = %sysfunc(exist(&src_lib..&src_table));\n    %if &src_exist = 0 %then %do;\n        %put ERROR: [extract_table] Source &src_lib..&src_table does not exist.;\n        %let job_rc = 1;\n        %return;\n    %end;\n\n    /* Extract with optional filter condition */\n    data &tgt_ds;\n        set &src_lib..&src_table;\n        %if %length(&filter) > 0 %then %do;\n            where &filter;\n        %end;\n\n        /* Add extraction metadata */\n        _extract_ts = datetime();\n        _source_system = "&src_lib..&src_table";\n        format _extract_ts datetime20.;\n    run;\n\n    /* Capture row count */\n    data _null_;\n        if 0 then set &tgt_ds nobs=_n;\n        call symputx('row_count', _n, 'L');\n        stop;\n    run;\n\n    %put NOTE: [extract_table] Extracted &row_count rows from &src_lib..&src_table;\n\n    /* Log to control table */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        insert into ctrl.extraction_log\n            (job_run_id, source_table, target_dataset, row_count,\n             filter_applied, extract_timestamp)\n        values\n            ("&job_run_id", "&src_lib..&src_table", "&tgt_ds", &row_count,\n             "&filter", %sysfunc(datetime()));\n    quit;\n\n%mend extract_table;\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: scd_type2_merge                                                 */\n/*   Implements SCD Type 2 merge logic for dimension tables.               */\n/*   Compares tracked columns between staging and existing dimension.      */\n/*   Expires changed rows (current_flag='N', sets effective_to) and        */\n/*   inserts new versions. Also inserts brand-new dimension members.       */\n/*   Parameters:                                                           */\n/*     dim         - Target dimension table in warehouse library           */\n/*     stage       - Staging table with new/changed records                */\n/*     natural_key - Natural business key column                           */\n/*     tracked_cols - Space-separated list of SCD-tracked columns          */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro scd_type2_merge(dim=, stage=, natural_key=, tracked_cols=);\n\n    %local i n_cols col change_expr new_cnt upd_cnt unchanged_cnt;\n\n    %put NOTE: [scd_type2_merge] Merging &stage into &dim on &natural_key;\n\n    /* Build change detection expression from tracked columns */\n    %let n_cols = %sysfunc(countw(&tracked_cols, %str( )));\n    %let change_expr = ;\n    %do i = 1 %to &n_cols;\n        %let col = %scan(&tracked_cols, &i, %str( ));\n        %if &i > 1 %then %let change_expr = &change_expr or ;\n        %let change_expr = &change_expr (\n            coalesce(strip(put(s.&col, best32.)), '') ne\n            coalesce(strip(put(d.&col, best32.)), '')\n        );\n    %end;\n\n    /* Classify records as NEW, CHANGED, or UNCHANGED */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        create table work._scd2_classified as\n        select s.*,\n               d.dim_key as _existing_key,\n               case\n                   when d.dim_key is null then 'NEW'\n                   when &change_expr then 'CHANGED'\n                   else 'UNCHANGED'\n               end as _scd_action\n        from &stage as s\n        left join &dim (where=(current_flag = 'Y')) as d\n            on s.&natural_key = d.&natural_key;\n\n        /* Capture classification counts */\n        select count(*) into :new_cnt trimmed\n            from work._scd2_classified where _scd_action = 'NEW';\n        select count(*) into :upd_cnt trimmed\n            from work._scd2_classified where _scd_action = 'CHANGED';\n        select count(*) into :unchanged_cnt trimmed\n            from work._scd2_classified where _scd_action = 'UNCHANGED';\n    quit;\n\n    %put NOTE: [scd_type2_merge] New=&new_cnt Changed=&upd_cnt Unchanged=&unchanged_cnt;\n\n    /* Step 1: Expire changed rows in the current dimension */\n    %if &upd_cnt > 0 %then %do;\n        proc sql;\n            update &dim\n            set current_flag = 'N',\n                effective_to = "&process_dt"d,\n                updated_by   = "&job_name",\n                updated_ts   = %sysfunc(datetime())\n            where current_flag = 'Y'\n              and &natural_key in (\n                  select &natural_key from work._scd2_classified\n                  where _scd_action = 'CHANGED'\n              );\n        quit;\n    %end;\n\n    /* Step 2: Insert new versions for changed + new records */\n    %if &new_cnt > 0 or &upd_cnt > 0 %then %do;\n        proc sql;\n            insert into &dim\n            select monotonic() + (select coalesce(max(dim_key), 0) from &dim)\n                       as dim_key,\n                   s.*,\n                   "&process_dt"d   as effective_from,\n                   '31DEC9999'd     as effective_to,\n                   'Y'              as current_flag,\n                   "&job_name"      as created_by,\n                   %sysfunc(datetime()) as created_ts,\n                   .                as updated_by,\n                   .                as updated_ts\n            from work._scd2_classified (drop=_existing_key _scd_action) as s\n            where s._scd_action in ('NEW', 'CHANGED');\n        quit;\n    %end;\n\n    /* Log dimension load metrics */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        insert into ctrl.dimension_load_log\n            (job_run_id, dimension_table, new_records, changed_records,\n             unchanged_records, load_timestamp)\n        values\n            ("&job_run_id", "&dim", &new_cnt, &upd_cnt,\n             &unchanged_cnt, %sysfunc(datetime()));\n    quit;\n\n    /* Cleanup */\n    proc datasets lib=work nolist; delete _scd2_classified; quit;\n\n%mend scd_type2_merge;\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: fact_load                                                       */\n/*   Loads a fact table from staging data. Performs dimension key          */\n/*   lookups to replace natural keys with surrogate keys. Calculates      */\n/*   derived measures. Appends to the target fact table.                   */\n/*   Parameters:                                                           */\n/*     fact_table  - Target fact table in warehouse                        */\n/*     stage_table - Staging table with transactional data                 */\n/*     dim_keys    - Space-separated: natural_key=dim_table pairs          */\n/*     measures    - Space-separated list of measure columns               */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro fact_load(fact_table=, stage_table=, dim_keys=, measures=);\n\n    %local i n_dims pair nat_key dim_tbl fact_count;\n\n    %put NOTE: [fact_load] Loading &fact_table from &stage_table;\n\n    %let n_dims = %sysfunc(countw(&dim_keys, %str( )));\n\n    /* Build staging table with surrogate key lookups */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        create table work._fact_with_keys as\n        select s.*\n        %do i = 1 %to &n_dims;\n            %let pair    = %scan(&dim_keys, &i, %str( ));\n            %let nat_key = %scan(&pair, 1, =);\n            %let dim_tbl = %scan(&pair, 2, =);\n            , d&i..dim_key as &nat_key._key\n        %end;\n        from &stage_table as s\n        %do i = 1 %to &n_dims;\n            %let pair    = %scan(&dim_keys, &i, %str( ));\n            %let nat_key = %scan(&pair, 1, =);\n            %let dim_tbl = %scan(&pair, 2, =);\n            left join &dim_tbl (where=(current_flag = 'Y')) as d&i\n                on s.&nat_key = d&i..&nat_key\n        %end;\n        ;\n    quit;\n\n    /* Append to target fact table */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        insert into &fact_table\n        select * from work._fact_with_keys;\n\n        select count(*) into :fact_count trimmed\n            from work._fact_with_keys;\n    quit;\n\n    %put NOTE: [fact_load] Loaded &fact_count rows into &fact_table;\n\n    /* Log fact load metrics */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        insert into ctrl.fact_load_log\n            (job_run_id, fact_table, rows_loaded, load_timestamp)\n        values\n            ("&job_run_id", "&fact_table", &fact_count, %sysfunc(datetime()));\n    quit;\n\n    proc datasets lib=work nolist; delete _fact_with_keys; quit;\n\n%mend fact_load;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 3: Watermark Retrieval \u2014 Get Last Successful Load Checkpoint      */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\nproc sql noprint;\n    select coalesce(max(watermark_value), '1900-01-01')\n        into :last_watermark trimmed\n    from ctrl.watermark_table\n    where job_name = "&job_name"\n      and status   = 'COMPLETE';\nquit;\n\n%put NOTE: [Watermark] Last successful watermark: &last_watermark;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 4: Incremental Extraction Loop                                    */\n/*   Iterates over source tables, extracting only rows modified since        */\n/*   the last watermark timestamp.                                           */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n%macro extract_all_sources;\n    %local i;\n\n    /* Define extraction configuration as indexed arrays */\n    %let ext_src1 = srcdb;  %let ext_tbl1 = customers;     %let ext_tgt1 = staging.stg_customers;\n    %let ext_src2 = srcdb;  %let ext_tbl2 = products;      %let ext_tgt2 = staging.stg_products;\n    %let ext_src3 = srcdb;  %let ext_tbl3 = orders;        %let ext_tgt3 = staging.stg_orders;\n    %let ext_src4 = srcdb;  %let ext_tbl4 = order_lines;   %let ext_tgt4 = staging.stg_order_lines;\n    %let ext_src5 = srcdb;  %let ext_tbl5 = suppliers;     %let ext_tgt5 = staging.stg_suppliers;\n\n    %do i = 1 %to &n_src_tables;\n        %put NOTE: [extract_all] Table &i of &n_src_tables: &&ext_tbl&i;\n        %extract_table(\n            src_lib   = &&ext_src&i,\n            src_table = &&ext_tbl&i,\n            tgt_ds    = &&ext_tgt&i,\n            filter    = modified_date >= "&last_watermark"d\n        );\n\n        /* Check for extraction errors */\n        %if &job_rc ne 0 %then %do;\n            %put ERROR: [extract_all] Extraction failed at table &i (&&ext_tbl&i). Halting.;\n            %return;\n        %end;\n    %end;\n\n    %put NOTE: [extract_all] All &n_src_tables tables extracted successfully.;\n%mend extract_all_sources;\n\n%extract_all_sources;\n\n/* Abort if extraction had errors */\n%if &job_rc ne 0 %then %do;\n    %put ERROR: Extraction phase failed. Aborting warehouse load.;\n    %goto EXIT_PROGRAM;\n%end;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 5: Staging Transforms \u2014 Cleansing, Mapping, and Type Conversion   */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n/* --- Customer staging transforms --- */\ndata staging.stg_customers_clean (drop=_:);\n    set staging.stg_customers;\n\n    /* Standardize name fields */\n    first_name = propcase(strip(first_name));\n    last_name  = propcase(strip(last_name));\n\n    /* Null handling for optional fields */\n    if missing(phone) then phone = 'N/A';\n    if missing(email) then email = 'N/A';\n\n    /* Status code mapping */\n    length customer_status $15;\n    select (upcase(status_code));\n        when ('A')  customer_status = 'ACTIVE';\n        when ('I')  customer_status = 'INACTIVE';\n        when ('S')  customer_status = 'SUSPENDED';\n        when ('C')  customer_status = 'CLOSED';\n        otherwise   customer_status = 'UNKNOWN';\n    end;\n\n    /* Type conversion for date fields stored as strings */\n    if not missing(registration_date_str) then\n        registration_date = input(registration_date_str, yymmdd10.);\n    else\n        registration_date = .;\n    format registration_date yymmdd10.;\n\n    /* Geography enrichment */\n    length country_name $50;\n    select (upcase(country_code));\n        when ('US') country_name = 'United States';\n        when ('CA') country_name = 'Canada';\n        when ('GB') country_name = 'United Kingdom';\n        when ('DE') country_name = 'Germany';\n        when ('FR') country_name = 'France';\n        otherwise   country_name = 'Other';\n    end;\nrun;\n\n/* --- Product staging transforms --- */\ndata staging.stg_products_clean (drop=_:);\n    set staging.stg_products;\n\n    /* Null handling */\n    if missing(unit_cost) then unit_cost = 0;\n    if missing(list_price) then list_price = 0;\n\n    /* Derive margin percentage */\n    if list_price > 0 then\n        margin_pct = (list_price - unit_cost) / list_price;\n    else\n        margin_pct = 0;\n\n    /* Product category standardization */\n    category = propcase(strip(category));\n    subcategory = propcase(strip(subcategory));\n\n    format margin_pct percent8.1;\nrun;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 6: Deduplication via PROC SORT                                    */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\nproc sort data=staging.stg_customers_clean\n          out=staging.stg_customers_dedup nodupkey;\n    by customer_id;\nrun;\n\nproc sort data=staging.stg_products_clean\n          out=staging.stg_products_dedup nodupkey;\n    by product_id;\nrun;\n\nproc sort data=staging.stg_orders\n          out=staging.stg_orders_dedup nodupkey;\n    by order_id;\nrun;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 7: Dimension Loading \u2014 SCD Type 2 Merge                          */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n/* Load customer dimension */\n%scd_type2_merge(\n    dim         = whouse.dim_customer,\n    stage       = staging.stg_customers_dedup,\n    natural_key = customer_id,\n    tracked_cols = first_name last_name email phone customer_status country_name\n);\n\n/* Load product dimension */\n%scd_type2_merge(\n    dim         = whouse.dim_product,\n    stage       = staging.stg_products_dedup,\n    natural_key = product_id,\n    tracked_cols = category subcategory unit_cost list_price margin_pct\n);\n\n/* Load supplier dimension */\n%scd_type2_merge(\n    dim         = whouse.dim_supplier,\n    stage       = staging.stg_suppliers,\n    natural_key = supplier_id,\n    tracked_cols = supplier_name contact_name city state country_code\n);\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 8: Dimension Key Lookup \u2014 Resolve Surrogate Keys for Facts        */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\nproc sql;\n    create table staging.orders_with_keys as\n    select o.*,\n           dc.dim_key  as customer_dim_key,\n           dp.dim_key  as product_dim_key,\n           ds.dim_key  as supplier_dim_key,\n           dd.dim_key  as date_dim_key\n    from staging.stg_orders_dedup as o\n    left join whouse.dim_customer (where=(current_flag = 'Y')) as dc\n        on o.customer_id = dc.customer_id\n    left join staging.stg_order_lines as ol\n        on o.order_id = ol.order_id\n    left join whouse.dim_product (where=(current_flag = 'Y')) as dp\n        on ol.product_id = dp.product_id\n    left join whouse.dim_supplier (where=(current_flag = 'Y')) as ds\n        on ol.supplier_id = ds.supplier_id\n    left join whouse.dim_date as dd\n        on o.order_date = dd.calendar_date;\nquit;\n\n/* Identify orphaned keys \u2014 orders without dimension matches */\nproc sql;\n    create table staging.orphan_keys as\n    select order_id,\n           case when customer_dim_key is null then 'CUSTOMER' else '' end as missing_customer,\n           case when product_dim_key is null then 'PRODUCT' else '' end as missing_product,\n           case when date_dim_key is null then 'DATE' else '' end as missing_date\n    from staging.orders_with_keys\n    where customer_dim_key is null\n       or product_dim_key is null\n       or date_dim_key is null;\n\n    select count(*) into :orphan_count trimmed from staging.orphan_keys;\nquit;\n\n%if &orphan_count > 0 %then %do;\n    %put WARNING: [Key Lookup] &orphan_count orders have missing dimension keys.;\n    %put WARNING: [Key Lookup] Check staging.orphan_keys for details.;\n%end;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 9: Fact Table Loading                                             */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n/* Prepare order fact staging with calculated measures */\ndata staging.fact_orders_stage;\n    set staging.orders_with_keys;\n    where customer_dim_key is not null;   /* Exclude orphans */\n\n    /* Calculate derived measures */\n    if quantity > 0 and unit_price > 0 then do;\n        line_total     = quantity * unit_price;\n        discount_amt   = line_total * coalesce(discount_pct, 0);\n        net_amount     = line_total - discount_amt;\n        tax_amount     = net_amount * coalesce(tax_rate, 0);\n        gross_amount   = net_amount + tax_amount;\n    end;\n    else do;\n        line_total   = 0;\n        discount_amt = 0;\n        net_amount   = 0;\n        tax_amount   = 0;\n        gross_amount = 0;\n    end;\n\n    format line_total discount_amt net_amount tax_amount gross_amount dollar15.2;\nrun;\n\n/* Load order fact table */\n%fact_load(\n    fact_table  = whouse.fact_orders,\n    stage_table = staging.fact_orders_stage,\n    dim_keys    = customer_id=whouse.dim_customer product_id=whouse.dim_product,\n    measures    = quantity unit_price line_total discount_amt net_amount\n);\n\n/* Load returns fact table */\n%fact_load(\n    fact_table  = whouse.fact_returns,\n    stage_table = staging.stg_returns,\n    dim_keys    = customer_id=whouse.dim_customer product_id=whouse.dim_product,\n    measures    = return_quantity refund_amount\n);\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 10: Watermark Update \u2014 Record This Run's Checkpoint               */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\ndata ctrl.watermark_update;\n    length job_name $50 watermark_value $30 status $15;\n    job_name        = "&job_name";\n    watermark_value = "&process_dt";\n    status          = "COMPLETE";\n    update_ts       = datetime();\n    format update_ts datetime20.;\n    output;\nrun;\n\nproc append base=ctrl.watermark_table data=ctrl.watermark_update force;\nrun;\n\n%put NOTE: [Watermark] Updated watermark to &process_dt;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 11: Error Detection and Rollback Logging                          */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n%if &syserr ne 0 or &job_rc ne 0 %then %do;\n    %let job_status = FAILED;\n\n    %put ERROR: Warehouse load encountered errors. SYSERR=&syserr JOB_RC=&job_rc;\n\n    /* Log error details to control table */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        insert into ctrl.error_log\n            (job_run_id, job_name, error_code, error_message, error_timestamp)\n        values\n            ("&job_run_id", "&job_name", &syserr,\n             "Warehouse load failed \u2014 review SAS log for details",\n             %sysfunc(datetime()));\n    quit;\n\n    /* Write error flag file for monitoring */\n    data _null_;\n        file '/dwh/flags/load_error.flag';\n        put "JOB_RUN_ID=&job_run_id";\n        put "JOB_NAME=&job_name";\n        put "ERROR_TIME=%sysfunc(datetime(), datetime20.)";\n        put "SYSERR=&syserr";\n        put "JOB_RC=&job_rc";\n    run;\n%end;\n%else %do;\n    %let job_status = COMPLETE;\n    %put NOTE: Warehouse load completed successfully.;\n%end;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 12: Job Status Update and Final Cleanup                           */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\nproc sql;\n    insert into ctrl.job_status_log\n        (job_run_id, job_name, job_status, process_date,\n         start_time, end_time, tables_processed)\n    values\n        ("&job_run_id", "&job_name", "&job_status", "&process_dt"d,\n         input("&job_run_id", datetime20.), %sysfunc(datetime()), &n_src_tables);\nquit;\n\n/* Clean up all staging datasets */\nproc datasets lib=staging nolist;\n    delete stg_customers stg_customers_clean stg_customers_dedup\n           stg_products stg_products_clean stg_products_dedup\n           stg_orders stg_orders_dedup stg_order_lines stg_suppliers\n           stg_returns orders_with_keys orphan_keys\n           fact_orders_stage;\nquit;\n\nproc datasets lib=work nolist; kill; quit;\n\n/* Final notification via CALL EXECUTE */\ndata _null_;\n    if "&job_status" = "FAILED" then do;\n        call execute('%nrstr(%put ERROR: ===== DWH LOAD FAILED =====;)');\n        call execute(cats(\n            '%nrstr(filename _email email to=("dwh-ops@company.com") ',\n            'subject="DWH LOAD FAILURE: ', "&process_dt", '";)'\n        ));\n    end;\n    else do;\n        call execute('%nrstr(%put NOTE: ===== DWH LOAD COMPLETE =====;)');\n    end;\nrun;\n\n%EXIT_PROGRAM:\n\noptions nomprint nomlogic nosymbolgen;\n\n%put NOTE: ===== gsh_03_warehouse_load.sas finished at %sysfunc(datetime(), datetime20.) =====;\n%put NOTE: ===== Job Status: &job_status =====;\n-gold_meta17]
[PASS] test_no_lineage_violations_in_deterministic[gsh_04_clinical_trial.sas-/******************************************************************************\n * Program Name : gsh_04_clinical_trial.sas\n * Author       : Biostatistics Department \u2014 Clinical Data Sciences\n * Study        : XYZ-301 Phase III Randomized Controlled Trial\n * Protocol     : XYZ-301-GLOBAL\n * Created      : 2025-06-15\n * Modified     : 2026-02-19\n * Purpose      : End-to-end clinical trial analysis pipeline for Phase III\n *                study XYZ-301. Derives ADaM-compliant analysis datasets\n *                (ADSL, ADAE), performs primary efficacy analysis (ANCOVA\n *                and logistic regression), and generates safety tables\n *                per ICH E9 guidelines.\n * Input        : SDTM domains (DM, AE, DS, EX, VS, LB)\n * Output       : ADaM datasets (ADSL, ADAE), TFLs (Tables 14.1-14.3)\n * Dependencies : study_macros.sas (shared clinical trial utility macros)\n * Validation   : Double programming per SOP-STAT-003\n * Change Log   :\n *   2025-06-15  v1.0  Initial ADSL / ADAE derivation         (J. Chen)\n *   2025-08-20  v1.1  Added efficacy analysis macros          (J. Chen)\n *   2025-10-10  v1.2  Safety tables with rate differences     (A. Patel)\n *   2025-12-01  v1.3  Population flag refinements per SAP v3  (J. Chen)\n *   2026-01-15  v1.4  Added PROC SQL demographics table       (A. Patel)\n *   2026-02-19  v1.5  Error handling and final QC checks      (J. Chen)\n ******************************************************************************/\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 1: Environment Setup and Library Definitions                      */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\noptions mprint mlogic symbolgen nocenter ls=132 ps=60\n        validvarname=v7 nofmterr msglevel=i;\n\n/* --- SDTM source data library (read-only) --- */\nlibname sdtm '/clinical/XYZ301/data/sdtm'\n             access=readonly;\n\n/* --- ADaM analysis dataset library --- */\nlibname adam '/clinical/XYZ301/data/adam';\n\n/* --- Output library for tables, figures, and listings --- */\nlibname output '/clinical/XYZ301/output/tables';\n\n/* --- Metadata and format library --- */\nlibname metadata '/clinical/XYZ301/metadata';\n\n/* Global study parameters */\n%let study_id    = XYZ-301;\n%let cutoff_date = 15NOV2025;\n%let sponsor     = PharmaCorp Inc.;\n%let sap_version = 3.0;\n%let run_dt      = %sysfunc(today(), date9.);\n%let run_tm      = %sysfunc(time(), time8.);\n%let analysis_rc = 0;\n\n/* Load shared clinical trial utility macros */\n%include '/clinical/XYZ301/macros/study_macros.sas';\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 2: ADSL \u2014 Subject-Level Analysis Dataset                          */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: derive_adsl                                                     */\n/*   Derives the subject-level analysis dataset (ADSL) per ADaM IG.       */\n/*   Merges SDTM domains DM, DS, and randomization list.                  */\n/*   Calculates age groups, study duration, population flags (ITT, PP).   */\n/*   Parameters:                                                          */\n/*     study= : Study identifier for title annotations                   */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro derive_adsl(study=);\n\n    %put NOTE: ========================================================;\n    %put NOTE: derive_adsl: Beginning ADSL derivation for study &study.;\n    %put NOTE: Cutoff date: &cutoff_date. | SAP version: &sap_version.;\n    %put NOTE: ========================================================;\n\n    /* ---- Sort input SDTM domains by USUBJID ---- */\n    proc sort data=sdtm.dm out=work.dm_sorted;\n        by usubjid;\n    run;\n\n    proc sort data=sdtm.ds out=work.ds_sorted;\n        by usubjid;\n    run;\n\n    proc sort data=metadata.randlist out=work.rand_sorted;\n        by usubjid;\n    run;\n\n    /* ---- Merge demographics, disposition, and randomization ---- */\n    data adam.adsl (label="Subject-Level Analysis Dataset \u2014 &study.");\n        merge work.dm_sorted  (in=in_dm)\n              work.ds_sorted  (in=in_ds\n                               keep=usubjid dsdecod dsstdtc dsendtc)\n              work.rand_sorted(in=in_rand\n                               keep=usubjid trt01p trt01pn randdt stratum);\n        by usubjid;\n\n        /* Only keep subjects present in DM domain */\n        if in_dm;\n\n        length agegr1 $10 agegr1n 8 racen 8 sexn 8\n               ittfl $1 ppfl $1 saffl $1 complfl $1\n               studydur 8 trtdur 8;\n\n        /* ---- Age group derivation ---- */\n        if      age < 18 then do; agegr1 = '<18';     agegr1n = 1; end;\n        else if age < 40 then do; agegr1 = '18-39';   agegr1n = 2; end;\n        else if age < 65 then do; agegr1 = '40-64';   agegr1n = 3; end;\n        else                  do; agegr1 = '65+';     agegr1n = 4; end;\n\n        /* ---- Sex numeric code ---- */\n        select (upcase(sex));\n            when ('M') sexn = 1;\n            when ('F') sexn = 2;\n            otherwise  sexn = 99;\n        end;\n\n        /* ---- Race numeric code ---- */\n        select (upcase(race));\n            when ('WHITE')                                    racen = 1;\n            when ('BLACK OR AFRICAN AMERICAN')                racen = 2;\n            when ('ASIAN')                                    racen = 3;\n            when ('AMERICAN INDIAN OR ALASKA NATIVE')         racen = 4;\n            when ('NATIVE HAWAIIAN OR OTHER PACIFIC ISLANDER') racen = 5;\n            when ('MULTIPLE')                                 racen = 6;\n            otherwise                                         racen = 99;\n        end;\n\n        /* ---- Study duration (days from first dose to cutoff) ---- */\n        if not missing(rfxstdtc) then do;\n            rfxstdt = input(rfxstdtc, yymmdd10.);\n            cutoff  = input("&cutoff_date.", date9.);\n            studydur = intck('day', rfxstdt, cutoff);\n        end;\n        else studydur = .;\n\n        /* ---- Treatment duration (first dose to last dose) ---- */\n        if not missing(rfxstdtc) and not missing(rfxendtc) then do;\n            rfxendt = input(rfxendtc, yymmdd10.);\n            trtdur  = intck('day', rfxstdt, rfxendt) + 1;\n        end;\n        else trtdur = .;\n\n        /* ---- Population flags per SAP v&sap_version ---- */\n        /* ITT: all randomized subjects */\n        if in_rand then ittfl = 'Y';\n        else ittfl = 'N';\n\n        /* Safety: randomized + received at least one dose */\n        if in_rand and not missing(rfxstdtc) then saffl = 'Y';\n        else saffl = 'N';\n\n        /* Per-protocol: ITT without major protocol deviations */\n        if ittfl = 'Y' and upcase(dsdecod) ne 'PROTOCOL VIOLATION'\n           and trtdur >= 28 then ppfl = 'Y';\n        else ppfl = 'N';\n\n        /* Completion flag */\n        if upcase(dsdecod) = 'COMPLETED' then complfl = 'Y';\n        else complfl = 'N';\n\n        /* ---- Assign treatment labels ---- */\n        if trt01pn = 1 then trt01p = 'XYZ 100mg';\n        else if trt01pn = 2 then trt01p = 'XYZ 200mg';\n        else if trt01pn = 3 then trt01p = 'Placebo';\n\n        format rfxstdt rfxendt cutoff date9.;\n\n        /* ---- Store first subject ID via CALL SYMPUTX ---- */\n        if _n_ = 1 then call symputx('adsl_first_subj', usubjid, 'G');\n    run;\n\n    /* ---- Capture ADSL record count ---- */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select count(*) into :adsl_nobs trimmed\n        from adam.adsl;\n    quit;\n\n    %put NOTE: ADSL derivation complete. N=&adsl_nobs subjects.;\n    %if &syserr > 0 %then %do;\n        %put ERROR: ADSL derivation failed with SYSERR=&syserr.;\n        %let analysis_rc = 1;\n    %end;\n\n%mend derive_adsl;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 3: ADAE \u2014 Adverse Event Analysis Dataset                          */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: derive_adae                                                     */\n/*   Derives the adverse event analysis dataset (ADAE) per ADaM IG.       */\n/*   Merges SDTM.AE with ADSL for population and treatment context.       */\n/*   Derives severity grades, SAE flags, AE duration, and onset day.      */\n/*   Parameters:                                                          */\n/*     study= : Study identifier for annotations                        */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro derive_adae(study=);\n\n    %put NOTE: ========================================================;\n    %put NOTE: derive_adae: Beginning ADAE derivation for study &study.;\n    %put NOTE: ========================================================;\n\n    /* ---- Sort AE domain ---- */\n    proc sort data=sdtm.ae out=work.ae_sorted;\n        by usubjid;\n    run;\n\n    /* ---- Sort ADSL for merge ---- */\n    proc sort data=adam.adsl out=work.adsl_for_ae;\n        by usubjid;\n    run;\n\n    /* ---- Merge AE with ADSL to get treatment and population context ---- */\n    data adam.adae (label="Adverse Event Analysis Dataset \u2014 &study.");\n        merge work.ae_sorted    (in=in_ae)\n              work.adsl_for_ae  (in=in_adsl\n                                 keep=usubjid trt01p trt01pn saffl ittfl\n                                      rfxstdtc agegr1 sex studydur);\n        by usubjid;\n\n        /* Only keep AEs for subjects in ADSL with safety flag */\n        if in_ae and in_adsl and saffl = 'Y';\n\n        length aesevn 8 aeser_flag $1 aedur 8 aestdy 8\n               aebodsys $200 aedecod $200 aereln 8;\n\n        /* ---- Severity grading (numeric) ---- */\n        select (upcase(aesev));\n            when ('MILD')     aesevn = 1;\n            when ('MODERATE') aesevn = 2;\n            when ('SEVERE')   aesevn = 3;\n            otherwise         aesevn = .;\n        end;\n\n        /* ---- Serious adverse event flag ---- */\n        if upcase(aeser) = 'Y' then aeser_flag = 'Y';\n        else aeser_flag = 'N';\n\n        /*
