========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 7: Main Program \u2014 Orchestrate Full Regulatory Filing              */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n%put NOTE: ================================================================;\n%put NOTE: REGULATORY FILING PIPELINE \u2014 START;\n%put NOTE: Run ID: &run_id;\n%put NOTE: Report Date: &report_date;\n%put NOTE: Filing Period: &filing_year Q&filing_quarter;\n%put NOTE: ================================================================;\n\n/* ----- Step 1: Calculate RWA for each asset class ----- */\n%put NOTE: --- Step 1: Risk-Weighted Assets Calculation ---;\n\n%calc_rwa(asset_class=CORPORATE, approach=IRB);\n\n%if &syserr > 0 %then %do;\n    %let reg_rc = 1;\n    %put ERROR: RWA calculation failed for CORPORATE.;\n    %goto reg_exit;\n%end;\n\n%calc_rwa(asset_class=SOVEREIGN, approach=STANDARDIZED);\n\n%if &syserr > 0 %then %do;\n    %let reg_rc = 2;\n    %put ERROR: RWA calculation failed for SOVEREIGN.;\n    %goto reg_exit;\n%end;\n\n%calc_rwa(asset_class=RETAIL, approach=IRB);\n\n%if &syserr > 0 %then %do;\n    %let reg_rc = 3;\n    %put ERROR: RWA calculation failed for RETAIL.;\n    %goto reg_exit;\n%end;\n\n%calc_rwa(asset_class=EQUITY, approach=STANDARDIZED);\n\n%if &syserr > 0 %then %do;\n    %let reg_rc = 4;\n    %put ERROR: RWA calculation failed for EQUITY.;\n    %goto reg_exit;\n%end;\n\n/* Consolidate RWA results across all asset classes */\nproc sql;\n    create table out.rwa_summary as\n    select * from work.rwa_corporate_summary\n    union all\n    select * from work.rwa_sovereign_summary\n    union all\n    select * from work.rwa_retail_summary\n    union all\n    select * from work.rwa_equity_summary\n    order by asset_class;\nquit;\n\n%put NOTE: Step 1 complete \u2014 RWA calculated for all 4 asset classes.;\n\n/* ----- Step 2: Calculate Capital Ratios ----- */\n%put NOTE: --- Step 2: Capital Adequacy Ratios ---;\n\n%capital_ratios;\n\n%if &syserr > 0 %then %do;\n    %let reg_rc = 5;\n    %put ERROR: Capital ratio calculation failed.;\n    %goto reg_exit;\n%end;\n\n%put NOTE: Step 2 complete \u2014 Capital ratios derived.;\n\n/* ----- Step 3: Run Stress Testing Scenarios ----- */\n%put NOTE: --- Step 3: Stress Testing Projections ---;\n\n/* Clear any prior stress results */\nproc datasets lib=out nolist;\n    delete stress_results;\nrun; quit;\n\n%stress_testing(scenario=BASE);\n\n%if &syserr > 0 %then %do;\n    %let reg_rc = 6;\n    %put ERROR: Stress testing failed for BASE scenario.;\n    %goto reg_exit;\n%end;\n\n%stress_testing(scenario=ADVERSE);\n\n%if &syserr > 0 %then %do;\n    %let reg_rc = 7;\n    %put ERROR: Stress testing failed for ADVERSE scenario.;\n    %goto reg_exit;\n%end;\n\n%stress_testing(scenario=SEVERELY_ADVERSE);\n\n%if &syserr > 0 %then %do;\n    %let reg_rc = 8;\n    %put ERROR: Stress testing failed for SEVERELY_ADVERSE scenario.;\n    %goto reg_exit;\n%end;\n\n%put NOTE: Step 3 complete \u2014 All 3 stress scenarios projected.;\n\n/* ----- Step 4: Generate Filing Schedules ----- */\n%put NOTE: --- Step 4: Filing Schedule Generation ---;\n\n%generate_schedules;\n\n%if &syserr > 0 %then %do;\n    %let reg_rc = 9;\n    %put ERROR: Schedule generation failed.;\n    %goto reg_exit;\n%end;\n\n%put NOTE: Step 4 complete \u2014 All schedules generated.;\n\n/* ----- Step 5: Cover Page Summary ----- */\n%put NOTE: --- Step 5: Cover Page and Validation ---;\n\nproc sql;\n    create table out.filing_cover_page as\n    select\n        "&run_id"                            as run_id,\n        "&filing_year Q&filing_quarter"      as filing_period,\n        "&report_date"d                      as report_date format=date9.,\n        &total_rwa                           as total_rwa format=comma20.2,\n        input("&cet1_ratio", best8.)         as cet1_ratio format=percent8.4,\n        input("&tier1_ratio", best8.)        as tier1_ratio format=percent8.4,\n        input("&total_ratio", best8.)        as total_ratio format=percent8.4,\n        (select count(*) from out.rwa_summary) as n_exposures,\n        &n_schedules                         as schedules_filed,\n        datetime()                           as filing_timestamp\n            format=datetime20.,\n        'PENDING_REVIEW'                     as filing_status length=20\n    from fin.balance_sheet(obs=1);  /* single-row driver table */\nquit;\n\n/* ----- Step 6: Validation Reports ----- */\n\n/* Print capital ratio validation */\nproc print data=out.capital_ratios noobs label;\n    title 'Regulatory Filing Validation: Capital Adequacy Ratios';\n    title2 "Filing Period: &filing_year Q&filing_quarter";\n    var ratio_name ratio_value regulatory_min surplus_deficit breach_flag;\n    format ratio_value regulatory_min surplus_deficit percent8.4;\nrun;\n\n/* Print RWA validation by asset class */\nproc print data=out.rwa_summary noobs label;\n    title 'Regulatory Filing Validation: RWA by Asset Class';\n    title2 "Filing Period: &filing_year Q&filing_quarter";\n    var asset_class approach n_exposures total_exposure total_rwa\n        avg_risk_weight;\nrun;\n\n/* Print stress testing worst-case ratios */\nproc print data=out.stress_results noobs label;\n    title 'Regulatory Filing Validation: Stress Test Projections';\n    title2 "Filing Period: &filing_year Q&filing_quarter";\n    var scenario_name total_exposure base_capital min_capital min_ratio\n        gdp_impact unemp_impact;\n    format min_ratio percent8.4 gdp_impact unemp_impact percent6.2;\nrun;\ntitle;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 8: Error Handling and Completion                                   */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n%reg_exit:\n\n%if &reg_rc ne 0 %then %do;\n    %put ERROR: ============================================================;\n    %put ERROR: REGULATORY FILING PIPELINE FAILED;\n    %put ERROR: Return code = &reg_rc;\n    %put ERROR: Run ID = &run_id;\n    %put ERROR: ============================================================;\n\n    /* Log error details for audit trail */\n    data out.filing_errors;\n        length run_id $40 error_step $60 error_code 8\n               error_message $200 error_datetime 8;\n        run_id         = "&run_id";\n        error_step     = "Step failed at reg_rc=&reg_rc";\n        error_code     = &reg_rc;\n        error_message  = "Pipeline terminated \u2014 check SAS log for details.";\n        error_datetime = datetime();\n        format error_datetime datetime20.;\n    run;\n%end;\n\n%else %do;\n    %put NOTE: ============================================================;\n    %put NOTE: REGULATORY FILING PIPELINE COMPLETE;\n    %put NOTE: Run ID        : &run_id;\n    %put NOTE: Report Date   : &report_date;\n    %put NOTE: Filing Period  : &filing_year Q&filing_quarter;\n    %put NOTE: CET1 Ratio    : &cet1_ratio;\n    %put NOTE: Tier1 Ratio   : &tier1_ratio;\n    %put NOTE: Total Ratio   : &total_ratio;\n    %put NOTE: Schedules Filed: &n_schedules;\n    %put NOTE: ============================================================;\n\n    /* Write completion flag for downstream systems */\n    data _null_;\n        file '/regulatory/output/filing/completion_flag.txt';\n        put "RUN_ID=&run_id";\n        put "STATUS=SUCCESS";\n        put "FILING_PERIOD=&filing_year.Q&filing_quarter";\n        put "CET1_RATIO=&cet1_ratio";\n        put "TIER1_RATIO=&tier1_ratio";\n        put "TOTAL_RATIO=&total_ratio";\n        put "SCHEDULES_FILED=&n_schedules";\n        put "COMPLETION_TIME=" datetime() datetime20.;\n    run;\n%end;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 9: Cleanup                                                        */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n/* Delete intermediate work datasets */\nproc datasets lib=work nolist;\n    delete rwa_corporate_std rwa_corporate_irb rwa_corporate_summary\n           rwa_sovereign_std rwa_sovereign_irb rwa_sovereign_summary\n           rwa_retail_std rwa_retail_irb rwa_retail_summary\n           rwa_equity_std rwa_equity_irb rwa_equity_summary\n           stress_base stress_adverse stress_severely_adverse;\nrun; quit;\n\n/* Reset options to defaults */\noptions nomprint nomlogic nosymbolgen;\n\n%put NOTE: Regulatory filing pipeline ended at %sysfunc(datetime(), datetime20.);\n%put NOTE: Final return code = &reg_rc;\n-gold_meta20]

[SKIP] test_no_lineage_violations_in_deterministic[gsh_07_migration_suite.sas-/******************************************************************************\n * Program Name : gsh_07_migration_suite.sas\n * Author       : Data Migration Team \u2014 Enterprise Systems Division\n * Created      : 2026-01-20\n * Modified     : 2026-02-19\n * Purpose      : Data migration validation suite for legacy-to-new-platform\n *                migration. Performs count reconciliation, column-level\n *                comparison, amount tolerance checking, referential integrity\n *                validation, and generates a comprehensive reconciliation\n *                report with overall migration quality score.\n * Migration    : Phase 3 \u2014 Data Validation & Reconciliation\n * Dependencies : migration_utilities.sas\n * Frequency    : On-demand during migration windows\n * Change Log   :\n *   2026-01-20  v1.0  Initial development                    (R. Torres)\n *   2026-02-05  v1.1  Added column-level comparison macro     (L. Nguyen)\n *   2026-02-12  v1.2  Enhanced tolerance logic and reporting  (R. Torres)\n *   2026-02-19  v1.3  Added referential integrity checks      (M. Khan)\n ******************************************************************************/\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 1: Environment Setup \u2014 Libraries, Options, Parameters             */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\noptions mprint mlogic symbolgen nocenter ls=200 ps=65\n        validvarname=v7 nofmterr;\n\n/* --- Library references for migration layers --- */\nlibname legacy   '/data/migration/legacy'    access=readonly;\nlibname target   '/data/migration/target';\nlibname reconcil '/data/migration/reconcile';\nlibname work     '%sysfunc(pathname(work))';\n\n/* --- Global migration parameters --- */\n%let batch_id      = MIG-2026-0219-001;\n%let run_date      = %sysfunc(today(), yymmdd10.);\n%let run_timestamp = %sysfunc(datetime(), datetime20.);\n%let tolerance     = 0.01;\n%let tol_type      = PCT;    /* ABS = absolute, PCT = percentage */\n%let migration_rc  = 0;\n%let total_checks  = 0;\n%let passed_checks = 0;\n%let failed_checks = 0;\n%let n_tables      = 8;\n%let n_crit_tables = 4;\n%let n_fin_tables  = 3;\n%let n_fk_pairs    = 5;\n\n/* --- Table lists for batch processing --- */\n%let table1 = customers;\n%let table2 = accounts;\n%let table3 = transactions;\n%let table4 = products;\n%let table5 = orders;\n%let table6 = order_lines;\n%let table7 = suppliers;\n%let table8 = employees;\n\n/* Include shared migration utilities */\n%include '/migration/common/migration_utilities.sas';\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 2: Macro Definitions                                              */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: count_reconcile                                                  */\n/*   Compares row counts between legacy source and migration target for    */\n/*   a given table. Logs pass/fail result to reconcil.count_results.       */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro count_reconcile(table=, lib_src=legacy, lib_tgt=target);\n    %local src_count tgt_count diff pct_diff status;\n\n    %put NOTE: ===== Count Reconciliation: &table =====;\n\n    /* Get row count from the legacy source system */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select count(*) into :src_count trimmed\n        from &lib_src..&table;\n    quit;\n\n    /* Get row count from the migration target system */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select count(*) into :tgt_count trimmed\n        from &lib_tgt..&table;\n    quit;\n\n    /* Compute difference and determine pass/fail status */\n    data reconcil.count_result_&table;\n        length table_name $32 status $10 batch_id $30 check_type $10\n               run_date $10;\n        table_name = "&table";\n        batch_id   = "&batch_id";\n        src_count  = &src_count;\n        tgt_count  = &tgt_count;\n        diff       = tgt_count - src_count;\n\n        /* Calculate percentage difference */\n        if src_count > 0 then\n            pct_diff = abs(diff) / src_count * 100;\n        else\n            pct_diff = 0;\n\n        /* Assess result: counts must match exactly */\n        if diff = 0 then do;\n            status = 'PASS';\n            call symputx('passed_checks',\n                         input(symget('passed_checks'), best.) + 1);\n        end;\n        else do;\n            status = 'FAIL';\n            call symputx('failed_checks',\n                         input(symget('failed_checks'), best.) + 1);\n        end;\n\n        check_type = 'COUNT';\n        run_date   = "&run_date";\n        output;\n    run;\n\n    /* Increment global counter */\n    %let total_checks = %eval(&total_checks + 1);\n\n    %put NOTE: &table \u2014 Source: &src_count | Target: &tgt_count | Diff: %eval(&tgt_count - &src_count);\n%mend count_reconcile;\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: column_compare                                                   */\n/*   Performs column-level comparison between legacy and target tables.     */\n/*   Joins on key columns and checks each compare column for value         */\n/*   mismatches. Identifies records missing in source, missing in target,  */\n/*   and individual column differences.                                    */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro column_compare(table=, key_cols=, compare_cols=, lib_src=legacy);\n    %local n_comp i col join_cond n_keys kcol n_mismatches;\n\n    %put NOTE: ===== Column Comparison: &table =====;\n\n    /* Count the number of columns to compare */\n    %let n_comp = %sysfunc(countw(&compare_cols, %str( )));\n    %let n_keys = %sysfunc(countw(&key_cols, %str( )));\n\n    /* Build the join condition dynamically from key columns */\n    %let join_cond = ;\n    %do i = 1 %to &n_keys;\n        %let kcol = %scan(&key_cols, &i, %str( ));\n        %if &i > 1 %then %let join_cond = &join_cond AND;\n        %let join_cond = &join_cond s.&kcol = t.&kcol;\n    %end;\n\n    /* Full outer join of source and target on key columns */\n    proc sql;\n        create table work._compare_&table as\n        select\n            coalesce(s.%scan(&key_cols, 1, %str( )),\n                     t.%scan(&key_cols, 1, %str( ))) as key_value,\n            case\n                when s.%scan(&key_cols, 1, %str( )) is null\n                    then 'MISSING_IN_SOURCE'\n                when t.%scan(&key_cols, 1, %str( )) is null\n                    then 'MISSING_IN_TARGET'\n                else 'MATCHED'\n            end as match_status length=20,\n            /* Select source and target versions of each compare column */\n            %do i = 1 %to &n_comp;\n                %let col = %scan(&compare_cols, &i, %str( ));\n                s.&col as src_&col,\n                t.&col as tgt_&col\n                %if &i < &n_comp %then ,;\n            %end;\n        from &lib_src..&table s\n        full outer join target.&table t\n        on &join_cond;\n    quit;\n\n    /* Analyze each compare column for mismatches using arrays */\n    data reconcil.col_compare_&table(drop=i _mismatch_flag);\n        set work._compare_&table;\n\n        length column_name $32 mismatch_detail $200 table_name $32\n               batch_id $30 check_type $10 run_date $10;\n\n        /* Arrays for paired source/target column values */\n        array _src(*) src_:;\n        array _tgt(*) tgt_:;\n\n        table_name = "&table";\n        batch_id   = "&batch_id";\n        check_type = 'COLUMN';\n        run_date   = "&run_date";\n\n        /* Handle records missing from one system */\n        if match_status = 'MISSING_IN_SOURCE' then do;\n            column_name     = '_KEY_';\n            mismatch_detail = 'Record exists in target but not in source';\n            output;\n        end;\n        else if match_status = 'MISSING_IN_TARGET' then do;\n            column_name     = '_KEY_';\n            mismatch_detail = 'Record exists in source but not in target';\n            output;\n        end;\n        else do;\n            /* Row matched on keys \u2014 compare each column value */\n            _mismatch_flag = 0;\n            do i = 1 to dim(_src);\n                if _src(i) ne _tgt(i) then do;\n                    column_name = vname(_src(i));\n                    mismatch_detail = catx(' | ',\n                        'Source=' || strip(put(_src(i), best32.)),\n                        'Target=' || strip(put(_tgt(i), best32.)));\n                    _mismatch_flag = 1;\n                    output;\n                end;\n            end;\n\n            /* If no column mismatches, record a PASS entry */\n            if _mismatch_flag = 0 then do;\n                column_name     = '_ALL_';\n                mismatch_detail = 'All columns match';\n                output;\n            end;\n        end;\n    run;\n\n    /* Summarize mismatch count for this table */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select count(*) into :n_mismatches trimmed\n        from reconcil.col_compare_&table\n        where column_name ne '_ALL_'\n          and mismatch_detail not contains 'All columns match';\n    quit;\n\n    /* Update global check counters */\n    %let total_checks = %eval(&total_checks + 1);\n\n    %if &n_mismatches > 0 %then %do;\n        %put WARNING: &table \u2014 &n_mismatches column-level mismatches detected.;\n        %let failed_checks = %eval(&failed_checks + 1);\n    %end;\n    %else %do;\n        %put NOTE: &table \u2014 All compared columns match.;\n        %let passed_checks = %eval(&passed_checks + 1);\n    %end;\n%mend column_compare;\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: sum_reconcile                                                    */\n/*   Reconciles summed amounts between legacy and target systems by key    */\n/*   grouping. Supports absolute and percentage tolerance thresholds.      */\n/*   Results are logged with breach details per amount column.             */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro sum_reconcile(table=, key_cols=, amount_cols=, tolerance=&tolerance);\n    %local n_amt i acol join_cond n_keys kcol n_breaches;\n\n    %put NOTE: ===== Sum Reconciliation: &table =====;\n\n    %let n_amt  = %sysfunc(countw(&amount_cols, %str( )));\n    %let n_keys = %sysfunc(countw(&key_cols, %str( )));\n\n    /* Build join condition from key columns */\n    %let join_cond = ;\n    %do i = 1 %to &n_keys;\n        %let kcol = %scan(&key_cols, &i, %str( ));\n        %if &i > 1 %then %let join_cond = &join_cond AND;\n        %let join_cond = &join_cond s.&kcol = t.&kcol;\n    %end;\n\n    /* Aggregate amounts by key from the legacy source */\n    proc sql;\n        create table work._sum_src_&table as\n        select\n            %do i = 1 %to &n_keys;\n                %scan(&key_cols, &i, %str( )),\n            %end;\n            %do i = 1 %to &n_amt;\n                %let acol = %scan(&amount_cols, &i, %str( ));\n                sum(&acol) as &acol format=comma20.2\n                %if &i < &n_amt %then ,;\n            %end;\n        from legacy.&table\n        group by\n            %do i = 1 %to &n_keys;\n                %scan(&key_cols, &i, %str( ))\n                %if &i < &n_keys %then ,;\n            %end;\n        ;\n    quit;\n\n    /* Aggregate amounts by key from the migration target */\n    proc sql;\n        create table work._sum_tgt_&table as\n        select\n            %do i = 1 %to &n_keys;\n                %scan(&key_cols, &i, %str( )),\n            %end;\n            %do i = 1 %to &n_amt;\n                %let acol = %scan(&amount_cols, &i, %str( ));\n                sum(&acol) as &acol format=comma20.2\n                %if &i < &n_amt %then ,;\n            %end;\n        from target.&table\n        group by\n            %do i = 1 %to &n_keys;\n                %scan(&key_cols, &i, %str( ))\n                %if &i < &n_keys %then ,;\n            %end;\n        ;\n    quit;\n\n    /* Merge source and target summaries and compare amounts */\n    data reconcil.sum_result_&table;\n        merge work._sum_src_&table(rename=(\n                %do i = 1 %to &n_amt;\n                    %let acol = %scan(&amount_cols, &i, %str( ));\n                    &acol = src_&acol\n                %end;\n              ))\n              work._sum_tgt_&table(rename=(\n                %do i = 1 %to &n_amt;\n                    %let acol = %scan(&amount_cols, &i, %str( ));\n                    &acol = tgt_&acol\n                %end;\n              ));\n        by %do i = 1 %to &n_keys;\n               %scan(&key_cols, &i, %str( ))\n           %end;\n        ;\n\n        length status $10 breach_detail $200 table_name $32\n               batch_id $30 check_type $10 run_date $10;\n\n        table_name = "&table";\n        batch_id   = "&batch_id";\n        check_type = 'SUM';\n        run_date   = "&run_date";\n        tolerance  = &tolerance;\n\n        /* Compute difference for each amount column */\n        %do i = 1 %to &n_amt;\n            %let acol = %scan(&amount_cols, &i, %str( ));\n\n            diff_&acol = coalesce(tgt_&acol, 0)\n                       - coalesce(src_&acol, 0);\n\n            /* Apply tolerance check based on configured tolerance type */\n            %if &tol_type = PCT %then %do;\n                /* Percentage tolerance mode */\n                if coalesce(src_&acol, 0) ne 0 then\n                    pct_diff_&acol = abs(diff_&acol)\n                                   / abs(src_&acol) * 100;\n                else\n                    pct_diff_&acol = 0;\n\n                if pct_diff_&acol > &tolerance * 100 then do;\n                    status = 'FAIL';\n                    breach_detail = catx(' ',\n                        "&acol breach:",\n                        strip(put(pct_diff_&acol, 8.4)) || '%',\n                        '> threshold',\n                        strip(put(&tolerance * 100, 8.4)) || '%');\n                end;\n            %end;\n            %else %do;\n                /* Absolute tolerance mode */\n                if abs(diff_&acol) > &tolerance then do;\n                    status = 'FAIL';\n                    breach_detail = catx(' ',\n                        "&acol breach:",\n                        strip(put(abs(diff_&acol), comma20.2)),\n                        '> threshold',\n                        strip(put(&tolerance, comma20.2)));\n                end;\n            %end;\n        %end;\n\n        /* Default to PASS if no breach was flagged */\n        if status = ' ' then status = 'PASS';\n    run;\n\n    /* Count tolerance breaches */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select count(*) into :n_breaches trimmed\n        from reconcil.sum_result_&table\n        where status = 'FAIL';\n    quit;\n\n    /* Update global counters */\n    %let total_checks = %eval(&total_checks + 1);\n\n    %if &n_breaches > 0 %then %do;\n        %put WARNING: &table \u2014 &n_breaches tolerance breaches found.;\n        %let failed_checks = %eval(&failed_checks + 1);\n    %end;\n    %else %do;\n        %put NOTE: &table \u2014 All amounts within tolerance (&tol_type=&tolerance).;\n        %let passed_checks = %eval(&passed_checks + 1);\n    %end;\n%mend sum_reconcile;\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: referential_integrity                                            */\n/*   Validates foreign key relationships by identifying orphaned records   */\n/*   in the target system where child FK values have no matching parent    */\n/*   record. Logs count and pass/fail status.                              */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro referential_integrity(parent_table=, child_table=, fk_col=);\n    %local n_orphans;\n\n    %put NOTE: ===== Referential Integrity: &child_table -> &parent_table (&fk_col) =====;\n\n    /* Identify orphaned child records with no matching parent */\n    proc sql;\n        create table reconcil.orphans_&child_table._&fk_col as\n        select c.*\n        from target.&child_table c\n        left join target.&parent_table p\n        on c.&fk_col = p.&fk_col\n        where p.&fk_col is null\n          and c.&fk_col is not null;\n\n        /* Capture orphan count into macro variable */\n        select count(*) into :n_orphans trimmed\n        from reconcil.orphans_&child_table._&fk_col;\n    quit;\n\n    /* Log referential integrity result */\n    data reconcil.fk_result_&child_table._&fk_col;\n        length parent_table $32 child_table $32 fk_column $32\n               status $10 batch_id $30 check_type $10 run_date $10;\n\n        parent_table = "&parent_table";\n        child_table  = "&child_table";\n        fk_column    = "&fk_col";\n        orphan_count = &n_orphans;\n        batch_id     = "&batch_id";\n        check_type   = 'FK';\n        run_date     = "&run_date";\n\n        if orphan_count = 0 then do;\n            status = 'PASS';\n            call symputx('passed_checks',\n                         input(symget('passed_checks'), best.) + 1);\n        end;\n        else do;\n            status = 'FAIL';\n            call symputx('failed_checks',\n                         input(symget('failed_checks'), best.) + 1);\n        end;\n    run;\n\n    /* Increment global check counter */\n    %let total_checks = %eval(&total_checks + 1);\n\n    %if &n_orphans > 0 %then\n        %put WARNING: &child_table.&fk_col \u2014 &n_orphans orphaned records found.;\n    %else\n        %put NOTE: &child_table.&fk_col \u2014 Referential integrity intact.;\n%mend referential_integrity;\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: generate_reconciliation_report                                   */\n/*   Compiles all reconciliation results into a unified dashboard and      */\n/*   generates a formatted PDF report with overall migration quality       */\n/*   score, count details, and FK validation results.                      */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro generate_reconciliation_report;\n    %local i;\n\n    %put NOTE: ===== Generating Reconciliation Report =====;\n\n    /* --- Step 1: Merge all count reconciliation results --- */\n    data reconcil.all_count_results;\n        set\n        %do i = 1 %to &n_tables;\n            reconcil.count_result_&&table&i\n        %end;\n        ;\n    run;\n\n    /* --- Step 2: Merge all FK validation results --- */\n    data reconcil.all_fk_results;\n        length parent_table $32 child_table $32 fk_column $32\n               status $10 batch_id $30 check_type $10 run_date $10;\n        set\n            reconcil.fk_result_orders_customer_id\n            reconcil.fk_result_order_lines_order_id\n            reconcil.fk_result_order_lines_product_id\n            reconcil.fk_result_transactions_account_id\n            reconcil.fk_result_employees_dept_id\n        ;\n    run;\n\n    /* --- Step 3: Build summary dashboard via PROC SQL --- */\n    proc sql;\n        create table reconcil.migration_dashboard as\n        select\n            "&batch_id"     as batch_id length=30,\n            "&run_date"     as run_date length=10,\n            &total_checks   as total_checks,\n            &passed_checks  as passed_checks,\n            &failed_checks  as failed_checks,\n            case\n                when &total_checks > 0\n                    then &passed_checks / &total_checks * 100\n                else 0\n            end as pass_rate format=8.2,\n            case\n                when calculated pass_rate >= 100 then 'CERTIFIED'\n                when calculated pass_rate >= 95  then 'CONDITIONAL'\n                when calculated pass_rate >= 80  then 'REVIEW_REQUIRED'\n                else 'FAILED'\n            end as migration_status length=20\n        ;\n    quit;\n\n    /* --- Step 4: Compute overall migration quality score --- */\n    data reconcil.quality_score;\n        set reconcil.migration_dashboard;\n\n        /* Weighted scoring components */\n        count_weight  = 0.30;\n        column_weight = 0.30;\n        sum_weight    = 0.25;\n        fk_weight     = 0.15;\n\n        /* Use pass rate as the quality score basis */\n        quality_score = pass_rate;\n\n        /* Determine go-live recommendation */\n        length recommendation $100;\n        if quality_score >= 100 then\n            recommendation =\n                'Migration validated. Proceed to go-live.';\n        else if quality_score >= 95 then\n            recommendation =\n                'Minor issues found. Review and remediate before go-live.';\n        else if quality_score >= 80 then\n            recommendation =\n                'Significant issues. Re-run migration for affected tables.';\n        else\n            recommendation =\n                'Migration failed validation. Full re-migration required.';\n\n        batch_id  = "&batch_id";\n        run_date  = "&run_date";\n        scored_at = put(datetime(), datetime20.);\n    run;\n\n    /* --- Step 5: Generate formatted PDF report via ODS --- */\n    ods pdf file="/reports/migration/reconciliation_&batch_id..pdf"\n        style=journal;\n\n    ods proclabel 'Migration Reconciliation Summary';\n\n    title1 "Data Migration Reconciliation Report";\n    title2 "Batch: &batch_id | Date: &run_date";\n\n    /* Overall quality score and recommendation */\n    proc report data=reconcil.quality_score nowd;\n        columns batch_id run_date total_checks passed_checks\n                failed_checks pass_rate quality_score\n                migration_status recommendation;\n\n        define batch_id          / display 'Batch ID';\n        define run_date          / display 'Run Date';\n        define total_checks      / display 'Total Checks';\n        define passed_checks     / display 'Passed';\n        define failed_checks     / display 'Failed';\n        define pass_rate         / display 'Pass Rate (%)' format=8.2;\n        define quality_score     / display 'Quality Score' format=8.2;\n        define migration_status  / display 'Status';\n        define recommendation    / display 'Recommendation' width=50;\n\n        compute migration_status;\n            if migration_status = 'FAILED' then\n                call define(_col_, 'style',\n                    'style=[color=red font_weight=bold]');\n            else if migration_status = 'CERTIFIED' then\n                call define(_col_, 'style',\n                    'style=[color=green font_weight=bold]');\n        endcomp;\n    run;\n\n    /* Row count reconciliation detail */\n    title3 "Section 1: Row Count Reconciliation";\n\n    proc report data=reconcil.all_count_results nowd;\n        columns table_name src_count tgt_count diff pct_diff status;\n\n        define table_name / display 'Table';\n        define src_count  / display 'Source Rows' format=comma12.;\n        define tgt_count  / display 'Target Rows' format=comma12.;\n        define diff       / display 'Difference'  format=comma12.;\n        define pct_diff   / display '% Diff'      format=8.4;\n        define status     / display 'Status';\n\n        compute status;\n            if status = 'FAIL' then\n                call define(_row_, 'style',\n                    'style=[background=lightyellow]');\n        endcomp;\n    run;\n\n    /* Referential integrity detail */\n    title3 "Section 2: Referential Integrity Validation";\n\n    proc report data=reconcil.all_fk_results nowd;\n        columns parent_table child_table fk_column orphan_count status;\n\n        define parent_table  / display 'Parent Table';\n        define child_table   / display 'Child Table';\n        define fk_column     / display 'FK Column';\n        define orphan_count  / display 'Orphan Records' format=comma12.;\n        define status        / display 'Status';\n\n        compute status;\n            if status = 'FAIL' then\n                call define(_row_, 'style',\n                    'style=[background=lightyellow]');\n        endcomp;\n    run;\n\n    title;\n    ods pdf close;\n\n    %put NOTE: Reconciliation report saved to /reports/migration/;\n%mend generate_reconciliation_report;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 3: Main Program \u2014 Execute Migration Validation Suite              */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n%put NOTE: ============================================================;\n%put NOTE: Migration Validation Suite \u2014 Batch &batch_id;\n%put NOTE: Run Date: &run_date;\n%put NOTE: Tolerance: &tolerance (&tol_type);\n%put NOTE: ============================================================;\n\n/* ----- Phase 1: Row Count Reconciliation for all 8 tables ------------ */\n%put NOTE: --- Phase 1: Count Reconciliation ---;\n\n%macro run_count_checks;\n    %do i = 1 %to &n_tables;\n        %count_reconcile(table=&&table&i);\n\n        /* Abort on critical system errors */\n        %if &syserr > 4 %then %do;\n            %put ERROR: System error during count check for &&table&i..;\n            %let migration_rc = 1;\n        %end;\n    %end;\n%mend run_count_checks;\n\n%run_count_checks;\n\n/* ----- Phase 2: Column Comparison for 4 critical tables -------------- */\n%put NOTE: --- Phase 2: Column Comparison ---;\n\n%column_compare(table=customers,\n    key_cols=customer_id,\n    compare_cols=first_name last_name email phone status);\n\n%column_compare(table=accounts,\n    key_cols=account_id,\n    compare_cols=account_type balance open_date status);\n\n%column_compare(table=products,\n    key_cols=product_id,\n    compare_cols=product_name category unit_price active_flag);\n\n%column_compare(table=orders,\n    key_cols=order_id,\n    compare_cols=order_date customer_id total_amount status);\n\n/* ----- Phase 3: Sum Reconciliation for 3 financial tables ------------ */\n%put NOTE: --- Phase 3: Sum Reconciliation ---;\n\n%sum_reconcile(table=transactions,\n    key_cols=account_id,\n    amount_cols=debit_amount credit_amount balance,\n    tolerance=&tolerance);\n\n%sum_reconcile(table=orders,\n    key_cols=customer_id,\n    amount_cols=total_amount tax_amount discount_amount,\n    tolerance=&tolerance);\n\n%sum_reconcile(table=order_lines,\n    key_cols=order_id,\n    amount_cols=line_total unit_price,\n    tolerance=&tolerance);\n\n/* ----- Phase 4: Referential Integrity for 5 FK relationships --------- */\n%put NOTE: --- Phase 4: Referential Integrity ---;\n\n%referential_integrity(parent_table=customers,\n    child_table=orders,\n    fk_col=customer_id);\n\n%referential_integrity(parent_table=orders,\n    child_table=order_lines,\n    fk_col=order_id);\n\n%referential_integrity(parent_table=products,\n    child_table=order_lines,\n    fk_col=product_id);\n\n%referential_integrity(parent_table=accounts,\n    child_table=transactions,\n    fk_col=account_id);\n\n%referential_integrity(parent_table=departments,\n    child_table=employees,\n    fk_col=dept_id);\n\n/* ----- Phase 5: Generate Reconciliation Report ----------------------- */\n%put NOTE: --- Phase 5: Reconciliation Report ---;\n\n%if &migration_rc = 0 %then %do;\n    %generate_reconciliation_report;\n%end;\n%else %do;\n    %put ERROR: Skipping report generation due to earlier system errors.;\n    %put ERROR: Review log for details. Batch: &batch_id;\n%end;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 4: Final Status Logging and Cleanup                               */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n/* Persist final validation status to reconciliation library */\ndata reconcil.validation_log;\n    length batch_id $30 status $20 detail $200;\n    batch_id = "&batch_id";\n    run_date = "&run_date";\n    total    = &total_checks;\n    passed   = &passed_checks;\n    failed   = &failed_checks;\n\n    if failed = 0 then\n        status = 'ALL_PASSED';\n    else\n        status = 'HAS_FAILURES';\n\n    detail = catx(' ',\n        'Checks:', strip(put(total, best.)),\n        '| Passed:', strip(put(passed, best.)),\n        '| Failed:', strip(put(failed, best.)));\n\n    scored_ts = datetime();\n    format scored_ts datetime20.;\nrun;\n\n/* Clean up temporary work datasets */\nproc datasets lib=work nolist nowarn;\n    delete _compare_: _sum_src_: _sum_tgt_:;\nquit;\n\n/* ---- Final summary messages ---- */\n%put NOTE: ============================================================;\n%put NOTE: Migration Validation Complete;\n%put NOTE: Batch: &batch_id;\n%put NOTE: Total Checks: &total_checks;\n%put NOTE: Passed: &passed_checks | Failed: &failed_checks;\n%put NOTE: ============================================================;\n\n/* Reset debugging options */\noptions nomprint nomlogic nosymbolgen;\n-gold_meta21]

control flags --- */\n%let skip_validation = 0;\n%let enable_email    = 1;\n%let debug_mode      = 0;\n%let archive_input   = 1;\n\n/* --- File format specifications --- */\n%let csv_delimiter  = %str(,);\n%let csv_encoding   = utf-8;\n%let fixed_reclen   = 250;\n\n/* Include shared batch utility macros */\n%include '/batch/common/batch_utilities.sas';\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 2: Macro Definitions                                              */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: init_batch                                                       */\n/*   Initializes a new batch run by inserting a control record into the    */\n/*   batch_runs table, setting global tracking variables, and logging      */\n/*   the batch start event.                                                */\n/* Parameters:                                                             */\n/*   batch_id \u2014 unique identifier for this batch execution                 */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro init_batch(batch_id=);\n    %local start_ts n_pending;\n\n    %let start_ts = %sysfunc(datetime(), datetime20.);\n\n    %put NOTE: ============================================================;\n    %put NOTE: Batch Processing Framework \u2014 Initialization;\n    %put NOTE: Batch ID   : &batch_id;\n    %put NOTE: Start Time : &start_ts;\n    %put NOTE: Max Retries: &max_retries;\n    %put NOTE: ============================================================;\n\n    /* Insert batch run record into the control table */\n    proc sql;\n        insert into control.batch_runs\n            (batch_id, start_time, status, total_files,\n             files_processed, files_failed, operator, run_date)\n        values\n            ("&batch_id", %sysfunc(datetime()), "RUNNING", 0,\n             0, 0, "&sysuserid", %sysfunc(today()));\n    quit;\n\n    /* Count pending files in the file queue */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select count(*) into :n_pending trimmed\n        from control.file_queue\n        where batch_id = "&batch_id"\n          and status = 'PENDING';\n    quit;\n\n    /* Set global tracking variables */\n    %let total_files = &n_pending;\n    %let files_success = 0;\n    %let files_failed = 0;\n    %let batch_rc = 0;\n\n    %put NOTE: Found &n_pending files pending for batch &batch_id;\n\n    /* Log initialization event to the batch audit table */\n    data batchlog.init_log;\n        length batch_id $30 event $50 detail $200 timestamp 8;\n        format timestamp datetime20.;\n        batch_id  = "&batch_id";\n        event     = "BATCH_INITIALIZED";\n        detail    = "Pending files: &n_pending | Max retries: &max_retries";\n        timestamp = datetime();\n        output;\n    run;\n\n%mend init_batch;\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: process_file                                                     */\n/*   Processes a single input file based on its type (CSV, FIXED, SAS).    */\n/*   Performs validation checks on row count, required columns, and data    */\n/*   types. Uses CALL EXECUTE for dynamic processing based on content.     */\n/* Parameters:                                                             */\n/*   file_path \u2014 full filesystem path to the input file                    */\n/*   file_type \u2014 format identifier: CSV, FIXED, or SAS                    */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro process_file(file_path=, file_type=);\n    %local dsname obs_count retry_count file_rc col_list n_cols;\n\n    %let retry_count = 0;\n    %let file_rc = 0;\n    %let dsname = work._incoming_%sysfunc(monotonic());\n\n    %put NOTE: --- Processing file: &file_path (type=&file_type) ---;\n\n    /* ================================================================= */\n    /* FILE INGESTION \u2014 Branch by file type                               */\n    /* ================================================================= */\n\n    /* ---- CSV file ingestion ---- */\n    %if &file_type = CSV %then %do;\n        data &dsname;\n            length _line $2000 _field1-_field50 $200;\n\n            /* Read comma-delimited file, skip header row */\n            infile "&file_path"\n                delimiter="&csv_delimiter"\n                missover dsd lrecl=2000\n                firstobs=2\n                encoding="&csv_encoding"\n                end=_eof;\n\n            /* Read header row on first iteration to capture column names */\n            if _n_ = 1 then do;\n                infile "&file_path" obs=1;\n                input _header $2000.;\n                call symputx('col_list', _header);\n            end;\n\n            /* Parse each data row into field variables */\n            input _field1-_field50;\n\n            /* Track row number for audit purposes */\n            _row_num = _n_;\n\n            /* Generate dynamic post-processing code via CALL EXECUTE */\n            if _n_ = 1 then do;\n                call execute('data &dsname._final; set &dsname; ');\n                call execute('run;');\n            end;\n        run;\n    %end;\n\n    /* ---- Fixed-width file ingestion ---- */\n    %else %if &file_type = FIXED %then %do;\n        data &dsname;\n            /* Read fixed-format file with specified record length */\n            infile "&file_path"\n                recfm=f lrecl=&fixed_reclen\n                end=_eof;\n\n            /* Column-position INPUT for fixed-width record layout */\n            input\n                @1   record_type    $2.\n                @3   account_id     $12.\n                @15  customer_name  $40.\n                @55  transaction_dt  yymmdd10.\n                @65  amount          12.2\n                @77  currency_code  $3.\n                @80  status_code    $2.\n                @82  region_code    $5.\n                @87  product_id     $10.\n                @97  description    $100.\n                @197 filler         $54.\n            ;\n            format transaction_dt yymmdd10. amount comma12.2;\n\n            /* Skip header (HD) and trailer (TR) records */\n            if record_type not in ('HD', 'TR');\n            _row_num = _n_;\n        run;\n    %end;\n\n    /* ---- SAS dataset copy ---- */\n    %else %if &file_type = SAS %then %do;\n        /* Copy dataset from input library to work */\n        proc copy in=indata out=work;\n            select %scan(&file_path, -1, /);\n        run;\n\n        /* Rename copied dataset to standardized working name */\n        proc datasets lib=work nolist;\n            change %scan(&file_path, -1, /) = %scan(&dsname, 2, .);\n        quit;\n    %end;\n\n    /* ---- Unsupported file type \u2014 raise error ---- */\n    %else %do;\n        %put ERROR: Unsupported file type: &file_type;\n        %let file_rc = 1;\n        %error_handler(step=PROCESS_FILE, severity=CRITICAL);\n        %return;\n    %end;\n\n    /* ================================================================= */\n    /* POST-INGESTION VALIDATION                                          */\n    /* ================================================================= */\n\n    %if &file_rc = 0 %then %do;\n\n        /* --- Check 1: Row count validation --- */\n        proc sql noprint;\n            select count(*) into :obs_count trimmed\n            from &dsname;\n        quit;\n\n        %if &obs_count = 0 %then %do;\n            %put WARNING: File &file_path produced 0 observations.;\n            %let file_rc = 2;\n            %error_handler(step=ROW_COUNT_CHECK, severity=WARNING);\n        %end;\n        %else %do;\n            %put NOTE: File &file_path loaded &obs_count observations.;\n            %let total_rows_in = %eval(&total_rows_in + &obs_count);\n        %end;\n\n        /* --- Check 2: Required columns validation --- */\n        proc sql noprint;\n            select count(*) into :n_cols trimmed\n            from dictionary.columns\n            where libname = 'WORK'\n              and memname = "%upcase(%scan(&dsname, 2, .))";\n        quit;\n\n        %if &n_cols < 3 %then %do;\n            %put ERROR: File &file_path has insufficient columns (&n_cols).;\n            %let file_rc = 3;\n            %error_handler(step=COLUMN_CHECK, severity=CRITICAL);\n        %end;\n\n        /* --- Check 3: Duplicate key detection --- */\n        proc sort data=&dsname nodupkey\n                  dupout=work._dupes_%sysfunc(monotonic())\n                  out=&dsname._dedup;\n            by account_id;\n        run;\n\n        %if &syserr > 0 %then %do;\n            %put WARNING: Duplicate detection encountered issues for &file_path;\n            %error_handler(step=DEDUP_CHECK, severity=WARNING);\n        %end;\n\n    %end;\n\n    /* ================================================================= */\n    /* UPDATE FILE QUEUE STATUS                                           */\n    /* ================================================================= */\n\n    /* Mark file as processed or failed in the control table */\n    proc sql;\n        update control.file_queue\n        set status      = ifc(&file_rc = 0, 'PROCESSED', 'FAILED'),\n            processed_at = datetime(),\n            row_count    = &obs_count,\n            return_code  = &file_rc\n        where file_path = "&file_path"\n          and batch_id  = "&batch_id";\n    quit;\n\n    /* Track cumulative success/failure counts */\n    %if &file_rc = 0 %then %do;\n        %let files_success = %eval(&files_success + 1);\n    %end;\n    %else %do;\n        %let files_failed = %eval(&files_failed + 1);\n    %end;\n\n%mend process_file;\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: transform_data                                                   */\n/*   Applies transformation rules from a rules dataset to the input data.  */\n/*   Uses CALL EXECUTE to generate dynamic DATA step code. Iterates over   */\n/*   transformation categories with a %DO loop.                            */\n/* Parameters:                                                             */\n/*   input_ds \u2014 work dataset to transform                                  */\n/*   rules_ds \u2014 dataset containing transformation rules                    */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro transform_data(input_ds=, rules_ds=);\n    %local n_rules n_categories i cat_name transform_rc;\n\n    %let transform_rc = 0;\n\n    %put NOTE: --- Applying transformations from &rules_ds to &input_ds ---;\n\n    /* Count total active transformation rules */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select count(*) into :n_rules trimmed\n        from &rules_ds\n        where active_flag = 'Y';\n    quit;\n\n    %put NOTE: Found &n_rules active transformation rules.;\n\n    /* Get distinct transformation categories ordered by priority */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select count(distinct category) into :n_categories trimmed\n        from &rules_ds\n        where active_flag = 'Y';\n\n        select distinct category into :cat1 - :cat99\n        from &rules_ds\n        where active_flag = 'Y'\n        order by priority;\n    quit;\n\n    /* ---- Iterate over each transformation category ---- */\n    %do i = 1 %to &n_categories;\n        %let cat_name = &&cat&i;\n\n        %put NOTE: Processing transformation category: &cat_name (&i of &n_categories);\n\n        /* Read rules for this category and generate dynamic DATA step code */\n        data _null_;\n            set &rules_ds end=_last;\n            where category = "&cat_name" and active_flag = 'Y';\n\n            /* Build DATA step header on first observation */\n            if _n_ = 1 then do;\n                call execute("data &input_ds._&cat_name;");\n                call execute("  set &input_ds;");\n            end;\n\n            /* Apply transformation based on rule type */\n            length _code $500;\n            select (rule_type);\n                /* Column rename transformation */\n                when ('RENAME')\n                    _code = cat('rename ', strip(source_col),\n                                ' = ', strip(target_col), ';');\n                /* Derived column computation */\n                when ('DERIVE')\n                    _code = cat(strip(target_col), ' = ',\n                                strip(expression), ';');\n                /* Format assignment */\n                when ('FORMAT')\n                    _code = cat('format ', strip(source_col),\n                                ' ', strip(expression), ';');\n                /* Row filter condition */\n                when ('FILTER')\n                    _code = cat('if ', strip(expression), ';');\n                /* Value recoding */\n                when ('RECODE')\n                    _code = cat('if ', strip(source_col), ' = ',\n                                quote(strip(old_value)),\n                                ' then ', strip(target_col), ' = ',\n                                quote(strip(new_value)), ';');\n                otherwise\n                    _code = cat('/* Unknown rule type: ',\n                                strip(rule_type), ' */');\n            end;\n\n            call execute('  ' || strip(_code));\n\n            /* Close DATA step after last rule in this category */\n            if _last then do;\n                call execute('run;');\n            end;\n        run;\n\n        /* Verify the transformation produced output without errors */\n        %if &syserr > 4 %then %do;\n            %put ERROR: Transformation category &cat_name failed with SYSERR=&syserr;\n            %let transform_rc = 1;\n            %error_handler(step=TRANSFORM_&cat_name, severity=CRITICAL);\n            %goto transform_exit;\n        %end;\n\n        /* Replace input dataset with transformed version for next category */\n        proc datasets lib=work nolist;\n            delete %scan(&input_ds, 2, .);\n            change %scan(&input_ds, 2, .)._&cat_name\n                   = %scan(&input_ds, 2, .);\n        quit;\n\n    %end;\n\n    /* --- Post-transformation row count validation --- */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select count(*) into :post_count trimmed\n        from &input_ds;\n    quit;\n\n    %put NOTE: Post-transformation row count: &post_count;\n\n    %transform_exit:\n\n    /* Log transformation summary to audit table */\n    data batchlog.transform_log (keep=batch_id input_ds n_rules n_categories\n                                      transform_rc timestamp);\n        length batch_id $30 input_ds $50 timestamp 8;\n        format timestamp datetime20.;\n        batch_id     = "&batch_id";\n        input_ds     = "&input_ds";\n        n_rules      = &n_rules;\n        n_categories = &n_categories;\n        transform_rc = &transform_rc;\n        timestamp    = datetime();\n    run;\n\n%mend transform_data;\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: load_target                                                      */\n/*   Loads processed data into target tables. Supports FULL (truncate and  */\n/*   reload) and INCREMENTAL (upsert) load strategies. Validates row       */\n/*   counts post-load and logs the operation.                              */\n/* Parameters:                                                             */\n/*   source_ds    \u2014 work dataset containing data to load                   */\n/*   target_table \u2014 name of the target table in outdata library            */\n/*   load_type    \u2014 FULL (truncate+insert) or INCREMENTAL (upsert)         */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro load_target(source_ds=, target_table=, load_type=);\n    %local src_count tgt_before tgt_after load_rc delta;\n\n    %let load_rc = 0;\n\n    %put NOTE: --- Loading &source_ds into &target_table (mode=&load_type) ---;\n\n    /* Get source row count before load */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select count(*) into :src_count trimmed\n        from &source_ds;\n    quit;\n\n    /* Get target row count before load for delta calculation */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select count(*) into :tgt_before trimmed\n        from outdata.&target_table;\n    quit;\n\n    /* ---- FULL load: delete all existing rows and insert ---- */\n    %if &load_type = FULL %then %do;\n\n        %put NOTE: Executing FULL load \u2014 truncating &target_table;\n\n        /* Delete all existing rows from the target table */\n        proc sql;\n            delete from outdata.&target_table;\n        quit;\n\n        /* Insert all rows from source into target */\n        proc sql;\n            insert into outdata.&target_table\n            select * from &source_ds;\n        quit;\n\n        %if &syserr > 4 %then %do;\n            %put ERROR: FULL load failed for &target_table (SYSERR=&syserr);\n            %let load_rc = 1;\n            %error_handler(step=LOAD_FULL_&target_table, severity=CRITICAL);\n        %end;\n\n    %end;\n\n    /* ---- INCREMENTAL load: merge/upsert logic ---- */\n    %else %do;\n\n        %put NOTE: Executing INCREMENTAL load \u2014 upserting into &target_table;\n\n        /* Update existing records that match on primary key */\n        proc sql;\n            update outdata.&target_table as t\n            set t.last_updated = datetime()\n            where exists (\n                select 1 from &source_ds as s\n                where s.record_key = t.record_key\n            );\n        quit;\n\n        /* Insert new records that do not already exist in target */\n        proc sql;\n            insert into outdata.&target_table\n            select s.*, datetime() as loaded_at format=datetime20.\n            from &source_ds as s\n            where not exists (\n                select 1 from outdata.&target_table as t\n                where t.record_key = s.record_key\n            );\n        quit;\n\n        %if &syserr > 4 %then %do;\n            %put ERROR: INCREMENTAL load failed for &target_table (SYSERR=&syserr);\n            %let load_rc = 1;\n            %error_handler(step=LOAD_INCR_&target_table, severity=CRITICAL);\n        %end;\n\n    %end;\n\n    /* ---- Post-load row count validation ---- */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select count(*) into :tgt_after trimmed\n        from outdata.&target_table;\n    quit;\n\n    /* Calculate net change in target table */\n    %let delta = %eval(&tgt_after - &tgt_before);\n    %let total_rows_out = %eval(&total_rows_out + &delta);\n\n    %put NOTE: Load complete \u2014 Source: &src_count | Before: &tgt_before | After: &tgt_after | Delta: &delta;\n\n    /* Verify row count consistency for FULL loads */\n    %if &load_type = FULL and &tgt_after ne &src_count %then %do;\n        %put WARNING: Row count mismatch after FULL load. Expected &src_count, got &tgt_after;\n        %error_handler(step=LOAD_VALIDATE_&target_table, severity=WARNING);\n    %end;\n\n    /* Log load operation to audit table */\n    data batchlog.load_log (keep=batch_id target_table load_type\n                                 src_count tgt_before tgt_after\n                                 load_rc timestamp);\n        length batch_id $30 target_table $50 load_type $20 timestamp 8;\n        format timestamp datetime20.;\n        batch_id     = "&batch_id";\n        target_table = "&target_table";\n        load_type    = "&load_type";\n        src_count    = &src_count;\n        tgt_before   = &tgt_before;\n        tgt_after    = &tgt_after;\n        load_rc      = &load_rc;\n        timestamp    = datetime();\n    run;\n\n%mend load_target;\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: error_handler                                                    */\n/*   Centralized error handling macro. Logs error details to the audit     */\n/*   table, sends email notifications for critical errors, and propagates  */\n/*   error state via CALL SYMPUT for downstream decision-making.           */\n/* Parameters:                                                             */\n/*   step     \u2014 name of the processing step where error occurred           */\n/*   severity \u2014 CRITICAL, WARNING, or INFO                                 */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro error_handler(step=, severity=);\n    %local err_timestamp err_msg;\n\n    %let err_timestamp = %sysfunc(datetime(), datetime20.);\n\n    %put NOTE: *** Error Handler Invoked ***;\n    %put NOTE: Step     : &step;\n    %put NOTE: Severity : &severity;\n    %put NOTE: SYSERR   : &syserr;\n    %put NOTE: SYSRC    : &sysrc;\n\n    /* Log error details to the error audit table */\n    data batchlog.error_log;\n        length batch_id $30 step_name $50 severity $10\n               error_code 8 error_msg $500 timestamp 8;\n        format timestamp datetime20.;\n\n        batch_id   = "&batch_id";\n        step_name  = "&step";\n        severity   = "&severity";\n        error_code = &syserr;\n        error_msg  = symget('syserrortext');\n        timestamp  = datetime();\n\n        /* Propagate error state to global macro scope */\n        call symputx('batch_rc', 1, 'G');\n        call symputx('last_error_step', "&step", 'G');\n        call symputx('last_error_time', put(datetime(), datetime20.), 'G');\n    run;\n\n    /* ---- Handle CRITICAL severity: email notification ---- */\n    %if &severity = CRITICAL %then %do;\n\n        %put ERROR: CRITICAL error in step &step \u2014 sending notification.;\n\n        /* Define email fileref for notification */\n        filename email_out email\n            to=("&email_list")\n            subject="CRITICAL: Batch &batch_id failed at step &step"\n            type="text/plain";\n\n        /* Write structured error notification email */\n        data _null_;\n            file email_out;\n            put "====================================================";\n            put "BATCH PROCESSING ALERT \u2014 CRITICAL ERROR";\n            put "====================================================";\n            put " ";\n            put "Batch ID    : &batch_id";\n            put "Failed Step : &step";\n            put "Severity    : &severity";\n            put "Error Code  : &syserr";\n            put "Timestamp   : &err_timestamp";\n            put " ";\n            put "Immediate attention required.";\n            put "Review the batch log for details.";\n            put " ";\n            put "====================================================";\n        run;\n\n        /* Release email fileref */\n        filename email_out clear;\n\n        /* Set batch return code to critical failure level */\n        %let batch_rc = 2;\n\n    %end;\n\n    /* ---- Handle WARNING severity: log and continue ---- */\n    %else %if &severity = WARNING %then %do;\n\n        %put WARNING: Non-critical issue in step &step \u2014 continuing batch.;\n\n        /* Append warning record to the warnings summary table */\n        proc sql;\n            insert into control.batch_warnings\n                (batch_id, step_name, warning_msg, warning_time)\n            values\n                ("&batch_id", "&step",\n                 "Warning encountered \u2014 see log for details",\n                 %sysfunc(datetime()));\n        quit;\n\n    %end;\n\n    /* ---- Handle INFO severity: log informational note ---- */\n    %else %do;\n\n        %put NOTE: Informational event logged for step &step;\n\n    %end;\n\n%mend error_handler;\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: finalize_batch                                                   */\n/*   Finalizes the batch run by updating the control table with end time,  */\n/*   final status, and summary metrics. Generates a batch summary dataset  */\n/*   and optionally archives processed input files.                        */\n/* Parameters:                                                             */\n/*   batch_id \u2014 unique batch identifier to finalize                        */\n/*   status   \u2014 final status: SUCCESS, COMPLETED_WITH_ERRORS, or FAILED   */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro finalize_batch(batch_id=, status=);\n    %local end_ts elapsed_sec elapsed_min;\n\n    %let end_ts = %sysfunc(datetime());\n    %let elapsed_sec = %sysevalf(&end_ts - &batch_start);\n    %let elapsed_min = %sysevalf(&elapsed_sec / 60, ceil);\n\n    %put NOTE: ============================================================;\n    %put NOTE: Batch Finalization \u2014 &batch_id;\n    %put NOTE: Status         : &status;\n    %put NOTE: Files OK       : &files_success of &total_files;\n    %put NOTE: Files Failed   : &files_failed;\n    %put NOTE: Elapsed Time   : &elapsed_min minutes;\n    %put NOTE: ============================================================;\n\n    /* Update batch control table with final status and metrics */\n    proc sql;\n        update control.batch_runs\n        set end_time        = &end_ts,\n            status          = "&status",\n            total_files     = &total_files,\n            files_processed = &files_success,\n            files_failed    = &files_failed,\n            rows_in         = &total_rows_in,\n            rows_out        = &total_rows_out,\n            elapsed_seconds = &elapsed_sec,\n            return_code     = &batch_rc\n        where batch_id = "&batch_id";\n    quit;\n\n    /* Generate batch summary dataset for reporting */\n    data batchlog.batch_summary;\n        length batch_id $30 status $20 detail $500 timestamp 8;\n        format timestamp datetime20. batch_start_dt datetime20.\n               batch_end_dt datetime20.;\n\n        batch_id       = "&batch_id";\n        status         = "&status";\n        total_files    = &total_files;\n        files_success  = &files_success;\n        files_failed   = &files_failed;\n        total_rows_in  = &total_rows_in;\n        total_rows_out = &total_rows_out;\n        batch_start_dt = &batch_start;\n        batch_end_dt   = &end_ts;\n        elapsed_min    = &elapsed_min;\n\n        /* Build human-readable summary detail string */\n        detail = catx(' | ',\n            cat('Files: ', put(total_files, 8. -l)),\n            cat('OK: ', put(files_success, 8. -l)),\n            cat('Fail: ', put(files_failed, 8. -l)),\n            cat('Rows In: ', put(total_rows_in, comma12. -l)),\n            cat('Rows Out: ', put(total_rows_out, comma12. -l)),\n            cat('Duration: ', put(elapsed_min, 8. -l), ' min')\n        );\n\n        timestamp = datetime();\n        output;\n    run;\n\n    /* Archive processed input files if archiving is enabled */\n    %if &archive_input = 1 %then %do;\n        %put NOTE: Archiving processed input files...;\n\n        data _null_;\n            set control.file_queue;\n            where batch_id = "&batch_id" and status = 'PROCESSED';\n\n            /* Build archive path and move file via system command */\n            length archive_path $500 cmd $1000;\n            archive_path = cats('/batch/archive/', "&batch_id", '/',\n                               scan(file_path, -1, '/'));\n            cmd = catx(' ', 'mv', strip(file_path), strip(archive_path));\n            call system(cmd);\n        run;\n    %end;\n\n%mend finalize_batch;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 3: Main Program Execution                                         */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n%put NOTE: ================================================================;\n%put NOTE: BATCH PROCESSING FRAMEWORK \u2014 MAIN EXECUTION;\n%put NOTE: Batch ID : &batch_id;\n%put NOTE: Run Date : &run_date;\n%put NOTE: ================================================================;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Step 1: Initialize the batch run                                      */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%init_batch(batch_id=&batch_id);\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Step 2: Read file list from control table and process each file       */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro run_batch_files;\n    %local i file_path_i file_type_i n_files;\n\n    /* Retrieve list of pending files from the file queue */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select count(*) into :n_files trimmed\n        from control.file_queue\n        where batch_id = "&batch_id"\n          and status = 'PENDING';\n\n        select file_path, file_type\n        into :fp1 - :fp999, :ft1 - :ft999\n        from control.file_queue\n        where batch_id = "&batch_id"\n          and status = 'PENDING'\n        order by priority, file_id;\n    quit;\n\n    %put NOTE: Processing &n_files files in batch &batch_id;\n\n    /* --- Loop over each pending file in the queue --- */\n    %do i = 1 %to &n_files;\n        %let file_path_i = &&fp&i;\n        %let file_type_i = &&ft&i;\n\n        %put NOTE: ========================================================;\n        %put NOTE: File &i of &n_files: &file_path_i (&file_type_i);\n        %put NOTE: ========================================================;\n\n        /* Step 2a: Ingest and validate the source file */\n        %process_file(file_path=&file_path_i, file_type=&file_type_i);\n\n        /* Check for critical failure \u2014 abort batch if needed */\n        %if &batch_rc >= 2 %then %do;\n            %put ERROR: Critical failure detected \u2014 aborting batch.;\n            %goto batch_abort;\n        %end;\n\n        /* Step 2b: Apply transformation rules to ingested data */\n        %transform_data(\n            input_ds=work._incoming_%eval(&i),\n            rules_ds=control.transform_rules\n        );\n\n        /* Check for transformation failure */\n        %if &syserr > 4 %then %do;\n            %put ERROR: Transformation failed for file &file_path_i;\n            %error_handler(step=TRANSFORM_FILE_&i, severity=CRITICAL);\n            %goto batch_abort;\n        %end;\n\n        /* Step 2c: Load transformed data to target table */\n        %load_target(\n            source_ds=work._incoming_%eval(&i),\n            target_table=%scan(&file_path_i, -1, /.),\n            load_type=INCREMENTAL\n        );\n\n        /* Check for load failure */\n        %if &syserr > 4 %then %do;\n            %put ERROR: Load failed for file &file_path_i;\n            %error_handler(step=LOAD_FILE_&i, severity=CRITICAL);\n        %end;\n\n    %end;\n\n    %batch_abort:\n\n%mend run_batch_files;\n\n/* Execute the batch file processing loop */\n%run_batch_files;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Step 3: Determine final batch status based on results                 */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro set_final_status;\n    %global final_status;\n\n    %if &batch_rc >= 2 %then %do;\n        %let final_status = FAILED;\n    %end;\n    %else %if &files_failed > 0 %then %do;\n        %let final_status = COMPLETED_WITH_ERRORS;\n    %end;\n    %else %do;\n        %let final_status = SUCCESS;\n    %end;\n\n    %put NOTE: Final batch status: &final_status;\n%mend set_final_status;\n\n%set_final_status;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Step 4: Finalize batch and generate summary                           */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%finalize_batch(batch_id=&batch_id, status=&final_status);\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Step 5: Print batch summary report                                    */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ntitle1 "Batch Processing Summary Report";\ntitle2 "Batch ID: &batch_id | Date: &run_date";\n\nproc print data=batchlog.batch_summary noobs label;\n    var batch_id status total_files files_success files_failed\n        total_rows_in total_rows_out elapsed_min;\n    label batch_id       = 'Batch ID'\n          status         = 'Status'\n          total_files    = 'Total Files'\n          files_success  = 'Successful'\n          files_failed   = 'Failed'\n          total_rows_in  = 'Rows Ingested'\n          total_rows_out = 'Rows Loaded'\n          elapsed_min    = 'Duration (min)';\nrun;\n\ntitle;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Step 6: Print error summary report if errors occurred                  */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%if &files_failed > 0 or &batch_rc > 0 %then %do;\n\n    title1 "Batch Error Summary";\n    title2 "Batch ID: &batch_id";\n\n    proc print data=batchlog.error_log noobs;\n        where batch_id = "&batch_id";\n        var step_name severity error_code error_msg timestamp;\n    run;\n\n    title;\n\n%end;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Step 7: Cleanup temporary work datasets                               */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nproc datasets lib=work nolist nowarn;\n    delete _incoming_: _dupes_:;\nquit;\n\n/* --- Reset session options to defaults --- */\noptions nomprint nomlogic nosymbolgen;\n\n/* --- Final completion banner --- */\n%put NOTE: ================================================================;\n%put NOTE: Batch &batch_id completed with status: &final_status;\n%put NOTE: Files processed: &files_success of &total_files;\n%put NOTE: Files failed   : &files_failed;\n%put NOTE: Total rows in  : &total_rows_in;\n%put NOTE: Total rows out : &total_rows_out;\n%put NOTE: ================================================================;\n-gold_meta22]
