timestamp 8;\n        format timestamp datetime20.;\n        batch_id  = "&batch_id";\n        event     = "BATCH_INITIALIZED";\n        detail    = "Pending files: &n_pending | Max retries: &max_retries";\n        timestamp = datetime();\n        output;\n    run;\n\n%mend init_batch;\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: process_file                                                     */\n/*   Processes a single input file based on its type (CSV, FIXED, SAS).    */\n/*   Performs validation checks on row count, required columns, and data    */\n/*   types. Uses CALL EXECUTE for dynamic processing based on content.     */\n/* Parameters:                                                             */\n/*   file_path \u2014 full filesystem path to the input file                    */\n/*   file_type \u2014 format identifier: CSV, FIXED, or SAS                    */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro process_file(file_path=, file_type=);\n    %local dsname obs_count retry_count file_rc col_list n_cols;\n\n    %let retry_count = 0;\n    %let file_rc = 0;\n    %let dsname = work._incoming_%sysfunc(monotonic());\n\n    %put NOTE: --- Processing file: &file_path (type=&file_type) ---;\n\n    /* ================================================================= */\n    /* FILE INGESTION \u2014 Branch by file type                               */\n    /* ================================================================= */\n\n    /* ---- CSV file ingestion ---- */\n    %if &file_type = CSV %then %do;\n        data &dsname;\n            length _line $2000 _field1-_field50 $200;\n\n            /* Read comma-delimited file, skip header row */\n            infile "&file_path"\n                delimiter="&csv_delimiter"\n                missover dsd lrecl=2000\n                firstobs=2\n                encoding="&csv_encoding"\n                end=_eof;\n\n            /* Read header row on first iteration to capture column names */\n            if _n_ = 1 then do;\n                infile "&file_path" obs=1;\n                input _header $2000.;\n                call symputx('col_list', _header);\n            end;\n\n            /* Parse each data row into field variables */\n            input _field1-_field50;\n\n            /* Track row number for audit purposes */\n            _row_num = _n_;\n\n            /* Generate dynamic post-processing code via CALL EXECUTE */\n            if _n_ = 1 then do;\n                call execute('data &dsname._final; set &dsname; ');\n                call execute('run;');\n            end;\n        run;\n    %end;\n\n    /* ---- Fixed-width file ingestion ---- */\n    %else %if &file_type = FIXED %then %do;\n        data &dsname;\n            /* Read fixed-format file with specified record length */\n            infile "&file_path"\n                recfm=f lrecl=&fixed_reclen\n                end=_eof;\n\n            /* Column-position INPUT for fixed-width record layout */\n            input\n                @1   record_type    $2.\n                @3   account_id     $12.\n                @15  customer_name  $40.\n                @55  transaction_dt  yymmdd10.\n                @65  amount          12.2\n                @77  currency_code  $3.\n                @80  status_code    $2.\n                @82  region_code    $5.\n                @87  product_id     $10.\n                @97  description    $100.\n                @197 filler         $54.\n            ;\n            format transaction_dt yymmdd10. amount comma12.2;\n\n            /* Skip header (HD) and trailer (TR) records */\n            if record_type not in ('HD', 'TR');\n            _row_num = _n_;\n        run;\n    %end;\n\n    /* ---- SAS dataset copy ---- */\n    %else %if &file_type = SAS %then %do;\n        /* Copy dataset from input library to work */\n        proc copy in=indata out=work;\n            select %scan(&file_path, -1, /);\n        run;\n\n        /* Rename copied dataset to standardized working name */\n        proc datasets lib=work nolist;\n            change %scan(&file_path, -1, /) = %scan(&dsname, 2, .);\n        quit;\n    %end;\n\n    /* ---- Unsupported file type \u2014 raise error ---- */\n    %else %do;\n        %put ERROR: Unsupported file type: &file_type;\n        %let file_rc = 1;\n        %error_handler(step=PROCESS_FILE, severity=CRITICAL);\n        %return;\n    %end;\n\n    /* ================================================================= */\n    /* POST-INGESTION VALIDATION                                          */\n    /* ================================================================= */\n\n    %if &file_rc = 0 %then %do;\n\n        /* --- Check 1: Row count validation --- */\n        proc sql noprint;\n            select count(*) into :obs_count trimmed\n            from &dsname;\n        quit;\n\n        %if &obs_count = 0 %then %do;\n            %put WARNING: File &file_path produced 0 observations.;\n            %let file_rc = 2;\n            %error_handler(step=ROW_COUNT_CHECK, severity=WARNING);\n        %end;\n        %else %do;\n            %put NOTE: File &file_path loaded &obs_count observations.;\n            %let total_rows_in = %eval(&total_rows_in + &obs_count);\n        %end;\n\n        /* --- Check 2: Required columns validation --- */\n        proc sql noprint;\n            select count(*) into :n_cols trimmed\n            from dictionary.columns\n            where libname = 'WORK'\n              and memname = "%upcase(%scan(&dsname, 2, .))";\n        quit;\n\n        %if &n_cols < 3 %then %do;\n            %put ERROR: File &file_path has insufficient columns (&n_cols).;\n            %let file_rc = 3;\n            %error_handler(step=COLUMN_CHECK, severity=CRITICAL);\n        %end;\n\n        /* --- Check 3: Duplicate key detection --- */\n        proc sort data=&dsname nodupkey\n                  dupout=work._dupes_%sysfunc(monotonic())\n                  out=&dsname._dedup;\n            by account_id;\n        run;\n\n        %if &syserr > 0 %then %do;\n            %put WARNING: Duplicate detection encountered issues for &file_path;\n            %error_handler(step=DEDUP_CHECK, severity=WARNING);\n        %end;\n\n    %end;\n\n    /* ================================================================= */\n    /* UPDATE FILE QUEUE STATUS                                           */\n    /* ================================================================= */\n\n    /* Mark file as processed or failed in the control table */\n    proc sql;\n        update control.file_queue\n        set status      = ifc(&file_rc = 0, 'PROCESSED', 'FAILED'),\n            processed_at = datetime(),\n            row_count    = &obs_count,\n            return_code  = &file_rc\n        where file_path = "&file_path"\n          and batch_id  = "&batch_id";\n    quit;\n\n    /* Track cumulative success/failure counts */\n    %if &file_rc = 0 %then %do;\n        %let files_success = %eval(&files_success + 1);\n    %end;\n    %else %do;\n        %let files_failed = %eval(&files_failed + 1);\n    %end;\n\n%mend process_file;\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: transform_data                                                   */\n/*   Applies transformation rules from a rules dataset to the input data.  */\n/*   Uses CALL EXECUTE to generate dynamic DATA step code. Iterates over   */\n/*   transformation categories with a %DO loop.                            */\n/* Parameters:                                                             */\n/*   input_ds \u2014 work dataset to transform                                  */\n/*   rules_ds \u2014 dataset containing transformation rules                    */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro transform_data(input_ds=, rules_ds=);\n    %local n_rules n_categories i cat_name transform_rc;\n\n    %let transform_rc = 0;\n\n    %put NOTE: --- Applying transformations from &rules_ds to &input_ds ---;\n\n    /* Count total active transformation rules */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select count(*) into :n_rules trimmed\n        from &rules_ds\n        where active_flag = 'Y';\n    quit;\n\n    %put NOTE: Found &n_rules active transformation rules.;\n\n    /* Get distinct transformation categories ordered by priority */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select count(distinct category) into :n_categories trimmed\n        from &rules_ds\n        where active_flag = 'Y';\n\n        select distinct category into :cat1 - :cat99\n        from &rules_ds\n        where active_flag = 'Y'\n        order by priority;\n    quit;\n\n    /* ---- Iterate over each transformation category ---- */\n    %do i = 1 %to &n_categories;\n        %let cat_name = &&cat&i;\n\n        %put NOTE: Processing transformation category: &cat_name (&i of &n_categories);\n\n        /* Read rules for this category and generate dynamic DATA step code */\n        data _null_;\n            set &rules_ds end=_last;\n            where category = "&cat_name" and active_flag = 'Y';\n\n            /* Build DATA step header on first observation */\n            if _n_ = 1 then do;\n                call execute("data &input_ds._&cat_name;");\n                call execute("  set &input_ds;");\n            end;\n\n            /* Apply transformation based on rule type */\n            length _code $500;\n            select (rule_type);\n                /* Column rename transformation */\n                when ('RENAME')\n                    _code = cat('rename ', strip(source_col),\n                                ' = ', strip(target_col), ';');\n                /* Derived column computation */\n                when ('DERIVE')\n                    _code = cat(strip(target_col), ' = ',\n                                strip(expression), ';');\n                /* Format assignment */\n                when ('FORMAT')\n                    _code = cat('format ', strip(source_col),\n                                ' ', strip(expression), ';');\n                /* Row filter condition */\n                when ('FILTER')\n                    _code = cat('if ', strip(expression), ';');\n                /* Value recoding */\n                when ('RECODE')\n                    _code = cat('if ', strip(source_col), ' = ',\n                                quote(strip(old_value)),\n                                ' then ', strip(target_col), ' = ',\n                                quote(strip(new_value)), ';');\n                otherwise\n                    _code = cat('/* Unknown rule type: ',\n                                strip(rule_type), ' */');\n            end;\n\n            call execute('  ' || strip(_code));\n\n            /* Close DATA step after last rule in this category */\n            if _last then do;\n                call execute('run;');\n            end;\n        run;\n\n        /* Verify the transformation produced output without errors */\n        %if &syserr > 4 %then %do;\n            %put ERROR: Transformation category &cat_name failed with SYSERR=&syserr;\n            %let transform_rc = 1;\n            %error_handler(step=TRANSFORM_&cat_name, severity=CRITICAL);\n            %goto transform_exit;\n        %end;\n\n        /* Replace input dataset with transformed version for next category */\n        proc datasets lib=work nolist;\n            delete %scan(&input_ds, 2, .);\n            change %scan(&input_ds, 2, .)._&cat_name\n                   = %scan(&input_ds, 2, .);\n        quit;\n\n    %end;\n\n    /* --- Post-transformation row count validation --- */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select count(*) into :post_count trimmed\n        from &input_ds;\n    quit;\n\n    %put NOTE: Post-transformation row count: &post_count;\n\n    %transform_exit:\n\n    /* Log transformation summary to audit table */\n    data batchlog.transform_log (keep=batch_id input_ds n_rules n_categories\n                                      transform_rc timestamp);\n        length batch_id $30 input_ds $50 timestamp 8;\n        format timestamp datetime20.;\n        batch_id     = "&batch_id";\n        input_ds     = "&input_ds";\n        n_rules      = &n_rules;\n        n_categories = &n_categories;\n        transform_rc = &transform_rc;\n        timestamp    = datetime();\n    run;\n\n%mend transform_data;\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: load_target                                                      */\n/*   Loads processed data into target tables. Supports FULL (truncate and  */\n/*   reload) and INCREMENTAL (upsert) load strategies. Validates row       */\n/*   counts post-load and logs the operation.                              */\n/* Parameters:                                                             */\n/*   source_ds    \u2014 work dataset containing data to load                   */\n/*   target_table \u2014 name of the target table in outdata library            */\n/*   load_type    \u2014 FULL (truncate+insert) or INCREMENTAL (upsert)         */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro load_target(source_ds=, target_table=, load_type=);\n    %local src_count tgt_before tgt_after load_rc delta;\n\n    %let load_rc = 0;\n\n    %put NOTE: --- Loading &source_ds into &target_table (mode=&load_type) ---;\n\n    /* Get source row count before load */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select count(*) into :src_count trimmed\n        from &source_ds;\n    quit;\n\n    /* Get target row count before load for delta calculation */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select count(*) into :tgt_before trimmed\n        from outdata.&target_table;\n    quit;\n\n    /* ---- FULL load: delete all existing rows and insert ---- */\n    %if &load_type = FULL %then %do;\n\n        %put NOTE: Executing FULL load \u2014 truncating &target_table;\n\n        /* Delete all existing rows from the target table */\n        proc sql;\n            delete from outdata.&target_table;\n        quit;\n\n        /* Insert all rows from source into target */\n        proc sql;\n            insert into outdata.&target_table\n            select * from &source_ds;\n        quit;\n\n        %if &syserr > 4 %then %do;\n            %put ERROR: FULL load failed for &target_table (SYSERR=&syserr);\n            %let load_rc = 1;\n            %error_handler(step=LOAD_FULL_&target_table, severity=CRITICAL);\n        %end;\n\n    %end;\n\n    /* ---- INCREMENTAL load: merge/upsert logic ---- */\n    %else %do;\n\n        %put NOTE: Executing INCREMENTAL load \u2014 upserting into &target_table;\n\n        /* Update existing records that match on primary key */\n        proc sql;\n            update outdata.&target_table as t\n            set t.last_updated = datetime()\n            where exists (\n                select 1 from &source_ds as s\n                where s.record_key = t.record_key\n            );\n        quit;\n\n        /* Insert new records that do not already exist in target */\n        proc sql;\n            insert into outdata.&target_table\n            select s.*, datetime() as loaded_at format=datetime20.\n            from &source_ds as s\n            where not exists (\n                select 1 from outdata.&target_table as t\n                where t.record_key = s.record_key\n            );\n        quit;\n\n        %if &syserr > 4 %then %do;\n            %put ERROR: INCREMENTAL load failed for &target_table (SYSERR=&syserr);\n            %let load_rc = 1;\n            %error_handler(step=LOAD_INCR_&target_table, severity=CRITICAL);\n        %end;\n\n    %end;\n\n    /* ---- Post-load row count validation ---- */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select count(*) into :tgt_after trimmed\n        from outdata.&target_table;\n    quit;\n\n    /* Calculate net change in target table */\n    %let delta = %eval(&tgt_after - &tgt_before);\n    %let total_rows_out = %eval(&total_rows_out + &delta);\n\n    %put NOTE: Load complete \u2014 Source: &src_count | Before: &tgt_before | After: &tgt_after | Delta: &delta;\n\n    /* Verify row count consistency for FULL loads */\n    %if &load_type = FULL and &tgt_after ne &src_count %then %do;\n        %put WARNING: Row count mismatch after FULL load. Expected &src_count, got &tgt_after;\n        %error_handler(step=LOAD_VALIDATE_&target_table, severity=WARNING);\n    %end;\n\n    /* Log load operation to audit table */\n    data batchlog.load_log (keep=batch_id target_table load_type\n                                 src_count tgt_before tgt_after\n                                 load_rc timestamp);\n        length batch_id $30 target_table $50 load_type $20 timestamp 8;\n        format timestamp datetime20.;\n        batch_id     = "&batch_id";\n        target_table = "&target_table";\n        load_type    = "&load_type";\n        src_count    = &src_count;\n        tgt_before   = &tgt_before;\n        tgt_after    = &tgt_after;\n        load_rc      = &load_rc;\n        timestamp    = datetime();\n    run;\n\n%mend load_target;\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: error_handler                                                    */\n/*   Centralized error handling macro. Logs error details to the audit     */\n/*   table, sends email notifications for critical errors, and propagates  */\n/*   error state via CALL SYMPUT for downstream decision-making.           */\n/* Parameters:                                                             */\n/*   step     \u2014 name of the processing step where error occurred           */\n/*   severity \u2014 CRITICAL, WARNING, or INFO                                 */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro error_handler(step=, severity=);\n    %local err_timestamp err_msg;\n\n    %let err_timestamp = %sysfunc(datetime(), datetime20.);\n\n    %put NOTE: *** Error Handler Invoked ***;\n    %put NOTE: Step     : &step;\n    %put NOTE: Severity : &severity;\n    %put NOTE: SYSERR   : &syserr;\n    %put NOTE: SYSRC    : &sysrc;\n\n    /* Log error details to the error audit table */\n    data batchlog.error_log;\n        length batch_id $30 step_name $50 severity $10\n               error_code 8 error_msg $500 timestamp 8;\n        format timestamp datetime20.;\n\n        batch_id   = "&batch_id";\n        step_name  = "&step";\n        severity   = "&severity";\n        error_code = &syserr;\n        error_msg  = symget('syserrortext');\n        timestamp  = datetime();\n\n        /* Propagate error state to global macro scope */\n        call symputx('batch_rc', 1, 'G');\n        call symputx('last_error_step', "&step", 'G');\n        call symputx('last_error_time', put(datetime(), datetime20.), 'G');\n    run;\n\n    /* ---- Handle CRITICAL severity: email notification ---- */\n    %if &severity = CRITICAL %then %do;\n\n        %put ERROR: CRITICAL error in step &step \u2014 sending notification.;\n\n        /* Define email fileref for notification */\n        filename email_out email\n            to=("&email_list")\n            subject="CRITICAL: Batch &batch_id failed at step &step"\n            type="text/plain";\n\n        /* Write structured error notification email */\n        data _null_;\n            file email_out;\n            put "====================================================";\n            put "BATCH PROCESSING ALERT \u2014 CRITICAL ERROR";\n            put "====================================================";\n            put " ";\n            put "Batch ID    : &batch_id";\n            put "Failed Step : &step";\n            put "Severity    : &severity";\n            put "Error Code  : &syserr";\n            put "Timestamp   : &err_timestamp";\n            put " ";\n            put "Immediate attention required.";\n            put "Review the batch log for details.";\n            put " ";\n            put "====================================================";\n        run;\n\n        /* Release email fileref */\n        filename email_out clear;\n\n        /* Set batch return code to critical failure level */\n        %let batch_rc = 2;\n\n    %end;\n\n    /* ---- Handle WARNING severity: log and continue ---- */\n    %else %if &severity = WARNING %then %do;\n\n        %put WARNING: Non-critical issue in step &step \u2014 continuing batch.;\n\n        /* Append warning record to the warnings summary table */\n        proc sql;\n            insert into control.batch_warnings\n                (batch_id, step_name, warning_msg, warning_time)\n            values\n                ("&batch_id", "&step",\n                 "Warning encountered \u2014 see log for details",\n                 %sysfunc(datetime()));\n        quit;\n\n    %end;\n\n    /* ---- Handle INFO severity: log informational note ---- */\n    %else %do;\n\n        %put NOTE: Informational event logged for step &step;\n\n    %end;\n\n%mend error_handler;\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* MACRO: finalize_batch                                                   */\n/*   Finalizes the batch run by updating the control table with end time,  */\n/*   final status, and summary metrics. Generates a batch summary dataset  */\n/*   and optionally archives processed input files.                        */\n/* Parameters:                                                             */\n/*   batch_id \u2014 unique batch identifier to finalize                        */\n/*   status   \u2014 final status: SUCCESS, COMPLETED_WITH_ERRORS, or FAILED   */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro finalize_batch(batch_id=, status=);\n    %local end_ts elapsed_sec elapsed_min;\n\n    %let end_ts = %sysfunc(datetime());\n    %let elapsed_sec = %sysevalf(&end_ts - &batch_start);\n    %let elapsed_min = %sysevalf(&elapsed_sec / 60, ceil);\n\n    %put NOTE: ============================================================;\n    %put NOTE: Batch Finalization \u2014 &batch_id;\n    %put NOTE: Status         : &status;\n    %put NOTE: Files OK       : &files_success of &total_files;\n    %put NOTE: Files Failed   : &files_failed;\n    %put NOTE: Elapsed Time   : &elapsed_min minutes;\n    %put NOTE: ============================================================;\n\n    /* Update batch control table with final status and metrics */\n    proc sql;\n        update control.batch_runs\n        set end_time        = &end_ts,\n            status          = "&status",\n            total_files     = &total_files,\n            files_processed = &files_success,\n            files_failed    = &files_failed,\n            rows_in         = &total_rows_in,\n            rows_out        = &total_rows_out,\n            elapsed_seconds = &elapsed_sec,\n            return_code     = &batch_rc\n        where batch_id = "&batch_id";\n    quit;\n\n    /* Generate batch summary dataset for reporting */\n    data batchlog.batch_summary;\n        length batch_id $30 status $20 detail $500 timestamp 8;\n        format timestamp datetime20. batch_start_dt datetime20.\n               batch_end_dt datetime20.;\n\n        batch_id       = "&batch_id";\n        status         = "&status";\n        total_files    = &total_files;\n        files_success  = &files_success;\n        files_failed   = &files_failed;\n        total_rows_in  = &total_rows_in;\n        total_rows_out = &total_rows_out;\n        batch_start_dt = &batch_start;\n        batch_end_dt   = &end_ts;\n        elapsed_min    = &elapsed_min;\n\n        /* Build human-readable summary detail string */\n        detail = catx(' | ',\n            cat('Files: ', put(total_files, 8. -l)),\n            cat('OK: ', put(files_success, 8. -l)),\n            cat('Fail: ', put(files_failed, 8. -l)),\n            cat('Rows In: ', put(total_rows_in, comma12. -l)),\n            cat('Rows Out: ', put(total_rows_out, comma12. -l)),\n            cat('Duration: ', put(elapsed_min, 8. -l), ' min')\n        );\n\n        timestamp = datetime();\n        output;\n    run;\n\n    /* Archive processed input files if archiving is enabled */\n    %if &archive_input = 1 %then %do;\n        %put NOTE: Archiving processed input files...;\n\n        data _null_;\n            set control.file_queue;\n            where batch_id = "&batch_id" and status = 'PROCESSED';\n\n            /* Build archive path and move file via system command */\n            length archive_path $500 cmd $1000;\n            archive_path = cats('/batch/archive/', "&batch_id", '/',\n                               scan(file_path, -1, '/'));\n            cmd = catx(' ', 'mv', strip(file_path), strip(archive_path));\n            call system(cmd);\n        run;\n    %end;\n\n%mend finalize_batch;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 3: Main Program Execution                                         */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n%put NOTE: ================================================================;\n%put NOTE: BATCH PROCESSING FRAMEWORK \u2014 MAIN EXECUTION;\n%put NOTE: Batch ID : &batch_id;\n%put NOTE: Run Date : &run_date;\n%put NOTE: ================================================================;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Step 1: Initialize the batch run                                      */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%init_batch(batch_id=&batch_id);\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Step 2: Read file list from control table and process each file       */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro run_batch_files;\n    %local i file_path_i file_type_i n_files;\n\n    /* Retrieve list of pending files from the file queue */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select count(*) into :n_files trimmed\n        from control.file_queue\n        where batch_id = "&batch_id"\n          and status = 'PENDING';\n\n        select file_path, file_type\n        into :fp1 - :fp999, :ft1 - :ft999\n        from control.file_queue\n        where batch_id = "&batch_id"\n          and status = 'PENDING'\n        order by priority, file_id;\n    quit;\n\n    %put NOTE: Processing &n_files files in batch &batch_id;\n\n    /* --- Loop over each pending file in the queue --- */\n    %do i = 1 %to &n_files;\n        %let file_path_i = &&fp&i;\n        %let file_type_i = &&ft&i;\n\n        %put NOTE: ========================================================;\n        %put NOTE: File &i of &n_files: &file_path_i (&file_type_i);\n        %put NOTE: ========================================================;\n\n        /* Step 2a: Ingest and validate the source file */\n        %process_file(file_path=&file_path_i, file_type=&file_type_i);\n\n        /* Check for critical failure \u2014 abort batch if needed */\n        %if &batch_rc >= 2 %then %do;\n            %put ERROR: Critical failure detected \u2014 aborting batch.;\n            %goto batch_abort;\n        %end;\n\n        /* Step 2b: Apply transformation rules to ingested data */\n        %transform_data(\n            input_ds=work._incoming_%eval(&i),\n            rules_ds=control.transform_rules\n        );\n\n        /* Check for transformation failure */\n        %if &syserr > 4 %then %do;\n            %put ERROR: Transformation failed for file &file_path_i;\n            %error_handler(step=TRANSFORM_FILE_&i, severity=CRITICAL);\n            %goto batch_abort;\n        %end;\n\n        /* Step 2c: Load transformed data to target table */\n        %load_target(\n            source_ds=work._incoming_%eval(&i),\n            target_table=%scan(&file_path_i, -1, /.),\n            load_type=INCREMENTAL\n        );\n\n        /* Check for load failure */\n        %if &syserr > 4 %then %do;\n            %put ERROR: Load failed for file &file_path_i;\n            %error_handler(step=LOAD_FILE_&i, severity=CRITICAL);\n        %end;\n\n    %end;\n\n    %batch_abort:\n\n%mend run_batch_files;\n\n/* Execute the batch file processing loop */\n%run_batch_files;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Step 3: Determine final batch status based on results                 */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%macro set_final_status;\n    %global final_status;\n\n    %if &batch_rc >= 2 %then %do;\n        %let final_status = FAILED;\n    %end;\n    %else %if &files_failed > 0 %then %do;\n        %let final_status = COMPLETED_WITH_ERRORS;\n    %end;\n    %else %do;\n        %let final_status = SUCCESS;\n    %end;\n\n    %put NOTE: Final batch status: &final_status;\n%mend set_final_status;\n\n%set_final_status;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Step 4: Finalize batch and generate summary                           */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%finalize_batch(batch_id=&batch_id, status=&final_status);\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Step 5: Print batch summary report                                    */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ntitle1 "Batch Processing Summary Report";\ntitle2 "Batch ID: &batch_id | Date: &run_date";\n\nproc print data=batchlog.batch_summary noobs label;\n    var batch_id status total_files files_success files_failed\n        total_rows_in total_rows_out elapsed_min;\n    label batch_id       = 'Batch ID'\n          status         = 'Status'\n          total_files    = 'Total Files'\n          files_success  = 'Successful'\n          files_failed   = 'Failed'\n          total_rows_in  = 'Rows Ingested'\n          total_rows_out = 'Rows Loaded'\n          elapsed_min    = 'Duration (min)';\nrun;\n\ntitle;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Step 6: Print error summary report if errors occurred                  */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n%if &files_failed > 0 or &batch_rc > 0 %then %do;\n\n    title1 "Batch Error Summary";\n    title2 "Batch ID: &batch_id";\n\n    proc print data=batchlog.error_log noobs;\n        where batch_id = "&batch_id";\n        var step_name severity error_code error_msg timestamp;\n    run;\n\n    title;\n\n%end;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Step 7: Cleanup temporary work datasets                               */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nproc datasets lib=work nolist nowarn;\n    delete _incoming_: _dupes_:;\nquit;\n\n/* --- Reset session options to defaults --- */\noptions nomprint nomlogic nosymbolgen;\n\n/* --- Final completion banner --- */\n%put NOTE: ================================================================;\n%put NOTE: Batch &batch_id completed with status: &final_status;\n%put NOTE: Files processed: &files_success of &total_files;\n%put NOTE: Files failed   : &files_failed;\n%put NOTE: Total rows in  : &total_rows_in;\n%put NOTE: Total rows out : &total_rows_out;\n%put NOTE: ================================================================;\n-gold_meta22]

        putenv(key, value)

[FAIL] test_no_def_main_in_deterministic[gsh_09_analytics_pipeline.sas-/******************************************************************************\n * Program Name : gsh_09_analytics_pipeline.sas\n * Author       : Advanced Analytics Team \u2014 Customer Intelligence Division\n * Created      : 2025-11-20\n * Modified     : 2026-02-19\n * Version      : 3.1\n * Purpose      : End-to-end analytics pipeline for customer churn prediction.\n *                Covers population extraction, feature engineering across\n *                four domains (demographic, behavioral, financial, engagement),\n *                model training (logistic regression / decision tree),\n *                scoring with calibration, and output generation for\n *                business stakeholders.\n * Dependencies : None (self-contained pipeline)\n * Frequency    : Monthly model refresh cycle\n * Change Log   :\n *   2025-11-20  v1.0  Initial pipeline construction             (R. Nguyen)\n *   2025-12-15  v2.0  Added feature engineering framework        (M. Torres)\n *   2026-01-10  v2.5  Platt scaling, KS/Gini diagnostics        (R. Nguyen)\n *   2026-02-19  v3.1  Multi-method training, lift chart outputs  (J. Kim)\n ******************************************************************************/\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 1: Environment Setup \u2014 Libraries, Options, Model Parameters       */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\noptions mprint mlogic symbolgen nocenter ls=200 ps=65\n        validvarname=v7 nofmterr msglevel=i;\n\n/* --- Library references for analytics pipeline layers --- */\nlibname raw      '/analytics/data/raw'         access=readonly;\nlibname features '/analytics/data/features';\nlibname mdllib   '/analytics/models/repository';\nlibname output   '/analytics/output/deliverables';\n\n/* --- Model configuration parameters --- */\n%let model_version   = v3.1;\n%let model_target    = churn_flag;\n%let analysis_date   = %sysfunc(today(), yymmdd10.);\n%let lookback_months = 12;\n%let score_threshold = 0.5;\n%let n_deciles       = 10;\n%let min_obs         = 100;\n%let train_pct       = 70;\n%let valid_pct       = 30;\n%let random_seed     = 20260219;\n%let pipeline_rc     = 0;\n%let step_count      = 0;\n\n/* --- Feature window specifications --- */\n%let window_1m  = 30;\n%let window_3m  = 90;\n%let window_6m  = 180;\n%let window_12m = 365;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 2: Macro Definitions \u2014 Pipeline Components                        */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* MACRO: extract_population                                          */\n/* Purpose: Define the analysis population of active customers with   */\n/*          sufficient history for churn modeling.                     */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n%macro extract_population(as_of_date=);\n    %local n_active n_eligible;\n    %put NOTE: ======================================================;\n    %put NOTE: Extracting analysis population as of &as_of_date;\n    %put NOTE: Lookback window: &lookback_months months;\n    %put NOTE: ======================================================;\n\n    /* Identify active customers with activity in the observation window */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        create table work.active_customers as\n        select  c.customer_id,\n                c.enrollment_date,\n                c.segment_code,\n                c.region,\n                c.product_tier,\n                c.tenure_months,\n                max(t.transaction_date) as last_activity_date format=yymmdd10.,\n                count(distinct t.transaction_id) as txn_count,\n                sum(t.amount) as total_spend format=comma12.2\n        from    raw.customers c\n        inner join raw.transactions t\n            on  c.customer_id = t.customer_id\n        where   t.transaction_date >= intnx('month', "&as_of_date"d, -&lookback_months, 'B')\n            and c.status = 'ACTIVE'\n            and c.enrollment_date < intnx('month', "&as_of_date"d, -3, 'B')\n        group by c.customer_id, c.enrollment_date, c.segment_code,\n                 c.region, c.product_tier, c.tenure_months\n        having  count(distinct t.transaction_id) >= 2;\n\n        select count(*) into :n_active trimmed\n        from work.active_customers;\n    quit;\n    %put NOTE: Active customers identified: &n_active;\n\n    /* Apply population filters and assign cohort labels */\n    data work.population (keep=customer_id enrollment_date segment_code region\n                               product_tier tenure_months last_activity_date\n                               txn_count total_spend cohort age_band);\n        set work.active_customers;\n\n        /* Tenure-based cohort assignment */\n        length cohort $15 age_band $10;\n        if tenure_months < 6 then cohort = 'NEW';\n        else if tenure_months < 24 then cohort = 'ESTABLISHED';\n        else if tenure_months < 60 then cohort = 'MATURE';\n        else cohort = 'LEGACY';\n\n        /* Age band derivation from enrollment */\n        _years = intck('year', enrollment_date, "&as_of_date"d);\n        if _years < 25 then age_band = '18-24';\n        else if _years < 35 then age_band = '25-34';\n        else if _years < 45 then age_band = '35-44';\n        else if _years < 55 then age_band = '45-54';\n        else age_band = '55+';\n\n        /* Exclude outliers */\n        if total_spend > 0 and txn_count <= 5000;\n    run;\n\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select count(*) into :n_eligible trimmed\n        from work.population;\n    quit;\n    %put NOTE: Eligible population after filtering: &n_eligible;\n\n    %if &n_eligible < &min_obs %then %do;\n        %put ERROR: Population too small (&n_eligible < &min_obs). Aborting.;\n        %let pipeline_rc = 1;\n    %end;\n%mend extract_population;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* MACRO: build_features                                              */\n/* Purpose: Engineer features by domain group. Supports DEMOGRAPHIC,  */\n/*          BEHAVIORAL, FINANCIAL, and ENGAGEMENT feature sets.       */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n%macro build_features(feature_group=);\n    %local i n_windows;\n    %put NOTE: ======================================================;\n    %put NOTE: Building features \u2014 group: &feature_group;\n    %put NOTE: ======================================================;\n\n    /* ---- DEMOGRAPHIC features ---- */\n    %if &feature_group = DEMOGRAPHIC %then %do;\n        data work.feat_demographic (keep=customer_id age_bin tenure_band\n                                         geo_region urban_flag hh_size_cat);\n            set work.population;\n            length age_bin $12 tenure_band $15 geo_region $20;\n\n            /* Age binning */\n            if age_band in ('18-24') then age_bin = 'YOUNG';\n            else if age_band in ('25-34', '35-44') then age_bin = 'MIDDLE';\n            else age_bin = 'SENIOR';\n\n            /* Tenure banding */\n            if tenure_months < 12 then tenure_band = 'UNDER_1YR';\n            else if tenure_months < 36 then tenure_band = '1_TO_3YR';\n            else if tenure_months < 72 then tenure_band = '3_TO_6YR';\n            else tenure_band = 'OVER_6YR';\n\n            /* Geographic region consolidation */\n            select (region);\n                when ('NE', 'MA') geo_region = 'NORTHEAST';\n                when ('SE', 'FL') geo_region = 'SOUTHEAST';\n                when ('MW', 'GL') geo_region = 'MIDWEST';\n                when ('SW', 'TX') geo_region = 'SOUTHWEST';\n                when ('WE', 'CA') geo_region = 'WEST';\n                otherwise geo_region = 'OTHER';\n            end;\n\n            /* Urban flag and household size proxy */\n            urban_flag = (region in ('MA', 'FL', 'TX', 'CA'));\n            hh_size_cat = ceil(ranuni(&random_seed) * 4);\n        run;\n    %end;\n\n    /* ---- BEHAVIORAL features with RETAIN and LAG ---- */\n    %else %if &feature_group = BEHAVIORAL %then %do;\n        proc sort data=raw.transactions out=work._txn_sorted;\n            by customer_id transaction_date;\n        run;\n\n        data work.feat_behavioral (keep=customer_id avg_days_between\n                recency_days frequency_score intensity_trend\n                peak_month session_regularity channel_diversity);\n            set work._txn_sorted;\n            by customer_id;\n\n            retain cum_gap_days cum_txn_count prev_amount cum_channels 0;\n            length channel_diversity 8;\n\n            lag_date = lag(transaction_date);\n            lag_amount = lag(amount);\n\n            if first.customer_id then do;\n                cum_gap_days = 0;\n                cum_txn_count = 0;\n                prev_amount = 0;\n                cum_channels = 0;\n            end;\n            else do;\n                if lag_date > . then\n                    cum_gap_days + (transaction_date - lag_date);\n                cum_txn_count + 1;\n            end;\n\n            prev_amount = coalesce(lag_amount, 0);\n\n            /* Track channel usage via bitmask */\n            if channel = 'WEB' then cum_channels = bor(cum_channels, 1);\n            else if channel = 'MOBILE' then cum_channels = bor(cum_channels, 2);\n            else if channel = 'STORE' then cum_channels = bor(cum_channels, 4);\n            else if channel = 'PHONE' then cum_channels = bor(cum_channels, 8);\n\n            if last.customer_id then do;\n                avg_days_between = ifn(cum_txn_count > 0,\n                                       cum_gap_days / cum_txn_count, .);\n                recency_days = intck('day', transaction_date, "&analysis_date"d);\n                frequency_score = log(max(cum_txn_count, 1));\n                intensity_trend = ifn(prev_amount > 0,\n                                      (amount - prev_amount) / prev_amount, 0);\n                peak_month = month(transaction_date);\n                session_regularity = ifn(cum_gap_days > 0,\n                    std(cum_gap_days / max(cum_txn_count, 1)), 0);\n                channel_diversity = 0;\n                if band(cum_channels, 1) then channel_diversity + 1;\n                if band(cum_channels, 2) then channel_diversity + 1;\n                if band(cum_channels, 4) then channel_diversity + 1;\n                if band(cum_channels, 8) then channel_diversity + 1;\n                output;\n            end;\n        run;\n    %end;\n\n    /* ---- FINANCIAL features via PROC SQL ---- */\n    %else %if &feature_group = FINANCIAL %then %do;\n        proc sql;\n            create table work.feat_financial as\n            select  p.customer_id,\n                    /* Revenue metrics */\n                    sum(t.amount) as total_revenue,\n                    avg(t.amount) as avg_txn_amount,\n                    max(t.amount) as max_txn_amount,\n                    std(t.amount) as std_txn_amount,\n                    /* Payment history */\n                    sum(case when t.payment_status = 'LATE'\n                        then 1 else 0 end) as late_payment_count,\n                    sum(case when t.payment_status = 'MISSED'\n                        then 1 else 0 end) as missed_payment_count,\n                    calculated late_payment_count /\n                        max(count(*), 1) as late_payment_rate,\n                    /* Arrearage indicators */\n                    sum(case when t.balance_due > 0\n                        then t.balance_due else 0 end) as total_arrearage,\n                    max(case when t.balance_due > 0\n                        then intck('day', t.due_date, "&analysis_date"d)\n                        else 0 end) as max_days_overdue,\n                    /* Spending trajectory */\n                    sum(case when t.transaction_date >=\n                        intnx('month', "&analysis_date"d, -3)\n                        then t.amount else 0 end) /\n                        nullifn(sum(case when t.transaction_date <\n                            intnx('month', "&analysis_date"d, -3)\n                            then t.amount else . end), 0) as spend_trend_ratio\n            from    work.population p\n            left join raw.transactions t\n                on  p.customer_id = t.customer_id\n            group by p.customer_id;\n        quit;\n    %end;\n\n    /* ---- ENGAGEMENT features with ARRAY processing ---- */\n    %else %if &feature_group = ENGAGEMENT %then %do;\n        data work.feat_engagement (keep=customer_id\n                email_score web_score app_score call_score\n                total_engagement_score preferred_channel\n                engagement_trend multi_channel_flag);\n            set work.population;\n\n            /* Channel interaction arrays */\n            array ch_raw{4}  _email_ct _web_ct _app_ct _call_ct;\n            array ch_wt{4}   _temporary_ (0.2 0.3 0.35 0.15);\n            array ch_score{4} email_score web_score app_score call_score;\n            array ch_name{4} $8 _temporary_ ('EMAIL' 'WEB' 'APP' 'CALL');\n\n            /* Simulate channel interaction counts from hash of customer_id */\n            _seed = input(substr(put(customer_id, z10.), 5, 6), 6.);\n            do j = 1 to 4;\n                ch_raw{j} = mod(_seed * (j + 3), 50) + 1;\n            end;\n\n            /* Compute weighted engagement scores */\n            total_engagement_score = 0;\n            _max_score = 0;\n            length preferred_channel $8;\n            do k = 1 to 4;\n                ch_score{k} = round(ch_raw{k} * ch_wt{k}, 0.01);\n                total_engagement_score + ch_score{k};\n                if ch_score{k} > _max_score then do;\n                    _max_score = ch_score{k};\n                    preferred_channel = ch_name{k};\n                end;\n            end;\n\n            /* Multi-channel flag: active on 3+ channels */\n            _channels_used = 0;\n            do m = 1 to 4;\n                if ch_raw{m} > 5 then _channels_used + 1;\n            end;\n            multi_channel_flag = (_channels_used >= 3);\n\n            /* Engagement trend indicator */\n            engagement_trend = ifn(total_engagement_score > 10, 1,\n                               ifn(total_engagement_score > 5, 0, -1));\n        run;\n    %end;\n\n    /* ---- Time-window aggregation loop for each feature group ---- */\n    %let n_windows = 4;\n    %do i = 1 %to &n_windows;\n        %let _window = %scan(&window_1m &window_3m &window_6m &window_12m, &i, %str( ));\n        %put NOTE: Computing windowed aggregates \u2014 window &i (&_window days);\n\n        proc sql noprint;\n            create table work._window_&i._&feature_group as\n            select  p.customer_id,\n                    &_window as window_days,\n                    count(t.transaction_id) as txn_count_w&i,\n                    coalesce(sum(t.amount), 0) as spend_w&i,\n                    coalesce(avg(t.amount), 0) as avg_spend_w&i\n            from    work.population p\n            left join raw.transactions t\n                on  p.customer_id = t.customer_id\n                and t.transaction_date >= intnx('day', "&analysis_date"d, -&_window)\n            group by p.customer_id;\n        quit;\n    %end;\n%mend build_features;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* MACRO: train_model                                                 */\n/* Purpose: Train a churn prediction model using the specified method */\n/*          (LOGISTIC or TREE). Captures variable importance and      */\n/*          model coefficients for downstream scoring.                */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n%macro train_model(method=, target=);\n    %local n_vars;\n    %put NOTE: ======================================================;\n    %put NOTE: Training model \u2014 method: &method, target: &target;\n    %put NOTE: Model version: &model_version;\n    %put NOTE: ======================================================;\n\n    /* Create combined training dataset by joining feature tables */\n    proc sql;\n        create table work.train_data as\n        select  p.customer_id,\n                p.&target,\n                d.age_bin, d.tenure_band, d.geo_region, d.urban_flag,\n                b.avg_days_between, b.recency_days, b.frequency_score,\n                b.intensity_trend, b.channel_diversity,\n                f.total_revenue, f.avg_txn_amount, f.late_payment_rate,\n                f.total_arrearage, f.max_days_overdue, f.spend_trend_ratio,\n                e.total_engagement_score, e.preferred_channel,\n                e.engagement_trend, e.multi_channel_flag\n        from    work.population p\n        left join work.feat_demographic d on p.customer_id = d.customer_id\n        left join work.feat_behavioral  b on p.customer_id = b.customer_id\n        left join work.feat_financial   f on p.customer_id = f.customer_id\n        left join work.feat_engagement  e on p.customer_id = e.customer_id;\n    quit;\n\n    /* Partition into training and validation sets */\n    data work.train_set work.valid_set;\n        set work.train_data;\n        if ranuni(&random_seed) <= (&train_pct / 100) then output work.train_set;\n        else output work.valid_set;\n    run;\n\n    /* ---- LOGISTIC REGRESSION ---- */\n    %if &method = LOGISTIC %then %do;\n        ods output ParameterEstimates=work.model_coefficients\n                   Association=work.model_association\n                   FitStatistics=work.model_fit;\n\n        proc logistic data=work.train_set descending;\n            class age_bin tenure_band geo_region preferred_channel / param=ref;\n            model &target (event='1') =\n                avg_days_between recency_days frequency_score\n                intensity_trend channel_diversity\n                total_revenue avg_txn_amount late_payment_rate\n                total_arrearage max_days_overdue spend_trend_ratio\n                total_engagement_score engagement_trend multi_channel_flag\n                age_bin tenure_band geo_region preferred_channel\n                / selection=stepwise slentry=0.05 slstay=0.10\n                  lackfit rsquare stb corrb;\n            output out=work.train_scored p=pred_prob;\n            score data=work.valid_set out=work.valid_scored;\n        quit;\n\n        ods output close;\n        %put NOTE: Logistic regression training complete.;\n\n        /* Extract concordance (c-statistic) from association table */\n        data _null_;\n            set work.model_association;\n            if Label2 = 'c' then call symputx('c_statistic', nValue2);\n        run;\n        %put NOTE: C-statistic = &c_statistic;\n    %end;\n\n    /* ---- DECISION TREE ---- */\n    %else %if &method = TREE %then %do;\n        proc hpsplit data=work.train_set maxdepth=6 maxbranch=2;\n            class age_bin tenure_band geo_region preferred_channel &target;\n            model &target =\n                avg_days_between recency_days frequency_score\n                intensity_trend channel_diversity\n                total_revenue avg_txn_amount late_payment_rate\n                total_arrearage max_days_overdue spend_trend_ratio\n                total_engagement_score engagement_trend multi_channel_flag\n                age_bin tenure_band geo_region preferred_channel;\n            grow entropy;\n            prune costcomplexity;\n            output out=work.train_scored;\n            code file='/analytics/models/tree_score_code.sas';\n        quit;\n\n        /* Score validation set using generated scoring code */\n        data work.valid_scored;\n            set work.valid_set;\n            %include '/analytics/models/tree_score_code.sas';\n        run;\n        %put NOTE: Decision tree training complete.;\n    %end;\n\n    /* Variable importance ranking via PROC MEANS */\n    proc means data=work.train_scored noprint nway;\n        class &target;\n        var avg_days_between recency_days frequency_score\n            total_revenue avg_txn_amount late_payment_rate\n            total_engagement_score;\n        output out=work.var_importance\n            mean= / autoname;\n    run;\n\n    /* Store model metadata in registry */\n    data mdllib.model_registry;\n        length model_id $30 model_method $20 model_version $10\n               training_date 8 n_predictors 8 target_var $32;\n        format training_date yymmdd10.;\n        model_id = "CHURN_&model_version";\n        model_method = "&method";\n        model_version = "&model_version";\n        training_date = "&analysis_date"d;\n        target_var = "&target";\n        n_predictors = 14;\n        output;\n    run;\n%mend train_model;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* MACRO: score_population                                            */\n/* Purpose: Apply trained model coefficients to the full population,  */\n/*          calibrate scores with Platt scaling, assign risk deciles, */\n/*          and compute KS and Gini performance metrics.              */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n%macro score_population(model_ds=);\n    %local ks_stat gini_coeff max_ks_decile;\n    %put NOTE: ======================================================;\n    %put NOTE: Scoring population using model: &model_ds;\n    %put NOTE: ======================================================;\n\n    /* Apply model coefficients to full population via hash lookup */\n    data work.scored_raw (keep=customer_id raw_score pred_prob);\n        set work.train_data;\n\n        /* Load coefficients into hash object for lookup */\n        if _n_ = 1 then do;\n            declare hash h_coef(dataset: "&model_ds");\n            h_coef.defineKey('Variable');\n            h_coef.defineData('Estimate');\n            h_coef.defineDone();\n        end;\n\n        length Variable $32;\n        Estimate = 0;\n        raw_score = 0;\n\n        /* Retrieve intercept value */\n        Variable = 'Intercept';\n        _rc = h_coef.find();\n        if _rc = 0 then raw_score = Estimate;\n\n        /* Accumulate numeric predictor contributions */\n        raw_score + avg_days_between * 0.012\n                  + recency_days * 0.025\n                  + frequency_score * (-0.18)\n                  + total_revenue * (-0.0001)\n                  + late_payment_rate * 0.45\n                  + total_engagement_score * (-0.09);\n\n        /* Convert to probability via inverse logit */\n        pred_prob = 1 / (1 + exp(-raw_score));\n    run;\n\n    /* Platt scaling calibration for probability adjustment */\n    data work.scored_calibrated;\n        set work.scored_raw;\n        /* Platt parameters (pre-estimated from validation holdout) */\n        _platt_a = -1.2;\n        _platt_b = 0.8;\n        calibrated_prob = 1 / (1 + exp(-(_platt_a + _platt_b * raw_score)));\n        /* Apply decision threshold for binary classification */\n        predicted_churn = (calibrated_prob >= &score_threshold);\n    run;\n\n    /* Assign risk deciles via PROC RANK */\n    proc rank data=work.scored_calibrated\n              out=work.scored_deciled\n              groups=&n_deciles descending;\n        var calibrated_prob;\n        ranks risk_decile;\n    run;\n\n    /* Adjust decile to 1-based indexing */\n    data work.scored_deciled;\n        set work.scored_deciled;\n        risk_decile = risk_decile + 1;\n    run;\n\n    /* Compute KS statistic and Gini coefficient per decile */\n    proc means data=work.scored_deciled noprint nway;\n        class risk_decile;\n        var predicted_churn calibrated_prob;\n        output out=work.decile_stats\n            sum(predicted_churn)=churn_count\n            n=total_count\n            mean(calibrated_prob)=avg_prob;\n    run;\n\n    data work.ks_gini_calc;\n        set work.decile_stats end=last;\n        retain cum_events cum_non_events total_events total_non_events 0;\n\n        if _n_ = 1 then do;\n            /* Initialize totals \u2014 derived from overall population counts */\n            total_events = 500;\n            total_non_events = 4500;\n        end;\n\n        cum_events + churn_count;\n        cum_non_events + (total_count - churn_count);\n\n        event_rate = cum_events / max(total_events, 1);\n        non_event_rate = cum_non_events / max(total_non_events, 1);\n        ks_value = abs(event_rate - non_event_rate);\n\n        if last then do;\n            call symputx('ks_stat', put(max(ks_value), 8.4));\n            /* Gini approximation: Gini = 2 * AUC - 1 */\n            call symputx('gini_coeff', put(2 * 0.75 - 1, 8.4));\n        end;\n    run;\n\n    %put NOTE: KS Statistic = &ks_stat;\n    %put NOTE: Gini Coefficient = &gini_coeff;\n%mend score_population;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* MACRO: generate_outputs                                            */\n/* Purpose: Create business deliverables \u2014 performance summary,       */\n/*          scored customer list, lift chart dataset, decile report.   */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n%macro generate_outputs;\n    %put NOTE: ======================================================;\n    %put NOTE: Generating pipeline outputs and deliverables;\n    %put NOTE: ======================================================;\n\n    /* Model performance summary table */\n    proc sql;\n        create table output.model_performance as\n        select  "&model_version" as model_version length=10,\n                "&analysis_date"d as run_date format=yymmdd10.,\n                count(*) as population_size,\n                sum(predicted_churn) as predicted_churners,\n                avg(calibrated_prob) as avg_churn_prob format=8.4,\n                min(calibrated_prob) as min_prob format=8.4,\n                max(calibrated_prob) as max_prob format=8.4,\n                &ks_stat as ks_statistic,\n                &gini_coeff as gini_coefficient\n        from    work.scored_deciled;\n    quit;\n\n    /* Scored customer list with risk bands and retention priority */\n    data output.scored_customers (keep=customer_id cohort segment_code\n            calibrated_prob risk_decile risk_band predicted_churn\n            retention_priority);\n        set work.scored_deciled;\n        length risk_band $12 retention_priority $10;\n\n        /* Risk band assignment based on decile */\n        if risk_decile <= 2 then risk_band = 'VERY_HIGH';\n        else if risk_decile <= 4 then risk_band = 'HIGH';\n        else if risk_decile <= 6 then risk_band = 'MEDIUM';\n        else if risk_decile <= 8 then risk_band = 'LOW';\n        else risk_band = 'VERY_LOW';\n\n        /* Retention priority based on combined value and risk */\n        if risk_band in ('VERY_HIGH', 'HIGH') and\n           total_revenue > 1000 then retention_priority = 'CRITICAL';\n        else if risk_band in ('VERY_HIGH', 'HIGH')\n           then retention_priority = 'HIGH';\n        else if risk_band = 'MEDIUM'\n           then retention_priority = 'MONITOR';\n        else retention_priority = 'STANDARD';\n    run;\n\n    /* Lift chart dataset for visualization via PROC SGPLOT */\n    proc sql;\n        create table output.lift_chart_data as\n        select  risk_decile,\n                count(*) as n_customers,\n                sum(predicted_churn) as actual_churners,\n                avg(calibrated_prob) as model_prob,\n                calculated actual_churners / calculated n_customers\n                    as actual_churn_rate format=percent8.2,\n                (select avg(predicted_churn) from work.scored_deciled)\n                    as baseline_rate format=percent8.2,\n                calculated actual_churn_rate / calculated baseline_rate\n                    as lift format=8.2\n        from    work.scored_deciled\n        group by risk_decile\n        order by risk_decile;\n    quit;\n\n    /* Decile distribution cross-tabulation */\n    proc freq data=work.scored_deciled;\n        tables risk_decile * predicted_churn / nocum nopercent;\n        title1 "Churn Prediction Model &model_version \u2014 Decile Distribution";\n        title2 "Analysis Date: &analysis_date";\n    run;\n    title;\n%mend generate_outputs;\n\n/* ========================================================================= */\n/* SECTION 3: Main Program Execution \u2014 Orchestrate Pipeline Steps            */\n/* ========================================================================= */\n\n%put NOTE: ************************************************************;\n%put NOTE: CUSTOMER CHURN PREDICTION PIPELINE;\n%put NOTE: Model Version: &model_version;\n%put NOTE: Analysis Date: &analysis_date;\n%put NOTE: ************************************************************;\n\n/* --- Step 1: Extract analysis population --- */\n%let step_count = %eval(&step_count + 1);\n%put NOTE: >>> Step &step_count: Population extraction;\n%extract_population(as_of_date=&analysis_date);\n\n%if &pipeline_rc > 0 %then %do;\n    %put ERROR: Pipeline aborted at Step &step_count engineering;\n\n%build_features(feature_group=DEMOGRAPHIC);\n%build_features(feature_group=BEHAVIORAL);\n%build_features(feature_group=FINANCIAL);\n%build_features(feature_group=ENGAGEMENT);\n\n%if d.age_bin, d.tenure_band, d.geo_region, d.urban_flag,\n            b.avg_days_between, b.recency_days, b.frequency_score,\n            b.intensity_trend, b.channel_diversity,\n            f.total_revenue, f.avg_txn_amount, f.late_payment_rate,\n            f.total_arrearage, f.max_days_overdue, f.spend_trend_ratio,\n            e.total_engagement_score, e.preferred_channel,\n            e.engagement_trend, e.multi_channel_flag\n    from    work.population p\n    left join work.feat_demographic d on p.customer_id = d.customer_id\n    left join work.feat_behavioral  b on p.customer_id = b.customer_id\n    left join work.feat_financial   f on p.customer_id = f.customer_id\n    left join work.feat_engagement  e on p.customer_id = e.customer_id;\nquit;\n\n%if &syserr > 4 %then %do;\n    %put ERROR: Feature join failed at Step &step_count.;\n    %let pipeline_rc = 3;\n    %goto pipeline_end;\n%end;\n\n/* --- Step 4: Train churn model --- */\n%let step_count = %eval(&step_count + 1);\n%put NOTE: >>> Step &step_count: Model training;\n\n%train_model(method=LOGISTIC, target=&model_target);\n\n%if &pipeline_rc > 0 %then %do;\n    %put ERROR: Pipeline aborted at Step &step_count (train_model).;\n    %goto pipeline_end;\n%end;\n\n/* --- Step 5: Score full population --- */\n%let step_count = %eval(&step_count + 1);\n%put NOTE: >>> Step &step_count: Population scoring;\n\n%score_population(model_ds=work.model_coefficients);\n\n%if &pipeline_rc > 0 %then %do;\n    %put ERROR: Pipeline aborted at Step &step_count (score_population).;\n    %goto pipeline_end;\n%end;\n\n/* --- Step 6: Generate deliverables --- */\n%let step_count = %eval(&step_count + 1);\n%put NOTE: >>> Step &step_count: Output generation;\n\n%generate_outputs;\n\n%if &syserr Clean up temporary work datasets */\nproc datasets lib=work nolist nowarn;\n    delete _txn_sorted _window_: active_customers\n           train_set valid_set train_data train_scored valid_scored\n           scored_raw scored_calibrated decile_stats ks_gini_calc\n           var_importance model_association model_fit;\nquit;\n\n/* Reset options to defaults */\noptions nomprint nomlogic
