Straight-line responding, low completion, extreme scores       */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\ndata tgt.survey_flagged;\n    set staging.survey_scored;\n\n    /* Flag low completion rate */\n    flag_incomplete = (pct_complete < 50);\n\n    /* Detect straight-line responding (zero variance in answers) */\n    array items{12} q1-q12;\n    _min = min(of items{*});\n    _max = max(of items{*});\n    flag_straightline = (_min = _max and not missing(_min));\n\n    /* Flag extreme outliers on overall score */\n    flag_outlier_low  = (overall_score < 1.5 and not missing(overall_score));\n    flag_outlier_high = (overall_score > 4.8 and not missing(overall_score));\n\n    /* Combined quality flag */\n    flag_quality_concern = (flag_incomplete or flag_straightline\n                           or flag_outlier_low or flag_outlier_high);\n\n    /* Build quality notes for flagged respondents */\n    length quality_notes $200;\n    quality_notes = '';\n    if flag_incomplete then\n        quality_notes = catx('; ', quality_notes, 'Low completion');\n    if flag_straightline then\n        quality_notes = catx('; ', quality_notes, 'Straight-line responding');\n    if flag_outlier_low then\n        quality_notes = catx('; ', quality_notes, 'Extremely low scores');\n    if flag_outlier_high then\n        quality_notes = catx('; ', quality_notes, 'Extremely high scores');\n\n    drop _min _max;\nrun;\n\n/* End of survey analysis program */\n-gold_meta35]

Compute 3-month and 6-month moving averages for trend analysis */\n/*   - Carry forward cumulative revenue with RETAIN                   */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\ndata staging.sales_moving_avg;\n    set raw.monthly_sales;\n    by region_id;\n\n    retain cumulative_revenue 0;\n\n    /* Reset cumulative counter at start of each region */\n    if first.region_id then cumulative_revenue = 0;\n    cumulative_revenue + net_revenue;\n\n    /* Lag values for moving average calculation */\n    lag1_rev = lag1(net_revenue);\n    lag2_rev = lag2(net_revenue);\n    lag3_rev = lag3(net_revenue);\n    lag4_rev = lag4(net_revenue);\n    lag5_rev = lag5(net_revenue);\n\n    /* 3-month moving average (requires at least 3 data points) */\n    if _n_ >= 3 then\n        ma_3m = mean(net_revenue, lag1_rev, lag2_rev);\n\n    /* 6-month moving average (requires at least 6 data points) */\n    if _n_ >= 6 then\n        ma_6m = mean(net_revenue, lag1_rev, lag2_rev,\n                     lag3_rev, lag4_rev, lag5_rev);\n\n    /* Month-over-month percent change */\n    if not missing(lag1_rev) and lag1_rev > 0 then\n        mom_change_pct = ((net_revenue - lag1_rev) / lag1_rev) * 100;\n\n    /* Trend direction indicator */\n    length trend_direction $10;\n    if mom_change_pct > 5 then trend_direction = 'UP';\n    else if mom_change_pct < -5 then trend_direction = 'DOWN';\n    else if not missing(mom_change_pct) then trend_direction = 'STABLE';\n    else trend_direction = 'N/A';\n\n    format cumulative_revenue comma15.2\n           ma_3m ma_6m comma12.2\n           mom_change_pct 6.1;\nrun;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 2: Interpolate missing months                                 */\n/*   - Fill gaps in time series with linear interpolation             */\n/*   - Detect date continuity breaks                                  */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\ndata staging.sales_interpolated;\n    set staging.sales_moving_avg;\n    by region_id sale_month;\n\n    /* Track previous known value for interpolation */\n    prev_rev = lag1(net_revenue);\n\n    /* Simple carry-forward interpolation for missing values */\n    if missing(net_revenue) and not missing(prev_rev) then do;\n        net_revenue = prev_rev;\n        interpolated_flag = 1;\n    end;\n    else interpolated_flag = 0;\n\n    /* Check for month continuity gaps */\n    expected_month = intnx('month', lag1(sale_month), 1, 'beginning');\n    if not missing(expected_month) and sale_month ne expected_month then\n        gap_detected = 1;\n    else\n        gap_detected = 0;\n\n    format expected_month monyy7.;\nrun;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 3: Year-over-year comparison via SQL                          */\n/*   - Join current period to same period prior year                  */\n/*   - Compute YoY change percentages for revenue and moving averages */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\nproc sql;\n    create table staging.yoy_comparison as\n    select\n        a.region_id,\n        a.sale_month,\n        year(a.sale_month) as current_year,\n        a.net_revenue as current_revenue,\n        b.net_revenue as prior_year_revenue,\n        case\n            when b.net_revenue > 0 then\n                ((a.net_revenue - b.net_revenue) / b.net_revenue) * 100\n            else .\n        end as yoy_change_pct format=6.1,\n        a.ma_3m as current_ma3,\n        b.ma_3m as prior_year_ma3,\n        a.cumulative_revenue\n    from staging.sales_moving_avg a\n    left join staging.sales_moving_avg b\n        on a.region_id = b.region_id\n        and a.sale_month = intnx('year', b.sale_month, 1, 'same')\n    order by a.region_id, a.sale_month;\nquit;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 4: Date arithmetic - compute fiscal periods and intervals     */\n/*   - Fiscal year begins in July                                     */\n/*   - Calculate fiscal quarter, days in month, daily average revenue */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\ndata staging.sales_fiscal;\n    set staging.yoy_comparison;\n\n    /* Fiscal year starts in July */\n    fiscal_year_start = intnx('year.7', sale_month, 0, 'beginning');\n    fiscal_year = year(fiscal_year_start);\n\n    /* Fiscal quarter within fiscal year */\n    months_into_fy = intck('month', fiscal_year_start, sale_month);\n    fiscal_quarter = floor(months_into_fy / 3) + 1;\n\n    /* Calendar days in the sales month */\n    month_start = intnx('month', sale_month, 0, 'beginning');\n    month_end   = intnx('month', sale_month, 0, 'end');\n    days_in_month = intck('day', month_start, month_end) + 1;\n\n    /* Daily average revenue */\n    if days_in_month > 0 then\n        daily_avg_revenue = current_revenue / days_in_month;\n\n    /* Months elapsed since baseline date */\n    months_since_start = intck('month', '01JAN2020'd, sale_month);\n\n    /* Revenue per day of month for normalization */\n    if days_in_month > 0 then\n        normalized_revenue = (current_revenue / days_in_month) * 30;\n\n    format fiscal_year_start month_start month_end date9.\n           daily_avg_revenue normalized_revenue comma10.2;\nrun;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 5: Seasonal aggregation with CLASS statement                  */\n/*   - Summarize revenue metrics by fiscal year and quarter           */\n/*   - Produce seasonal performance summary for reporting             */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\nproc means data=staging.sales_fiscal n mean sum std median;\n    class fiscal_year fiscal_quarter;\n    var current_revenue daily_avg_revenue yoy_change_pct;\n    output out=tgt.seasonal_summary\n        mean(current_revenue)  = avg_revenue\n        sum(current_revenue)   = total_revenue\n        mean(yoy_change_pct)   = avg_yoy_change\n        std(current_revenue)   = std_revenue\n        n(current_revenue)     = n_months;\n    title 'Seasonal Revenue Summary by Fiscal Year and Quarter';\nrun;\n\n/* End of time series analysis program */\n-gold_meta36]

[SKIP] test_no_def_main_in_deterministic[gsm_09_cohort_analysis.sas-/*****************************************************************************\n * Program: gsm_09_cohort_analysis.sas\n * Purpose: Clinical cohort analysis for treatment outcome study\n * Author:  Biostatistics Team\n * Date:    2026-02-19\n * Description:\n *   Defines patient cohorts based on inclusion/exclusion criteria,\n *   constructs age-sex matched pairs, computes follow-up duration,\n *   and analyzes treatment outcomes with baseline characteristics.\n *****************************************************************************/\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Global settings and library references                             */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\noptions mprint mlogic nocenter ls=120 ps=60;\n\nlibname raw     'C:/data/clinical/raw' access=readonly;\nlibname staging 'C:/data/clinical/staging';\nlibname tgt     'C:/data/clinical/output';\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 1: Define cohort inclusion/exclusion criteria                  */\n/*   - Include adults 18+ with diagnosis code in target range         */\n/*   - Exclude patients with prior treatment or missing consent       */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\ndata staging.eligible_patients;\n    set raw.patient_registry;\n\n    /* Inclusion criteria */\n    age_at_index = intck('year', date_of_birth, index_date);\n    include_age = (age_at_index >= 18 and age_at_index <= 85);\n    include_diagnosis = (primary_dx_code in ('E11.0','E11.1','E11.2',\n                                             'E11.3','E11.4','E11.5'));\n    include_enrollment = (enrollment_start <= index_date\n                         and enrollment_end >= index_date + 365);\n\n    /* Exclusion criteria */\n    exclude_prior_tx = (prior_treatment_flag = 1);\n    exclude_no_consent = (informed_consent ne 'Y');\n    exclude_comorbidity = (charlson_index > 6);\n\n    /* Apply all criteria */\n    if include_age and include_diagnosis and include_enrollment\n       and not exclude_prior_tx and not exclude_no_consent\n       and not exclude_comorbidity;\n\n    /* Assign treatment group based on treatment_arm variable */\n    length cohort_group $10;\n    if treatment_arm = 1 then cohort_group = 'TREATMENT';\n    else if treatment_arm = 0 then cohort_group = 'CONTROL';\n    else cohort_group = 'UNKNOWN';\n\n    /* Age group for matching */\n    length age_group $10;\n    if age_at_index < 30 then age_group = '18-29';\n    else if age_at_index < 45 then age_group = '30-44';\n    else if age_at_index < 60 then age_group = '45-59';\n    else if age_at_index < 75 then age_group = '60-74';\n    else age_group = '75-85';\n\n    format index_date enrollment_start enrollment_end date9.;\nrun;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 2: Build matched cohort pairs using age-sex matching          */\n/*   - Match each treatment patient with a control of same age/sex   */\n/*   - Use 1:1 greedy matching via SQL                               */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\nproc sql;\n    /* Create matched pairs: treatment to control on age_group + gender */\n    create table staging.matched_cohort as\n    select\n        t.patient_id as treatment_id,\n        c.patient_id as control_id,\n        t.age_group,\n        t.gender,\n        t.age_at_index as treatment_age,\n        c.age_at_index as control_age,\n        abs(t.age_at_index - c.age_at_index) as age_diff\n    from staging.eligible_patients t\n    inner join staging.eligible_patients c\n        on t.age_group = c.age_group\n        and t.gender = c.gender\n        and t.cohort_group = 'TREATMENT'\n        and c.cohort_group = 'CONTROL'\n    group by t.patient_id\n    having age_diff = min(age_diff)\n    order by t.patient_id;\n\n    /* Count matched vs unmatched */\n    title 'Cohort Matching Summary';\n    select cohort_group,\n           count(*) as n_patients\n    from staging.eligible_patients\n    group by cohort_group;\nquit;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 3: Compute follow-up duration with RETAIN                     */\n/*   - Track cumulative days at risk per patient                      */\n/*   - Flag censoring events                                          */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\ndata staging.followup;\n    set raw.patient_encounters;\n    by patient_id encounter_date;\n\n    retain days_at_risk 0 first_encounter_date;\n\n    /* Reset at start of each patient */\n    if first.patient_id then do;\n        days_at_risk = 0;\n        first_encounter_date = encounter_date;\n    end;\n\n    /* Accumulate days since previous encounter */\n    if not first.patient_id then\n        days_at_risk + intck('day', lag1(encounter_date), encounter_date);\n\n    /* Total follow-up from first to current encounter */\n    total_followup_days = intck('day', first_encounter_date, encounter_date);\n\n    /* Censor at 365 days or study end date */\n    study_end = '31DEC2025'd;\n    if encounter_date > study_end then do;\n        censored = 1;\n        total_followup_days = intck('day', first_encounter_date, study_end);\n    end;\n    else censored = 0;\n\n    /* Flag outcome event (primary endpoint) */\n    outcome_event = (outcome_code in ('HOSP','ER','DEATH'));\n\n    format first_encounter_date encounter_date study_end date9.;\nrun;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 4: Outcome analysis using PROC FREQ                           */\n/*   - Chi-square test for outcome events by treatment group          */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\nproc freq data=staging.followup;\n    where last.patient_id;\n    tables cohort_group * outcome_event / chisq relrisk nocum;\n    title 'Outcome Event Rates by Treatment Group';\nrun;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 5: Baseline characteristics table                             */\n/*   - Summarize demographics and clinical measures by cohort group   */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\nproc means data=staging.eligible_patients n mean std median min max;\n    class cohort_group;\n    var age_at_index charlson_index baseline_hba1c baseline_bmi\n        systolic_bp diastolic_bp;\n    output out=tgt.baseline_characteristics\n        mean(age_at_index)    = mean_age\n        mean(charlson_index)  = mean_charlson\n        mean(baseline_hba1c)  = mean_hba1c\n        mean(baseline_bmi)    = mean_bmi\n        std(age_at_index)     = sd_age\n        std(baseline_hba1c)   = sd_hba1c\n        n(age_at_index)       = n_patients;\n    title 'Baseline Characteristics by Cohort';\nrun;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 6: Compute hazard indicators                                  */\n/*   - Create time-to-event dataset with hazard rate estimates        */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\ndata tgt.hazard_indicators;\n    set staging.followup;\n    by patient_id;\n\n    /* Keep only the last record per patient */\n    if last.patient_id;\n\n    /* Compute hazard rate estimate (events / person-time) */\n    person_years = total_followup_days / 365.25;\n    if person_years > 0 then\n        hazard_rate = outcome_event / person_years;\n    else\n        hazard_rate = .;\n\n    /* Risk category based on follow-up and outcome */\n    length risk_category $12;\n    if outcome_event = 1 and total_followup_days <= 90 then\n        risk_category = 'HIGH';\n    else if outcome_event = 1 then\n        risk_category = 'MODERATE';\n    else if censored = 1 then\n        risk_category = 'CENSORED';\n    else\n        risk_category = 'LOW';\n\n    format hazard_rate 8.4 person_years 6.2;\nrun;\n\n/* End of cohort analysis program */\n-gold_meta38]

[SKIP] test_no_def_main_in_deterministic[gsm_10_marketing_roi.sas-/*****************************************************************************\n * Program: gsm_10_marketing_roi.sas\n * Purpose: Marketing campaign ROI analysis and performance reporting\n * Author:  Marketing Analytics Team\n * Date:    2026-02-19\n * Description:\n *   Computes campaign-level costs, conversions, and revenue attribution.\n *   Calculates channel ROI across 12 months, ranks campaign performance,\n *   and produces an executive dashboard dataset.\n *****************************************************************************/\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Global settings and library references                             */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\noptions mprint mlogic nocenter ls=132 ps=60;\n\nlibname raw     'C:/data/marketing/raw' access=readonly;\nlibname staging 'C:/data/marketing/staging';\nlibname tgt     'C:/data/marketing/output';\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 1: Compute campaign-level costs and conversions               */\n/*   - Aggregate spend, impressions, clicks, and conversions          */\n/*   - Calculate click-through rate and cost per acquisition          */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\ndata staging.campaign_metrics;\n    set raw.campaign_daily_stats;\n    by campaign_id;\n\n    retain total_spend total_impressions total_clicks total_conversions 0;\n\n    /* Reset accumulators at start of each campaign */\n    if first.campaign_id then do;\n        total_spend = 0;\n        total_impressions = 0;\n        total_clicks = 0;\n        total_conversions = 0;\n    end;\n\n    /* Accumulate daily metrics */\n    total_spend + daily_spend;\n    total_impressions + daily_impressions;\n    total_clicks + daily_clicks;\n    total_conversions + daily_conversions;\n\n    /* Output summary row at end of each campaign */\n    if last.campaign_id then do;\n        /* Click-through rate */\n        if total_impressions > 0 then\n            ctr = (total_clicks / total_impressions) * 100;\n        else ctr = 0;\n\n        /* Cost per click */\n        if total_clicks > 0 then\n            cpc = total_spend / total_clicks;\n        else cpc = .;\n\n        /* Cost per acquisition */\n        if total_conversions > 0 then\n            cpa = total_spend / total_conversions;\n        else cpa = .;\n\n        format total_spend cpc cpa dollar12.2\n               total_impressions total_clicks total_conversions comma12.\n               ctr 6.2;\n        output;\n    end;\nrun;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 2: Join campaigns with revenue attribution (multi-touch)      */\n/*   - Link campaign touches to downstream revenue events             */\n/*   - Apply linear multi-touch attribution model                     */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\nproc sql;\n    create table staging.campaign_revenue as\n    select\n        c.campaign_id,\n        c.campaign_name,\n        c.channel,\n        c.total_spend,\n        c.total_conversions,\n        count(distinct t.customer_id) as attributed_customers,\n        sum(t.touch_weight * r.revenue_amount) as attributed_revenue format=dollar15.2,\n        /* ROI calculation: (revenue - cost) / cost */\n        case\n            when c.total_spend > 0 then\n                (calculated attributed_revenue - c.total_spend) / c.total_spend * 100\n            else .\n        end as campaign_roi format=8.1,\n        /* Revenue per conversion */\n        case\n            when c.total_conversions > 0 then\n                calculated attributed_revenue / c.total_conversions\n            else .\n        end as revenue_per_conversion format=dollar10.2\n    from staging.campaign_metrics c\n    left join raw.touchpoint_log t\n        on c.campaign_id = t.campaign_id\n    left join raw.revenue_events r\n        on t.customer_id = r.customer_id\n        and t.touch_date <= r.purchase_date\n        and r.purchase_date <= t.touch_date + 30\n    group by c.campaign_id, c.campaign_name, c.channel,\n             c.total_spend, c.total_conversions\n    order by calculated campaign_roi desc;\nquit;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 3: Calculate channel ROI across 12 months using ARRAYS        */\n/*   - Monthly revenue and cost arrays per channel                    */\n/*   - Compute rolling and cumulative ROI by channel                  */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\ndata staging.channel_monthly_roi;\n    set raw.channel_monthly_data;\n    by channel;\n\n    /* Monthly revenue and cost arrays */\n    array rev{12} rev_m01-rev_m12;\n    array cost{12} cost_m01-cost_m12;\n    array roi{12} roi_m01-roi_m12;\n    array cum_rev{12} _temporary_;\n    array cum_cost{12} _temporary_;\n\n    /* Compute monthly ROI and cumulative figures */\n    do m = 1 to 12;\n        if cost{m} > 0 then\n            roi{m} = ((rev{m} - cost{m}) / cost{m}) * 100;\n        else\n            roi{m} = .;\n\n        /* Cumulative */\n        if m = 1 then do;\n            cum_rev{m} = rev{m};\n            cum_cost{m} = cost{m};\n        end;\n        else do;\n            cum_rev{m} = cum_rev{m-1} + rev{m};\n            cum_cost{m} = cum_cost{m-1} + cost{m};\n        end;\n    end;\n\n    /* Annual totals */\n    annual_revenue = sum(of rev{*});\n    annual_cost = sum(of cost{*});\n    if annual_cost > 0 then\n        annual_roi = ((annual_revenue - annual_cost) / annual_cost) * 100;\n    else annual_roi = .;\n\n    /* Break-even month: first month where cumulative ROI >= 0 */\n    breakeven_month = .;\n    do m = 1 to 12;\n        if cum_cost{m} > 0 and cum_rev{m} >= cum_cost{m}\n           and missing(breakeven_month) then\n            breakeven_month = m;\n    end;\n\n    format annual_revenue annual_cost dollar15.2\n           annual_roi 8.1;\n    drop m;\nrun;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 4: Campaign performance summary statistics                    */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\nproc means data=staging.campaign_revenue n mean std median min max;\n    class channel;\n    var total_spend attributed_revenue campaign_roi\n        total_conversions revenue_per_conversion;\n    output out=staging.performance_summary\n        mean(campaign_roi)          = avg_roi\n        mean(total_spend)           = avg_spend\n        mean(attributed_revenue)    = avg_revenue\n        sum(attributed_revenue)     = total_attributed_rev\n        n(campaign_id)              = n_campaigns;\n    title 'Campaign Performance Summary by Channel';\nrun;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 5: Campaign tiering with PROC RANK                            */\n/*   - Rank campaigns by ROI into quartile tiers                      */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\nproc rank data=staging.campaign_revenue\n          out=staging.campaign_ranked groups=4;\n    var campaign_roi;\n    ranks roi_quartile;\nrun;\n\n/* Assign tier labels based on rank */\ndata staging.campaign_tiered;\n    set staging.campaign_ranked;\n\n    length campaign_tier $12;\n    select (roi_quartile);\n        when (3) campaign_tier = 'PLATINUM';\n        when (2) campaign_tier = 'GOLD';\n        when (1) campaign_tier = 'SILVER';\n        when (0) campaign_tier = 'BRONZE';\n        otherwise campaign_tier = 'UNRANKED';\n    end;\nrun;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* Step 6: Executive dashboard output dataset                         */\n/*   - Final summary with KPIs for leadership review                  */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\ndata tgt.executive_dashboard;\n    set staging.campaign_tiered;\n\n    /* Key performance indicators for dashboard */\n    length kpi_status $10;\n    if campaign_roi >= 200 then kpi_status = 'EXCEEDS';\n    else if campaign_roi >= 100 then kpi_status = 'ON TARGET';\n    else if campaign_roi >= 0 then kpi_status = 'BELOW';\n    else kpi_status = 'AT RISK';\n\n    /* Efficiency score: composite of CPA, CTR, and ROI */\n    if not missing(campaign_roi) then\n        efficiency_score = (campaign_roi / 100) * 0.5\n                         + (total_conversions / max(total_spend, 1)) * 0.3\n                         + (attributed_revenue / max(total_spend, 1)) * 0.2;\n\n    /* Flag top performers for increased investment */\n    recommend_increase = (campaign_tier = 'PLATINUM' and kpi_status = 'EXCEEDS');\n\n    format efficiency_score 6.3;\nrun;\n\n/* End of marketing ROI analysis program */\n-gold_meta39]

  where risk_flag = 'V';\n    var ltv_ratio dti_ratio credit_util payment_score;\n    output out=staging.population_benchmarks\n        mean=avg_ltv avg_dti avg_util avg_payment\n        std=std_ltv (ltv_ratio <= 0.75) ltv_points = 30;\n        when (ltv_ratio <= 0.85) ltv_points = 20;\n        when (ltv_ratio <= 0.95) ltv_points = 10;\n        otherwise                ltv_points = 0;\n    end;\n\n    /* DTI score component */\n    select;\n        when (dti_ratio <= 0.28) dti_points = 30;\n        when (dti_ratio <= 0.36) dti_points = 20;\n        when (dti_ratio <= 0.43) dti_points = 10;\n        otherwise                dti_points = 0;\n    end;\n\n    /* FICO score component */\n    select;\n        when (fico_adj >= 760) fico_points = 50;\n        when (fico_adj >= 700) fico_points = 35;\n        when (fico_adj >= 660) fico_points = 20;\n        when (fico_adj >= 620) fico_points = 10;\n        otherwise              fico_points = 0;\n    end;\n\n    /* Total raw score */\n    raw_score = ltv_points + dti_points + fico_points;\n\n    /* Assign risk bucket */\n    select;\n        when (raw_score >= 100) risk_bucket = 'PRIME';\n        when (raw_score >= 70)  risk_bucket = 'NEAR_PRIME';\n        when (raw_score >= 40)  risk_bucket = 'SUBPRIME';\n        otherwise               risk_bucket = 'DEEP_SUB';\n    end;\nrun;\n\n/* model\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ndata staging.pd_estimates;\n    set staging.risk_scored;\n\n    /* Model intercept and coefficients */\n    _intercept = -3.258;\n    _beta_ltv  =  2.145;\n    _beta_dti  =  1.873;\n    _beta_fico = -0.0042;\n    _beta_util =  0.952;\n    _beta_inq  =  0.187;\n\n    /* Linear predictor (log-odds) */\n    logit = _intercept\n          + _beta_ltv  * ltv_ratio\n          + _beta_dti  * dti_ratio\n          + _beta_fico * fico_adj\n          + _beta_util * credit_util\n          + _beta_inq  * inquiries_6m;\n\n    /* Probability of default */\n    prob_default = 1 / (1 + exp(-logit));\n\n    /* Expected loss given default assumption */\n    lgd = 0.40;\n    expected_loss = prob_default * lgd * loan_amount;\n\n    drop _intercept _beta_: logit;\n    format prob_default percent8.4 expected_loss dollar12.2;\nrun;\n\n/* end;\n\n    decision_date = today();\n    format decision_date date9.;\nrun;\n-gold_meta40]

 2026-02-19\n ******************************************************************************/\n\n/* --- Library references for supply chain data --- */\nlibname raw     '/data/supplychain/raw';\nlibname staging '/data/supplychain/staging';\nlibname tgt     '/data/supplychain/target';\n\noptions mprint nocenter;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 1: Compute lead times and delivery reliability per supplier\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ndata staging.delivery_metrics;\n    set raw.purchase_orders;\n    where order_status = 'DELIVERED';\n\n    /* Actual lead time in calendar days */\n    lead_time_days = delivery_date - order_date;\n\n    /* Promised vs actual delivery variance */\n    delivery_variance = delivery_date - promised_date;\n\n    /* On-time flag: delivered on or before promised date */\n    if delivery_variance <= 0 then on_time = 1;\n    else on_time = 0;\n\n    /* Quality acceptance flag */\n    if qty_accepted >= qty_ordered * 0.95 then quality_pass = 1;\n    else quality_pass = 0;\n\n    format delivery_date order_date promised_date date9.;\nrun;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 2: Aggregate supplier performance metrics\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nproc sql;\n    create table staging.supplier_scorecard as\n    select as std_lead_time,\n           sum(d.on_time) / count(*) as otd_rate format=percent8.1,\n           sum(d.quality_pass) / count(*) as quality_rate format=percent8.1,\n           sum(d.qty_accepted * d.unit_cost) as total_spend format=dollar12.2\n    from staging.delivery_metrics as d\n    inner join raw.suppliers as s\n        on d.supplier_id = s.supplier_id\n    group by s.supplier_id, s.supplier_name, s.supplier_region\n    having count(*) >= 5;\nquit;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 3: Track backorder accumulation using RETAIN\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nproc sort data=raw.inventory_transactions out=work.inv_sorted;\n    by product_id transaction_date;\nrun;\n\ndata staging.backorder_tracking;\n    set work.inv_sorted;\n    by product_id;\n    retain running_backorder 0;\n\n    /* Reset accumulator at start of each product */\n    if first.product_id then running_backorder = 0;\n\n    /* Accumulate backorders: positive = demand, negative = receipt */\n    if transaction_type = 'DEMAND' then\n        running_backorder = running_backorder + quantity;\n    else if transaction_type = 'RECEIPT' then\n        running_backorder = max(0, running_backorder - quantity);\n\n    /* Flag critical backorder levels */\n    if running_backorder > reorder_point then\n        backorder_alert = 'CRITICAL';\n    else if running_backorder > 0 then\n        backorder_alert = 'WARNING';\n    else\n        backorder_alert = 'OK';\nrun;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 4: Safety stock computation using demand variability\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nproc means data=raw.daily_demand\n           mean std noprint;\n    by product_id;\n    var daily_qty;\n    output out=staging.demand_stats\n        mean=avg_daily_demand\n        std=std_daily_demand;\nrun;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 5: ABC staging.demand_stats;\n\n    /* Service level z-score (95% = 1.645) */\n    z_score = 1.645;\n    avg_lead_time = 14;\n    std_lead_time = 3;\n\n    /* Safety stock formula */\n    safety_stock = z_score * sqrt(\n        (avg_lead_time * std_daily_demand**2) +\n        (avg_daily_demand**2 * std_lead_time**2)\n    );\n\n    reorder_point = (avg_daily_demand * avg_lead_time) + safety_stock;\n\n    /* ABC classification thresholds by category */\n    array rev_pct{3} _temporary_ (0.80 0.95 1.00);\n    array abc_label{3} $1 _temporary_ ('A' 'B' 'C');\n\n    /* Assign ABC class based on cumulative revenue percentage */\n    length abc_class $1;\n    if cumulative_rev_pct <= rev_pct{1} then abc_class = abc_label{1};\n    else if cumulative_rev_pct <= rev_pct{2} then abc_class = abc_label{2};\n    else abc_class = abc_label{3};\n\n    /* Set review frequency based on ABC class */\n    array review_days{3} _temporary_ (7 14 30);\n    if abc_class = 'A' then review_cycle = review_days{1};\n    else if abc_class = 'B' then review_cycle = review_days{2};\n    else review_cycle = review_days{3};\n\n    format safety_stock reorder_point comma10.;\n    drop z_score avg_lead_time std_lead_time;\nrun;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 6: Generate exception report for critical items\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nproc sort data=tgt.product_classification\n          out=work.exceptions;\n    by abc_class descending safety_stock;\n    where abc_class = 'A' and safety_stock > 1000;\nrun;\n\ntitle 'Supply Chain Exception Report - High Safety Stock A-Items';\nproc print data=work.exceptions noobs label;\n    var product_id abc_class p.product_name,\n           p.product_category,\n           p.product_line,\n           c.customer_name,\n           c.customer_segment,\n           c.industry,\n           e.net_revenue,\n staging.sales_fact;\n    by rep_id fiscal_year fiscal_qtr;\n    retain qtd_revenue;\n\n    /* Accumulate revenue within rep-quarter */\n    if first.fiscal_qtr then qtd_revenue = 0;\n    qtd_revenue + net_revenue;\n\n    /* Output one record per rep per quarter */\n    if last.fiscal_qtr then do;\n        if quarterly_quota > 0 then\n            attainment_pct = qtd_revenue / quarterly_quota;\n [SKIP] test_no_def_main_in_deterministic[gsm_15_ab_testing.sas-/******************************************************************************\n * Program: gsm_15_ab_testing.sas\n * Purpose: A/B testing analysis pipeline for digital experiments\n *          Defines test groups, computes summary statistics, tests for\n *          significance, and generates experiment conclusion reports\n * Author:  Product Analytics Team\n * Date:    2026-02-19\n ******************************************************************************/\n\n/* --- Library references for experiment data --- */\nlibname raw     '/data/experiments/raw';\nlibname staging '/data/experiments/staging';\nlibname tgt     '/data/experiments/target';\n\noptions mprint nocenter;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 1: Define test/control groups from assignment log\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ndata staging.experiment_groups;\n    set raw.assignment_log;\n    where experiment_id = 'EXP-2026-042';\n    length group_label $10;\n\n    /* Validate group assignment */\n    if group_code = 'T' then group_label = 'TEST';\n    else if group_code = 'C' then group_label = 'CONTROL';\n    else delete;\n\n    /* Compute days since exposure */\n    exposure_days = today() - assignment_date;\n\n    /* Only include users with sufficient observation window */\n    if exposure_days >= 14;\n\n    /* Flag users with valid conversion events */\n    if conversion_date ne . then do;\n        converted = 1;\n        days_to_convert = conversion_date - assignment_date;\n    end;\n    else do;\n        converted = 0;\n        days_to_convert = .;\n    end;\n\n    /* Revenue attribution */\n    if revenue = . then revenue = 0;\n\n    format assignment_date conversion_date date9.;\nrun;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 2: Compute group summary statistics\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nproc means data=staging.experiment_groups\n           n mean std stderr min max noprint;\n    class group_label;\n    var converted revenue days_to_convert;\n    output out=staging.group_stats\n        n=n_users\n        mean(converted)=conversion_rate\n        mean(revenue)=avg_revenue\n        std(converted)=std_conversion\n        std(revenue)=std_revenue\n        stderr(converted)=se_conversion\n        stderr(revenue)=se_revenue;\nrun;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 3: Statistical significance testing\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nproc ttest data=staging.experiment_groups\n           alpha=0.05;\n    class group_label;\n    var converted revenue;\n    ods output ttests=staging.ttest_results\n              statistics=staging.ttest_stats;\nrun;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 4: Compute lift and confidence intervals via SQL\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nproc sql;\n    create table staging.lift_analysis as\n    select t.group_label,\n           t.conversion_rate,\n           t.avg_revenue,\n           t.n_users,\n           t.se_conversion,\n           t.se_revenue,\n           /* Conversion lift vs control */\n           case when t.group_label = 'TEST' then\n               (t.conversion_rate - c.conversion_rate) / c.conversion_rate\n           else 0 end as conversion_lift format=percent8.2,\n           /* Revenue lift vs control */\n           case when t.group_label = 'TEST' then\n               (t.avg_revenue - c.avg_revenue) / c.avg_revenue\n           else 0 end as revenue_lift format=percent8.2,\n           /* 95% CI lower bound for conversion difference */\n           case when t.group_label = 'TEST' then\n               (t.conversion_rate - c.conversion_rate)\n               - 1.96 * sqrt(t.se_conversion**2 + c.se_conversion**2)\n           else 0 end as conv_ci_lower,\n           /* 95% CI upper bound for conversion difference */\n           case when t.group_label = 'TEST' then\n               (t.conversion_rate - c.conversion_rate)\n               + 1.96 * sqrt(t.se_conversion**2 + c.se_conversion**2)\n           else 0 end as conv_ci_upper\n    from staging.group_stats(where=(_type_=1)) as t,\n         staging.group_stats(where=(_type_=1 and group_label='CONTROL')) as c;\nquit;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 5: Flag winners/losers and compute minimum detectable effect\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ndata staging.experiment_decision;\n    set staging.lift_analysis;\n    where group_label = 'TEST';\n    length winner_flag $12 decision $20;\n\n    /* Merge with t-test p-value */\n    set staging.ttest_results(where=(variable='converted') keep=variable probt);\n\n    /* Statistical significance check */\n    if probt < 0.05 then do;\n        stat_significant = 1;\n        if conversion_lift > 0 then do;\n            winner_flag = 'TEST_WINS';\n            decision = 'LAUNCH';\n        end;\n        else do;\n            winner_flag = 'CONTROL_WINS';\n            decision = 'REVERT';\n        end;\n    end;\n    else do;\n        stat_significant = 0;\n        winner_flag = 'INCONCLUSIVE';\n        decision = 'EXTEND_OR_STOP';\n    end;\n\n    /* Minimum Detectable Effect (MDE) for the given sample size */\n    /* MDE = 2.8 * sqrt(p*(1-p) / n) for 80% power, alpha=0.05 */\n    baseline_rate = conversion_rate\n                  - (conversion_lift * conversion_rate / (1 + conversion_lift));\n    mde = 2.8 * sqrt(baseline_rate * (1 - baseline_rate) / n_users);\n\n    format mde percent8.4 probt pvalue6.4;\nrun;\n\n/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   STEP 6: Experiment conclusion report dataset\n   ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ndata tgt.experiment_report;\n    length experiment_id $20 metric_name $30 metric_value 8\n           conclusion $100;\n\n    set staging.experiment_decision;\n\n    experiment_id = 'EXP-2026-042';\n    report_date = today();\n\n    /* Output key metrics as name-value pairs */\n    metric_name = 'CONVERSION_LIFT';\n    metric_value = conversion_lift;\n    conclusion = catx(' ', 'Conversion lift:', put(conversion_lift, percent8.2),\n                       '| p-value:', put(probt, pvalue6.4));\n    output;\n\n    metric_name = 'REVENUE_LIFT';\n    metric_value = revenue_lift;\n    conclusion = catx(' ', 'Revenue lift:', put(revenue_lift, percent8.2));\n    output;\n\n    metric_name = 'SAMPLE_SIZE';\n    metric_value = n_users;\n    conclusion = catx(' ', 'Test group size:', put(n_users, comma10.));\n    output;\n\n    metric_name = 'MDE';\n    metric_value = mde;\n    conclusion = catx(' ', 'Minimum Detectable Effect:', put(mde, percent8.4));\n    output;\n\n    metric_name = 'DECISION';\n    metric_value = (decision = 'LAUNCH');\n    conclusion = catx(' ', 'Final decision:', decision, '| Winner:', winner_flag);\n    output;\n\n    keep experiment_id report_date metric_name metric_value conclusion;\n    format report_date date9.;\nrun;\n-gold_meta44]

------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 1: Environment Setup                                        */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Source operational database */\nlibname raw '/data/source/operational' access=readonly;\n\n/* ETL staging area */\nlibname */\n%let etl_run_id = %sysfunc(datetime(), datetime20.);\n%let batch_size = 50000;\n%let default_watermark = 01JAN2020;\n\noptions mprint mlogic symbolgen compress=yes;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 2: Incremental Load Macro Definition                        */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Macro for delta-load with watermark-based change detection */\n%macro incremental_load(src_lib=, src_table=, tgt_lib=, tgt_table=,\n                        key_col=, ts_col=modified_dt);\n\n    %put NOTE: ================================================;\n    %put NOTE: Processing &src_lib..&src_table -> &tgt_lib..&tgt_table;\n    %put NOTE: Key: &key_col | Timestamp column: &ts_col;\n    %put NOTE: ================================================;\n\n    /* Retrieve last successful watermark from control table */\n    %local watermark delta_count;\n    %let watermark = ;\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select put(max(watermark_ts), datetime20.)\n            into :watermark trimmed\n        from ctl.etl_watermarks\n        where table_name = "&tgt_table";\n    quit;\n\n    /* Determine load type based on watermark existence */\n    %if &watermark = %then %do;\n        /* ------- INITIAL LOAD: no prior watermark found ------- */\n        %put NOTE: No watermark found - performing INITIAL LOAD for &tgt_table;\n\n        data staging.delta_&tgt_table;\n            set &src_lib..&src_table;\n            /* Add ETL tracking metadata */\n            etl_load_dt = datetime();\n            etl_run_id = "&etl_run_id";\n            etl_load_type = "INITIAL";\n            format etl_load_dt datetime20.;\n        run;\n    %end;\n    %else %do;\n        /* ------- INCREMENTAL LOAD: extract only changed records ------- */\n        %put NOTE: Watermark &watermark found - INCREMENTAL LOAD for &tgt_table;\n\n        data staging.delta_&tgt_table;\n            set &src_lib..&src_table;\n            where &ts_col > input("&watermark", datetime20.);\n            /* Add ETL tracking metadata */\n            etl_load_dt = datetime();\n            etl_run_id = "&etl_run_id";\n            etl_load_type = "INCREMENTAL";\n            format etl_load_dt datetime20.;\n        run;\n    %end;\n\n    /* Count extracted delta records */\n    proc sql noprint;\n        select count(*) into :delta_count trimmed\n        from staging.delta_&tgt_table;\n    quit;\n\n    %put NOTE: Extracted &delta_count records for &tgt_table;\n\n    /* Upsert: remove stale rows then insert fresh delta */\n    proc sql;\n        delete from &tgt_lib..&tgt_table\n        where &key_col in (\n            select &key_col from staging.delta_&tgt_table\n        );\n\n        insert into &tgt_lib..&tgt_table\n        select * from staging.delta_&tgt_table;\n    quit;\n\n    /* Update watermark control table */\n    proc sql;\n        insert into ctl.etl_watermarks\n            (table_name, watermark_ts, records_loaded, load_type, load_dt)\n        select "&tgt_table",\n               max(&ts_col),\n               &delta_count,\n               case when "&watermark" = ""\n                    then "INITIAL" else "INCREMENTAL" end,\n               datetime()\n        from staging.delta_&tgt_table;\n    quit;\n\n    %put NOTE: Watermark updated for &tgt_table;\n\n%mend incremental_load;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 3: Batch Processing via Loop                                */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Table configuration for batch ETL */\n%let table_count = 4;\n%let tbl1_src = customers;  %let tbl1_tgt = dim_customer;  %let tbl1_key = customer_id;\n%let tbl2_src = products;   %let tbl2_tgt = dim_product;   %let tbl2_key = product_id;\n%let tbl3_src = orders;     %let tbl3_tgt = fact_orders;   %let tbl3_key = order_id;\n%let tbl4_src = shipments;  %let tbl4_tgt = fact_shipping; %let tbl4_key = shipment_id;\n\n/* Driver macro: loop over configured tables and invoke ETL */\n%macro run_etl_batch;\n    %do i = 1 %to &table_count;\n        %put NOTE: --- Processing table &i of &table_count ---;\n\n        %incremental_load(\n            src_lib   = raw,\n            src_table = &&tbl&i._src,\n            tgt_lib   = tgt,\n            tgt_table = &&tbl&i._tgt,\n            key_col   = &&tbl&i._key,\n            ts_col    = */\n%if &syserr > 0 %then %do;\n    %put ERROR: ETL batch failed (SYSERR=&syserr). Halting validation.;\n    data staging.etl_error_log;\n        length process $50 error_msg $200;\n        process = "INCREMENTAL_ETL";\n        error_msg = "Batch failed with SYSERR=&syserr";\n        error_dt = datetime();\n        format error_dt datetime20.;\n    run;\n%end;\n%else %do;\n    %put NOTE: ETL batch completed without errors;\n%end;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 5: Load Summary Record                                      */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Create audit record for this ETL run */\ndata tgt.etl_load_summary;\n    length batch_id $30 load_status $20;\n    batch_id = "&etl_run_id";\n    load_dt = datetime();\n    tables_processed = &table_count;\n    load_status = "COMPLETE";\n    format load_dt datetime20.;\nrun;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 6: Post-Load Validation                                     */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Validate target table row counts after load */\nproc sql;\n    title "Post-Load Target Table Counts";\n    select "dim_customer" as table_name, count(*) as row_count\n        from tgt.dim_customer\n    union all\n    select "dim_product", count(*)\n        from tgt.dim_product\n    union all\n    select "fact_orders", count(*)\n        from tgt.fact_orders\n    union all\n    select "fact_shipping", count(*)\n        from tgt.fact_shipping;\nquit;\n\ntitle;\n\n/*  Longitudinal panel analysis across multiple survey waves\n * Author:      Survey Analytics Team\n * Created:     2026-02-19\n * Environment: SAS 9.4M5+\n * Description: Processes multiple survey *****************************************************************************/\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 1: Environment Setup                                        */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Panel survey data library */\nlibname raw '/data/survey/raw' access=readonly;\n\n/* Staging and analysis output */\nlibname staging '/data/survey/staging';\nlibname tgt '/data/survey/analysis';\n\n/* Wave configuration */\n%let total_waves = 4;\n%let wave1_date = 01JAN2024;\n%let wave2_date = 01APR2024;\n%let wave3_date = 01JUL2024;\n%let wave4_date = 01OCT2024;\n\noptions mprint mlogic symbolgen nocenter;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 2: Wave Processing Macro                                    */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Macro to extract and summarize a single survey wave */\n%macro process_wave(wave_num=, wave_date=);\n\n    %put NOTE: =============================================;\n    %put NOTE: Processing Wave &wave_num (date: &wave_date);\n    %put NOTE: =============================================;\n\n    /* Check if wave data exists before processing */\n    %let dsid = %sysfunc(open(raw.survey_responses));\n    %if &dsid = 0 %then %do;\n        %put WARNING: Cannot open raw.survey_responses for wave &wave_num;\n        %put WARNING: Skipping wave &wave_num processing;\n        %goto wave_exit;\n    %end;\n    %let rc = %sysfunc(close(&dsid));\n\n    /* Extract respondents for this specific wave */\n    data staging.wave_&wave_num;\n        set raw.survey_responses;\n        where wave_id = &wave_num\n          and response_date >= "&wave_date"d;\n\n        /* Derive composite satisfaction score */\n        satisfaction_score = mean(of q1_rating q2_rating q3_rating\n                                    q4_rating q5_rating);\n\n        /* Classification of engagement level */\n        length engagement $15;\n        if satisfaction_score >= 4.0 then engagement = "HIGH";\n        else if satisfaction_score >= 2.5 then engagement = "MODERATE";\n        else engagement = "LOW";\n\n        /* Track completion rate */\n        array questions{5} q1_rating q2_rating q3_rating\n                           q4_rating q5_rating;\n        items_answered = 0;\n        do j = 1 to 5;\n            if questions{j} > 0 then items_answered + 1;\n        end;\n        completion_pct = (items_answered / 5) * 100;\n\n        wave_label = "Wave &wave_num";\n        format response_date date9.;\n        drop j;\n    run;\n\n    /* Wave-level descriptive statistics */\n    proc means data=staging.wave_&wave_num\n               n mean std min max median maxdec=2;\n        title "Wave &wave_num Summary Statistics";\n        var satisfaction_score completion_pct\n            q1_rating q2_rating q3_rating q4_rating q5_rating;\n        output out=staging.stats_wave_&wave_num\n            n=n_respondents\n            mean=avg_satisfaction\n            std=sd_satisfaction;\n    run;\n\n    %wave_exit:\n    %put NOTE: Wave &wave_num processing complete;\n\n%mend process_wave;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 3: Process All Waves via Loop                               */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Driver macro to iterate over all configured waves */\n%macro run_all_waves;\n    %do w = 1 %to &total_waves;\n        %process_wave(\n            wave_num = &w,\n            wave_date = &&wave&w._date\n        );\n    %end;\n%mend run_all_waves;\n\n/* Execute wave processing */\n%run_all_waves;\n\n/* Open-code conditional: verify all waves processed */\n%if &syserr > 0 %then %do;\n    %put ERROR: Wave processing encountered errors (SYSERR=&syserr);\n          w2.satisfaction_score as score_w2,\n           w3.satisfaction_score as score_w3,\n           w4.satisfaction_score as score_w4,\n           w1.engagement as engage_w1,\n           w4.engagement as engage_w4,\n           /* Participation tracking */\n           (w1.respondent_id is not null) as in_wave1,\n           (w2.respondent_id is not null) as in_wave2,\n           (w3.respondent_id is not null) as in_wave3,\n           (w4.respondent_id is not null) as in_wave4\n    from staging.wave_1 as w1\n    full join staging.wave_2 as w2\n        on w1.respondent_id = w2.respondent_id\n    full join staging.wave_3 as w3\n        on coalesce(w1.respondent_id, w2.respondent_id)\n           = w3.respondent_id\n    full join staging.wave_4 as w4\n        on coalesce(w1.respondent_id, w2.respondent_id,\n                    w3.respondent_id) = w4.respondent_id\n    order by calculated respondent_id;\nquit;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 5: Longitudinal Change Analysis                             */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Compute wave-over-wave changes using LAG function */\ndata tgt.wave_changes;\n    set staging.longitudinal_panel;\n\n    /* Count number of waves each respondent participated in */\n    waves_participated = sum(in_wave1, in_wave2, in_wave3, in_wave4);\n\n    /* Calculate overall trajectory */\n    if score_w1 > 0 and score_w4 > 0 then\n        overall_change = score_w4 - score_w1;\n    else\n        overall_change = .;\n\n    /* Classify trajectory */\n    length trajectory $15;\n    if overall_change > 0.5 then trajectory = "IMPROVING";\n    else if overall_change < -0.5 then trajectory = "DECLINING";\n    else if overall_change ne . then trajectory = "STABLE";\n    else trajectory = "INSUFFICIENT";\n\n    /* Flag attrition risk: participated in wave 1 but not wave 4 */\n    if in_wave1 = 1 and in_wave4 = 0 then attrition_flag = 1;\n    else attrition_flag = 0;\n\n    format overall_change 8.2;\nrun;\n\n/* Trajectory distribution report */\nproc freq data=tgt.wave_changes;\n    title "Respondent Trajectory Distribution";\n    tables trajectory / nocum;\n    tables trajectory * waves_participated / nocum nopercent;\nrun;\n\ntitle;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 6: Cleanup                                                  */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\nlibname raw clear;\nlibname staging clear;\n\n%put NOTE: Longitudinal analysis completed at %sysfunc(datetime(), datetime20.);\n-gold_meta48]

Uses parameterized\n *              macro with loop-driven batch processing for multiple tables.\n *****************************************************************************/\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 1: Environment Setup                                        */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Current production snapshot */\nlibname curr '/data/production/current' access=readonly;\n\n/* Previous day snapshot for comparison */\nlibname prev '/data/production/previous' access=readonly;\n\n/* Audit trail output library */\nlibname audit '/data/governance/audit_trail';\n\n/* Staging library for intermediate results */\nlibname staging '/data/governance/staging';\n\n/* Audit control parameters */\n%let audit_date = %sysfunc(today(), date9.);\n%let audit_user = %sysget(USERNAME);\n%let log_level = DETAIL;\n\noptions mprint mlogic symbolgen nocenter;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 2: Audit Detection Macro                                    */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Macro to detect changes between current and previous snapshots */\n%macro audit_table(table_name=, pk_col=);\n\n    %put NOTE: =============================================;\n    %put NOTE: Auditing table: &table_name;\n    %put NOTE: Primary key: &pk_col;\n    %put NOTE: Audit date: &audit_date;\n    %put NOTE: =============================================;\n\n    /* Step 1: Identify new records (INSERTs) - in current but not previous */\n    proc sql;\n        create table staging.ins_&table_name as\n        select c.*,\n               "INSERT" as change_type length=10,\n               "&audit_date"d as audit_dt format=date9.\n        from curr.&table_name as c\n        left join prev.&table_name as p\n            on c.&pk_col = p.&pk_col\n        where p.&pk_col is null;\n    quit;\n\n    /* Step 2: Identify removed records (DELETEs) - in previous but not current */\n    proc sql;\n        create table staging.del_&table_name as\n        select p.*,\n               "DELETE" as change_type length=10,\n               "&audit_date"d as audit_dt format=date9.\n        from prev.&table_name as p\n        left join curr.&table_name as c\n            on p.&pk_col = c.&pk_col\n        where c.&pk_col is null;\n    quit;\n\n    /* Step 3: Identify modified records (UPDATEs) via checksum comparison */\n    proc sql;\n        create table staging.upd_&table_name as\n        select c.*,\n               "UPDATE" as change_type length=10,\n               "&audit_date"d as audit_dt format=date9.\n        from curr.&table_name as c\n        inner join prev.&table_name as p\n            on c.&pk_col = p.&pk_col\n        where md5(cats(of c.:)) ne md5(cats(of p.:));\n    quit;\n\n    /* Step 4: Combine all detected changes into unified audit log */\n    data staging.changes_&table_name;\n        set staging.ins_&table_name\n            staging.del_&table_name\n            staging.upd_&table_name;\n\n        /* Add audit metadata */\n        length table_source $50 audit_user $30;\n        table_source = "&table_name";\n        audit_user = "&audit_user";\n        audit_seq = _n_;\n\n        format audit_dt date9.;\n    run;\n\n    /* Conditional logging based on log level */\n    %if &log_level = DETAIL %then %do;\n        /* Detailed per-record logging */\n        proc sql noprint;\n            select count(*) into :n_ins trimmed\n            from staging.ins_&table_name;\n            select count(*) into :n_del trimmed\n            from staging.del_&table_name;\n            select count(*) into :n_upd trimmed\n            from staging.upd_&table_name;\n        quit;\n\n        %put NOTE: &table_name audit results:;\n        %put NOTE:   Inserts: &n_ins;\n        %put NOTE:   Deletes: &n_del;\n        %put NOTE:   Updates: &n_upd;\n    %end;\n    %else %do;\n        /* Summary-only logging */\n        proc sql noprint;\n            select count(*) into :n_total trimmed\n            from staging.changes_&table_name;\n        quit;\n        %put NOTE: &table_name - Total changes: &n_total;\n    %end;\n\n%mend audit_table;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 3: Batch Audit via Loop                                     */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Configure tables to monitor */\n%let n_tables = 5;\n%let tab1 = customer_master;  %let pk1 = customer_id;\n%let tab2 = product_catalog;  %let pk2 = product_id;\n%let tab3 = order_header;     %let pk3 = order_id;\n%let tab4 = supplier_info;    %let pk4 = supplier_id;\n%let tab5 = employee_roster;  %let pk5 = employee_id;\n\n/* Driver macro: iterate over all monitored tables */\n%macro run_audit_batch;\n    %do t = 1 %to &n_tables;\n        %put NOTE: --- Audit batch item &t of &n_tables ---;\n        %audit_table(\n            table_name = &&tab&t,\n            pk_col     = &&pk&t\n        );\n    %end;\n%mend run_audit_batch;\n\n/* Execute the batch audit */\n%run_audit_batch;\n\n/* Open-code conditional: verify audit batch completed */\n%if &syserr > 0 %then %do;\n    %put ERROR: Audit batch encountered errors (SYSERR=&syserr);\n    %put ERROR: Some tables may not have been audited;\n%end;\n%else %do;\n    %put NOTE: Audit batch completed for all &n_tables tables;\n%end;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 4: Consolidated Audit Report                                */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Merge all table-level change logs into master audit trail */\ndata audit.master_audit_trail;\n    set staging.changes_customer_master\n        staging.changes_product_catalog\n        staging.changes_order_header\n        staging.changes_supplier_info\n        staging.changes_employee_roster;\n\n    /* Global sequence number */\n    master_seq = _n_;\nrun;\n\n/* Change type distribution by table */\nproc freq data=audit.master_audit_trail;\n    title "Audit Trail Summary - &audit_date";\n    tables table_source * change_type / nocum nopercent;\n    tables change_type / nocum;\nrun;\n\n/* Top tables by change volume */\nproc sql;\n    title "Change Volume by Table";\n    select table_source,\n           change_type,\n           count(*) as change_count\n    from audit.master_audit_trail\n    group by table_source, change_type\n    order by calculated change_count desc;\nquit;\n\ntitle;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n/* SECTION 5: Cleanup                                                  */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */\n\nlibname curr clear;\nlibname prev clear;\nlibname staging clear;\n\n%put NOTE: Audit trail generation completed at
